.mana/migration-conflicts/267-fix-native-imp-worker-openai-route-failure-when-sp.md.txt:19:Investigate and fix the native imp worker/provider path so spawned unit runs do not fail on the OpenAI Responses route with `HTTP 400 Bad Request: {"detail":"Instructions are required"}` when no explicit `system_prompt` is passed. Repro came from using the native `imp` tool in unit mode; the same work succeeded when forced onto `provider=openai-codex` with a non-empty `system_prompt`. Inspect `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/imp.rs`, `imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs`, `imp/crates/imp-core/src/imp_session.rs`, and `imp/crates/imp-llm/src/providers/openai.rs` / `openai_codex.rs` to determine why worker runs can reach the OpenAI route with empty instructions and whether the fix belongs in system-prompt assembly, provider routing, or request building. Keep the fix small, add a regression test if practical, and verify with focused imp-core/imp-llm tests.
.mana/50.23-specify-cli-first-approval-policy-and-blocked-stat.md:20:Write a rebuild-spec doc for CLI-first approval policy in imp, sequenced after visible planning and grounded in the existing review/approval docs. The note should define how typed allow/ask/block policy should surface in a CLI-first runtime across `imp chat`, `imp run`, and machine-readable modes: inline ask/confirm flows for human shells, structured approval-required/blocked results for machine consumers, policy scope layering (built-in defaults, user config, project config, temporary session override), and the boundary between imp runtime enforcement versus mana durable approval/review records. Explain why this is deeper than confirmation UX and why it depends on visible plan/checkpoint/evidence context first. Keep scope architectural/spec-level, not implementation.
.mana/50.23-specify-cli-first-approval-policy-and-blocked-stat.md:25:- links to review/approval durability without collapsing imp policy into mana storage
.mana/50.23-specify-cli-first-approval-policy-and-blocked-stat.md:36:- docs/rebuild/mana-between-turn-review-affordance.md
.imp/workflows/remove-mana-vocabulary/workflow.yaml:2:id: remove-mana-vocabulary
.imp/workflows/remove-mana-vocabulary/workflow.yaml:3:title: Remove mana vocabulary from imp codebase
.imp/workflows/remove-mana-vocabulary/workflow.yaml:15:    Remove the obsolete mana product/config vocabulary from active imp code and replace it with workflow terminology, without preserving compatibility aliases or legacy config names.
.imp/workflows/remove-mana-vocabulary/workflow.yaml:17:    Keep imp's product and code surface coherent: workflows are the durable orchestration primitive, and old mana references are bloat that make settings, config, docs, and runtime code harder to understand.
.imp/workflows/remove-mana-vocabulary/workflow.yaml:19:    - Preserve compatibility for old `mana` config keys, type names, action names, or UI labels.
.imp/workflows/remove-mana-vocabulary/workflow.yaml:20:    - Redesign workflow behavior beyond renaming/removing obsolete mana vocabulary.
.imp/workflows/remove-mana-vocabulary/workflow.yaml:24:      text: Active Rust code, config structs, settings UI, and tests use workflow terminology instead of mana terminology.
.imp/workflows/remove-mana-vocabulary/workflow.yaml:27:        - mana_reference_inventory_complete
.imp/workflows/remove-mana-vocabulary/workflow.yaml:31:      text: User config uses workflow/workflows keys and types; old mana compatibility aliases are not retained.

.mana/migration-conflicts/44-rethink-imp-extensions-as-guest-runtimes-with-opti.md.txt:9:acceptance: 'There is a coherent design captured in mana for: (1) guest-runtime extension substrate, (2) script tool boundaries and policy, and (3) a phased implementation path for imp.'
.mana/migration-conflicts/44-rethink-imp-extensions-as-guest-runtimes-with-opti.md.txt:13:  Loaded and followed the `mana` skill before authoring units. Created a parent epic and decomposed it into three concrete child jobs: 44.1 substrate proposal, 44.2 script-tool boundaries/policy, and 44.3 phased implementation plan depending on the first two. Each unit includes inspected file context, scope boundaries, and targeted verify gates so later workers can execute cold without redoing the design framing.
.mana/migration-conflicts/44-rethink-imp-extensions-as-guest-runtimes-with-opti.md.txt:21:verify: test -n "guest-runtime-design-tracked-in-mana"

.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:3:title: Revise post-turn assessment to use structured mana evidence
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:4:slug: revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:9:acceptance: Post-turn assessment relies on structured mana action class/review evidence where available, avoids treating planning/checkpoint mana writes as task completion, and avoids nudging unnecessary mana creation for generic planning language or conversational/meta discussion when no durable state changed. Existing continuation behavior has regression tests.
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:13:  Verify command in unit is invalid for cargo because multiple test filters were passed as positional args: `cargo test -p imp-core post_turn mana_externalization execution_debt` fails with unexpected argument. Will use targeted individual filters and full `agent:: --no-run`/`cargo check` as replacement evidence, then update unit verify if needed.
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:17:  Implemented structured post-turn mana evidence improvements. Execution debt/evidence helpers now use `ManaActionClass` instead of raw action groups. Externalization nudge now requires durable-state or explicit mana signals rather than generic plan-like words, reducing over-eager mana creation/checkpointing. System prompt updated to reinforce anti-overuse and checkpoint-vs-completion semantics. Verify command corrected because original multi-filter cargo command was invalid. Verification passed: `cargo test -p imp-core mana_externalization`, `cargo test -p imp-core execution_debt`, `cargo test -p imp-core agent:: --no-run`, plus earlier `cargo check -p imp-core`.
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:22:  error: unexpected argument 'mana_externalization' found
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:30:- mana
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:36:verify: cargo test -p imp-core post_turn mana_externalization execution_debt
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:50:    error: unexpected argument 'mana_externalization' found
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:60:- crates/imp-core/src/mana_review.rs
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:80:Goal: revise post-turn continuation/stop heuristics to use structured mana loop evidence and reduce general overuse of mana creation/checkpointing.
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:82:Current state: `tool_results_indicate_execution_debt`, `tool_results_indicate_execution_evidence`, `tool_results_indicate_work_completed`, and `mana_review_stop_reason` combine raw `ToolResult.details.action` checks, `TurnManaReview`, and assistant text parsing. This can make mana writes feel like completion bookkeeping and can also trigger unnecessary externalization nudges.
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:84:Important clarification: meta-discussion about mana behavior was only one example of overuse. The problem is broader: imp should not create/update mana reflexively for every plan-like sentence, conversational aside, small one-pass task, or completion bookkeeping. It should externalize only when durable project/work state actually changed or when mana-backed orchestration/handoff/recovery is needed.
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:89:1. Thread mana action class/policy evidence from tool execution into post-turn assessment, or derive it through the classification API from tool result details.
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:90:2. Replace raw action string groups in execution debt/evidence/work-completed helpers with `ManaActionClass`-based logic.
.mana/330.5-revise-post-turn-assessment-to-use-structured-mana.md:93:5. Revisit `should_queue_mana_externalization_follow_up` so it only nudges when the assistant produced durable structure that should survive the turn and did not externalize it. Avoid nudges for small one-pass work, completion bookkeeping, meta-discussion without durable decisions, or generic planning language with no durable project state change.

.mana/259-audit-panic-usage-and-detached-task-failure-policy.md:16:  Analysis pass completed. Findings: (1) Most `panic!` sites reported by grep are inside `#[cfg(test)]` modules in `imp-core::{context,compaction,imp_session,tools::mana,tools::bash}`, `imp-cli::lib`, and `imp-tui::app`; these are low priority. (2) Important production detach/supervision patterns: `imp-tui::app` supervises `agent_task` and `login_task` via `JoinHandle::is_finished` + await and surfaces `JoinError` as runtime signals; `imp-core::imp_session` awaits `agent_task` and converts panic to `Error::Config("Agent task panicked: ...")`; `imp-cli` RPC mode supervises spawned agent runs via stored join handles and emits protocol errors on join failure. (3) Real fire-and-forget runtime tasks remain in `imp-core::hooks` (non-blocking shell hooks spawned and ignored), `imp-core::retry::stream_with_retry` (spawned stream forwarder with no external join handle), provider streaming internals in `imp-llm::{anthropic,openai,openai_compat,google}` (spawn and forward bytes/events to channels), and small UI follow-up tasks in `imp-tui::app` / `imp-core::tools::mana`. (4) Production unwrap/unreachable worth cleanup: `imp-cli::run_interactive` still has `unwrap_or_else(|| SessionManager::new(...).unwrap())`; `imp-core::tools::bash` uses `child.stdout.take().unwrap()` and `child.stderr.take().unwrap()` after piping configuration; `imp-core::tools::extend` has `_ => unreachable!()` in an action match that can be replaced with an explicit error branch even if logically impossible. (5) Policy recommendation: use `Result` for world/external failures, reserve panic for invariants/bugs and tests, and require supervision or explicit loss-tolerance for spawned tasks. (6) Important nuance: `catch_unwind` catches unwinds but the panic hook still fires first, so interactive terminal protection may still require a minimal panic-hook restore for panics escaping through the TUI while the alt-screen is active.
.mana/355-audit-workflownightlyrelease-regressions-against-m.md:3:title: Audit workflow/nightly/release regressions against mana and commits
.mana/355-audit-workflownightlyrelease-regressions-against-m.md:10:acceptance: Report includes branch topology, relevant mana units/claims, commit ranges, changed areas, test coverage gaps, suspected regressions, and recommended verification/fixes.
.mana/355-audit-workflownightlyrelease-regressions-against-m.md:20:  - Workflow integration range 290a362..2267023 is huge: ~130 files, ~39k insertions/~5.9k deletions including agent loop split, reference monitor, workflow contract, verification runner, trace/evidence, mana_next ledger, TUI runtime/off-input changes, tool schema rewrites, and removal of imp tool.
.mana/355-audit-workflownightlyrelease-regressions-against-m.md:27:  2. `imp run`/mana worker contract drift: mana unit 28.1.3 documented `imp --mode json run <unit-id> --mana-dir <path> --defer-verify` verified in /Users/asher/tower, but current imp release includes large independent workflow controller changes. Need explicit CLI contract test in imp and mana together.
.mana/355-audit-workflownightlyrelease-regressions-against-m.md:30:  5. Packaging/build metadata: release uses published mana crates while nightly uses workspace/path-ish state; this can mask local mana behavior regressions.
.mana/355-audit-workflownightlyrelease-regressions-against-m.md:35:  - Add/execute an integration smoke for `imp --mode json run <unit> --mana-dir <tmp> --defer-verify` using a tiny mana graph.

Produce an evidence-backed audit without modifying code.

Treat `50.10.3.1` as the live execution carrier for the remaining setup verification until that lookup inconsistency is reconciled.
.mana/365.8-decide-mana-harness-product-identity-daemon-topolo.md:3:title: Decide mana harness product identity, daemon topology, and platform/runtime split
.mana/365.8-decide-mana-harness-product-identity-daemon-topolo.md:4:slug: decide-mana-harness-product-identity-daemon-topolo
.mana/365.8-decide-mana-harness-product-identity-daemon-topolo.md:13:  Planning discussion checkpoint: user clarified this is explicitly a cutting/slimming of imp plus a large update to mana into a daemon-backed harness product. Open questions to resolve in this decision task now include: (1) whether the harness is still called mana or gets a new/more legible product identity; (2) whether mana should become a daemon now; (3) whether the daemon/backend stays Rust; (4) how separate the two products are: imp as runtime, harness as work/mission-control product; (5) whether UX should lean mystical/game-like (Agent Wizard, Agentic Quest, mission/quest language) or stay professional/workbench-oriented. Initial bias to evaluate: keep Rust for core/daemon because existing mana/imp runtime substrate is Rust and needs low-level process/filesystem/concurrency correctness; product name can be separate from internal mana crate/name; game-like language can help if it maps to real work concepts (quests/missions/runs/evidence) but should not obscure professional software-work workflows.
.mana/365.8-decide-mana-harness-product-identity-daemon-topolo.md:17:  External framing source from user strengthens daemon/topology decision: background agents need long-running execution, crash recovery, persistent state, event-driven pause/resume/HITL/cancel, and structured traces. These are daemon/harness concerns more than imp runtime concerns. Product split: imp remains the agent runtime/product; mana/harness likely needs a service/daemon shape to coordinate durable background execution and GUI subscriptions. Naming/product identity should communicate mission-control/orchestration without obscuring that the substrate is durable execution, not an agent-pattern framework.
.mana/365.8-decide-mana-harness-product-identity-daemon-topolo.md:21:  Verification checkpoint after adding Inngest framing: `mana verify 365.8` fails because required deliverable `docs/product/mana-harness-product-identity.md` has not been authored yet. The task remains open. Next action: decide daemon topology, naming/product identity, Rust/backend split, and metaphor guidance in light of background-agent orchestration requirements.
.mana/365.8-decide-mana-harness-product-identity-daemon-topolo.md:27:- mana

.mana/365.8-decide-mana-harness-product-identity-daemon-topolo.md:33:- crates/mana-core
.mana/365.8-decide-mana-harness-product-identity-daemon-topolo.md:37:Resolve the major product/platform fork raised in planning: this pivot is both a cutting/slimming of imp and a large update to mana into a daemon-backed harness product. Decide whether the harness remains named mana, gets a new product name, or uses a codename such as wurk / Agent Wizard / Agentic Quest-style game-like identity.
.mana/365.8-decide-mana-harness-product-identity-daemon-topolo.md:40:- Is the harness product still `mana`, or should mana become the internal/platform name under a more legible product name?
.mana/50.13-plan-extraction-of-shared-fullscreen-consumed-runt.md:20:Write a focused rebuild plan describing how `imp/crates/imp-tui/src/app.rs` should stop owning shared runtime semantics and instead consume explicit shared surfaces. Ground the plan in the current inspected code: `app.rs` currently mixes TUI-local state (`UiMode`, panes, selection, overlays, render caches, sidebar/tool inspection) with runtime-heavy concerns (`AgentBuilder`, `AgentHandle`/`AgentEvent`, `SessionManager`, `Config`, `AuthStore`, `ModelRegistry`, config/auth/personality/setup persistence, and session open/continue logic). The plan should identify the first extraction seams needed so CLI and TUI can share the same runtime/session/config/auth/personality capability layer without redesigning the UI. Include concrete target seams for: shared startup/runtime assembly, shared session truth/projections, shared config/auth/personality/setup operations, and shared event/UI request contracts consumed by `tui_interface.rs`. Keep scope architectural/spec-level, not implementation. Relevant files: `docs/rebuild/imp-tui-adapter-boundary.md`, `imp/crates/imp-tui/src/app.rs`, `imp/crates/imp-tui/src/interactive.rs`, `imp/crates/imp-tui/src/tui_interface.rs`, `imp/crates/imp-cli/src/main.rs`. Acceptance: produces a doc that explicitly contrasts TUI-local presentation state vs shared runtime ownership and names a migration sequence for peeling runtime concerns out of `app.rs` without breaking the current `InteractiveRunner` handoff.

.mana/migration-conflicts/44.1-plan-phased-implementation-of-imp-native-code-inte.md.txt:3:title: Plan phased implementation of imp-native code intelligence and mana evidence promotion
.mana/migration-conflicts/44.1-plan-phased-implementation-of-imp-native-code-inte.md.txt:12:  Decomposed the follow-on plan into four child jobs to keep the delta visible: (1) read-oriented `imp` semantic surfaces, (2) write-oriented semantic actions/policy, (3) durable promotion rules into `mana`, and (4) the `imp`-local subsystem/crate boundary. This externalizes the post-29.2 plan into root mana rather than leaving it only in chat.
.mana/migration-conflicts/44.1-plan-phased-implementation-of-imp-native-code-inte.md.txt:16:  2026-04-09 follow-up externalization: treat `44.1` as the canonical post-`29.2` decomposition for implementation planning. Root scope is intentional because the work crosses the `imp` live-runtime boundary and the `mana` durable-evidence boundary. The visible slices are: `44.1.1` read-oriented semantic inspection in `imp`; `44.1.2` guarded write-oriented semantic actions/policy; `44.1.3` durable promotion rules for diagnostics/verify evidence/facts into `mana`; `44.1.4` the `imp`-local subsystem/crate boundary. Source artifact: `docs/design/code-intelligence-strategy.md`.
.mana/migration-conflicts/44.1-plan-phased-implementation-of-imp-native-code-inte.md.txt:20:  Externalized follow-on plan confirmed against the completed design note `docs/design/code-intelligence-strategy.md`. This epic is now the durable decomposition of that note's implementation-planning recommendations: Phase 1 read-oriented semantic inspection in `imp`, later guarded write-oriented semantic actions, selective durable promotion of outcomes into `mana`, and an `imp`-local subsystem seam for code intelligence. Keep this root-scoped because it spans the mana ↔ imp contract but does not assign live orchestration to `mana`.
.mana/migration-conflicts/44.1-plan-phased-implementation-of-imp-native-code-inte.md.txt:36:  This preserves the delta visibly in mana instead of leaving the later implementation structure only in `docs/design/imp-write-oriented-code-intelligence-policy.md`.
.mana/migration-conflicts/44.1-plan-phased-implementation-of-imp-native-code-inte.md.txt:44:  Conversation-time externalization update: the completed read-oriented plan from `docs/design/imp-read-oriented-code-intelligence-plan.md` is now decomposed in root mana under new epic `44.1.6` so the implementation sequence is durable and visible. The canonical Phase 1 read order is now explicit in the graph: `44.1.6.1` shared normalization envelopes -> `44.1.6.2` diagnostics + AST alignment -> `44.1.6.3` document symbols + go-to-definition -> `44.1.6.4` find references + workspace symbols -> `44.1.6.5` hover + signature enrichment. All slices stay read-only, keep runtime orchestration in `imp`, preserve AST structural inspection and semantic inspection as complementary rather than merged, and avoid locking a single protocol/backend choice at planning time.
.mana/migration-conflicts/44.1-plan-phased-implementation-of-imp-native-code-inte.md.txt:49:  - Code-intelligence outputs are transient by default in `imp`; `mana` should receive only promoted consequences.
.mana/migration-conflicts/44.1-plan-phased-implementation-of-imp-native-code-inte.md.txt:57:  Resolved decomposition guidance after child 44.1.3: durable promotion policy is now specified. Follow-on planning should treat evidence-promotion as a narrow mana-facing seam and keep code-intelligence orchestration, freshness, and LSP lifecycle imp-local. Any later shared types should be limited to promoted verify/check summaries and diagnostic blocker summaries.
.mana/migration-conflicts/44.1-plan-phased-implementation-of-imp-native-code-inte.md.txt:74:  Carry forward the settled boundary from `44.1.4`: prefer an imp-local `imp-core/src/codeintel/` seam first; keep tool entrypoints thin in `tools/`; do not create a shared live `mana`/`imp` code-intelligence runtime crate; if any shared boundary appears later, keep it limited to narrow durable promoted-evidence/result summaries rather than live runtime orchestration.
.mana/migration-conflicts/44.1-plan-phased-implementation-of-imp-native-code-inte.md.txt:78:  Planning delta from child `44.1.5.1`: the write-oriented semantic-action branch now has an implementation-facing typed policy target in mana. The canonical shape is an imp-local `SemanticWritePolicyModel` with closed action classes (`RenameSymbol`, `OrganizeImports`, typed `CodeAction`), backend/language capability tiers, explicit trust/risk signals, separate approval vs verify posture fields, a typed escalation/risk input record, and mandatory preview/result metadata envelopes for policy evaluation. Carry this forward as the required contract before any semantic-write implementation units are created.
.mana/migration-conflicts/44.1-plan-phased-implementation-of-imp-native-code-inte.md.txt:82:  Visible root-scope delta: the write-oriented semantic-action plan under `44.1.5` now has one completed implementation-facing planning slice. `44.1.5.1` is the canonical typed semantic-write policy model contract and should be treated as the prerequisite for later execution-contract work (`44.1.5.2`) and backend/language allowlist work (`44.1.5.3`). This keeps the decomposition explicit at root scope for cross-project mana↔imp planning rather than burying the typed-policy step inside the doc alone.

.mana/280.2-adopt-dirac-inspired-code-intelligence-and-precise.md:33:Goal: Turn the Dirac comparison into executable imp tooling specs that workers can implement cold. Dirac's strongest inspected advantages are AST/symbol-native code context, hash/anchor-backed edits, same-turn edit batching, diagnostics feedback, and benchmark discipline. imp should adopt those primitives on top of its existing mana/runtime/checkpoint strengths, not clone Dirac's IDE-first architecture.
.mana/280.2-adopt-dirac-inspired-code-intelligence-and-precise.md:38:- imp has `scan`, `read`, `edit`, `multi_edit`, checkpoints, mana, and worker evidence. Local inspection confirmed:
.mana/280.2-adopt-dirac-inspired-code-intelligence-and-precise.md:53:- Specs preserve imp's architecture direction: runtime/mana/checkpoint/evidence first; code-intelligence tools as native/runtime capabilities.

.mana/350-audit-and-improve-imp-tui-responsiveness.md:20:  - Other synchronous UI-thread hotspots found but not yet fixed: @ file finder walks project tree synchronously; /resume lists sessions synchronously; /mana loads mana navigator synchronously; startup skill inspector reads selected skill file synchronously; reload discovers Lua extensions synchronously; status/improve commands run multiple git commands synchronously.
.mana/326-explore-github-repository-search-and-open-source-c.md:24:  - Output: structured web-tool result with concise recommendation, evidence-backed GitHub links, risk flags, and optional mana follow-up tasks.
.mana/326-explore-github-repository-search-and-open-source-c.md:31:  - Integrate with existing `web`, `scan`, `git`, `audit_scan`, and `mana` flows rather than a parallel CLI workflow.

.mana/263.2-classify-mana-core-stderr-writes-for-embedded-risk.md:3:title: Classify mana-core stderr writes for embedded risk
.mana/263.2-classify-mana-core-stderr-writes-for-embedded-risk.md:4:slug: classify-mana-core-stderr-writes-for-embedded-risk
.mana/263.2-classify-mana-core-stderr-writes-for-embedded-risk.md:9:acceptance: Produce a classified inventory for mana-core stderr writes, identify any high-risk embedded paths, and either patch them or record concrete follow-up actions with file-level evidence.
.mana/263.2-classify-mana-core-stderr-writes-for-embedded-risk.md:15:  Mana-core still contains library-level eprintln sites (ctx_assembler warnings, ops/context, post-create/post-update hook warnings, hooks spawn warning). These were not changed in this pass because the immediate user goal was TUI stability in imp. Imp-side embedded runtime crates are now free of eprintln!, which substantially reduces terminal-corrupting side effects while ratatui owns the screen.
.mana/263.2-classify-mana-core-stderr-writes-for-embedded-risk.md:20:  rg: mana/crates/mana-core: IO error for operation on mana/crates/mana-core: No such file or directory (os error 2)
.mana/263.2-classify-mana-core-stderr-writes-for-embedded-risk.md:22:design: Initial scan found 12 mana-core eprintln sites, concentrated in ctx_assembler.rs plus hook/update/create/context paths. create verify-lint was just fixed as a concrete example of the embedded-risk pattern. Prioritize remaining call sites that run during imp tool invocation or context assembly.
.mana/263.2-classify-mana-core-stderr-writes-for-embedded-risk.md:26:- mana
.mana/263.2-classify-mana-core-stderr-writes-for-embedded-risk.md:29:verify: test -f .mana/263.2-classify-mana-core-stderr-writes-for-embedded-risk.md && rg -q '^id:' .mana/263.2-classify-mana-core-stderr-writes-for-embedded-risk.md
.mana/263.2-classify-mana-core-stderr-writes-for-embedded-risk.md:38:  output_snippet: 'rg: mana/crates/mana-core: IO error for operation on mana/crates/mana-core: No such file or directory (os error 2)'
.mana/263.2-classify-mana-core-stderr-writes-for-embedded-risk.md:42:Audit library-level stderr writes in mana-core and identify which ones can leak into imp or other embedded callers versus which are acceptable or need migration to structured diagnostics.

.mana/300.1-add-cratesio-ready-dependency-versions-and-package.md:13:  Completed manifest metadata/dependency patch pass: root workspace metadata now has MIT, repository/homepage/readme/keywords/categories; internal imp workspace deps now have version=0.1.0 path deps; mana-core/mana-cli deps now have version=0.3.0 path deps; all member crates use license.workspace=true and have repository/homepage/readme/keywords/categories metadata; added small crate-level README.md files for imp-llm, imp-core, imp-lua, imp-tui, imp-cli. Verification: cargo metadata shows member crates license MIT and versioned path deps. `cargo package -p imp-llm --allow-dirty` passes. `cargo package -p imp-core --allow-dirty` now advances past req=* but blocks because imp-llm v0.1.0 is not yet on crates.io, expected until imp-llm is published or package verification is run after publish. No cargo publish run.
.mana/300.1-add-cratesio-ready-dependency-versions-and-package.md:23:Patch imp Cargo manifests for crates.io packaging. Add `version = "0.1.0"` to internal workspace path dependencies for imp-llm, imp-core, imp-lua, imp-tui, and imp-cli. Add crates.io metadata where appropriate: repository, homepage/documentation, readme, keywords, categories, and crate-specific descriptions. Preserve root `imp-install` as publish=false. Do not publish crates. Coordinate mana dependency versions only after the mana publishing work provides published versions.

.mana/300.4-replace-imp-local-mana-deps-with-publishedversione.md:4:slug: replace-imp-local-mana-deps-with-publishedversione
.mana/300.4-replace-imp-local-mana-deps-with-publishedversione.md:9:acceptance: imp manifests depend on published/versioned mana crates or document the remaining external mana prerequisite exactly.
.mana/300.4-replace-imp-local-mana-deps-with-publishedversione.md:13:- mana-deps
.mana/300.4-replace-imp-local-mana-deps-with-publishedversione.md:19:Update imp's mana dependencies after the separate mana publishing work provides crate versions. Current imp deps use local path-only mana-core and mana-cli with req=*. Replace/augment with published version requirements while retaining path for local development if desired, e.g. `mana-core = { version = "...", path = "../mana/crates/mana-core" }` and `mana-cli = { version = "...", path = "../mana/crates/mana-cli", package = "mana-cli" }`. Do not modify /Users/asher/mana in this unit.
.mana/37-add-first-class-usage-accounting-and-reporting-to.md:28:2. The canonical future store is session-local structured custom entries in the existing session JSONL, not mana and not a separate global ledger.
.mana/37-add-first-class-usage-accounting-and-reporting-to.md:46:Keep the implementation local to imp. Do not add mana-level storage, do not depend on claude.ai, and do not introduce a separate canonical global usage ledger. A derived cache/index is acceptable later, but not as the source of truth in this epic.
.mana/365.4-specify-the-production-mana-gui-harness-product-sh.md:3:title: Specify the production mana GUI harness product shape
.mana/365.4-specify-the-production-mana-gui-harness-product-sh.md:4:slug: specify-the-production-mana-gui-harness-product-sh
.mana/365.4-specify-the-production-mana-gui-harness-product-sh.md:13:  - Spec states what GUI reads/writes through mana rather than imp.
.mana/365.4-specify-the-production-mana-gui-harness-product-sh.md:19:  Repo Prompt video direction: GUI product spec should include live worker/subagent observability, not just board + chat. The task detail/run monitor should show active workers, dense activity summaries, status/waiting/blocker states, touched files/artifacts, verification progress, and controls where supported (interrupt/cancel/steer). Persistent board/task state remains mana-owned; bounded imp subagents may appear as live runtime activity mirrored into mana events when hosted.
.mana/365.4-specify-the-production-mana-gui-harness-product-sh.md:23:  Correction: do not cite or depend on the Repo Prompt video in the GUI product spec. Keep the generalized requirement: the GUI should expose live worker/run observability—active workers, activity summaries, blockers, touched files, artifacts, verification progress, and supported controls—through mana-owned state.

.mana/28.5.7-render-between-turn-mana-review-packets-across-imp.md:3:title: Render between-turn mana review packets across imp TUI and CLI/RPC surfaces
.mana/28.5.7-render-between-turn-mana-review-packets-across-imp.md:4:slug: render-between-turn-mana-review-packets-across-imp
.mana/28.5.7-render-between-turn-mana-review-packets-across-imp.md:12:  Contract input from 28.5.3: rendering should be a formatting layer over `TurnManaDelta`, not the contract itself. Required visible distinctions are explicit `no_change`, `changed`, and `needs_decision`. Compact render should surface `touched_units`, `proposed_children`, note/decision events, and only ask the user when `unresolved_consequential_choices` is non-empty. Preserve line-oriented compatibility so TUI widget tray and CLI/RPC footer/event flows can render the same underlying packet.
.mana/28.5.7-render-between-turn-mana-review-packets-across-imp.md:16:  Rendering work should consume the canonical TurnManaDelta contract from docs/rebuild/mana-turn-delta-contract.md across TUI and CLI/RPC surfaces. UI adapters own formatting only. They should not re-derive semantics from raw ToolOutput.details. TUI can render a compact widget from anchor_unit/touched_units/proposed_children with explicit no durable changes this turn for no_change and concise unresolved choice surfacing for needs_decision. CLI/RPC should render/emit the same semantics, not a separate contract.
.mana/28.5.7-render-between-turn-mana-review-packets-across-imp.md:20:  Follow-on decomposition from `docs/rebuild/imp-between-turn-mana-review-ui.md`: rendering unit should consume the canonical `TurnManaDelta` and implement the first shippable imp-native surfaces. Concrete split: (1) compact tray renderer in `imp-tui` using `WidgetContent::Lines`, (2) explicit `off` / `compact_on_change` / `pinned` control modes, (3) compact default block with a hard 2-line essential fallback because current `imp-tui/src/app.rs` widget tray is fixed at 5 rows and the `mana` key itself consumes one inner row, (4) expanded textual inspector reusing existing sidebar/detail line rendering rather than introducing a graph browser, (5) CLI/RPC mirror formatting over the same packet semantics, and (6) question gating that asks only when `unresolved_consequential_choices` is non-empty and asks one concise question at a time.
.mana/28.5.7-render-between-turn-mana-review-packets-across-imp.md:24:  Externalized implementation decomposition from `docs/rebuild/imp-between-turn-mana-review-ui.md` into root mana. Planned execution split: (1) add shared imp-core presentation-neutral review rendering + `ManaReviewMode` config (`off` / `compact_on_change` / `pinned`) over canonical `TurnManaDelta`, (2) wire imp-tui to replace per-action mana widget noise with a compact turn-batched widget-tray block plus a sidebar/detail textual inspector, and (3) wire imp-cli/RPC to emit the same compact/footer semantics and preserve question-gating so only consequential unresolved choices trigger an ask. The design constraint from current code is that first ship stays line-oriented (`WidgetContent::Lines`, existing sidebar detail surfaces) and does not require a graph browser or component widgets.
.mana/28.5.7-render-between-turn-mana-review-packets-across-imp.md:28:  Externalized the implementation decomposition from docs/rebuild/imp-between-turn-mana-review-ui.md into concrete root-scope child units. Created 28.5.7.4 for shared imp-core review mode + presentation-neutral TurnManaDelta renderers, 28.5.7.5 for imp-tui widget tray + sidebar textual inspector wiring, and 28.5.7.6 for imp-cli/RPC rendering and consequential-choice gating. This preserves the cross-surface split from the UI spec: shared semantics/formatting first, then TUI adapter, then CLI/RPC adapter.
.mana/28.5.7-render-between-turn-mana-review-packets-across-imp.md:32:  Project-wide blocker review (2026-04-13): the between-turn mana review UI path is currently gated by two shared seams: 28.5.6 (review packet aggregation) and 28.5.7.4 (presentation-neutral TurnManaDelta renderers + ManaReviewMode config). Both need to land before downstream wiring work 28.5.7.5 (imp-tui) and 28.5.7.6 (imp-cli/RPC) can proceed cleanly.

.mana/263-audit-and-isolate-library-level-stderr-writes-that.md:9:acceptance: '1) Inventory non-CLI stderr writes in imp-core, imp-llm, imp-lua, and mana-core with a risk classification for embedded/TUI disruption. 2) For high-risk call sites, replace direct stderr writes with structured errors, UI/status surfaces, returned diagnostics, or tracing/logging that does not corrupt interactive screens. 3) Preserve intentional CLI-facing messaging in imp-cli and mana-cli. 4) Add focused regression tests or evidence for any changed high-risk path. 5) Document residual intentional stderr sites and why they remain acceptable.'
.mana/263-audit-and-isolate-library-level-stderr-writes-that.md:11:  Candidate high-risk files from scan: imp/crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs, session.rs, mana_worker.rs, tools/shell.rs; imp/crates/imp-llm/src/providers/anthropic.rs; imp/crates/imp-lua/src/lib.rs and bridge.rs; mana/crates/mana-core/src/ctx_assembler.rs, ops/context.rs, ops/update.rs, ops/create.rs, hooks.rs.
.mana/263-audit-and-isolate-library-level-stderr-writes-that.md:15:  Scoped the TUI-protection pass to library/runtime stderr writes reachable while ratatui owns the terminal. Initial classification from code inspection: high-risk immediate fixes are imp-core/src/agent.rs: OAuth refresh failure currently eprintlns during live agent execution, and imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs: run_verify_command currently eprintlns failed verify stdout/stderr and is reachable via the imp tool's unit-spawn path. Lower-priority/deferred candidates include imp-core session malformed-line warnings, imp-core shell tool definition load warnings, imp-llm anthropic retry/SSE parse warnings, imp-lua runtime/extension/event-handler warnings, and mana-core ctx_assembler/context/hook post-create/post-update warnings. No eprintln occurrences were found in imp-tui itself.
.mana/263-audit-and-isolate-library-level-stderr-writes-that.md:16:design: 'Current known non-CLI eprintln counts from repo scan: imp-core=5, imp-llm=4, imp-lua=4, mana-core=12. No eprintln occurrences were found in imp-tui itself. Highest immediate suspicion is library/runtime code reachable from imp''s TUI or in-process tool execution. Prior incident: mana-core create verify-lint printed to stderr and broke ratatui flow; fixed by moving detail into structured error text and keeping CLI printing at the CLI layer.'
.mana/263-audit-and-isolate-library-level-stderr-writes-that.md:27:Cross-project follow-up after fixing mana create verify-lint output breaking imp TUI flow. Audit remaining eprintln!/println! usage in library/runtime crates that may be invoked in-process by imp or other embedded callers. Prefer root scope because this touches the mana ↔ imp runtime boundary and output/side-effect expectations across projects.
.mana/44.1-author-guest-runtime-extension-substrate-proposal.md:66:Current state: imp has a runtime-specific Lua path (`lua_tool_loader` in `crates/imp-core/src/builder.rs`, `crates/imp-lua/src/*`), and the current `extend` tool is focused on skills/Lua reference rather than general extension management.
.mana/44.1-author-guest-runtime-extension-substrate-proposal.md:83:- `docs/proposals/imp-memory-architecture-and-mana-boundary.md` (read — proposal style precedent)
.mana/44.1-author-guest-runtime-extension-substrate-proposal.md:84:- `docs/proposals/mana-aware-runtime-context-read-path.md` (read — proposal style precedent)
.mana/266-cross-codebase-review-compare-imp-and-hermes-agent.md:16:  - imp focus: crates/imp-core (agent loop, system_prompt, tools, mana integration, session/compaction, guardrails, worker path), crates/imp-cli, crates/imp-tui, crates/imp-llm, extension seams.
.mana/266-cross-codebase-review-compare-imp-and-hermes-agent.md:45:  Ground findings in inspected code paths, not README claims alone. Because this is cross-project work, keep root-scope mana as source of truth.
.mana/266-cross-codebase-review-compare-imp-and-hermes-agent.md:64:Perform a read-only comparative review of /tower/imp and ~/hermes-agent. Identify architectural, runtime, tooling, context-assembly, extension, evaluation, UX, and operational gaps in imp; identify concrete ideas and existing implementations in hermes-agent worth adopting into imp; separate near-term adoptable patterns from longer-term architectural borrowings; ground findings in inspected files and code paths rather than high-level README claims. Produce a concise written assessment and, if useful, follow-up mana units for high-value adoption opportunities.

.mana/296-commit-imp-settings-shell-cleanup-separately.md:194:Prepare a focused commit for the /settings shell cleanup. Current state: working tree contains relevant changes in crates/imp-core/src/config.rs and crates/imp-tui/src/views/settings.rs, plus unrelated/pre-existing changes in README.md and .mana/28.1-make-imp-run-the-canonical-mana-worker-runtime-whi.md that should not be included unless explicitly requested. Before committing, verify whether crates/imp-core/src/tools/bash.rs already contains the intended bash default/custom shell behavior in HEAD because it is not currently present in the working diff. Then stage only the relevant settings files and commit with an imperative message such as `Clean up settings shell configuration`.
.mana/245.1.1-define-vnext-manaimp-subagent-handoff-packet-for-o.md:3:title: Define vNext mana→imp subagent handoff packet for object-native memory
.mana/245.1.1-define-vnext-manaimp-subagent-handoff-packet-for-o.md:4:slug: define-vnext-manaimp-subagent-handoff-packet-for-o
.mana/245.1.1-define-vnext-manaimp-subagent-handoff-packet-for-o.md:9:acceptance: 'A root architecture doc defines the next-generation mana→imp subagent handoff packet in a way that preserves the current boundary: mana supplies a presentation-neutral specialized task packet backed by durable memory objects and derived views; imp remains the final prompt assembler and live runtime. The doc should specify packet fields, fallback behavior when only unit-native memory exists, and how the packet relates to current `WorkerAssignment`/`TaskContext` without blocking the current 81.5–81.8 MVP path.'
.mana/245.1.1-define-vnext-manaimp-subagent-handoff-packet-for-o.md:13:  Seeded from the current handoff/runtime discussion. The migration-critical framing is: improve what mana hands to imp, not who owns final prompt assembly. Use the current `load_assignment*() -> build_task_context() -> system_prompt` seam as the anchor. Define a presentation-neutral vNext packet that adds object-backed inherited context (checkpoint/current-state/evidence/claims-or-precursors/lessons/receipts) while keeping mixed-state fallback to current unit-native fields during migration.
.mana/245.1.1-define-vnext-manaimp-subagent-handoff-packet-for-o.md:17:  Execution-order note from the current discussion. The vNext mana→imp subagent handoff packet work remains open, but it should follow the blocker and explicit human-close docs so inherited blocker context and explicit-human-close distinctions are stable before packet fields are specified.

.mana/338-land-cross-project-mana-run-shutdown-reset-fix.md:3:title: Land cross-project mana run shutdown reset fix
.mana/338-land-cross-project-mana-run-shutdown-reset-fix.md:4:slug: land-cross-project-mana-run-shutdown-reset-fix
.mana/338-land-cross-project-mana-run-shutdown-reset-fix.md:9:acceptance: The stale SHUTDOWN_REQUESTED root cause is represented in root mana; mana-cli fix is reviewed and verified; unrelated dirty mana changes are not accidentally included; imp integration checks still pass; landing plan separates mana and imp changes.
.mana/338-land-cross-project-mana-run-shutdown-reset-fix.md:13:  Architecture clarification from user: native imp mana run should not be embedding mana-cli. Desired direction is mana run prompt assembly from mana-core plus launching imp subagents through runtime/subagent APIs. Current mana-cli embedding is transitional compatibility and caused issues like CLI signal handlers/global shutdown state leaking into long-lived imp TUI.
.mana/338-land-cross-project-mana-run-shutdown-reset-fix.md:14:verify: 'cd /Users/asher/mana && cargo test -p mana-cli commands::run --quiet && cd /Users/asher/imp && cargo test -p imp-core tools::mana::tests:: --quiet && cargo check -p imp-core -p imp-tui'
.mana/338-land-cross-project-mana-run-shutdown-reset-fix.md:17:- /Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/mod.rs
.mana/338-land-cross-project-mana-run-shutdown-reset-fix.md:18:- /Users/asher/imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs
.mana/338-land-cross-project-mana-run-shutdown-reset-fix.md:21:Cross-project follow-up from imp native mana run queue-stall audit. Evidence: imp native mana run embeds mana-cli run_with_stream_capture_and_sink in a long-lived TUI process. mana-cli has a process-global SHUTDOWN_REQUESTED flag set by SIGINT/SIGTERM; if stale true, a later embedded run can plan/emit queued units with jobs=4 but ready_queue refuses to spawn workers. Current working-tree fix in /Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/mod.rs adds reset_shutdown_requested() and calls it at cmd_run and run_with_stream_capture_and_sink start. Related imp-side prior changes in /Users/asher/imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs clamp jobs>=1 and improve run_state agent/model display, but the jobs=4 root fix is in mana-cli. Next steps: review existing dirty /Users/asher/mana work to avoid mixing unrelated changes, add/adjust a focused regression test if feasible for stale shutdown reset, verify mana-cli run tests and imp integration checks, then land coordinated changes in mana and imp as separate focused commits/PRs if approved.
.mana/365.6-define-non-imp-agent-adapter-contract-for-the-mana.md:3:title: Define non-imp agent adapter contract for the mana harness
.mana/365.6-define-non-imp-agent-adapter-contract-for-the-mana.md:4:slug: define-non-imp-agent-adapter-contract-for-the-mana
.mana/365.6-define-non-imp-agent-adapter-contract-for-the-mana.md:23:  Verification checkpoint after adding Inngest framing: `mana verify 365.6` fails because required deliverable `docs/architecture/mana-agent-adapter-contract.md` has not been authored yet. The task remains open. Next action: define agent-neutral adapter contract with capability levels for imp/pi/codex/opencode/goose and durable event/outcome normalization.
.mana/365.6-define-non-imp-agent-adapter-contract-for-the-mana.md:27:  Adapter contract update: non-imp agents should be able to execute mana runbook steps. The adapter contract should define capability levels for runbook support: read-only step instructions, structured step outcome, condition/decision reporting, subagent/child-run request, artifact/evidence reporting, cancellation/HITL, and full runbook interpreter support. Agents should not need imp-specific Lua APIs to participate.
.mana/365.6-define-non-imp-agent-adapter-contract-for-the-mana.md:31:  Verification checkpoint after runbook ownership correction: `mana verify 365.6` still fails because required deliverable `docs/architecture/mana-agent-adapter-contract.md` has not been authored. New adapter requirement is recorded in notes: non-imp agents should be able to execute mana runbook steps via capability levels, without imp-specific Lua APIs. No closure attempted.

.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:3:title: Define the mana harness target architecture and agent contract
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:4:slug: define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:14:  - The doc explicitly separates mana responsibilities from imp runtime responsibilities.
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:19:  External framing source from user: Inngest blog `Background agents are here. Your orchestration isn't ready.` This should directly shape the mana harness architecture. The target harness should abstract stable execution guarantees, not current agent topology. Core durable primitives to incorporate: durable steps/checkpoints, persistent external state, parallel work coordination, event-driven control flow/HITL/cancellation, and structured execution observability/traces. Architecture risk: do not make mana another agent-framework bet like a fixed graph/roles/conversation topology; mana should be the durable harness around changing agent patterns. This strengthens daemon/service framing for long-running background agents and separates mana orchestration from imp runtime logic.
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:23:  Verification checkpoint after adding Inngest framing: `mana verify 365.2` fails because required deliverable `docs/architecture/mana-agent-harness-target.md` has not been authored yet. The task remains open. Current blocker is design/doc authoring, not implementation failure. Next action: write the target architecture doc using durable orchestration primitives from the Inngest source plus existing imp/mana boundary decisions.
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:27:  Direction update: do not block imp runtime improvements on the mana/work graph. Mana harness architecture remains important, but near-term implementation should make imp excellent as a standalone agent harness/runtime first. The work graph can follow once imp no longer owns durable workflow/task state.
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:31:  Post-change verification checkpoint: `mana verify 365.2` fails because `docs/architecture/mana-agent-harness-target.md` is not yet authored. This is an expected open deliverable. No code blocker; next action is still to write the target architecture doc when we switch from imp runtime implementation back to mana harness planning.
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:35:  Runbook architecture update: mana harness target architecture should include agent-neutral runbooks as first-class durable orchestration artifacts. A runbook is task/epic-aligned programmable execution plan/decision tree whose steps can be executed by any capable agent. mana owns runbook definitions, step state, branching decisions, evidence, and durable outcomes. Agent runtimes consume runbook assignments through the harness contract. The canonical contract should be a stable runbook IR/API, not an imp-only language binding.
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:39:  Verification checkpoint after runbook ownership correction: `mana verify 365.2` still fails because required deliverable `docs/architecture/mana-agent-harness-target.md` has not been authored. New runbook requirement is recorded in notes: mana owns agent-neutral runbooks as durable orchestration artifacts/IR, not imp. No closure attempted. Next useful action when switching to mana design is to author the target architecture doc including runbook IR/API, step state, branching decisions, evidence/outcomes, and agent-neutral consumption by imp/codex/opencode/pi/goose.
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:43:  Repo Prompt video direction: mana harness architecture should explicitly include a manager-agent flow over durable mana state. Human/user goals become mana tasks/runbooks/context packs; an agent adapter launches imp/codex/opencode/etc.; runtimes emit activity/events/outcomes; mana records durable state, evidence, verification, and blockers; GUI consumes mana state rather than agent internals. Context packs and stable plan/runbook artifacts are first-class mana-owned inputs to runtime execution, while clean-context planning/review can be performed by runtimes.
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:47:  Correction: do not cite or depend on the Repo Prompt video in the harness architecture deliverable. Keep the generalized requirement: mana should support manager-agent flows over durable mana state, with tasks/runbooks/context packs as mana-owned artifacts and agent runtimes emitting events/outcomes through an agent-neutral contract.
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:49:- mana
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:54:verify: cd /Users/asher/imp && test -f docs/architecture/mana-agent-harness-target.md && rg -q "agent-neutral" docs/architecture/mana-agent-harness-target.md && rg -q "GUI" docs/architecture/mana-agent-harness-target.md
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:57:- crates/mana-core

.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:63:Specify mana as the shared work harness for imp and other agents. This is the core architecture document for the pivot.
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:76:- policy boundaries between mana and agent runtimes
.mana/365.2-define-the-mana-harness-target-architecture-and-ag.md:78:Important boundary: mana coordinates and records; agent runtimes execute and emit structured results.

User wants emphasis on features, mana integration, and influences. Preserve accurate shipped/current-state claims from existing README: Rust-native coding agent, TUI/cockpit, CLI chat, tools, sessions, modes, providers, secure secrets, Lua current stable extension path, TypeScript limited compatibility/future path, mana native work coordination, SDK preview. Improve organization and tone; reduce stale/internal planning language; include influences without overclaiming lineage. Do not change code.

.mana/50.21-specify-visible-planning-artifacts-and-checklist-b.md:20:Write a rebuild-spec doc for visible planning in the CLI-first imp surface, sequenced after output/session/checkpoint surfacing and before approval policy. Keep scope architectural/spec-level, not implementation.

.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:3:title: Design imp-native delegation tool around imp run and mana orchestration
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:12:  Inspected current repo state. Key grounding: (1) `imp run <unit-id>` already exists in `imp-cli` and `run_headless_mode()` loads `WorkerAssignment` through `imp_core::mana_worker`, assembles context/prefill, runs the session, and optionally verifies/closes. (2) `mana-cli run_native()` still treats `mana run` as a compatibility orchestration entrypoint and checks for `imp` on PATH in direct mode. (3) `mana-cli spawner.rs` still shells out via `sh -c`, claims units, sets `IMP_MODE`, and captures logs. (4) `imp-core tools/mana.rs` already consumes native mana run/status/state surfaces; `builder.rs` registers `ManaTool` as a native tool. Early design read: the first `imp`-calls-`imp` surface should likely sit in `imp` as a native tool/runtime seam, but durable delegation should still externalize work into mana and use the canonical `imp run` worker path rather than inventing a second durable orchestration channel.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:16:  Externalized initial plan in mana before further drafting. Working recommendation to carry into the spec unless contradicted by deeper repo evidence: use a two-layer delegation model where `imp` owns live child-worker invocation, but any durable or mutating delegation must route through mana and reuse the canonical `imp run <unit-id>` worker path. Keep direct prompt-only child calls, if allowed at all, explicitly transient and non-authoritative in v1.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:20:  Decomposition externalized into root mana now. Added child units: 81.2 for the delegation target model decision, 81.3 for the parent-visible result contract, and 81.4 for policy/isolation/migration constraints. 81.1 tracks the decomposition layer itself so the visible graph captures the architecture split before implementation drafting continues.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:24:  User confirmed the consequential choice: adopt the two-layer v1 delegation model. Treat this as the settled design baseline unless repo constraints force revision. Interpretation: `imp` may gain a native delegation surface, but durable or mutating delegation must externalize into mana and reuse the canonical `imp run <unit-id>` worker path; any direct prompt-only child calls remain explicitly transient and secondary.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:28:  Drafted `docs/rebuild/imp-delegation-tool-and-runtime.md` and verified the parent gate strings. The note now locks in the user-confirmed two-layer model: mana-backed delegation is the primary path for durable or mutating work and reuses canonical `imp run <unit-id>` / `WorkerAssignment`; ad hoc delegation is explicitly transient, read-oriented, and non-authoritative in v1. The note also defines a parent-visible result contract, conservative mode/policy rules, and migration guidance that keeps `mana run` compatibility secondary while leaving room for a later runner-backed adapter.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:32:  Externalized the next implementation decomposition into root mana. Added 81.5 to extract a reusable imp-core worker runner from the existing `imp run` path, 81.6 to implement the first native `imp` tool with unit-backed delegation over that canonical worker path, 81.7 to add the restricted transient ad hoc delegation mode, and 81.8 to enforce mode/policy gating and focused tests. Dependency shape: 81.6 and 81.7 both depend on 81.5; 81.8 depends on 81.6 and 81.7.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:36:  Tightened the implementation decomposition for 81.5–81.8 using the loaded `mana` skill guidance. The units now name exact files/functions/patterns from inspected repo state, specify the chosen approach instead of leaving design decisions to the worker, and use sharper verify gates. Grounded details embedded into the child units include: `imp-cli/src/main.rs::run_headless_mode()`, `imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs`, `builder.rs::register_native_tools()`, `config.rs::allowed_tool_names()`, and the existing `agent_mode_enforcement_*` test area in `imp-core/src/agent.rs`.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:42:  - There is still no native `imp` tool module in `imp-core` today. The current real single-unit path is `imp run <unit-id>` in `imp-cli`, backed by `imp_core::mana_worker`.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:45:  - `mana-core::unit::Unit` likewise currently exposes only `model: Option<String>` in frontmatter; there is no durable per-unit `provider` or `thinking` field today.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:52:  3. Treat durable per-unit provider/thinking support as a separate cross-project decision because it changes `mana-core::Unit` and `tower-contracts::worker::WorkerAssignment`, not just imp tooling.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:64:     - This path should not require changing mana durable unit schema.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:67:     - Decide later whether durable per-unit `provider` and `thinking` belong in mana-backed unit metadata and `tower-contracts::worker::WorkerAssignment`, rather than being delegation-time-only overrides.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:68:     - Treat that as a distinct root-scope architecture/unit-contract question because it affects `mana-core::Unit`, `tower-contracts`, and imp worker loading/runtime together.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:77:  Current implementation-status audit (2026-04-13) externalized from chat. Repo-grounded findings: the delegation architecture/design is in place in `docs/rebuild/imp-delegation-tool-and-runtime.md`, but there is still no native `imp` tool implementation in `imp-core`. Verified gaps from inspected code: `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/imp.rs` is missing; `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mod.rs` has no `mod imp;`; `imp/crates/imp-core/src/builder.rs::register_native_tools()` does not register `ImpTool`; `imp/crates/imp-core/src/config.rs::allowed_tool_names()` does not include `"imp"` for `Orchestrator`; and `imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs` does not yet expose a shared `run_worker_assignment(...)`, `WorkerRunOptions`, or `WorkerRunOutcome`. Practical decomposition/status: MVP implementation remains 81.5 (extract shared worker runner), 81.6 (unit-backed native imp tool), 81.7 (restricted transient ad hoc mode), 81.8 (mode/policy gating), with 81.9 as the still-open architecture decision on whether `provider` and `thinking` belong durably in mana-backed worker metadata. Product-grade but non-MVP follow-on work is already tracked elsewhere rather than duplicated here: runner/attach-path migration under 45.4.x, capability hardening under 45.7.x, and richer result/presentation surfaces under existing imp CLI/TUI/runtime units such as 28.5.7 / 50.14 / 50.17 / 44.1.12.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:85:  Conversation-time planning externalization (2026-04-13): the current v1 native `imp` delegation slice is fully plannable now with no new blocker questions. Durable MVP execution order remains: 81.5 extract the reusable single-unit worker runner out of `imp-cli::run_headless_mode()` into `imp-core::mana_worker`; 81.6 build the native unit-backed `imp` tool on top of that shared runner; 81.7 add the restricted transient `ad_hoc` child-delegation path; 81.8 enforce registration-time + execution-time mode policy and focused tests. Treat 81.9 (durable ownership of `provider` / `thinking`) as non-blocking architecture follow-up by assuming transient runtime overrides for v1. Treat root runner/attach-path migration work under 45.4.x and deeper hardening under 45.7.x as important post-MVP integration work, not blockers for planning or implementing the first two-layer native `imp` tool.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:89:  Delegation/subagent relevance from current memory-architecture discussion: improving mana from unit-centric memory to object-native durable memory directly improves `imp` subagent delegation quality. Child workers should inherit not just raw unit fields and attempt summaries, but compact checkpoint/handoff objects, relevant evidence/claim context, promoted lessons, and receipts that prevent repeated side effects. This strengthens `imp run <unit>` and native imp delegation by giving subagents a better specialized contract without making mana the final prompt renderer.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:93:  Concrete delegation/runtime decomposition from the current discussion: native imp delegation should continue to ride the canonical worker seam rather than inventing a prompt-only child runtime path for durable work. Near-term, v1 delegation can keep using the current unit-centric `WorkerAssignment`/`TaskContext` seam. Follow-on architecture work should define a `WorkerAssignment`/task-packet vNext that upgrades child-worker inheritance from raw unit fields + attempt summaries to object-backed context: latest checkpoint, accepted/current-state summary, evidence refs, lessons/runbooks, receipts, and open-decision/conflict context. This follow-on should remain non-blocking for the current 81.5 -> 81.8 MVP, but it is the right path for making subagents inherit more specialized durable state without making mana the final prompt renderer.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:97:  Created follow-on root architecture unit 245.1.1 to define the vNext mana→imp subagent handoff packet / `WorkerAssignment` shape for object-native memory. This is the contract-level follow-on that connects the memory migration to future delegation quality, but it is explicitly non-blocking for the current 81.5 -> 81.8 MVP implementation path.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:103:  - Native `imp` delegation tool is still NOT implemented in repo reality. Confirmed gaps: no `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/imp.rs`; `imp-core/src/tools/mod.rs` has no `mod imp;`; `builder.rs::register_native_tools()` registers `ask/bash/edit/extend/mana/memory/multi_edit/read/write/scan/session_search/web` only; `config.rs::allowed_tool_names()` does not include `"imp"` for any non-Full mode.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:104:  - Canonical single-unit `imp run` path IS real and reasonably well-wired today. `imp-cli::run_headless_mode()` loads `WorkerAssignment` through `imp_core::mana_worker::load_assignment_with_mana_dir()`, assembles file prefill via `assemble_prefill()`, builds `TaskContext` via `build_task_context()`, loads task facts via `mana_prompt_context::load_task_prompt_context()`, passes all of that into `ImpSession::create()`, prompts with `build_task_prompt()`, then optionally runs verify and `mana close` inline.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:105:  - Prompt assembly from mana exists in two layers today: (1) session/system prompt assembly in `AgentBuilder` uses `mana_prompt_context::load_session_prompt_context()` for relevant facts + project memory status; (2) `imp run` task mode adds task-scoped facts filtered by `context_paths` using `load_task_prompt_context()`. This is real mana-backed prompt/context assembly, not just ad hoc text.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:107:  - Related repo evidence: ripgrep finds no current runtime surface named `codemap` or `code map`; current code-intelligence work is tracked separately in root mana under `44.*` and related follow-ons. So if the requirement is 'prompt assembly based on mana and codemaps', mana is partially wired today, codemap is still a substantive gap rather than a near-finished integration.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:108:  - Practical MVP implication for 81.5 -> 81.8: native `imp` tool implementation should reuse the current canonical `imp run` worker seam and inherit its mana-backed task context + prefill behavior first. Treat semantic codemap/context-map integration as an explicit follow-on seam unless the MVP scope is expanded intentionally.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:115:  - V1 delegation context baseline is now explicit across the 81.5 -> 81.8 sequence: reuse existing mana-backed prompt assembly plus current reference-file prefill from the `imp run` path.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:116:  - Longer-term desired direction remains a semantic/LSP-backed workspace map so a parent imp can curate richer subagent context similarly to mana job shaping, but that belongs to the separate follow-on codemap/context seam tracked under 81.10.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:122:  2026-04-13 workflow clarification from user: when escalating hard questions to GPT-5.4-pro, do not assume it knows `imp`, `mana`, or any repo-specific terminology. External prompts should be highly abstracted and system-design-oriented, with local project names and repo facts translated into generic roles/boundaries first. Bring the abstract answer back and then map it onto Tower locally.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:226:    - keep v1 context simple: mana-backed facts + explicit reference-file/context prefill, defer semantic workspace map (matches current MVP scope / 81.10 split)

.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:842:verify: cd /Users/asher/imp && test -f docs/rebuild/imp-delegation-tool-and-runtime.md && rg -q 'imp run' docs/rebuild/imp-delegation-tool-and-runtime.md && rg -q 'WorkerAssignment' docs/rebuild/imp-delegation-tool-and-runtime.md && rg -q 'mana' docs/rebuild/imp-delegation-tool-and-runtime.md && rg -q 'delegation' docs/rebuild/imp-delegation-tool-and-runtime.md
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:853:- 'Working direction: v1 should prefer a two-layer delegation model. `imp` owns live child-worker invocation, but durable or mutating delegation must externalize into mana and reuse the canonical `imp run <unit-id>` path. Any direct prompt-only child calls, if allowed in v1, should remain explicitly transient, read-oriented, and non-authoritative until a stronger runner/policy contract exists.'
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:854:- 'Implementation decomposition rule now externalized: 81.5 extracts the reusable canonical worker runner from `run_headless_mode()` into `imp-core::mana_worker`; 81.6 builds only unit-backed native `imp` delegation on top of that runner; 81.7 adds the restricted transient `ad_hoc` path separately; 81.8 applies mode/tool gating and tests after both delegation paths exist. This ordering is intentional so workers do not invent alternate runtime centers or mix policy work into runtime extraction.'
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:858:- Capture the workflow rule for external-model escalations so future workers do not send repo-specific imp/mana assumptions into a model that lacks local context.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:860:- Capture the durable architectural conclusions mapped from the abstract GPT answer so future workers inherit the implementation shape directly from mana rather than reconstructing it from chat.

.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:862:- Capture the current durable execution decomposition for the native imp-tool MVP so future workers inherit the exact remaining work and ordering from mana rather than chat.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:863:- Capture the durable architecture decision from the latest 81.5 review so future workers inherit the event-stream-preserving extraction shape directly from root mana.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:864:- Record the user's workflow requirement and the current externalization checkpoint so future workers keep the native-imp-tool planning state in mana rather than only in chat.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:877:  Architecture clarification from current code inspection: mana already owns the active scheduling engine in Tower (`mana run`, `mana-pool`, `mana-core::ops::run`). The old Pi mana extension scheduler is useful historical reference but should not be treated as the canonical scheduler for new imp surfaces. Therefore future imp command/tool design should distinguish:
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:878:  - one-worker-on-one-unit execution (`imp mana <id>`-style surface)
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:879:  - mana-backed multi-unit orchestration/monitoring (`imp mana run/status/...`-style surfaces)
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:881:- 'Native imp-tool boundary decision from the latest discussion: the native `imp` tool should participate in the same split as the CLI. It may launch one child worker on one unit or one bounded transient helper, but it should not become an independent multi-unit scheduler. When a parent agent wants multiple subagents over workable jobs / an epic, the imp-side surface should hand that off to mana-backed orchestration/monitoring rather than owning a second scheduler.'
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:882:- 'Scheduler-of-record decision: mana remains the scheduler of record for ready-queue selection, dependency ordering, parallel dispatch, verify batching, and durable run state. Imp owns worker execution and operator UX surfaces, including native tool/CLI entrypoints, but orchestration initiated from inside imp should still route through mana-owned scheduling rather than reviving the old Pi extension scheduler or inventing a new imp-local one.'
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:883:- 'Native-tool/runtime principle from the latest discussion: for ordinary agent work, mana-backed behavior should be available as first-class native tools/runtime surfaces so it ''just works'' for the agent. Do not make agents shell out to `imp` CLI commands via bash for the common path. The `imp` CLI remains an operator-facing surface and compatibility entrypoint, while agent-to-mana / agent-to-worker interactions should prefer native tool calls and embedded runtime seams.'
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:884:- 'Native tool boundary tightened by the latest discussion: for agent-to-agent work, the parent should call native tools/runtime seams directly, not spawn `imp` CLI commands through bash for normal operation. The native `imp` tool remains the agent-facing child-worker surface; native `mana` remains the durable substrate/orchestration surface. Shelling out to `imp` should be reserved for operator workflows, compatibility paths, or debugging, not the common in-agent execution path.'
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:892:- `run_headless_mode()` loads a canonical `WorkerAssignment` through `imp_core::mana_worker::load_assignment_with_mana_dir`, assembles prefill/context, runs the session, and optionally verifies/closes inline.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:893:- `mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/mod.rs` still treats `mana run` as a native orchestration entrypoint and checks for `imp` on PATH when using direct spawn mode.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:894:- `mana/crates/mana-cli/src/spawner.rs` still shells out through `sh -c`, claims units, sets `IMP_MODE`, and captures logs.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:895:- `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` already consumes mana run/status/state surfaces as a native tool.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:896:- `imp/crates/imp-core/src/builder.rs` registers native tools, including `ManaTool`, and `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mod.rs` defines the shared Tool/ToolRegistry surface.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:903:   - mana-addressed unit IDs only
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:905:   - or a two-layer model where ad hoc delegation is transient but durable work must route through mana
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:908:   - `mana` owns durable orchestration state, work graph, and inherited outcomes
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:913:   - pointers into mana/log/session artifacts
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:915:5. How should this relate to the longer-term runner protocol and the `mana run` → `imp run` migration?
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:924:- Do not bypass mana for durable orchestration claims without an explicit decision.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:925:- Prefer `imp` as the runtime owner and `mana` as durable truth, consistent with root docs.
.mana/81-design-imp-native-delegation-tool-around-imp-run-a.md:929:- `imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs`

.mana/45.7.4-evaluate-whether-imp-should-add-a-native-gitrepo-t.md:12:  Implementation pivot: proceeding from evaluation into a first native imp git/repo tool. Planned v1 scope is intentionally narrow and local-only: inspect repo status/diff/log/current branch/merge base/worktree detection, plus guarded local stage/commit/restore and local worktree add/remove. No remote operations, push/pull/fetch, rebase, reset --hard, or arbitrary git config mutation. Reuse mana-core worktree helpers where practical so repo isolation semantics stay aligned with the existing Tower worktree model. Integrate as a native imp tool with mode-aware availability and focused tests.
.mana/45.7.4-evaluate-whether-imp-should-add-a-native-gitrepo-t.md:16:  Implemented a first native imp git tool in `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/git.rs` and registered it in the native tool registry. Current v1 actions: `status`, `diff`, `log`, `merge_base`, `worktree_info`, `stage`, `commit`, `restore`, `worktree_add`, `worktree_remove`. Mutating actions are blocked outside Full/Worker mode inside the tool; Planner/Reviewer/Auditor/Orchestrator still get readonly git access through mode allowlists. The tool is local-only and intentionally omits push/pull/fetch/rebase/reset-hard/config mutation. It reuses `mana_core::worktree::detect_worktree` for aligned worktree context and snapshots files before `restore` via imp checkpoint state. Verification: `cargo check -p imp-core` and `cargo test -p imp-core git_ -- --nocapture` both passed.
.mana/45.7.4-evaluate-whether-imp-should-add-a-native-gitrepo-t.md:30:verify: test -f .mana/45.7.4-evaluate-whether-imp-should-add-a-native-gitrepo-t.md && rg -q '^id:' .mana/45.7.4-evaluate-whether-imp-should-add-a-native-gitrepo-t.md
.mana/45.7.4-evaluate-whether-imp-should-add-a-native-gitrepo-t.md:46:- The architectural question is not whether git commands are possible today, but whether a native tool would materially improve safety, determinism, isolation, provenance, and mana-integrated execution.
.mana/45.7.4-evaluate-whether-imp-should-add-a-native-gitrepo-t.md:53:4. Which responsibilities belong in imp runtime policy vs mana durable state vs bash fallback. Be explicit about where worktree isolation metadata and evidence should live.
