.mana/27.14-define-attempt-scoped-autonomy-observation-record.md:25:  2026-04-09 inspection notes: read imp/crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs and imp/crates/imp-core/src/config.rs. The current imp-local continue hook is `should_queue_confidence_continue_follow_up(...)`; it only triggers after a visible mana tool call (`assistant_message_contains_mana_tool_call`) plus positive completion/next-step language, with thresholds set by ContinuePolicy (conservative=3, balanced=2, aggressive=1). This confirms the upstream record should capture typed visible evidence only, not raw confidence or heuristic text export.
.mana/27.14-define-attempt-scoped-autonomy-observation-record.md:29:  2026-04-09 durability inspection: `Unit` persists `history: Vec<RunRecord>` and `attempt_log: Vec<AttemptRecord>` in mana/crates/mana-core/src/unit/mod.rs, and `record_attempt(...)` appends directly to attempt_log in mana/crates/mana-core/src/api/mod.rs. claim.rs creates a fresh AttemptRecord at claim time; close.rs writes RunRecord entries on verify pass/fail and finalizes AttemptRecord on success/failure/release. This supports adding the typed autonomy observation on attempt/result records rather than on the top-level Unit.
.mana/27.14-define-attempt-scoped-autonomy-observation-record.md:33:  2026-04-09 implementation plan: add `AutonomyObservation`, `VisibilityState`, and `AutonomyProvenance` to mana-core unit types; attach `autonomy_observation: Option<AutonomyObservation>` to both AttemptRecord and RunRecord so the visible-evidence input remains attempt/result-scoped. Keep all existing imp-local continue behavior unchanged in agent.rs.
.mana/27.14-define-attempt-scoped-autonomy-observation-record.md:37:  2026-04-09 dependency update from 27.13: mana-core now already contains a minimal `AutonomyObservation` type plus optional `autonomy_observation` fields on `RunRecord` and `AttemptRecord`, added because existing claim/close/context/api code already referenced them and `cargo check -p mana-core` otherwise failed. Remaining scope for 27.14 is no longer introducing the first observation type; it should refine/enrich the attempt-scoped observation schema for imp-visible evidence and provenance beyond this compatibility baseline.
.mana/27.14-define-attempt-scoped-autonomy-observation-record.md:41:  2026-04-09 implementation complete. Added/reshaped attempt/result-scoped `AutonomyObservation` in mana/crates/mana-core/src/unit/types.rs so it carries only typed visible-evidence inputs: `visibility`, `provenance`, `source_attempt`, `observed_at`, and optional `continuation_budget_delta`. Attached it as `autonomy_observation: Option<AutonomyObservation>` on both `AttemptRecord` and `RunRecord`, preserving attempt/result scoping and excluding raw confidence, thresholds, model self-report, scheduler commands, and free-text heuristic export. Confirmed current imp-local continue behavior remains in imp/crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs at `should_queue_confidence_continue_follow_up(...)`, gated by visible mana use plus positive completion/next-step language and `ContinuePolicy` in imp/crates/imp-core/src/config.rs. Verify passed: `cd /Users/asher/tower && rg -q 'AutonomyObservation|AutonomyProvenance|visibility' mana/crates/mana-core/src/unit && cargo check -p mana-core -p imp-core`.
.mana/27.14-define-attempt-scoped-autonomy-observation-record.md:45:  2026-04-09 follow-up after loading mana skill with read: re-inspected the worker contract and re-ran the unit verify gate. Verify still passes: `cd /Users/asher/tower && rg -q 'AutonomyObservation|AutonomyProvenance|visibility' mana/crates/mana-core/src/unit && cargo check -p mana-core -p imp-core`. Current repo state already satisfies the unit scope; no additional code changes were required in this pass.
.mana/27.14-define-attempt-scoped-autonomy-observation-record.md:50:  - No further decomposition is needed for 27.14 itself; the durable design for this slice remains the attempt/result-scoped observation contract already captured in mana-core.
.mana/27.14-define-attempt-scoped-autonomy-observation-record.md:52:  - Boundary remains explicit: imp gathers visible evidence and keeps confidence heuristics local; mana-core later normalizes these typed observations into scheduler-facing policy (tracked by 27.20).
.mana/27.14-define-attempt-scoped-autonomy-observation-record.md:53:  - Verified again in current repo state with the unit gate: `cd /Users/asher/tower && rg -q 'AutonomyObservation|AutonomyProvenance|visibility' mana/crates/mana-core/src/unit && cargo check -p mana-core -p imp-core`.
.mana/27.14-define-attempt-scoped-autonomy-observation-record.md:58:  2026-04-09 follow-up verification: loaded the `mana` skill before continuing, re-inspected the unit, and tightened `mana/crates/mana-core/src/unit/types.rs` with explicit schema docs/tests for `AutonomyObservation`. Added coverage confirming the observation serializes only typed fields (`visibility`, `provenance`, optional `source_attempt`, `observed_at`, `continuation_budget_delta`) and omits any `confidence`, `threshold`, or `summary` export. Also added an `AttemptRecord` round-trip test showing the observation remains attempt-scoped. Reconfirmed imp-local behavior is unchanged: `should_queue_confidence_continue_follow_up(...)` in `imp/crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs` still gates on visible mana tool use plus positive completion/next-step language, with `ContinuePolicy` thresholds in `imp/crates/imp-core/src/config.rs`. Verify still passes: `rg -q 'AutonomyObservation|AutonomyProvenance|visibility' mana/crates/mana-core/src/unit && cargo check -p mana-core -p imp-core`; targeted tests also passed.

.mana/50-define-cli-first-operator-surface-for-imp-with-tui.md:443:  Externalized the CLI-native affordance sequence from docs/rebuild/imp-cli-affordance-sequence.md into root mana as concrete follow-on units with explicit ordering/dependency shape. Created 50.17 stable human vs machine output contracts (depends on shared UI/runtime seam 50.14); 50.18 CLI-first session browsing/search and session_search surface (depends on existing viewer split 50.11); 50.20 checkpoint productization (depends on 50.18 browse/search surface); 50.22 visible planning workflow and plan artifact (depends on 50.14 and 50.17); 50.24 approval policy surface linked to durable mana gating (depends on 50.14 plus rebuild review/approval policy work 45.6 and fact 52); 50.26 detached/background local execution model (depends on 50.17, 50.18, 50.20, 50.22, 50.24). This captures the durable order as output -> browse/search -> checkpoints -> planning -> approval -> detached/background, with runtime-heavy items separated from mostly surface/productization items.
.mana/50-define-cli-first-operator-surface-for-imp-with-tui.md:471:- 'Decision: adopt a CLI-first imp command grammar centered on explicit surfaces. Canonical families are `imp chat` for human authoring, `imp prompt` for one-shot prompt/print, `imp run <unit-id>` for the machine-facing mana-worker runtime, `imp view` for browsing/inspection, and `imp tui` for explicit fullscreen presentation. Plain `imp` should eventually equal `imp chat` after a staged compatibility migration rather than remaining fullscreen by default or becoming a heuristic launcher.'
.mana/50-define-cli-first-operator-surface-for-imp-with-tui.md:480:- Externalizing CLI-affordance sequencing from completed unit 50.5 onto the stable root parent so follow-on work inherits the order directly from mana, not only from the doc.
.mana/50-define-cli-first-operator-surface-for-imp-with-tui.md:507:- blur mana/imp ownership while discussing operator surface
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:3:title: 'Improve mana-pool: competitive-grade dispatch engine'
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:4:slug: improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:12:  2026-04-09 durable handoff from unit 27.1: the verified slice that closed was narrow `mana-pool` dispatch coverage, not a new pool-side policy surface. In this checkout `mana-pool` is still effectively standalone (no observed mana-cli wiring into `mana-pool` APIs), and archived unit 27.1 had no title/description beyond the slug/verify. The successful implementation path was to add/verify explicit `budget`-named tests in `mana/crates/mana-pool/src/dispatch.rs` so the unit gate exercised concrete behavior directly: `budget_enforces_max_concurrent_limit` proves dispatch never exceeds `max_concurrent`, and `budget_circuit_breaker_stops_new_spawns_after_failure` proves no new units spawn after first failure when `keep_going=false`. Exact verify gate now passes: `cd /Users/asher/tower && cargo test -p mana-pool budget -- 2>&1 | grep 'test result' | grep -v '0 passed'`. For future 27.x work: inspect staged/worktree state before editing, because this checkout already contained the matching `dispatch.rs` test additions when triaged.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:17:  - Result of 27.7 policy analysis: do not move raw confidence scoring into mana-pool.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:18:  - Current boundary should remain: imp owns confidence computation, evidence interpretation, visibility of the mana delta, and the local decision to continue into the next small bounded step.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:19:  - mana-pool owns only mechanical dispatch/scheduling once work is eligible: dependency readiness, ordering, path conflicts, memory/capacity limits, retry context, batch-verify coordination, and binary keep_going failure policy.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:26:  - Scope/ownership: root-scope cross-project slice because the change spans shared mana unit frontmatter in `mana-core` and deferred verification behavior in `mana-pool`.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:28:    1. Add shared frontmatter field `verify_fast: Option<String>` to `mana-core/src/unit/mod.rs` (`Unit` + `UnitWire`) so unit YAML can read/write an optional fast preflight verify command.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:29:    2. Add `verify_fast: Option<String>` to `mana-pool/src/types.rs` `DispatchUnit` so dispatch has the preflight command available alongside `verify_command`.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:30:    3. Change `mana-pool/src/dispatch.rs` layered batch-verify behavior to run grouped `verify_fast` commands first, skip grouped full `verify_command` for units whose fast verify failed, and mark those units failed with message `fast verify failed`.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:33:  - Tests added in `mana/crates/mana-pool/src/dispatch.rs`:
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:37:  - Shared serde tests updated in `mana-core/src/unit/mod.rs` so `verify_fast` round-trips and is omitted when unset.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:39:    - exact unit gate passed: `cd /Users/asher/tower && cargo test -p mana-pool layered_verify -- 2>&1 | grep 'test result' | grep -v '0 passed'`
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:41:    - implementation-time safety check also passed: `cargo check -p mana-core`
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:45:  2026-04-09 recovery decision for former 27.1: metadata repair path selected. Confirmed in this checkout that the old verify gate maps to existing narrow dispatch tests in `mana/crates/mana-pool/src/dispatch.rs`: `budget_enforces_max_concurrent_limit` and `budget_circuit_breaker_stops_new_spawns_after_failure`. The exact historical gate passes (`cargo test -p mana-pool budget ...` → 2 passed). Do not interpret former 27.1 as an unfinished new pool-side policy surface; explicit circuit-breaker logic still belongs to close/failure handling in `mana-core` / `mana-cli`. If future product intent wants a real pool-side budget/circuit-breaker policy, create a fresh child under 27 with explicit behavior, files, and tests instead of reusing 27.1.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:50:  - Current checkout already contains the narrow `mana-pool` dispatch coverage that matched former 27.1's visible verify gate.

.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:52:  - Exact tests in `mana/crates/mana-pool/src/dispatch.rs`:
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:56:  - Boundary confirmation from current code context: circuit-breaker attempt/close policy lives in `mana/crates/mana-core/src/ops/close.rs` and `mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/close/failure.rs`, not as a separate `mana-pool` dispatch policy surface.

.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:65:  - Concrete tests in `mana/crates/mana-pool/src/dispatch.rs`: `budget_enforces_max_concurrent_limit`, `budget_circuit_breaker_stops_new_spawns_after_failure`.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:71:  1. Inspect `mana/crates/mana-pool/src/dispatch.rs` for `budget`-named tests instead of inferring scope from the missing archived body.

.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:73:  3. Use `mana-core` / `mana-cli` close-failure code only as ownership context: confirm circuit-breaker attempt policy lives there, not in a new `mana-pool` dispatch feature.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:80:  - Current inspected code does not prove a real mana-pool-side need for autonomy eligibility inputs beyond imp-local continuation.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:81:  - `mana-pool` remains mechanical dispatch only: dependency/path/resource scheduling, retry context, verify grouping, and coarse `keep_going` stop-on-failure behavior.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:82:  - `mana run` unresolved decisions are still surfaced as caller/operator warnings, not pool-native typed gates.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:85:  - Anti-goals preserved: no opaque confidence scalar in pool types, no duplicate imp-local confidence engine in `mana-pool`, and no invisible autonomous continuation.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:90:  - 27.13 `Add canonical autonomy disposition and blocker vocabulary to mana-core`

.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:176:- 'Former unit 27.1 (''budget-and-circuit-breaker-for-dispatch'') is currently inconsistent: list/tree show it as closed, but its verify gate targets no matching mana-pool tests and its durable description is missing. .mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:177:- Interpret archived unit 27.1 narrowly as explicit `mana-pool` dispatch test coverage aligned to its verify gate, not as evidence of an unwired new pool-side budget/circuit-breaker runtime policy surface. If future work wants real runtime behavior here, create a new child under 27 with explicit CLI/pool integration scope and verify gate instead of reopening 27.1 on the slug alone.
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:178:- '2026-04-09 formal recovery decision for missing archived child 27.1: treat the former work as already-completed narrow `mana-pool` dispatch test coverage, not as a latent request for new pool-side runtime budget/circuit-breaker policy. Basis: `mana/crates/mana-pool/src/dispatch.rs` currently contains `budget_enforces_max_concurrent_limit` and `budget_circuit_breaker_stops_new_spawns_after_failure`; the historical gate `cd /Users/asher/tower && cargo test -p mana-pool budget -- 2>&1 | grep ''test result'' | grep -v ''0 passed''` passes; and explicit circuit-breaker ownership remains in `mana/crates/mana-core/src/ops/close.rs` and `mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/close/failure.rs`. If future product intent wants a real pool-side policy surface, create a new child under 27 with explicit files, behavior, and tests rather than attempting to reuse inaccessible 27.1 metadata.'
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:180:- 'Grounding: inspected `mana-pool` scheduling remains mechanical (`types.rs`, `dispatch.rs`), `mana run` still handles unresolved decisions as operator warnings/confirmation, and imp owns visible confidence-based continuation in `imp-core/src/agent.rs`. Unresolved decisions, reviewability, verify quality, evidence quality, risk, provenance, and freshness should become explicit gating inputs only if a real cross-run dispatch need is proven.'
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:181:- 'After inspecting current `mana-pool`, `mana run`, and `imp` continuation code, preserve the current boundary: do not add pool-side autonomy/confidence inputs now. If a real cross-run scheduling need later emerges, the only valid pool-facing extension is a narrow typed eligibility/disposition record with explicit blocker reason codes, review/human-pause flags, verify/evidence quality bands, risk band, and provenance/freshness. imp retains ownership of confidence and evidence interpretation.'
.mana/27-improve-mana-pool-competitive-grade-dispatch-engin.md:182:- '2026-04-09 recovery rule from closed fact 47 / former 27.1 verify drift: when an archived or recovered 27.x slice has ambiguous body metadata, first verify the visible narrow test gate against the checkout and inspect the named tests before proposing new `mana-pool` behavior. If the targeted coverage already exists, treat the work as metadata repair and record the corrected gate in root mana; only create a new child if product intent explicitly asks for additional pool-side behavior beyond the existing dispatch tests.'
.mana/350.4.4-phase-4-reduce-streaming-render-invalidation-and-l.md:16:  Committed 2f4eb4f Reuse TUI stream render caches across animation ticks. Removed tick from ChatRenderCacheKey and SidebarStreamCacheKey so active streaming animation frames no longer invalidate full chat/sidebar stream line caches when message/tool content and view settings are unchanged. This reduces per-frame work during streaming while preserving cache invalidation via chat_render_epoch, focus, width, config, theme, and activity_state. Added/passed test chat_and_sidebar_stream_cache_ignore_animation_tick. Reran key adjacent tests before commit: send_message_defers_agent_start_until_after_echo_redraw, event_source::tests, session_picker, mana_navigator, status_command_opens_background_task, clean_command_opens_background_task.

.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:3:title: Reduce duplicate verbose mana-change narration in imp chat
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:4:slug: reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:9:acceptance: '1. System prompt no longer redundantly instructs the model twice to summarize the same mana delta in normal replies. 2. Interactive mana tool flows still surface visible mana state, but background orchestration does not force a second redundant chat summary when the UI/widget already shows it. 3. Any skill wording changed should reinforce concise mana reporting rather than repeated restatement. 4. Focused tests pass.'
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:13:  Implemented the first over-reporting fix after auditing prompt + runtime layers. Changes landed in imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs, imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs, imp/.imp/skills/mana/SKILL.md, and imp/.imp/skills/mana-delegation/SKILL.md. Specific fixes: (1) removed the duplicated execution-policy instruction that separately required a concise mana delta summary and replaced it with a non-duplicative rule to update mana before the substantive reply while avoiding verbose restatement when tool output/UI already shows the delta; (2) gated background-run AgentCommand::FollowUp summaries in the native mana tool behind !ui.has_ui(), so open interactive imp chats rely on the visible mana widget/status instead of also getting a second chat summary; headless/non-UI flows still receive the follow-up summary; (3) tightened local mana skills to say at most one brief delta summary and only when the tool/UI did not already make the change obvious. Verification: cargo test -p imp-core system_prompt_includes_conversation_time_mana_planning_doctrine -- --nocapture; cargo test -p imp-core background_run_enqueues_follow_up_on_completion_without_ui -- --nocapture; cargo test -p imp-core background_run_with_ui_does_not_enqueue_follow_up_on_completion -- --nocapture. While running focused tests, the build surfaced an unrelated but current compile blocker in many imp-core tool tests: ToolContext test helpers were missing the newer turn_mana_review field. I patched those helper initializers across imp-core tool test modules so focused imp-core tests compile again; these are test-only support changes, not runtime behavior changes.
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:18:  1. Remaining mana-specific audit if continuing: inspect interactive reply generation after native mana mutations to find any remaining duplicate narration beyond the background-run follow-up path. Focus on imp/crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs follow-up nudges, confidence-continue text, turn mana review surfaces, and any places where assistant chat may restate widget-visible `mana delta` output.
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:19:  2. Broader verbosity audit if continuing: inspect global prompt/personality/skill layers that may over-encourage chat narration even when tool/UI output already carries the state. Focus on imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs working-style and execution-policy wording, any soul/personality rendering that affects verbosity, and mana-related skills under imp/.imp/skills/.
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:20:  3. Preference carried forward: in interactive/UI mode, prefer one visible mana state surface over redundant duplicate chat summaries; reserve extra chat narration for headless/no-UI flows or when the tool/UI did not make the change obvious.
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:24:  Second-pass audit completed. New findings + fixes: (1) there was still a prompt-level contradiction in imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs: the main mana doctrine still said to summarize the mana delta for the user, while the execution-policy layer had already been tightened to avoid verbose restatement when the tool/UI already shows the delta. Rewrote that remaining doctrine line to: update mana before the substantive reply; summarize only when it adds value beyond what the mana tool/UI already made visible. (2) inspected agent follow-up nudges in imp/crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs and found wording that still nudged visibility but did not explicitly discourage duplicate chat restatement. Tightened mana_externalization_follow_up_text() and confidence_continue_follow_up_text() so they preserve momentum while explicitly avoiding repeated summary when the delta is already visible. (3) discovered that Tower-root chats do not load imp/.imp/skills/... when cwd is /Users/asher/tower; skill discovery walks cwd ancestry and user config, and there was no /Users/asher/tower/.imp/skills override. That meant root chats were still inheriting the older global ~/.imp/skills/mana/SKILL.md. Added Tower-root overrides at /Users/asher/tower/.imp/skills/mana/SKILL.md and /Users/asher/tower/.imp/skills/mana-delegation/SKILL.md so root chats now inherit the tighter non-duplicative mana guidance. Verification for second pass: cargo test -p imp-core system_prompt_includes_conversation_time_mana_planning_doctrine -- --nocapture; cargo test -p imp-core agent_queues_confidence_continue_follow_up_after_visible_mana_turn -- --nocapture; cargo test -p imp-core agent_queues_mana_externalization_follow_up_after_planning_turn -- --nocapture. All passed. Residual note: the user-global ~/.imp/skills/mana/SKILL.md remains older and more verbose, but Tower-root chats are now overridden by the root-local skill files.
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:28:  User reported still seeing the old nudge string: 'Before you continue: externalize the durable plan or decomposition you just described into mana now ... and keep the delta visible.' Repo-grounded check confirms that exact old string no longer exists in current Tower source; current agent.rs wording now says to avoid extra chat restatement when the mana tool/UI already makes the delta obvious. Likely explanation: already-open imp session / previously queued follow-up / older built binary still running. Practical follow-up if needed: verify which imp binary/path interactive chats are launching from and whether fresh sessions pick up the changed runtime text.
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:32:  Verified install/runtime path and updated the main binary. Evidence: `which imp` resolved to /Users/asher/bin/imp, and before reinstall that binary still contained the old string ending with 'keep the delta visible'. Built fresh with `cargo build -p imp-cli`, verified /Users/asher/tower/target/debug/imp contained the new wording ('avoid extra chat restatement...' and the tightened confidence nudge), backed up the installed binary to /Users/asher/bin/.backup/imp.pre-mana-verbosity-fix.20260413-171033, then replaced /Users/asher/bin/imp with the fresh build. Post-install verification: /Users/asher/bin/imp sha256 now matches /Users/asher/tower/target/debug/imp, and `strings /Users/asher/bin/imp` shows the new wording, not the old 'keep the delta visible' string. Fresh imp sessions should now use the updated runtime text.
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:36:  Patched the user-global mana skill at /Users/asher/.imp/skills/mana/SKILL.md to match the tightened non-duplicative guidance used in Tower-local overrides. This matters for non-Tower chats or any cwd that does not have a nearer .imp/skills/mana override. There is no user-global mana-delegation skill present currently, so only the global mana skill needed patching. Fresh sessions outside Tower should now stop inheriting the older verbose mana guidance.
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:40:  Created the missing user-global delegation skill at /Users/asher/.imp/skills/mana-delegation/SKILL.md using the tightened non-duplicative version aligned with Tower-local guidance. Global mana-related skill coverage is now consistent for fresh non-Tower sessions: both ~/.imp/skills/mana/SKILL.md and ~/.imp/skills/mana-delegation/SKILL.md now discourage redundant mana delta restatement when tool/UI output already shows the change.
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:44:- mana
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:46:verify: cargo test -p imp-core system_prompt_ -- --nocapture && cargo test -p imp-core mana_tool_ -- --nocapture
.mana/249-reduce-duplicate-verbose-mana-change-narration-in.md:58:Audit imp's prompt/runtime/skill layers for over-reporting mana changes in interactive chat, identify where the same mana delta is being surfaced multiple times, and implement the smallest coherent fix. Current inspected evidence: imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs repeats between-turn mana update / mana delta summary guidance in both Mana doctrine and Execution discipline; imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs emits `mana delta: ...` tool outputs and widgets for create/update/close/etc.; background orchestration also queues an AgentCommand::FollowUp summary via make_follow_up_summary(); agent confidence follow-up text explicitly says the mana delta is already visible. Goal: keep mana visible without making the assistant restate the same change twice. Scope: prompt wording, mana tool follow-up behavior, and the mana skill text if needed. Verify by running focused imp-core tests covering system prompt wording and mana tool behavior; add/adjust tests if necessary.

.mana/290-complete-imp-codebase-quality-audit.md:9:acceptance: A report exists in chat or mana notes covering codebase-wide quality, naming, organization, major risks, and prioritized recommendations, with scope caveats and evidence from inspected files. Inventory of inspected file categories is recorded.
.mana/290-complete-imp-codebase-quality-audit.md:13:  Audit inventory: 147 code/config files included by extension (.rs, .sh, .py, .html, .toml) excluding target/.git/.mana/node_modules/dist/build; 92,100 total lines. Rust source breakdown: imp-core 62 files/45,180 lines; imp-tui 32/22,362; imp-llm 20/11,511; imp-cli 3/6,903; imp-lua 4/2,358. Full scanner availability wrapper could not run semgrep/qlty because commands were not installed despite reported availability. `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` currently fails in imp-llm oauth/kimi_code.rs with redundant_closure at line 173 and unnecessary_get_then_check at line 181. Large hotspots found: imp-tui/src/app.rs 7,094 lines; imp-cli/src/lib.rs 5,852; imp-core/src/agent.rs 5,786; imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs 3,525; imp-llm/src/providers/anthropic.rs 2,463; imp-core/src/session.rs 2,334. Root contains several zero-byte stray files likely from accidental shell redirection: CreateParams and files whose names begin with `{`. No code modified.
.mana/290-complete-imp-codebase-quality-audit.md:17:  User approved audit steps 1 and 2: fixed strict clippy issues in crates/imp-llm/src/oauth/kimi_code.rs, removed zero-byte accidental root files (CreateParams and five files whose names started with `{`). User also asked to use unit 290 to plan large-file splits and preferred AGENTS.md over crate READMEs. Added module-organization guidance to /Users/asher/imp/AGENTS.md: preserve behavior first, split by responsibility, keep API churn minimal, move tests with behavior, avoid mixing moves with semantic changes, and run narrow crate-level checks after each extraction. Recorded decomposition targets: imp-tui app.rs, imp-core agent.rs, imp-cli lib.rs, imp-core tools/mana.rs.
.mana/290-complete-imp-codebase-quality-audit.md:52:Audit the imp codebase for general code quality, variable naming, organization, and maintainability. User explicitly requested reading every code file completely. Approach: inventory code files, exclude generated/build/vendor artifacts, read code files in full in manageable batches, run quality scanners where useful, record findings with evidence paths/lines, and produce a concise audit report with prioritized recommendations. Do not modify code unless separately requested.
.mana/309-review-warp-open-source-repo-for-imp-ideas.md:9:acceptance: 'A concise review exists with: (1) what Warp is, (2) 5-10 concrete ideas applicable to imp, (3) source paths inspected in ~/warp, (4) risk/licensing notes, and (5) suggested mana follow-up tasks for high-value ideas.'

.mana/45.5.3-write-a-compact-imp-decomposition-order-for-post-c.md:12:  Use the completed 45.5.2 worker-boundary spec as an ordering anchor. Repo-grounded follow-on discovered while writing the spec: `imp-cli/src/main.rs` still contains stale `#[cfg(test)]` duplicate mana-unit parsing helpers (`ManaUnit`, `load_mana_unit`, `find_mana_dir`, `parse_mana_unit`), while `run_headless_mode()` already uses `imp_core::mana_worker::*` as the canonical path. Also, a repo scan found no current direct `imp_core::mana_worker::WorkerAssignment|WorkerAttempt|WorkerResult|WorkerStatus` call sites, so the immediate concrete decomposition slice after the spec is parser-helper quarantine/cleanup in `imp-cli`, not a broad contracts-callsite migration. Sequence 45.5.3 accordingly.
.mana/45.5.3-write-a-compact-imp-decomposition-order-for-post-c.md:16:  Execution delta to sequence explicitly in the decomposition-order note: 45.5.4 is now completed and should be treated as the first landed structural cleanup after the worker-boundary spec. After this change, the canonical repo reading is stronger: `imp-cli` no longer presents a shadow mana-unit parser path, and local CLI coverage should prefer `imp_core::mana_worker` helpers over test-only duplicate parsing models. Sequence the remaining post-contracts work from that state rather than from the earlier mixed ownership state.
.mana/272-add-native-video-context-ingestion-architecture-fo.md:22:Cross-project/root planning container for native YouTube/video interpretation support across Tower. Current chosen direction: imp should implement YouTube ingestion through a pure Rust/HTTP path first, not yt-dlp or media download, so the runtime has granular control over metadata, transcript choice, diagnostics, attribution, privacy/network behavior, and future context shaping. Initial target remains imp's existing `web.read` seam, where YouTube URLs are currently rejected. Related project-local planning exists in `/Users/asher/imp/.mana` as epic 271 and child 271.1; keep root as source of truth for architecture/decomposition if the work expands beyond imp-core.

.mana/271.2-harden-imp-spawn-and-mana-closetool-execution-agai.md:3:title: Harden imp spawn and mana close/tool execution against indefinite waits
.mana/271.2-harden-imp-spawn-and-mana-closetool-execution-agai.md:4:slug: harden-imp-spawn-and-mana-closetool-execution-agai
.mana/271.2-harden-imp-spawn-and-mana-closetool-execution-agai.md:13:  1. Replace imp-core mana_worker inline verify shell execution with mana_core::ops::verify::run_verify_command so worker verify honors per-unit/config verify_timeout instead of waiting forever.
.mana/271.2-harden-imp-spawn-and-mana-closetool-execution-agai.md:14:  2. Replace mana-cli MCP close handler's raw std::process::Command::output() verify path with canonical mana-core verify helper so close tool calls also honor timeout.
.mana/271.2-harden-imp-spawn-and-mana-closetool-execution-agai.md:20:  Starting implementation of the first hardening slice: route imp worker inline verify and mana MCP close verify through mana-core's timeout-aware verify helper, then add regression coverage for timed-out verify so stuck verify commands fail terminally instead of hanging.
.mana/271.2-harden-imp-spawn-and-mana-closetool-execution-agai.md:25:  - imp-core mana_worker verify now resolves per-unit/config verify_timeout from .mana and runs through mana_core::ops::verify::run_verify_command inside spawn_blocking, so worker inline verify returns terminal failure on timeout instead of hanging forever.
.mana/271.2-harden-imp-spawn-and-mana-closetool-execution-agai.md:26:  - mana-cli MCP close handler now uses the same canonical verify helper and timeout resolution before closing a unit.
.mana/271.2-harden-imp-spawn-and-mana-closetool-execution-agai.md:28:  - Verification passed: cargo check -p imp-core -p mana-cli; cargo test -p imp-core run_verify_command_ -- --nocapture; cargo test -p mana-cli mcp_close_unit_ -- --nocapture.

.mana/archive/2026/05/44.1.8-normalize-read-oriented-code-intelligence-queryres.md:12:  2026-04-09 follow-on note after verifying child `44.1.4` closure. This implementation/runtime contract epic should now treat the subsystem-boundary note `docs/design/imp-code-intelligence-subsystem.md` as canonical. Concretely: shape `44.1.8` around an imp-local code-intelligence seam (likely `imp-core/src/codeintel/`) that owns backend discovery, normalization, routing, freshness semantics, policy hooks, summarization, and evidence extraction. Keep tool schemas/registration/thin wrappers in `imp-core/src/tools/` for now. Do not turn this work into a shared Tower runtime contract and do not assign live backend lifecycle/orchestration to `mana`.
.mana/340-build-imp-gui-with-egui.md:13:  Decomposition direction after initial shell: prioritize the core imp-gui runtime boundary before live mana/agent wiring. Sequence: (1) inspect current GUI by running `cargo run -p imp-gui`; (2) implement internal runtime shell so egui sends commands and consumes events/snapshots instead of owning all state inline; (3) iterate visual/structural polish from hands-on app feedback; (4) add readonly real project/mana data; (5) add controlled run/agent execution flows with explicit approvals and streamed events. Keep runtime integration incremental and avoid coupling egui callbacks directly to imp-core internals.

If the resulting API/auth path fails, imp should fail immediately and show an explanatory error rather than silently degrading. Long-running mana work should surface completion both as a notification/status event and as an inline follow-up path.'

.mana/350.5-move-manastatusimprove-blocking-operations-off-the.md:20:Move synchronous mana navigator loading and status/improve git operations behind background tasks or cached snapshots. Current evidence: open_mana_navigator calls ManaNavigatorState::load synchronously; active_status_text/clean/improve merge paths call multiple run_git commands synchronously. These are command-triggered rather than per-frame but can make the TUI appear hung.

.mana/archive/2026/05/350.4.2-phase-2-convert-app-loop-to-tokio-select-with-rend.md:20:  Implemented direct wake for agent stream events. start_agent_task now moves AgentHandle.event_rx into a forwarding task that awaits events and sends RuntimeSignal::AgentEvent through App.runtime_signal_tx. App keeps command_tx/cancel_token in a replacement AgentHandle with an inert receiver for existing cancellation/control code. This means agent stream/tool events wake the tokio::select loop immediately rather than relying on idle polling. Added agent_event_task tracking and abort it when agent work is cleared/cancelled. collect_runtime_signals still has legacy try_recv drain against inert receiver for compatibility, but no longer carries live stream events. Verified send-message, agent task completion/failure, cancel, event_source, session_picker, mana_navigator, and slash mana tests.

.mana/306-improve-imp-tui-tool-call-display-and-inspector-ta.md:30:  Namespace/domain exploration update. User likes `teh33` as a personal handle and is considering company namespace. `Mana Systems` is name-squatted; user is not in love with Tower and wants to avoid generic infra names. User likes/mentioned `aethergraph` and `33works`; `33.works` is available but feels too ambiguous; `aethergraph.org` available. Ran tldx exploratory check over com/org/dev/io/ai/works. Results: aethergraph available on .ai/.org/.works but not .com/.io; 33works available on .ai/.dev/.io/.org/.works but not .com; hexwell available on .ai/.dev/.io/.org/.works but not .com; sigilware available .ai/.io/.org/.works but not .com/.dev; tehworks available .ai/.dev/.io/.org/.works but not .com; familiarworks available .ai/.dev/.org/.works but not .com/.io; spriteworks available .ai/.dev/.io/.works but not .com/.org; runewell available .dev/.io/.org/.works but not .com/.ai. Aetherforge unavailable across checked TLDs. Aethergrid only .works available among checked.
.mana/306-improve-imp-tui-tool-call-display-and-inspector-ta.md:34:  Additional namespace candidate: `managraph` is available on GitHub per user and domains checked by tldx: managraph.ai/.app/.dev/.io/.org/.systems/.works available; managraph.com unavailable. Compared with aethergraph: aethergraph.ai/.app/.dev/.org/.systems/.works available; aethergraph.com/.io unavailable. User says they think they like `aethergraph` more but is unsure. Naming assessment: managraph is clearer and closer to mana/work graph but more literal and still long; aethergraph is more distinctive/proper-noun, less literal, and probably stronger as a company namespace if the goal is not to overcenter mana as a product name.

Notes link the commits/regressions that caused this work.'

.mana/331-fix-mana-run-dispatch-and-terminal-slowdown-regres.md:21:Goal: fix the real `mana run` defects, not just observability.
.mana/331-fix-mana-run-dispatch-and-terminal-slowdown-regres.md:24:1. `mana run` queued an agent but the queued agent never actually ran.
.mana/331-fix-mana-run-dispatch-and-terminal-slowdown-regres.md:25:2. While `mana run` is active, the imp TUI/terminal becomes dramatically slower and text entry becomes difficult.

.mana/331-fix-mana-run-dispatch-and-terminal-slowdown-regres.md:36:- Avoid broad mana platform rewrites unless evidence points there.
.mana/331-fix-mana-run-dispatch-and-terminal-slowdown-regres.md:39:1. Audit the native run dispatch path from imp tool call → mana run orchestration → agent queue/spawn → worker process/session.
.mana/331-fix-mana-run-dispatch-and-terminal-slowdown-regres.md:40:2. Create or use a tiny disposable mana unit to reproduce whether worker starts and completes; inspect run_state, agents, and logs.

.mana/344.1-improve-native-git-commit-tool-for-targeted-commit.md:19:  Implemented first pass in `crates/imp-core/src/tools/git.rs`: added `preserve_index` schema, file-targeted commit path using temporary `GIT_INDEX_FILE` seeded from HEAD, stages only requested files from worktree, creates commit via `commit-tree`, moves HEAD with `update-ref`, and leaves real index/worktree untouched. Added tests for preserving staged/unrelated dirty changes and rejecting no-op targeted paths. Verification `cargo test -p imp-core git --lib` is currently blocked by unrelated existing compile errors in `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` against local mana API (`compute_run_plan` signature, `NativeRunParams` missing run_model/run_thinking in initializers, ReadyUnit fields). Need either fix/rebase mana integration or use narrower compile strategy after addressing those blockers.
.mana/344.1-improve-native-git-commit-tool-for-targeted-commit.md:23:  Checked mana close commit behavior in `/Users/asher/mana`. Current `mana-core` close path is target-scoped but not index-preserving: `ops/close.rs` builds `close_commit_target_paths(...)`, rebuilds index, then in non-worktree `auto_commit_on_close(...)` runs `git add -A -- <target_paths>` followed by plain `git commit -m`. Worktree close calls `handle_worktree_merge(...)`, which calls `worktree::commit_worktree_paths(...)`; that also runs `git add -A -- <paths>` then plain `git commit -m`. This scopes what gets staged, but because the commit is plain `git commit`, any unrelated changes that were already staged before `mana close` will also be included. Older CLI close helper in `mana-cli/src/commands/close/worktree_merge.rs` still has non-core `auto_commit_on_close` using unscoped `git add -A`, but current core close appears to use targeted paths.
.mana/344.1-improve-native-git-commit-tool-for-targeted-commit.md:48:Improve imp's native git commit tool so agents can make targeted commits without sweeping unrelated staged/dirty changes. Current pain: committing the /mana navigator slice in a dirty repo required manual temp worktree/index machinery. The git tool should make this safe and routine.
.mana/archive/2026/05/364.4-wire-native-imp-work-tool-to-global-project-scoped.md:29:- mana-replacement

.mana/archive/2026/05/333-fix-native-mana-run-worker-naming-responsiveness-a.md:3:title: Fix native mana run worker naming, responsiveness, and live inspector output
.mana/archive/2026/05/333-fix-native-mana-run-worker-naming-responsiveness-a.md:4:slug: fix-native-mana-run-worker-naming-responsiveness-a
.mana/archive/2026/05/333-fix-native-mana-run-worker-naming-responsiveness-a.md:9:acceptance: Worker runtime/name is imp, not pi; invoking native mana run avoids severe slowdown in the parent imp instance or isolates/backgrounds work appropriately; inspector sidebar receives/displays live run output; targeted tests/checks pass or blockers are documented.
.mana/archive/2026/05/333-fix-native-mana-run-worker-naming-responsiveness-a.md:15:close_reason: 'Implemented native mana run fixes: normalized legacy runtime direct_agent=pi to imp in run state/output, defaulted mana action=run to background execution to avoid blocking/slowing the invoking imp instance unless explicitly background=false, and made inspector sidebar show live streaming output plus run_id while mana runs execute. Verified with cargo test -p imp-core tools::mana::tests:: --quiet, cargo test -p imp-tui views::sidebar::tests:: --quiet, and cargo check -p imp-core && cargo check -p imp-tui.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/333-fix-native-mana-run-worker-naming-responsiveness-a.md:20:- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs
.mana/archive/2026/05/333-fix-native-mana-run-worker-naming-responsiveness-a.md:36:Investigate and fix imp's native mana run tool issues: (1) worker runtime/name currently appears as pi but should be imp, (2) using mana run from an agent severely slows down the active imp instance, (3) inspector sidebar should show live output while runs execute. Scope likely includes native mana tool implementation and TUI inspector/run-state rendering. Inspect current code paths before changing; prefer minimal fixes and targeted verification.
.mana/267-adopt-highest-value-product-lessons-from-opencode.md:9:acceptance: There is a durable epic in project mana that records the chosen opencode-inspired focus areas, their intent, and follow-up decomposition targets for imp.
.mana/267-adopt-highest-value-product-lessons-from-opencode.md:13:  User explicitly picked three opencode-inspired focus areas as high-value for imp: 1) first-class worktrees, 2) delegation UX, 3) packaging/install/docs polish. Treat these as productization layers on top of mana/imp, not replacements for the substrate.

.mana/349.2-persist-and-reconcile-agent-recovery-checkpoints-a.md:13:  Implemented first durable recovery reconciliation slice: added `agent/recovery.rs` with `RecoveryLedger`, `RecoveryReconciliation`, `IncompleteToolRecovery`, and `IncompleteToolState`. The ledger consumes persisted/emitted `RecoveryCheckpoint`s and can reconcile latest or specified turn into retryable vs unsafe incomplete tool side effects. It distinguishes planned-not-started, started-not-completed, and completed-not-appended; uses `ToolPlanCreated.success` as the initial retry-safety bit so read-only planned calls are retryable while mutable/external calls are unsafe by default. Added unit tests covering appended complete tools, read-only planned-not-started retryability, mutable started-not-completed unsafety, and completed-not-appended incompleteness. Exported recovery types from agent module. Verified with `cargo test -p imp-core agent::recovery --lib` and `cargo check -p imp-core`. Note: full `cargo check -p imp-core -p imp-cli -p imp-tui` previously exposed unrelated mana API drift in existing worktree; targeted imp-core check now passes after cleaning the local mana test fixture that referenced removed ReadyUnit fields.
.mana/349.2-persist-and-reconcile-agent-recovery-checkpoints-a.md:21:  Persisted recovery linkage slice: `SessionManager` now exposes `recovery_checkpoints()` and `recovery_ledger()` to rebuild a `RecoveryLedger` from persisted recovery checkpoint custom entries. `ImpSession::new` seeds the agent's ledger from the selected session, and `ImpSession::prompt` refreshes the ledger from session persistence before spawning the run. Added a session unit test proving recovery checkpoints round-trip into the ledger and reconcile as retryable incomplete work. Verified with `cargo test -p imp-core recovery --lib` and `cargo check -p imp-core --tests`.

Treat this as a migration/deprecation design decision, not an immediate file move. The migration plan should distinguish: (1) imp-work concepts to port to mana/work graph, (2) workflow/extensibility concepts worth preserving for imp, (3) code to archive temporarily, (4) code to delete after parity. Avoid moving crates until dependencies are removed and review/rollback path is clear.

It also explicitly calls out overloaded terms like profile, run, worker, and view for future normalization.
.mana/257-draft-imp-ontologymd-for-shared-featureruntime-lan.md:27:  - The draft already covers surfaces, modes, runs, sessions, context/memory layers, mana coordination terms, policy/capability terms, tools/extensions, providers/models, personality/config, and output/observability.
.mana/257-draft-imp-ontologymd-for-shared-featureruntime-lan.md:32:  User now wants a dedicated imp_ontology.md focused on naming the future-facing architecture pieces and candidate names, not only descriptive ontology cleanup. The document should enumerate the parts that need names, give a small description of each, and propose strong candidate names for iteration. This should reflect the broader future-facing discussion: imp as reusable runtime/API, generalized durable state engine beyond strict file-native mana, TypeScript-first extensions, and alignment with the /agents vision.
.mana/257-draft-imp-ontologymd-for-shared-featureruntime-lan.md:36:  Created /Users/asher/imp/imp_ontology.md as a future-facing naming workshop distinct from ontology.md. The new file enumerates major architecture parts, gives short descriptions, and proposes candidate names/current leans for ecosystem layers, runtime concepts, durable state concepts, extensibility concepts, and shell/surface concepts. It explicitly reflects current discussion: imp as reusable runtime/API, generalized durable state engine beyond strict file-native mana, TypeScript-first extensions, and alignment with the /agents object/assertion/event/rule worldview.
.mana/257-draft-imp-ontologymd-for-shared-featureruntime-lan.md:44:  Naming discussion to explore next: invert current public framing so `mana` names the underlying platform/substrate that apps are built on, while `imp` names the agent and possibly the primary human-facing shell built on that substrate. Important caveat to evaluate: if `mana` is renamed upward to the runtime/platform, the durable state layer beneath it still needs a clear code/function-oriented name (`mana state`, `state engine`, similar) so the architecture does not lose its distinction between execution and durable truth.
.mana/257-draft-imp-ontologymd-for-shared-featureruntime-lan.md:48:  New naming pressure: user questions whether `shell` should remain a distinct term at all, and suggests `imp` may simply be the human-facing environment/shell synonym rather than requiring a separate umbrella term. Need evaluate whether separating agent identity from human-facing environment is still useful, or whether `imp` should directly name both the agent and the primary interactive environment built on mana.
.mana/257-draft-imp-ontologymd-for-shared-featureruntime-lan.md:52:  imp_ontology.md was tightened again. `shell` was removed as a core ontology term. Current short framing is now: `mana` = platform, `imp` = agent + default human-facing environment, with neutral internal names `runtime`, `state`, `module system`, and `adapter layer`. User is now asking for the next practical plan and expects the refactor process to reveal holes, mismatches, and hardening opportunities.
.mana/257-draft-imp-ontologymd-for-shared-featureruntime-lan.md:60:  Naming set chosen for the compact imp ontology: `mana`, `imp`, `runtime`, `graph`, `extension`, `action`, and `task`. This replaces earlier tentative preferences for `state`, `module`, and `capability` in the short alignment glossary.
.mana/257-draft-imp-ontologymd-for-shared-featureruntime-lan.md:67:  - prompt rendering is currently seen as imp-owned rather than mana-owned, though mana may still assemble the structured scoped input that imp renders from
.mana/257-draft-imp-ontologymd-for-shared-featureruntime-lan.md:77:Create an ontology document for imp that establishes a stable shared vocabulary for describing imp's features, runtime surfaces, workflow concepts, and boundaries with mana. Start by surveying current imp docs and code-facing terms, then draft a first-pass ontology.md with term definitions, category structure, and explicit notes on ambiguous or overloaded language that should be normalized later.

.mana/archive/2026/05/50.6-design-the-cli-first-interactive-shell-path-for-im.md:323:  Durable decomposition externalized here because closed fact unit 54 is archived and native update/show did not resolve it post-close in this session. For 54 (`docs/rebuild/imp-cli-shell-transcript-ux.md`), the execution plan was intentionally minimal: (1) inspect the document, (2) check the exact verify literals (`compact tool notices`, `turn summaries`, `multiline input`, `:view`, and the fullscreen-independence sentence), (3) if only a literal phrase is missing, apply a one-line wording patch instead of broader edits, (4) rerun the exact verify command, and (5) close the fact unit. This keeps the durable delta visible in root mana even though the fact itself is archived.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-add-native-imp-work-tool-for-createlist-work-items.md:10:acceptance: imp-core exposes a native `work` tool backed by imp-work with actions to create and list aggregate work items for task, epic, memory, decision, and prototype; tests verify create/list behavior without mana.

Added crates/imp-core/src/tools/work.rs with WorkTool exposing actions create/list for task, epic, memory, decision, and prototype. Tool uses WorkStore aggregate APIs and writes to .imp/work without mana. Registered tool module and native tool in builder. Tests added: work_tool_creates_and_lists_task and work_tool_creates_multiple_item_kinds. Verification passed: cargo fmt -p imp-core; cargo test -p imp-core work_tool (2 passed); cargo test -p imp-work (46 passed). Existing unrelated imp-core import.rs warnings remain.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-add-native-imp-work-tool-for-createlist-work-items.md:56:Add the first imp-core tool surface for imp-work so agents can create/list native work items without going through mana. Keep scope narrow: local file-backed WorkStore actions for create and list.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-introduce-standalone-imp-core-build-boundary.md:11:  - `imp-core` has an explicit standalone/default build path that does not require runtime mana graph/imp-work state for normal agent runs.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-introduce-standalone-imp-core-build-boundary.md:12:  - Mana/work tools or modules are gated as optional integrations or proven not to affect standalone normal runs.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-introduce-standalone-imp-core-build-boundary.md:18:  Implementation progress: introduced imp-core feature boundaries for native mana/work tool registration. `imp-work` is now optional behind `work-integration`; `tools::work` and native WorkTool registration are gated. `tools::mana` and native ManaTool registration are gated behind `mana-integration` (mana crates remain normal deps for deeper compatibility modules for now). Prototype tool now runs standalone without imp-work, using local DTOs and no-op recording when `work-integration` is disabled. Verified standalone core build with `cargo check -p imp-core --no-default-features`; verified integration build/clippy with `cargo check -p imp-core --features mana-integration,work-integration` and `cargo clippy -p imp-core --lib --features mana-integration,work-integration -- -D warnings`. Remaining: run the full task verify gate before closing; decide whether to further gate/remove mana-core/mana-cli deeper compatibility modules in follow-up.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-introduce-standalone-imp-core-build-boundary.md:52:Make imp-core able to build in a standalone profile that does not require mana-cli, mana-core, or imp-work integrations for normal agent runtime. Gate mana/work durable graph tools behind optional features or adapter seams. Start with Cargo/features and module registration boundaries; do not delete imp-work in this slice.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:13:  User interrupted before review continuation to require mana skill loading. Mana skill loaded with read. Must now continue the actual spawn code review directly, not just create/update mana. Follow mana doctrine: treat current review as audit/planning, use notes for durable findings, no implementation yet, and only create worker-ready follow-up units after enough code inspection and clear recommendations.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:17:  Spawn code review inspection completed enough for initial findings. Files inspected: `crates/imp-core/src/tools/imp.rs` (schema, resolve_spawn_request, execute_unit_spawn, execute_ad_hoc_spawn, tests), `crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs` (WorkerRunOptions, prepare/finalize/run_worker_assignment, verify/close behavior), `crates/imp-core/src/imp_session.rs` (SessionOptions, create/prompt/wait/cancel/abort), `crates/imp-core/src/config.rs` (AgentMode tool allowlists). Key findings: schema is broad and mode-conditional but flat/optional; `action=delegate` compatibility and `delegation_mode` duplicate add token noise; `idempotency_key` appears metadata-only in spawn tool; unit spawn has no timeout/cancellation wrapper unlike ad_hoc; ad_hoc has timeout/cancel/abort but returns timeout as Err rather than structured ToolOutput details; numeric options use u64->u32 casts without upper bounds; `system_prompt` override can replace worker system prompt and weaken worker constraints; `no_tools` means different things across modes (unit no tools, ad_hoc Reviewer mode + no tools); `mana_dir` can target arbitrary resolved path/project root; output details are useful but mixed/duplicative and omit some stable fields like cwd/mana_dir/effective mode/max_turns/timeout for unit; schema could be more token-efficient by removing action/delegate, making mode required or using separate minimal mode-specific fields, and tightening descriptions/defaults.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:21:  User raised architecture/product question after spawn review: perhaps `spawn` should only handle ad_hoc transient helper workers, while durable mana unit work should be owned by the native `mana` tool (which already has run/run_state/logs/agents and unit orchestration semantics). This would reduce spawn schema complexity and separate transient delegation from durable mana execution. Need evaluate against current overlap: `spawn mode=unit` wraps `mana_worker::run_worker_assignment` directly; `mana` tool also supports run orchestration. Review should consider migration path: remove/deprecate unit mode from spawn, route user/agent guidance to mana for units, and keep spawn as a simple bounded ad_hoc helper tool.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:25:  Mana skill reloaded at user request before continuing spawn design. Must follow mana unit design/decomposition/retry/handoff guidance. Current open architecture question: make `spawn` ad_hoc-only and leave durable mana-unit execution to native `mana` tool. Need answer as design recommendation, then if accepted create worker-ready units with exact files and verify gates.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:29:  Mana skill loaded again on explicit user instruction before continuing. User has decided `prompt` should be the only required core field for the simplified ad_hoc helper tool. Open design questions to answer next: whether `spawn` is the best agent-facing name; whether tool access should be boolean (`tools` true/false) or a richer worker type; and what `worker type` enum should be if used. Must answer as design discussion, then update child unit 304.3.1.1 once design settles.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:33:  User wants configurable defaults per `ask_agent` worker_type rather than hardcoded runtime knobs. Example: `worker` may default to low reasoning, while `no_tools` may want extra-high/high reasoning. This implies config-driven profiles for worker_type defaults: thinking, model, max_turns, max_tokens, timeout, tool/mode policy. Need include in ask_agent implementation/design. User agrees with ask_agent direction but wants one more naming pass for adjacent alternatives before finalizing. Next tool after this naming/config discussion is likely native `mana`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:37:  Mana skill loaded on explicit user instruction before continuing. Follow it for unit design, decomposition, retries, and worker handoff. Current required next steps: finish ask_agent implementation unit details, then begin native mana tool schema review starting with schema inspection before recommendations.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:43:  - 304.3.1.2 `Update guidance for spawn ad_hoc and mana unit split`: docs/system-prompt guidance after runtime change.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:44:  Next discussion moves to native mana tool schema audit/review.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:57:- 'Decision direction: split responsibilities so `spawn` is an ad_hoc transient helper-agent tool only, while durable mana unit execution/orchestration belongs to the native `mana` tool (`run`, `run_state`, `logs`, `agents`, verify/close flows). This removes schema overlap and clarifies tool choice: use `spawn` for bounded helper prompts; use `mana` for tracked units.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:58:- 'Decision: for the simplified ad_hoc-only helper tool, `prompt` should be the only required core field in the model-facing schema/runtime. Unit/mana execution remains out of scope for spawn and belongs to native mana.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:59:- 'Decision proposal to carry into spawn simplification: rename the ad_hoc-only helper-agent tool from `spawn` to `ask_agent` because it is more intention-revealing for agents than runtime-oriented `spawn`, and avoids confusion with durable mana unit execution. `delegate` is rejected as too easily confused with tracked mana delegation.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:61:- 'Final naming decision: the ad_hoc helper-agent tool should be named `ask_agent`. It replaces the current `spawn` model-facing surface for transient helper prompts. Durable unit execution remains in native `mana`.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:69:- `crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs` provides the canonical mana unit worker runtime used by unit spawn.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:75:2. Inspect relevant worker/session integration only as needed: `crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs`, `crates/imp-core/src/imp_session.rs`, and config/mode filtering.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:78:5. Identify hardening gaps: ad_hoc runaway risk, model/provider validation, prompt/system_prompt injection risk, no_tools semantics, idempotency being metadata-only if confirmed, path/mana_dir targeting, cancellation/timeout cleanup, and permission boundaries.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1-review-spawn-tool-code-and-schema.md:85:- Prefer mana notes/decisions for durable findings.
.mana/28.5.1-patch-imp-system-prompt-with-mana-first-planning-d.md:3:title: Patch imp system prompt with mana-first planning doctrine

.mana/28.5.1-patch-imp-system-prompt-with-mana-first-planning-d.md:9:acceptance: The assembled prompt explicitly teaches conversation-time mana externalization triggers, artifact mapping, confirmation thresholds, and between-turn delta reporting behavior in a concise form, and tests assert the new doctrine appears.
.mana/28.5.1-patch-imp-system-prompt-with-mana-first-planning-d.md:17:  2026-04-09: Loaded mana skill before proceeding. Dependency 28.2 is not present in the active index but does exist as closed archived unit `.mana/archive/2026/04/28.2-specify-clean-ownership-boundary-between-mana-and-imp.md`; parent 28 notes also call out a traceability inconsistency for 28.1/28.2/28.3. Proceeding with prompt/test patch based on the archived closed dependency and current unit description/verify gate.
.mana/28.5.1-patch-imp-system-prompt-with-mana-first-planning-d.md:21:  Loaded the mana skill as requested. Plan: patch imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs by tightening the mana doctrine and execution discipline with conversation-time externalization triggers, artifact mapping (epic/child job/note/decision vs fact), confirmation thresholds, and a between-turn mana-delta rule; then add prompt-assembly tests that assert the new doctrine text. Dependency 28.2 is still unresolved in the active index, but proceeding against the explicit task contract and verify gate provided here unless inspection reveals a conflicting spec.
.mana/28.5.1-patch-imp-system-prompt-with-mana-first-planning-d.md:25:  2026-04-09: Patched `imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs` to encode conversation-time mana-first planning doctrine. Added explicit triggers for proactive planning/design externalization, artifact mapping across epic/child job/note/decision with facts reserved for verifiable claims, confirmation-threshold guidance, and between-turn mana-delta update/reporting language. Added prompt-assembly test `system_prompt_includes_conversation_time_mana_planning_doctrine`. Verify passed: `grep -q "between-turn" imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs && grep -q "epic, child job, note, or decision" imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs && cargo test -p imp-core system_prompt`.
.mana/28.5.1-patch-imp-system-prompt-with-mana-first-planning-d.md:29:  Verified imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs now includes the conversation-time mana planning doctrine: proactive externalization during planning/design, artifact mapping for epic/child job/note/decision vs facts, confirmation thresholds, and a between-turn mana-delta instruction. Prompt tests include system_prompt_includes_conversation_time_mana_planning_doctrine and the task verify gate passed: grep for 'between-turn', grep for 'epic, child job, note, or decision', and cargo test -p imp-core system_prompt (45 passed).
.mana/28.5.1-patch-imp-system-prompt-with-mana-first-planning-d.md:33:  2026-04-09 durable implementation summary: landed the prompt-side slice only in `imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs`. The assembled system prompt now teaches (1) proactive conversation-time externalization during planning/design turns, (2) artifact selection across epic, child job, note, or decision, with facts reserved for verifiable claims, (3) confirmation thresholds that allow capturing plan artifacts without asking but still require confirmation for consequential scope/architecture/destructive/execution commitments, and (4) between-turn mana update + mana-delta reporting behavior. Coverage added in `system_prompt_includes_conversation_time_mana_planning_doctrine`; verify passed with the unit verify gate.
.mana/28.5.1-patch-imp-system-prompt-with-mana-first-planning-d.md:37:  Durable implementation mapping captured from this turn: (1) identity-layer Mana doctrine now covers conversation-time planning/design externalization triggers, artifact selection across epic/child job/note/decision, fact reservation for verifiable claims, confirmation thresholds, and the between-turn update/report rule; (2) execution-discipline layer reinforces that when durable planning state changes, mana must be updated before the substantive reply and the response should summarize the mana delta; (3) tests assert the doctrine text so prompt assembly regressions fail visibly. This unit is implementation-complete and ready to close on the existing verify gate.

.mana/354.1-guard-tui-agent-start-request-against-pre-redraw-p.md:10:acceptance: A focused regression test or check fails if agent_start_request() performs Lua tool initialization, mana prompt context loading, or other expensive setup before the deferred background agent start path. Existing echo-redraw test still passes.
.mana/354.1-guard-tui-agent-start-request-against-pre-redraw-p.md:18:  2026-05-19: Trace showed actual 14.6s startup delay was phase=prompt_context, i.e. imp_core::mana_prompt_context::load_session_prompt_context called in TUI start_agent_from_request before AgentBuilder::build and before first AgentStart event. This was the same preload-work regression pattern. Removed TUI preloading of prompt_context entirely so AgentBuilder loads mana_prompt_context in its own build path; TUI no longer blocks first event on mana_core::api::memory_context. Verified agent_start_request_keeps_expensive_startup_work_deferred, send_message_defers_agent_start_until_after_echo_redraw, cargo check -p imp-tui -p imp-core, then installed.
