.mana/archive/2026/05/365.12-design-minimal-workflow-cli-and-structured-executa.md:13:  - Map the best mana concepts to workflow equivalents: unit handoff, context construction, acceptance, dependencies, verification, evidence, decisions, notes, orchestration across many units.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.12-design-minimal-workflow-cli-and-structured-executa.md:21:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.12-design-minimal-workflow-cli-and-structured-executa.md:47:Explore the new direction that a standalone `workflow` tool may replace mana as the executable orchestration layer for agent work. Keep CLI surface minimal while moving richness into a highly structured workflow schema/engine. Preserve the strongest mana idea: a unit can be handed to an agent with enough assembled context, acceptance, verification, and evidence requirements to perform accurate work; workflows add programmatic planning/execution and guardrails to systematically mitigate common LLM-generated-code weaknesses. This is design-only first; do not implement until the artifact model, command surface, context assembly model, and imp integration boundary are accepted.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:3:title: Implement turn-scoped mana review packet aggregation in imp-core
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:4:slug: implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:12:  Contract input from 28.5.3: implementation should realize the canonical `TurnManaDelta` shape and materiality policy from `docs/rebuild/mana-turn-delta-contract.md`. Grounded mutable source actions to normalize are: `create`, `close`, `update`, `notes_append`, `decision_add`, `decision_resolve`, `reopen`, `fail`, `delete`, `dep_add`, `dep_remove`, and `fact_create`. Treat `claim`/`release` as non-material for between-turn review by default. `update` must fan out into field-level semantics rather than remain an opaque action. Assembly boundary remains one assistant turn (`TurnStart` -> tool mutations -> `TurnEnd`).
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:16:  Implementation should assemble TurnManaDelta at the imp turn boundary in agent/runtime flow, not inside the mana tool itself. Source inputs are successful mutating mana tool results from imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs; they must be normalized into one turn payload by merging repeated unit changes, coalescing note/decision events, deriving anchor_unit after all mutations are known, dropping net-zero changes, and emitting explicit no_change when no reviewable durable mutation remains. Exclude read/evaluate/orchestration actions and exclude claim/release from v1 review semantics.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:20:  Inspected the mana skill, unit 28.5.6, docs/rebuild/mana-between-turn-review-affordance.md, docs/rebuild/mana-turn-delta-contract.md, imp-core agent/tool seams, and mana tool detail shapes. Implementation plan is to add explicit turn-scoped review packet and accumulator types in imp-core, thread an accumulator handle through ToolContext, have the runtime record successful mutating mana ToolResult details into the accumulator at ToolExecutionEnd rather than assembling inside the tool, normalize action-local details into canonical turn fields (including update field fan-out and decision resolution text normalization), and attach the aggregated packet to TurnEnd so future CLI/TUI/RPC consumers can render it without depending on the current mana widget.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:24:  Implementation plan grounded in current code and contract: add a new imp-core turn-batched mana review module with canonical `TurnManaDelta` / `ManaReviewState` semantics plus an internal accumulator; thread an accumulator handle through `ToolContext`; have `tools/mana.rs` record action-local mutation inputs with pre/post unit snapshots for mutating actions (`create`, `close`, `update`, `notes_append`, `decision_add`, `decision_resolve`, `reopen`, `fail`, `delete`, `dep_add`, `dep_remove`, `fact_create`) while preserving existing per-action widget/status UX; finalize one turn packet in `agent.rs` at the assistant turn boundary and store the frozen review packet there. Normalization will compare pre/post unit snapshots for field-level semantics, derive proposed children / anchor unit, strip note timestamp noise by using authored fragments from tool params, derive decision events from initial vs final decision sets, exclude `claim`/`release`/run-state actions, and emit explicit `no_change` when no reviewable durable net change survives the turn.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:28:  Follow-on decomposition from `docs/rebuild/imp-between-turn-mana-review-ui.md`: this unit should own only the canonical turn-batched runtime packet and accumulator, not presentation. Concrete runtime split: (1) define canonical `TurnManaDelta` / `ManaReviewState` data plus accumulator and frozen per-turn packet, (2) collect mutating mana action results across a single assistant turn, (3) normalize into distinct `anchor_unit`, `touched_units`, `proposed_children`, `material_field_changes`, `notes_appended`, `decision_events`, and `unresolved_consequential_choices`, (4) emit explicit `no_change` / `changed` / `needs_decision`, and (5) attach the frozen packet at the turn boundary for downstream renderers. Important carry-forward from the UI spec: do not collapse note/decision semantics back into generic update noise; renderer-facing counts and truncation belong to 28.5.7, not here.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:32:  Cross-unit blocker surfaced while verifying 51.5 (`cargo test -p imp-core mana_prompt_context`): current imp-core compile drift in the turn-scoped mana review work is preventing unrelated prompt-layer tests from building. Concrete failures observed: missing `Agent::finish_turn_mana_review`, `AgentEvent::TurnEnd` initializers/patterns not yet updated for `mana_review`, `ToolContext` initializers missing `turn_mana_review`, and `mana_review.rs` enums used in `BTreeSet` still missing `Ord` derives (`ManaTouchKind`, `ManaReviewScopeKind`). This unit now blocks 51.5 verify closure even though 51.5's touched files are locally complete.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:36:- mana
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:42:verify: cd /Users/asher/imp && rg -q 'TurnManaReview|ManaReviewState|needs_decision|no_change' crates/imp-core/src && cargo check -p imp-core
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:55:Implement the runtime-side turn-scoped mana review packet described in `docs/rebuild/mana-between-turn-review-affordance.md`.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:58:Shift from per-call, last-write-wins mana delta widget updates to a turn-level aggregated review packet that another UI surface can render after each conversational turn.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:61:- `docs/rebuild/mana-between-turn-review-affordance.md` is the design source of truth.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:62:- `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` currently emits per-action `set_mana_delta_widget(...)` updates such as created/updated/notes/decision-added/decision-resolved.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:68:1. Introduce a runtime-side aggregated mana review representation for one conversational turn.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:76:4. Stop relying on only the last mana widget update as the durable between-turn story.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:81:- Thread an accumulator through the runtime so mana mutations during a turn append structured records rather than only mutating a widget.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:82:- Reuse existing structured `ToolOutput.details` from mana actions where possible instead of inventing a second ad hoc parser.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:84:- Prefer adding a small explicit helper/API for mana mutation recording over burying JSON-shape assumptions across the agent loop.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:87:- `docs/rebuild/mana-between-turn-review-affordance.md` (read for contract)
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:90:- `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` (modify — emit structured mutation records into the accumulator)
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:94:- `imp-core` contains explicit turn-level mana review types or equivalent runtime abstraction.
.mana/28.5.6-implement-turn-scoped-mana-review-packet-aggregati.md:96:- Mana tool mutations can accumulate into the turn-level structure instead of only overwriting a widget string.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.5-persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state.md:3:title: Persist and classify interrupted mana run state
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.5-persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state.md:4:slug: persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.5-persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state.md:9:acceptance: A stale or interrupted background mana run is visible after process restart or run_state reload with enough persisted state to avoid presenting it as confidently running forever.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.5-persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state.md:13:  Starting implementation. Scope: only crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs. Need to inspect current NativeRunState/ManaRunStore/update_with_event/load_persisted/run_state rendering/tests, then persist material events and classify stale/interrupted without claiming restart support.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.5-persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state.md:17:  Implementation mostly complete, but verification hit an environment/toolchain blocker: cargo now fails manifest parsing because Cargo.toml uses edition = "2026" and installed cargo reports it only supports up to 2024, even with cargo +nightly (cargo 1.96.0-nightly 2026-03-21). This is unrelated to the mana.rs change; earlier checks in this session passed before the toolchain/manifest mismatch surfaced.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.5-persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state.md:19:  Code changes currently made in crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs:
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.5-persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state.md:24:  - Added mana_run_state tests for material event tracking and stale running-run classification.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.5-persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state.md:26:  Need next step: resolve cargo edition/toolchain mismatch or use the repo's expected build wrapper if one exists, then run `cargo test -p imp-core mana_run_state --lib`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.5-persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state.md:35:  - Implementation changes are made, but final cargo verification is blocked by the current worktree manifest/toolchain mismatch: Cargo.toml has edition = "2026" from pre-existing/unrelated dirty changes, while available cargo (stable 1.93.1 and nightly 1.96.0-nightly) reject edition 2026 and only support up to 2024. I did not edit/revert Cargo.toml because it is unrelated user work. Non-cargo verify passed: rg "interrupted|stale|run_state" crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs. Attempted cargo test -p imp-core mana_run_state --lib and cargo +nightly -Zunstable-options test -p imp-core mana_run_state --lib; both failed before compile while parsing Cargo.toml. Implementation details: added last_event_at_ms to NativeRunState, persisted run store after each stream event, classified stale starting/running persisted runs as interrupted on load, surfaced interrupted/stale recovery details in run_state output, and added focused mana_run_state tests.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.5-persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state.md:39:  `rg "interrupted|stale|run_state" crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs && cargo test -p imp-core mana_run_state --lib`
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.5-persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state.md:41:close_reason: 'Verified and closed. Implementation in crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs persists native mana run state after material stream events, tracks last_event_at_ms, classifies stale persisted starting/running runs as interrupted on load, and surfaces interrupted/stale status plus next actions/stale_workers in run_state output. Focused tests for material event tracking and stale running-run classification pass. Verification passed: rg "interrupted|stale|run_state" crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs && cargo test -p imp-core mana_run_state --lib; cargo check -p imp-core. Previous Cargo.toml edition blocker is no longer present in the current worktree.'

.mana/archive/2026/05/317.5-persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state.md:134:Goal: Harden native mana run-state persistence for interrupted/background runs so long-session recovery can reason about orchestration state after crashes or disconnects.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.5-persist-and-classify-interrupted-mana-run-state.md:136:Current state: 317.3 inspection found ManaRunStore updates in memory for each run event, but persists run_state.json only on finish/fail. Background runs update NativeRunState through an event sink, but a process crash mid-run can lose recent event updates and leave stale running state depending on prior persistence.

.mana/331.3-reject-zero-effective-concurrency-in-mana-run.md:3:title: Reject zero effective concurrency in mana run
.mana/331.3-reject-zero-effective-concurrency-in-mana-run.md:4:slug: reject-zero-effective-concurrency-in-mana-run
.mana/331.3-reject-zero-effective-concurrency-in-mana-run.md:9:acceptance: mana-cli/imp reject or clearly error on jobs=0 or max_concurrent=0; no run can emit queued units then finish with zero starts/dones silently; regression tests cover this edge case.
.mana/331.3-reject-zero-effective-concurrency-in-mana-run.md:12:- mana-run
.mana/331.3-reject-zero-effective-concurrency-in-mana-run.md:15:verify: cd /Users/asher/mana && cargo test -p mana-cli zero_concurrency --lib && cd /Users/asher/imp && cargo test -p imp-core mana_validation --no-default-features
.mana/331.3-reject-zero-effective-concurrency-in-mana-run.md:22:- With `.mana/config.yaml` containing `max_concurrent: 0`, `mana run <id> --json-stream -j 1` emits RunStart with the unit in queued state, then RoundStart/RoundEnd/RunEnd with zero closed/failed/skipped and no UnitStart/UnitDone. The unit remains open.
.mana/331.3-reject-zero-effective-concurrency-in-mana-run.md:26:1. In `/Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/mod.rs`, validate effective concurrency before emitting RunStart or scheduling: jobs and config.max_concurrent must produce max_jobs >= 1 when there are runnable units.
.mana/331.3-reject-zero-effective-concurrency-in-mana-run.md:27:2. In `/Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/ready_queue.rs`, add a defensive guard in `run_ready_queue_direct` returning an error or stream error if `cfg.max_jobs == 0`.
.mana/331.3-reject-zero-effective-concurrency-in-mana-run.md:28:3. In imp native mana tool (`/Users/asher/imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs`), validate model-facing `jobs` as >= 1 to prevent passing zero.
.mana/331.3-reject-zero-effective-concurrency-in-mana-run.md:29:4. Add regression tests in mana-cli for max_concurrent=0 / jobs=0 and in imp-core for `jobs:0` validation.
.mana/45.5-phase-4-rebuild-imp-around-stable-workerruntime-se.md:17:  Boundary checkpoint from completed spec 45.5.2: treat `imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs` as the explicit canonical worker-runtime seam for mana-assigned single-unit execution inside `imp`. Durable split to preserve through Phase 4: shared contracts live in `tower_contracts::worker`; imp-local worker execution logic stays in `imp-core` (`load_assignment*`, task/context shaping, prefill assembly, assignment-scoped dependency summaries, outcome production). First narrow follow-on sequence after the contracts move: (1) keep `mana_worker.rs` as the runtime center, (2) keep CLI/tool surfaces as adapters around that center, and (3) remove or quarantine stale duplicate mana-unit parsing helpers still left in `imp-cli/src/main.rs` so they no longer read as a second runtime path.
.mana/45.5-phase-4-rebuild-imp-around-stable-workerruntime-se.md:21:  Phase 4 decomposition checkpoint after 45.5.4: the first narrow code-move implied by `docs/rebuild/imp-worker-boundary-plan.md` is now landed in code. `imp/crates/imp-cli/src/main.rs` no longer contains the stale duplicate mana-unit parsing/loading helpers (`ManaUnit`, `load_mana_unit`, `find_mana_dir`, `parse_mana_unit`). Current intended reading to preserve through subsequent slices: `tower_contracts::worker` owns shared worker vocabulary, `imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs` remains the canonical single-unit worker-runtime seam, and `imp-cli` is a thin adapter around that runtime for `imp run <unit-id>`. Do not reintroduce CLI-local worker contract or parser ownership in follow-on slices; broader decomposition remains deferred to 45.5.1 / 45.5.3.
.mana/45.5-phase-4-rebuild-imp-around-stable-workerruntime-se.md:25:  Execution delta externalized from the worker-boundary cleanup slice: imp/crates/imp-cli/src/main.rs no longer carries the stale duplicate mana-unit helper set under the old names (ManaUnit/load_mana_unit/find_mana_dir/parse_mana_unit), and test coverage now targets imp_core::mana_worker prompt/context helpers rather than a shadow CLI-side parser path. Durable fact recorded as unit 69. Residual blocker from the original verify command was unrelated workspace breakage in mana-core at the time of checking, with missing autonomy-related types/fields causing cargo check -p imp-cli to fail while compiling mana-core.
.mana/45.5-phase-4-rebuild-imp-around-stable-workerruntime-se.md:44:Goal: shrink authority concentration in `imp-core` and move `imp` onto the explicit contract + runner boundary. Current inspected state: `imp-core/src/agent.rs`, `tools/mod.rs`, `session.rs`, `tools/mana.rs`, `config.rs`, and `mana_worker.rs` are the main hot spots. This phase depends on the earlier contract and runner work.
.mana/46-broaden-imp-attention-beyond-toolsprompting-under.md:11:  2. Each track distinguishes existing open mana work from missing or lightly implemented areas discovered in the current codebase.
.mana/46-broaden-imp-attention-beyond-toolsprompting-under.md:28:     - Gaps/concerns: discoverability epic still open, mana status surfaces still partial, settings surface is large, capabilities like memory/session search/planning/checkpoints remain easy to miss.
.mana/46-broaden-imp-attention-beyond-toolsprompting-under.md:41:     - Evidence from current scans: `imp-tui/src/app.rs` ~5145 lines, `imp-core/src/agent.rs` ~3578, `imp-cli/src/main.rs` ~2915, `imp-llm/src/providers/anthropic.rs` ~2451, `imp-core/src/session.rs` ~2026, `imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` ~1804.
.mana/46-broaden-imp-attention-beyond-toolsprompting-under.md:56:Goal: identify the imp subsystems that have not received the same implementation depth, polish, and follow-through as the native tool work and system prompt builder, then turn that analysis into mana-tracked follow-up.
.mana/46-broaden-imp-attention-beyond-toolsprompting-under.md:60:- Current scans show multiple large, mixed-responsibility modules that likely slowed polish elsewhere: `crates/imp-tui/src/app.rs` (~5145 lines), `crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs` (~3578), `crates/imp-cli/src/main.rs` (~2915), `crates/imp-llm/src/providers/anthropic.rs` (~2451), `crates/imp-core/src/session.rs` (~2026), `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` (~1804).
.mana/46-broaden-imp-attention-beyond-toolsprompting-under.md:61:- Existing open work already clusters around under-attended areas: mana/TUI surfaces (`27`, `29`), guardrails/policy (`31.2`-`31.4`), usage/reporting follow-through (`37.5`), compaction and long-session behavior (`.4.2`, `.5`), auth/secrets (`.3`), extension architecture (`44`), and exploratory planning/runtime architecture (`45`).
.mana/46-broaden-imp-attention-beyond-toolsprompting-under.md:66:- Reconcile existing open mana work with current source evidence.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304-audit-and-harden-imp-tool-schemas.md:13:  Mana skill reloaded and applied as operating guidance for the remaining tool audit. Going forward: keep epic 304 as the parent planning container; create worker-ready child tasks only after enough code inspection to choose approach; use notes for partial audit findings; use decisions for accepted tool interface/security tradeoffs; keep implementation units narrow (1 tool/family, 1 outcome, concrete verify); do not retry failed worker runs unchanged; prefer native mana actions over shell for mana state.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304-audit-and-harden-imp-tool-schemas.md:17:  Tool audit coverage checkpoint: substantial imp-core native tool audits/plans are now captured for bash, ask_user, ask_agent/spawn, mana, scan, git/worktree, web, recall, and extend. Remaining major imp-core native tool audits are the file tools: read, write, and edit. Host/runtime-provided tools outside the imp-core registry that may still merit optional review include audit_scan, color_palette, and openrouter_secret_run.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304-audit-and-harden-imp-tool-schemas.md:21:  User raised new toolset design question before remaining read/write/edit audits: should imp add native grep/glob/list tools, and should it add native test/lint/verify tools? Need answer as product/tool-boundary analysis. Consider current bash can handle rg/fd/ls/test commands but native tools could improve schema/token efficiency, output contracts, safety, and discoverability. Must weigh tool sprawl vs agent reliability. Potential direction: add small read-only discovery tools only if they outperform bash+rg/fd in structured output; add verify/test as project-configured command runner only if it ties into mana verify and avoids generic command sprawl.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304-audit-and-harden-imp-tool-schemas.md:25:  Implementation sequencing recommendation after completing tool audit planning: do not attempt all fixes as one broad wave. Start with inventory 304.1, then one contained low-risk schema cleanup such as read 304.8.3.1. Avoid starting mana rework (workers already failed unchanged), ask_agent (session/mode/config heavy), bash policy (permission/parser dependency), git/worktree (repo mutation safety), or TypeScript extensions (design-heavy) as first implementation. If using workers, run one at a time initially because prior two-worker wave failed.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304-audit-and-harden-imp-tool-schemas.md:49:- Phase 2 audit high-risk tools first: shell, edit/write/read, git, mana, spawn, web.
.mana/archive/2026/03/39-teach.md:3:title: Teach imp to decompose broad work into scoped mana child jobs
.mana/archive/2026/03/39-teach.md:10:  imp has explicit decomposition guidance or behavior for broad work that should become child mana jobs.
.mana/archive/2026/03/39-teach.md:12:  The work stays inside imp boundaries and reuses mana as the planning and delegation substrate.
.mana/archive/2026/03/39-teach.md:21:  Implemented as part of MANA_DELEGATION_GUIDANCE const in system_prompt.rs. The const includes: decomposition heuristics (when to create child jobs vs do it yourself), child job description quality rules (concrete steps, file paths, embedded context, scope boundaries, anti-patterns), verify command guidance (existence checks, avoid bare runners), and sizing rules (one outcome, 1-5 files, pick the approach). Guidance is injected for Full, Orchestrator, and Planner modes when the mana tool is present. 3 regression tests added: delegation appears in Full mode with mana, skipped for Worker, skipped without mana tool.
.mana/archive/2026/03/39-teach.md:24:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/03/39-teach.md:36:Once the delegated child-job contract exists, imp should start using it when work becomes too broad or multi-stage for one coherent worker pass. Right now decomposition is not yet explicit enough: broad work can remain in one thread instead of being turned into sharply scoped mana child jobs.
.mana/archive/2026/03/39-teach.md:39:Teach imp to decompose broad work into scoped mana child jobs.
.mana/archive/2026/03/39-teach.md:48:- `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs`
.mana/archive/2026/03/39-teach.md:60:- Do **not** build a second planning system outside mana
.mana/archive/2026/03/39-teach.md:72:- imp has explicit decomposition guidance or behavior for broad work that should become child mana jobs

.mana/archive/2026/05/341.4-add-regression-tests-for-native-mana-run-model-and.md:125:close_reason: 'Acceptance met with focused verification: added pure helper tests for native mana run model/thinking propagation and ran `cargo test -p imp-core native_worker_options` (2 passed) plus `cargo test -p imp-core native_run_runtime_info` (1 passed). Also ran `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --no-run` successfully. The stored broad verify `cargo test -p imp-core mana` currently fails in unrelated `tools::mana::tests::reparent_moves_child_between_parents` because reparent is disabled against the current mana-core API; that is not part of the model/thinking propagation scope.'

.mana/archive/2026/05/341.4-add-regression-tests-for-native-mana-run-model-and.md:193:  notes: 'Acceptance met with focused verification: added pure helper tests for native mana run model/thinking propagation and ran `cargo test -p imp-core native_worker_options` (2 passed) plus `cargo test -p imp-core native_run_runtime_info` (1 passed). Also ran `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --no-run` successfully. The stored broad verify `cargo test -p imp-core mana` currently fails in unrelated `tools::mana::tests::reparent_moves_child_between_parents` because reparent is disabled against the current mana-core API; that is not part of the model/thinking propagation scope.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/341.4-add-regression-tests-for-native-mana-run-model-and.md:211:Audit finding: `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` recently changed around `NativeRunParams` API drift. `runtime_info_for_run` now uses `ctx.config.model`, and `run_direct_unit` uses `unit.model.or(ctx.config.model)` plus `ctx.config.thinking`. There is no focused regression proving native mana run uses the expected model/thinking precedence after the removal of `run_model`/`run_thinking`. Add targeted tests around pure/helper seams or extract a small helper if needed. Expected behavior to lock down: unit-level model overrides config model for a direct worker run; config model is used when unit has no model; config thinking is passed to `WorkerRunOptions`; run-state/runtime info reflects configured model consistently. Avoid live provider calls.
.mana/45.10.5-update-docs-for-mana-platform-substrate-and-imp-pr.md:3:title: Update docs for mana-platform substrate and imp product-loop direction
.mana/45.10.5-update-docs-for-mana-platform-substrate-and-imp-pr.md:4:slug: update-docs-for-mana-platform-substrate-and-imp-pr
.mana/45.10.5-update-docs-for-mana-platform-substrate-and-imp-pr.md:16:  - docs/architecture/mana-platform-target-architecture.md
.mana/45.10.5-update-docs-for-mana-platform-substrate-and-imp-pr.md:22:  1. Clarified that mana owns the lower platform/runtime substrate concerns: durable graph/task state, task/packet APIs, approvals/policy contracts, evidence/closure contracts, and isolation/worktrees.
.mana/45.10.5-update-docs-for-mana-platform-substrate-and-imp-pr.md:23:  2. Clarified that imp remains the flagship Rust-native agent product and agent runtime experience above mana, rather than being treated as the whole platform or as a thin client.
.mana/45.10.5-update-docs-for-mana-platform-substrate-and-imp-pr.md:42:- mana
.mana/45.10.5-update-docs-for-mana-platform-substrate-and-imp-pr.md:45:verify: cd /Users/asher/imp && test -f README.md && test -f VISION.md && test -f docs/architecture/mana-platform-target-architecture.md && test -f imp_ontology.md
.mana/45.10.5-update-docs-for-mana-platform-substrate-and-imp-pr.md:61:- The updated direction is: mana owns the platform/runtime substrate; imp remains the flagship Rust-native agent product and agent runtime experience; imp should be described with an explicit `/ask /plan /work /improve` workflow and product-first framing.
.mana/45.10.5-update-docs-for-mana-platform-substrate-and-imp-pr.md:66:2. Mana owns the lower substrate/runtime concerns: graph, task/packet APIs, approvals/policy, isolation/worktrees, durable evidence/contracts, and broad external integrability.
.mana/45.10.5-update-docs-for-mana-platform-substrate-and-imp-pr.md:68:4. Mana should be broadly integrable by external agents/products; imp does not need an exclusive technical moat to justify itself.
.mana/45.10.5-update-docs-for-mana-platform-substrate-and-imp-pr.md:74:- docs/architecture/mana-platform-target-architecture.md
.mana/45.10.5-update-docs-for-mana-platform-substrate-and-imp-pr.md:78:- any nearby docs that currently imply imp should be absorbed into mana runtime/platform ownership

.mana/290.4-split-native-mana-tool-implementation-into-focused.md:4:slug: split-native-mana-tool-implementation-into-focused
.mana/290.4-split-native-mana-tool-implementation-into-focused.md:9:acceptance: crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs is materially smaller and separates schema/action dispatch from native run orchestration or run-state persistence, with no intentional behavior changes.

.mana/290.4-split-native-mana-tool-implementation-into-focused.md:16:- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana/* or equivalent modules
.mana/290.4-split-native-mana-tool-implementation-into-focused.md:20:Decompose crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs without behavior changes. Current file is ~3.5k lines and mixes mana directory resolution, action schema, action dispatch, native run orchestration, run-state persistence, background task handling, output formatting, next scoring/tree rendering, and tests. Plan: extract tools/mana/ modules such as schema.rs, actions.rs, run.rs, run_store.rs, render.rs, policy.rs. Keep the public ManaTool registration path stable from tools/mod.rs. Start with run-store/render helpers because they are relatively isolated.
.mana/archive/2026/04/297.1-make-readme-publish-ready-with-coding-agent-first.md:9:acceptance: 'README.md is publication-ready for crates.io/GitHub: positions imp primarily as a coding agent, explains mana in plain terms as included durable task coordination, highlights features and active development, and keeps current limitations accurate.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/297.1-make-readme-publish-ready-with-coding-agent-first.md:15:close_reason: Rewrote README to be coding-agent-first for publishing readiness. It now introduces imp as a local coding agent with terminal UI, native tools, durable sessions, providers, secrets, modes, extensions, and included mana coordination. Mana appears after core features/tools and is explained plainly for readers who do not know it. Added active-development positioning and preserved limitations around Lua stable, TypeScript limited, SDK preview, and imp run as direct mana task execution. Verify script passed.

.mana/archive/2026/05/329-audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu.md:3:title: Audit and harden native mana run tool UX and execution behavior
.mana/archive/2026/05/329-audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu.md:4:slug: audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu
.mana/archive/2026/05/329-audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu.md:9:acceptance: Root cause identified and fixed or explicitly documented; mana run provides actionable real-time feedback in native tool output/summary and inspector sidebar; targeted verification passes.
.mana/archive/2026/05/329-audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu.md:13:  Progress: inspected native mana tool and TUI sidebar/chat rendering. Found likely UX bug: background run result details lacked action=run/status/timeout fields, so inspector formatting could not identify it as a mana run; background execution also only wrote run-store events and updated the widget at start/end, so long runs looked queued/stuck. Implemented live run widget helper, periodic background status/widget refresh from run_store, foreground throttled status/widget updates from stream events, and richer background run ToolOutput details. Verified with `cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-default-features` and `cargo test -p imp-tui mana --no-default-features`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/329-audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu.md:15:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/05/329-audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu.md:20:close_reason: 'Implemented and verified. Root cause/UX gap: background mana runs returned a result without action=run metadata and only updated UI at start/end, so they appeared queued/stuck despite run-store events being captured. Fix adds rich background ToolOutput metadata, a shared live run widget summary, periodic background status/sidebar refresh from run_store, and throttled foreground UI updates from stream events. Verify: cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-default-features; cargo test -p imp-tui mana --no-default-features.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/329-audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu.md:21:verify: cargo test -p imp-core mana && cargo test -p imp-tui mana
.mana/archive/2026/05/329-audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu.md:36:Goal: make the native imp mana tool's `run` action a leading feature by diagnosing why it can queue an agent without visible progress, reducing/avoiding instance slowdown, and adding real-time feedback in three places: tool streaming/status output, tool summary line, and inspector sidebar.
.mana/archive/2026/05/329-audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu.md:38:Current reported issue: user observed `mana run` queued an agent and did not do useful work, while slowing down the instance. Need audit before changes.
.mana/archive/2026/05/329-audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu.md:41:1. Inspect current native mana tool implementation and TUI surfaces.
.mana/archive/2026/05/329-audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu.md:44:4. Add clearer real-time feedback in the mana tool result/summary and inspector sidebar.
.mana/archive/2026/05/329-audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu.md:45:5. Verify with targeted Rust tests/checks and a local mana run/state smoke test if safe.
.mana/archive/2026/05/329-audit-and-harden-native-mana-run-tool-ux-and-execu.md:47:Scope boundaries: focus on imp's native mana tool and imp UI feedback surfaces. Avoid broad mana platform rewrites unless a small boundary fix is required.

.mana/archive/2026/05/280.2.2-implement-read-oriented-symbol-extraction-and-skel.md:26:  2026-05-21 continuation: user referred to mana 611.3, but this repo has no 611/611.3 unit. Located the relevant active scan-scope unit as 280.2.2 under parent 280.2. The prior verify failure was diagnostic: `cargo test -p imp-core scan symbol skeleton -- --nocapture` is invalid Cargo syntax because Cargo accepts a single test name filter, not multiple positional filters. Preflighted current implementation before edits: `crates/imp-core/src/tools/scan/mod.rs` already includes expanded actions (`directory`, `files`, `extract`, `search`, `tests`, `related`, `references`, `impact`), compact skeleton output, symbol/range/line extraction, structured block details, and Rust/TypeScript skeleton tests. Narrow check passed: `cargo test -p imp-core scan -- --nocapture` (49 passed, existing unrelated import.rs warnings). Next useful step: update the stale verify gate to a valid command and then run `cargo check -p imp-core`; inspect whether any remaining acceptance gap exists beyond verify drift.
.mana/312-assess-article-relevance-to-imp-improvements.md:9:acceptance: After the article is provided, the user receives a concise assessment with thesis, useful ideas, imp relevance, concrete improvement options, and defer/ignore items. Any durable follow-up work is represented in mana.
.mana/312-assess-article-relevance-to-imp-improvements.md:13:  Article provided: https://openai.com/index/speeding-up-agentic-workflows-with-websockets/. Initial read shows thesis: OpenAI added Responses API WebSocket mode for long-running, tool-call-heavy workflows to reduce per-turn HTTP/TLS/request overhead; clients keep a persistent /v1/responses connection and continue turns by sending only new input items plus previous_response_id. Relevant to imp because imp has repeated model-tool round trips in agentic coding, mana orchestration, and long sessions. Potential follow-ups: provider transport abstraction for persistent sessions; latency instrumentation around model/tool loops; capability detection/fallback for providers; maybe OpenAI-specific WebSocket transport behind existing provider boundary. Do not assume general provider support.
.mana/312-assess-article-relevance-to-imp-improvements.md:25:3. Map the useful ideas to imp's current focus areas: agent quality, runtime boundaries, context assembly, policy enforcement, tool behavior, embedding/hostability, extension seams, and structured outcomes back into mana.
.mana/312-assess-article-relevance-to-imp-improvements.md:34:- ../docs/architecture/mana-platform-target-architecture.md (read — cross-project architecture context)
.mana/312-assess-article-relevance-to-imp-improvements.md:41:- Root/project architecture implications if the article affects mana/imp split or platform boundaries.

.mana/archive/2026/05/337-continue-native-mana-run-queue-stall-audit-for-job.md:3:title: Continue native mana run queue stall audit for jobs=4 case
.mana/archive/2026/05/337-continue-native-mana-run-queue-stall-audit-for-job.md:4:slug: continue-native-mana-run-queue-stall-audit-for-job
.mana/archive/2026/05/337-continue-native-mana-run-queue-stall-audit-for-job.md:13:  User clarified the observed stalled run showed jobs=4. Prior jobs=0 clamp is not sufficient. New leading hypothesis: mana-cli computes effective max_jobs as params.jobs.min(config.max_concurrent); if project config max_concurrent is 0, run_state args still show jobs=4 while ready_queue sees max_jobs=0 and never dispatches. Will inspect config and code path before changing.
.mana/archive/2026/05/337-continue-native-mana-run-queue-stall-audit-for-job.md:17:  Inspected mana-cli dispatch. Found stronger jobs=4-compatible root cause candidate: mana-cli run uses process-global static SHUTDOWN_REQUESTED. run_with_stream_capture_and_sink installs SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers but never resets SHUTDOWN_REQUESTED at the start of an embedded/native run. In a long-lived imp TUI process, once any SIGINT/SIGTERM sets this flag, later native mana runs in the same process can plan/emit RunStart with jobs=4, then ready_queue sees shutdown_requested() and refuses to spawn workers. That leaves run_state units initialized as queued even though no workers dispatch. This also intersects the keyboard issue because embedded mana run mutates process signal handlers inside the active TUI process.
.mana/archive/2026/05/337-continue-native-mana-run-queue-stall-audit-for-job.md:21:  Implemented upstream mana-cli fix for stronger jobs=4 root cause: reset global SHUTDOWN_REQUESTED at start of cmd_run and embedded run_with_stream_capture_and_sink. Verified mana run tests and imp checks. Note: /Users/asher/mana was already dirty with multiple pre-existing changes; this audit touched ../mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/mod.rs only. /Users/asher/imp still has prior tools/mana.rs modifications from unit 336.
.mana/archive/2026/05/337-continue-native-mana-run-queue-stall-audit-for-job.md:23:close_reason: 'Found jobs=4-compatible root cause and implemented fix in ../mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/mod.rs: process-global SHUTDOWN_REQUESTED could remain true in a long-lived embedded imp/TUI host after prior SIGINT/SIGTERM, so later native runs could plan and emit queued units with jobs=4 but refuse to spawn workers. Added reset_shutdown_requested() and call it at cmd_run/run_with_stream_capture_and_sink start. Verified with cargo test -p mana-cli commands::run --quiet from /Users/asher/mana, and cargo test -p imp-core tools::mana::tests:: --quiet && cargo check -p imp-core -p imp-tui from /Users/asher/imp.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/337-continue-native-mana-run-queue-stall-audit-for-job.md:30:- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs
.mana/archive/2026/05/337-continue-native-mana-run-queue-stall-audit-for-job.md:31:- '../mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/mod.rs'
.mana/archive/2026/05/337-continue-native-mana-run-queue-stall-audit-for-job.md:32:- '../mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/ready_queue.rs'
.mana/archive/2026/05/337-continue-native-mana-run-queue-stall-audit-for-job.md:37:  notes: 'Found jobs=4-compatible root cause and implemented fix in ../mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/mod.rs: process-global SHUTDOWN_REQUESTED could remain true in a long-lived embedded imp/TUI host after prior SIGINT/SIGTERM, so later native runs could plan and emit queued units with jobs=4 but refuse to spawn workers. Added reset_shutdown_requested() and call it at cmd_run/run_with_stream_capture_and_sink start. Verified with cargo test -p mana-cli commands::run --quiet from /Users/asher/mana, and cargo test -p imp-core tools::mana::tests:: --quiet && cargo check -p imp-core -p imp-tui from /Users/asher/imp.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/337-continue-native-mana-run-queue-stall-audit-for-job.md:55:Follow-up to archived/closed unit 336. User clarified the observed native mana run stall had jobs=4, so the prior jobs=0 clamp does not explain the bug. Continue audit focused on why run_state/dispatch can show agents queued and not dispatched when jobs is normal. Inspect imp wrapper and mana ready_queue/direct dispatch behavior for: global stream sink cross-run races, background spawn_blocking/runtime interactions, direct worker command availability/path/env, memory reserve/file locking/path conflicts causing no UnitStart, and run_state reporting stale queued rows after dispatch actually failed. Preserve prior harmless fixes unless evidence says to revert. Verify with targeted tests/checks and record exact root cause or blocker.

.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:3:title: Recenter work orchestration on mana and slim imp into a production agent runtime
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:4:slug: recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:11:  - A mana-first target architecture is documented and accepted.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:12:  - imp runtime ownership is narrowed and explicit, with task-management responsibilities removed or marked for migration.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:13:  - mana has a concrete harness contract for agents, runs, context packs, claims, verification, logs, outcomes, and GUI consumption.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:14:  - The GUI product shape is specified as a mana harness, including board/chat/activity/log workflows.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:23:  User pivot accepted: stop treating imp-work as the long-term task manager and recenter durable work orchestration on mana. imp should become a focused production agent runtime that executes work inside mana rather than owning work management itself.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:26:  1. 365.1 — inventory imp-work concepts and map each to mana ownership. This must run first because it determines what should be ported, preserved, or deprecated.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:27:  2. 365.2 — define the mana harness target architecture and agent-neutral contract. Depends on 365.1 so it can absorb the best imp-work semantics deliberately.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:28:  3. 365.3 — define the slim imp runtime boundary after the mana pivot. Depends on 365.1 and 365.2 so imp can be narrowed against the new mana contract rather than guessed in isolation.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:29:  4. 365.4 — specify the production mana GUI harness product shape. Depends on 365.2 because the GUI should read/write mana harness state, not imp-work internals.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:31:  Important missing/implicit dimension now made explicit in the epic description and child tasks: non-imp agent compatibility. The mana harness must be agent-neutral enough for imp, pi, codex, opencode, etc. imp should be the reference/best runtime, not the only supported worker.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:34:  - Accidentally rebuilding imp-work inside mana without pruning weak ideas.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:35:  - Letting GUI product design drive private imp-only APIs instead of a mana harness API.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:36:  - Slimming imp too early before the mana harness contract is specified.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:48:  - 365.2 mana harness target architecture and agent contract.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:49:  - 365.3 slim imp runtime boundary after the mana pivot.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:50:  - 365.4 production mana GUI harness product shape.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:51:  - 365.5 migration/deprecation path from imp-work back to mana.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:52:  - 365.6 non-imp agent adapter contract so mana can host pi/codex/opencode/etc., not only imp.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:57:  - 365.2 depends on 365.1: mana harness absorbs chosen imp-work semantics.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:58:  - 365.3 depends on 365.1 + 365.2: imp runtime can be slimmed only once mana ownership is clear.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:59:  - 365.4 depends on 365.2: GUI product shape should sit on mana harness state.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:60:  - 365.5 depends on 365.1 + 365.2: migration must know both source inventory and target mana contract.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:62:  - 365.7 depends on 365.2 + 365.4: GUI API follows both target mana architecture and product flow.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:64:  This addresses the earlier question of what was missing: explicit migration/deprecation planning, non-imp agent adapter contract, and GUI-facing mana API/event surface.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:68:  Planning correction: begin with 365.3, the independent imp runtime-boundary definition. The earlier graph over-weighted migration back from imp-work to mana. The pivot has two related but distinct axes: (1) cut/slim imp into a more agnostic, flexible, hostable runtime; (2) make mana/harness larger and daemon-backed. 365.3 should lead because it defines what imp is regardless of the eventual harness migration path. Migration work (365.1/365.5) remains important but should not dominate the runtime-boundary design.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:72:  External framing source from user: Inngest blog post `Background agents are here. Your orchestration isn't ready.` Key concepts that map well to imp/mana split: do not couple infrastructure to a single agent pattern; build a durable harness/orchestration layer around changing agent logic; stable primitives are durable steps, persistent external state, parallel work coordination, event-driven control flow, and structured execution observability/traces. Mapping: mana/harness should own durable orchestration primitives and long-running/background-agent reliability; imp should own the agent/runtime logic and remain competitive as an opencode/pi/goose-class runtime. This strengthens the product split: imp = agent logic/runtime, mana = durable orchestration harness. It also supports the user's desire for mana daemon/harness and imp as independent agent product with work graph separate.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:76:  Verification checkpoint after updating epic verify: parent `mana verify 365` now fails as expected because most child deliverable docs are not authored yet: docs/design/imp-work-to-mana-inventory.md, docs/architecture/mana-agent-harness-target.md, docs/product/mana-gui-harness.md, docs/design/imp-work-to-mana-migration.md, docs/architecture/mana-agent-adapter-contract.md, docs/architecture/mana-gui-api-surface.md, docs/product/mana-harness-product-identity.md. The imp runtime boundary doc exists and contains the required system-of-record boundary. Epic remains open; no close attempted. Next useful action is to author the child deliverables, likely starting with 365.8 daemon/product identity or 365.2 harness architecture.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:80:  Direction confirmed: use the Inngest framing as a north star, update mana planning first, then begin streamlining/removing imp code. Product split is now explicit: imp should compete directly with opencode/pi/goose as a standalone agent runtime/operator product; mana owns the durable work graph/orchestration/harness. Next implementation should start in clean worktree `/Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary` on branch `runtime-boundary`, with small reversible cuts that move workflow/work-graph behavior out of imp core without weakening standalone CLI/TUI agent UX.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:84:  Implementation checkpoint after user requested continuing: created and completed a second runtime streamlining slice (ID collision: task displayed as 365.9) in clean worktree `/Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary`. The slice moved direct workflow-controller turn hooks out of `agent/run_loop.rs` and behind integration methods in `agent/workflow_integration.rs`, then further replaced the public `Agent.workflow_controller` field with `workflow_layer: WorkflowRuntimeLayer`. This preserves behavior while making mana/workflow control a named runtime layer rather than part of the core Agent field surface. Verified with `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work`, `cargo check -p imp-core`, `mana verify 365.9`, and direct rg check confirming `run_loop.rs` no longer directly calls `workflow_controller.tick_turn`, `save_to_path`, `snapshot`, or `enforce_closeout_status`. Native `work next` in the implementation worktree reports 0 ready task(s).
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:88:  Strategic direction update from user: prioritize making imp a fantastic standalone agent harness/runtime first, then return to the work graph. Big-picture expectation is to remove imp-work eventually, possibly by moving it under an archive area such as `imp-archive`, but do not do that immediately as a mechanical move. Workflows may not be core to imp except where they provide useful extensibility; preserve good workflow/extensibility ideas while removing durable work-graph ownership from imp. This strengthens near-term focus: imp runtime/operator UX/extensibility first; mana/work graph second.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:92:  Post-change verification checkpoint: after strategic notes were appended, inspected tree/next/show for 365, 365.2, and 365.5. Parent 365 remains open because required deliverable docs are still missing (inventory, harness target architecture, GUI product/API, migration, adapter contract, product identity). `mana verify 365` fails as expected. Runtime branch checks still pass in `/Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary`: `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work`, `cargo check -p imp-core`, and native `work validate` reports 0 errors/0 warnings. No units closed from this note-only change.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:96:  Imp-side completion criteria proposed in response to user question: work on imp is done for this phase when imp is clearly a standalone opencode/pi/goose-class agent runtime/harness and no longer owns the durable work graph. Concrete gates: (1) clean commits/checkpointed branch, (2) imp-core agent loop has a small message-loop core with named runtime layers/capabilities, (3) workflow/mana/imp-work behavior is optional integration or removed from core Agent fields/run loop, (4) standalone CLI/TUI/chat/run UX still works and is improved/not weakened, (5) hostable execution contract exists for prompt/session/task/run input -> runtime events -> outcome, (6) cargo fmt/check/tests/smoke pass for affected crates, (7) no active dependency on imp-work for core runtime except explicit temporary compatibility, (8) follow-up mana/work graph tasks own durable orchestration/migration. Do not define done as deleting every workflow idea; preserve useful workflow/extensibility concepts that make imp better.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:100:  Verification checkpoint after user asked how to know imp-side work is done. Inspected `mana tree 365`, `mana show 365`, `mana next`, and `mana verify 365`. Parent epic remains open and `mana verify 365` fails as expected because the remaining design deliverables are not authored yet: imp-work inventory, mana harness target architecture, GUI product spec, migration plan, non-imp adapter contract, GUI API surface, product identity/daemon topology. Runtime implementation branch `/Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary` remains dirty but healthy: `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work` and `cargo check -p imp-core` pass; native `work next` reports 0 ready task(s), `work validate` reports 0 errors/0 warnings. No additional unit closed in this checkpoint.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:108:  Workflow product decision from user: workflows in imp should behave similarly to Goose recipes. They are something imp can do — reusable runtime recipes/extensions/patterns — but they are not central to the runtime and should not define core agent execution. Durable work graph/orchestration remains mana/harness territory. This changes the target: imp workflows should be optional recipes over the agent runtime, not a mandatory workflow controller embedded in the core loop.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:112:  Clarification from user: imp should be able to autonomously orchestrate its own bounded work. The boundary is not `imp has no orchestration`; it is `imp owns runtime orchestration inside a run, mana/harness owns durable work orchestration across runs`. Updated target: workflows/recipes in imp are optional runtime recipes that can guide bounded subagent spawning, verification, evidence, and merge behavior; they must not own durable work graph/task lifecycle unless a host provides that capability. Rule of thumb: if child work must survive the parent run, appear on a board, be assigned independently, or own lifecycle state, it belongs to mana; if it is a bounded helper inside current execution, it belongs to imp.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:116:  Git/version-control direction discussed: user is thinking about jj-vcs-like ideas, not replacing git. Potential mana direction: mana as a versioned work ledger with jj-inspired ergonomics and possibly a local git-server-backed substrate. Ideas to evaluate: operation log for work graph changes; first-class changesets for agent runs; cheap branching/undo/rebase of work plans; immutable run/evidence records linked to git commits; local git server/worktree orchestration for agent branches; Git compatibility for source while mana owns work ledger. Guardrail: do not turn mana into source VCS; use git/jj ideas to make durable work state reviewable, branchable, replayable, and recoverable.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:120:  Direction confirmed: continue focusing on imp until it is sufficiently reverted/slimmed/stabilized to ship as a new 0.2.x update via update-imp, then return to mana work. Near-term imp goal: make the runtime clean, standalone, recipe/subagent-ready, and releaseable without continuing to deepen imp-work/work-graph ownership. Current next steps: commit verified bounded-subagent doc + workflow integration split, then implement new bounded subagent runtime vocabulary/API slices.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:128:  Runbook direction from user: task/epic-specific runbooks should be tightly aligned with mana tasks. Avoid JavaScript/TypeScript for now because requiring a Node runtime is undesirable. Lua is promising because imp already has shipped Lua extension support, but the runbook model should be machine-readable/programmatic rather than freeform markdown. Target idea: an agent can author a mana unit plus an executable/structured runbook that contains steps and can programmatically control bounded imp subagents. Boundary: mana remains system of record for the task/runbook/step state; imp executes the runbook and subagents as runtime orchestration. Need design a safe, capability-limited runbook DSL/API so executable plans do not become arbitrary workflow engine code or durable graph ownership inside imp.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:132:  Runbook ownership correction from user: runbooks should be a mana feature, not an imp feature. imp may execute/run against mana runbooks as a reference/best runtime, but mana owns the runbook model, storage, step state, decisions, and durable execution ledger. Target: any capable agent (imp, codex, opencode, pi, goose, etc.) should be able to create and execute mana runbooks. A mana runbook is a programmable decision tree/execution plan for coding/engineering work aligned with mana tasks/epics. This means the language/runtime decision belongs to mana, not imp; the stable contract should be an agent-neutral runbook IR/API with optional authoring frontends. Guardrail: do not make runbooks an imp-only Lua feature. Lua may still be a possible frontend/executor for imp, but mana should define the canonical runbook representation and orchestration semantics.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:152:  Direction clarified by user: imp must be able to run independently from mana. Release/readiness and slimming work should remove or gate mana/imp-work assumptions so standalone imp CLI/TUI/runtime works without a mana daemon, mana graph, or imp-work store. Mana integration can remain optional/adapter-based, but imp must not require mana to execute normal agent runs.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:156:  Checkpoint commit on runtime-boundary: `6c078c9 Add standalone imp runtime boundaries`. Includes clippy cleanup, optional/gated imp-work integration for imp-core, mana/work tool registration gates, runtime hiding of mana tool unless `mana` executable is on PATH, and standalone prototype recording no-op without imp-work.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:168:  Checkpoint commit on runtime-boundary: `50fd6f1 Archive imp-work and gate mana CLI integration`. imp-work archived outside repo at `/Users/asher/imp-archive/imp-work`, old imp-core WorkTool archived at `/Users/asher/imp-archive/imp-core-tools-work.rs`, active workspace no longer contains imp-work, and imp-core mana-cli dependency is optional behind `mana-integration`.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:172:  Repo Prompt video synthesis accepted as direction. Source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3gxFQ-ynJU0 ("Smarter AI Agents with Repo Prompt - With Eric Provencher"). Key lesson: frontier coding-agent quality comes from dense task-specific context construction, oracle-style clean-context planning/review, stable plan artifacts, bounded worker/subagent threads with clean contexts, and strong observability/steering of delegated work. This reinforces the current epic boundary: imp should be a high-quality standalone bounded execution runtime, while mana should be the durable manager/harness layer.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:174:  Imp implications: invest in runtime context hygiene, context-pack consumption, repo/code maps, bounded subagent lifecycle/control, dense activity summaries, structured worker outcomes, review/verification execution, and standalone operation without mana or imp-work. Do not rebuild durable task/board/runbook state inside imp.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:176:  Mana implications: own persistent manager/board/task/runbook state, context pack artifacts/freshness, execution ledger, claims/leases, verification/evidence, GUI activity/event streams, adapter contracts, and agent-readiness reports. Mana should support a manager-agent style workflow, but the durable plan/task/runbook artifact remains mana-owned and agent-neutral.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:178:  Guardrail: do not recreate imp-work under a new name inside imp. Rule remains: if child work must survive the parent run, appear on a board, be assigned independently, own lifecycle state, or be visible in the durable GUI, it belongs to mana. If it is a bounded helper inside the current execution context, it belongs to imp.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:182:  Correction: do not make the external Repo Prompt video a normative citation or dependency for this epic. Treat the prior synthesis as conversation-derived planning input only. Durable architecture docs and task deliverables should state the generalized principles directly—dense context construction, clean planning/review contexts, bounded observable runtime workers, stable mana-owned plan/runbook artifacts, and mana-owned durable orchestration—without referencing the video by name or URL.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:186:  Checkpoint commit on runtime-boundary: `0377b4e Make mana deps optional in imp-core`. imp-core no-default dependency tree no longer includes mana crates; mana-core and mana-cli are optional via `mana-integration`. TUI still opts into mana integration.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:190:  Workflow-tool exploration direction from user: consider whether an independent `workflow` CLI could replace much of mana for agent execution by turning best mana ideas into planned, verifiable, actionable, self-executing workflows rather than a durable system of record. Candidate shape: standalone CLI first with actions like plan/execute/update/status/verify, then port/embed into imp as a native runtime tool. Each workflow is its own bounded orchestration layer; imp can self-execute and orchestrate it. Important distinction to evaluate: this would not be a board/task database, but a portable workflow artifact plus execution engine. It may supersede parts of the current mana-first durable ledger direction if it can provide enough recovery, evidence, verification, and handoff without becoming another system of record.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:194:  Workflow-tool planning refinement from user: keep the CLI surface minimal; take the best ideas from mana, but make workflows much more structured and programmatically executable. The key mana idea to preserve is that a unit can be handed to an agent and the system constructs the correct context for accurate work; across many units this becomes orchestratable. Workflow adds explicit programmatic planning/execution on top of that: high-detail workflows should encode context assembly, checks, review/verification, and guardrails that systematically alleviate common LLM-generated-code weaknesses. This argues for a small CLI over a rich workflow schema/engine, not a large command surface.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:198:  Planning-only workflow-tool checkpoint after graph inspection: inspected 365 tree/show/next plus active 365.11/365.11.1 after user requested no premature DONE. Parent 365 remains open; `mana verify 365` fails as expected because required architecture/product/migration docs are still missing. 365.11.1 is in_progress and unrelated to this workflow-tool planning turn; no close attempted. Workflow-tool direction should likely become a new design task rather than modifying existing acceptance in this planning-only turn.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:202:  Runtime-boundary cleanup checkpoint: corrected failed verification commands and resolved feature split issues. Root causes found: `cargo fmt --check --workspace` was invalid syntax for rustfmt wrapper; fixed with `cargo fmt --all --check`. Combined multiple cargo test filters in one command was invalid; reran each filter separately. `imp-core --features mana-api` failed because tests and agent mana-loop references still need the mana tool/CLI module, while the new feature split had removed it from `mana-api`; fixed by splitting features into `mana-api = [dep:mana-core]` and `mana-tool = [mana-api, dep:mana]`, gating `tools::mana` on `mana-tool`, and updating imp-cli/imp-tui `mana-ui` to enable `imp-core/mana-tool`.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:204:  Checks now passing in /Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary: `cargo fmt --all --check`; `cargo check -p imp-core`; `cargo check -p imp-core --features mana-api`; `cargo check -p imp-cli`; `cargo check -p imp-cli --features mana-ui`; `cargo check -p imp-tui --no-default-features`; `cargo check -p imp-tui --features mana-ui`; targeted imp-cli mana-ui tests for worker status, mana namespace parsing, and headless output mode; targeted imp-core mana-tool tests for mana_run_state and mana_tool. Default `cargo tree -p imp-cli | rg 'imp-work|mana-core|mana-cli'` returns no matches.
.mana/365-recenter-work-orchestration-on-mana-and-slim-imp-i.md:208:  Post-mana-update inspection per instruction: inspected `mana tree 365`, `mana show 365`, parent-scoped/auto `mana next`, and `mana verify 365`. Parent 365 remains open and verify still fails because the required architecture/product/migration deliverable docs are not authored. There is no child runtime-cleanup unit currently visible under 365; previously closed implementation units are no longer shown in open tree. This supersedes the old mana-first assumption for 0.3 planning: epic 365 should not continue blindly toward mana harness docs without first reconciling/superseding the epic direction around imp-native workflows.

.mana/266.3-design-adoption-path-extension-seams-and-product-s.md:20:Turn the review finding into a scoped design/implementation plan for imp extension and product-surface evolution. Compare imp hooks/Lua/runtime/TUI/SDK surfaces against Hermes plugin discovery, gateway/TUI split, cron scheduler, multi-environment execution backends, and ACP-style host integration. Recommend what imp should adopt now, later, or avoid. Keep the proposal aligned with imp as the Rust-native agent product on mana; avoid importing broad product sprawl without clear architectural fit.

Large session dirs or slow disks can stall input/redraw.

.mana/archive/2026/05/347-fix-tui-queued-message-visibility-and-delivery.md:13:  Implemented queue visibility/delivery changes in imp TUI: queued message preview now passed into EditorView, rendered on a dedicated bottom line inside the prompt/editor area with queued glyph and half-terminal-width truncation using existing unicode ellipsis. Streaming submit/follow-up now use queue_streaming_message; if a previous queued item exists it is surfaced as a visible user message, persisted to session, and sent to current agent as AgentCommand::Steer. AgentEnd drains remaining queued messages through send_message so they print into chat stream and spawn/send as next prompt rather than disappearing. Verified cargo check -p imp-tui passes; warnings are pre-existing dead-code warnings in mana-cli/imp-core.
.mana/327-audit-premature-stop-after-writing-next-step-to-ma.md:3:title: Audit premature stop after writing next step to mana

.mana/327-audit-premature-stop-after-writing-next-step-to-ma.md:23:Goal: Diagnose why imp sometimes says the next step, writes it to mana, says done, and stops instead of continuing execution.
