.mana/45.4-phase-3-introduce-runner-protocol-and-local-adapte.md:17:  User suspects the boundary language around `mana run` may be wrong. Preferred reframing: `imp run {mana_id}` makes clearer that the imp runtime reads mana state and performs the task, rather than mana being the primary runtime calling imp. Future runner/orchestration decisions should revisit command and ownership language from that lens.
.mana/45.4-phase-3-introduce-runner-protocol-and-local-adapte.md:21:  Decision from user on 2026-04-09: the architecture should actively move toward `imp run {mana_id}` as the primary execution model, with `mana run` treated as legacy/compatibility behavior. Phase 3 runner/orchestration work should now be framed around imp as the orchestrator/runtime reading mana state, not mana as the primary runtime dispatch command.
.mana/45.4-phase-3-introduce-runner-protocol-and-local-adapte.md:25:  Execution-boundary answer snapshot: imp is the orchestrator/runtime. Promote `imp run {mana_id}` as the primary execution model. Treat current mana spawning/dispatch (`mana-cli/src/spawner.rs`) and `mana run`-centered language as transitional compatibility behavior to be phased out.
.mana/45.4-phase-3-introduce-runner-protocol-and-local-adapte.md:29:  Visible decomposition delta from `docs/rebuild/imp-run-primary-execution-path.md`: the minimum sequence for imp-run primacy is now externalized across existing root units. 45.4.3 carries the `mana run` narrowing / `spawner.rs` compatibility track; 45.3.4 carries the task-packet shift needed to demote `prompt.rs` from prompt-authoring center; 45.6.4 carries convergence of worker result semantics across inline and deferred verify; 45.5.2 carries cleanup/quarantine of stale duplicate imp-side mana loading paths; 45.9 carries the cross-track readiness checklist tying these slices together.
.mana/45.4-phase-3-introduce-runner-protocol-and-local-adapte.md:33:  Runner protocol surface now concretized by docs/rebuild/runner-protocol-surface.md. Visible first-slice decomposition: shared Tower-level request/result groups for worktree, sandbox, command execution, capture, and cancellation; explicit binding to durable execution/worktree/sandbox lease IDs; first implementation remains an imp-local adapter rather than a root runner subtree. Follow-on unit 45.4.2 should consume this spec by mapping shared request/result groups into imp-local adapter responsibilities across agent.rs, mana_worker.rs, and tools/mana.rs. Follow-on unit 45.4.3 should use this spec to narrow spawner.rs into compatibility translation/delegation around the same typed runner boundary instead of shell-template canonical ownership.
.mana/45.4-phase-3-introduce-runner-protocol-and-local-adapte.md:37:  2026-04-13 decomposition externalized from the current conversation: created child `45.4.4 Plan the cutover from current imp run plus mana run coexistence to a lease-based imp attach path`. This child should turn the newly recorded migration stages into a repo-grounded cutover sequence: canonical mana run/lease/event semantics first, then dual-write, then shadow scheduling/validation, then lease-based imp attach/heartbeat/checkpoint/resolve, then transcript ownership fully in imp, then `mana run` deprecation/narrowing. It must stay audit-plus-sequencing oriented and preserve `imp run {mana_id}` as the preferred live boundary.
.mana/45.4-phase-3-introduce-runner-protocol-and-local-adapte.md:52:- 'Architectural direction settled from the repo-grounded execution-path note: treat `imp run <unit-id>` as the preferred primary execution framing for single-unit work. Treat `mana run` as legacy/compatibility orchestration behavior during migration, not as the intended long-term execution center.'
.mana/45.4-phase-3-introduce-runner-protocol-and-local-adapte.md:56:Goal: move orchestration toward a typed runner boundary for worktrees, sandboxing, command execution, and artifact capture. This phase depends on the initial contracts spine and mana durable lease/artifact planning.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:3:title: Evaluate a drastic imp vNext centered on native mana substrate, portable host runtime, and post-Lua extensibility
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:10:  Conversation prompt from user: curious about a drastic fork/rework of imp making mana a more native task+memory tool, with deeper extensibility beyond Lua and a more flexible portable runtime. Need answer oriented around architecture tradeoffs and likely path, not implementation yet.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:14:  Expanded discussion scope beyond Tower-local rebuild. User wants future-facing architecture spanning imp, wizard, familiar, and a separate early-stage /Users/asher/agents product. Strong signals from user: (1) imp should become a library/API and more portable host runtime, (2) TypeScript extensions are likely the strongest long-term extensibility surface, (3) imp may need more ownership of mana-like task/memory behavior than the current strict Tower split implies, while still preserving what makes mana durable and strong, (4) user wants de-abstracted product/runtime language like task, memory, approvals, shell, capabilities rather than only Tower-specific terminology, (5) evaluate imp as a reusable runtime substrate for products beyond Tower, not only the Tower worker for mana units. Comparative context requested: pi-mono and hermes-agent as current OSS references.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:18:  New user signals: (1) leaning toward option B — generalized state engine behind imp's public task/memory/evidence/approval abstractions rather than imp-over-mana as the explicit long-term public model; (2) open to dropping mana's strict file-native requirement because current project-folder placement can cause cross-project state to land in the wrong local tree and later feel lost; (3) if TypeScript extensions land, user likely wants to retire Lua rather than maintain dual extension ecosystems; (4) user is pressure-testing implementation language and asks whether Rust remains correct for imp, with Zig as a possible alternative. This should inform future-facing architecture across imp, wizard, familiar, and /agents, especially alignment with the broader object/assertion/event/rule vision in /Users/asher/agents.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:22:  Created docs/architecture/mana-platform-target-architecture.md as the first compact target-architecture note using the new vocabulary. The note frames mana as the platform, imp as the agent and default human-facing environment, runtime as the live execution layer, graph as the durable layer, extension as the packaged extensibility term, and wizard/familiar as apps built on mana. It also explicitly states that the refactor should reveal blurred runtime-vs-graph boundaries, file-local assumption leaks, prompt-shaped policy gaps, and overprivileged extension surfaces.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:26:  User is specifically interested in how mana would be reengineered into the new work-memory graph. Likely next architecture question: map the migration from current unit/fact/verify-centric durable substrate toward a mana platform whose durable layer is a graph of objects, assertions, events, rules, tasks, evidence, approvals, and workflows, with compatibility bridges for current file-native unit flows during transition.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:30:  Subagent workflow requirement clarified in discussion: preserve and improve the current mana-driven handoff where a parent imp agent points a subagent at a bounded job and the worker receives an exact scoped context window with no need to make decisions outside task scope. Emerging future shape: parent/planner owns decomposition, graph slicing, and decision-making; worker/subagent receives a scoped task packet over the graph containing task contract, task-local graph slice, relevant assertions/events/evidence/artifacts only, allowed actions, approval state, verify/close contract, and explicit non-goals.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:34:  Resuming architecture exploration after lost conversation. Current discussion anchor set is docs/architecture/mana-platform-target-architecture.md plus units 45.10 and 257. Likely next exploration areas: (1) what gets extracted from current imp into mana runtime vs stays imp-specific, (2) how current unit/fact/verify mana evolves into broader graph primitives without losing strong task handoff and verify semantics, (3) how scoped subagent task packets should be represented over the graph/runtime boundary, (4) extension/adapter boundary and TypeScript-first model with host-enforced policy, and (5) migration path: in-place extraction vs shadow vNext inside repo.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:39:  1. Preserve and carry forward the strongest current mana properties: verify gates, bounded units/tasks, dependency graphs, explicit closure semantics, and the features that make mana especially attractive for agents.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:40:  2. `task` is explicitly first-class and should remain analogous to current mana units, not be demoted to a mere projection.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:41:  3. User is questioning language/runtime ownership: if the mana platform is implemented in Rust and exposed cleanly via API, imp may not need to remain Rust unless there is a host-authority or embedding reason.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:43:  5. Concrete target flow to preserve/improve: a parent agent calls a subagent, assembles a scoped task packet from mana, and imp renders the final worker prompt from that packet. This implies a strong boundary between durable task packet assembly and agent-specific prompt rendering.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:47:  Comparative architecture pressure from external project `badlogic/pi-mono` is now part of the evaluation. Observed from public docs: pi-mono already offers a TypeScript-first agent runtime/SDK (`pi-agent-core`) with tool execution, event streaming, session handling, interactive/JSON/RPC/SDK modes, and strong extension packaging in the coding agent. It explicitly de-scopes built-in subagents, plan mode, permission popups, and durable task/work substrate features, pushing those to extensions. This sharpens the build-vs-adopt question: Tower should justify mana+imp work only where it offers differentiated value beyond a flexible coding harness—especially durable first-class tasks, verify gates, dependency graphs, approvals, scoped cold-start handoff/task packets, host-authoritative policy, and graph-backed multi-agent coordination.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:51:  Strategic narrowing pressure from user: if the durable graph / durable task substrate is the true differentiated feature, then the deep mana-platform refactor may be too ambitious right now and Tower may instead continue improving mana closer to its current shape. This reframes the main product question as: why continue investing heavily in imp if projects like pi already provide a strong extensible coding-agent runtime? Need evaluate whether imp has enough differentiated value beyond pi, whether imp should become much thinner / more mana-specific, or whether Tower should borrow/adopt more from pi-like runtimes while focusing innovation on mana's durable task/verify/dependency/handoff substrate.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:55:  User pushed back against over-narrowing imp to a mana-only worker. New requirement: imp should remain ambitious and still be a very strong coding agent even without mana. User's strategic question is now: what can imp do that could not simply be added to pi via extension + mana integration? User notes imp may currently compete more with hermes-agent than pi, but pi is a respected reference point and may align with the broader /agents product vision in some ways. This means evaluation must distinguish: (a) what is merely possible to bolt onto an extensible coding harness like pi, versus (b) what deserves to be first-class in imp because the product philosophy, runtime authority, and integrated experience would be materially different. Need next compare imp-vs-pi on first-class vs extension-shaped features, especially subagents, task packets, host-enforced policy, isolated execution, verify-aware closure, and context assembly.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:59:  User clarified exploration posture: statements are exploratory, not hard commitments. Important preference update: user is comfortable with imp existing primarily as a product, even if a pi agent could in principle author a mana integration/extension that uses mana comparably well. User explicitly values imp's product qualities today: prefers imp's UI over pi's, likes imp's stronger default tool set, and sees Rust implementation as a positive attribute. This suggests one valid strategic justification for imp is product taste/integration/default experience rather than exclusive technical capability moat.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:67:  - platform-side extensibility should remain first-class so mana can dogfood itself, not only expose APIs to external apps
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:68:  - imp, not mana, should render final worker prompts from structured task inputs
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:71:  Open uncertainty to explore next: whether prompt-construction logic belongs partly in mana/runtime or entirely in imp, especially if mana is assembling scoped task slices and imp is rendering the final worker prompt.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:76:  - mana should hand imp pure structured data only; no canonical brief or prompt fragments from the platform
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:77:  - prompt rendering belongs in a split model: mana/runtime may provide safe context-shaping primitives, but imp owns final prompt construction
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:78:  - mana should remain role-agnostic and not encode planner/worker/reviewer prompt semantics
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:89:  Latest planning question from user: with imp product direction now clarified, the likely next strategic focus may shift back to mana implementation strategy—specifically engineering the path from the current file-native / markdown-heavy substrate toward a graph-oriented durable model and possibly away from `.md` as the primary storage/materialization format. This is exploratory, not yet a decision. Need evaluate what should change in storage/modeling first versus what should remain file-native/materialized for human inspectability and git-friendliness.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:93:  User is open to mana's future canonical store being SQLite or another embedded structured store rather than remaining strictly file-native. Important preference: they like the idea of a human/file view layered on top of the canonical store. This shifts the storage discussion toward comparing embedded-store, typed-file, and hybrid architectures rather than preserving markdown as primary truth.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:97:  Compared freshly cloned ~/pi-mono against current imp repo for a broad product/runtime gap analysis. High-confidence gap buckets now visible: (1) extensibility/runtime embedding — pi ships mature TypeScript extension API, SDK embedding surface, RPC/extension UI, package distribution; imp currently ships Lua-first extensions plus CLI/TUI/RPC surfaces but lacks comparable TS extension/SDK breadth. (2) provider/auth surface — pi-ai exposes a much broader provider matrix and OAuth set (Copilot, Gemini CLI, Antigravity, Vercel AI Gateway, broader OpenAI-compatible customization) than imp’s current built-ins. (3) customization/community packaging — pi has first-class themes, prompt templates, skills, TS extensions, pi packages, /reload; imp has skills/prompts/Lua tooling/import-from-pi but weaker packaged customization story. (4) UI/product philosophy — imp is stronger on opinionated workflow, native mana integration, modes, worker execution contract, guardrails; pi is stronger on hostability/adaptability and extension-defined UI. (5) architecture/runtime seams — pi is split into ai/agent/coding-agent/tui/web-ui/pods/mom packages; imp remains more concentrated in imp-core despite active rebuild plans to isolate worker/runtime/context/tools/policy seams. Likely follow-on work areas: TS extension seam for imp, stronger SDK/embedding contract, broader provider/OAuth coverage, packaged customization/distribution model, and clearer shared runtime/UI seam for non-TUI hosts.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:101:  Proposed imp catch-up roadmap versus pi, preserving imp’s mana-native workflow thesis rather than copying pi wholesale. Recommended phases: Phase 1 foundations = define shared runtime/UI seam across CLI/TUI/view/embed, plus public embedding/SDK contract and post-Lua TypeScript extension contract. Phase 2 developer platform = ship TS extension runtime with tools/commands/events/UI hooks, reload loop, packaged extension distribution, and prompt/theme/package loading model. Phase 3 provider parity = broaden provider/auth/custom-provider support (Azure, Bedrock, wider OpenAI-compatible surface, strategic OAuth additions) behind a cleaner provider abstraction. Phase 4 host surfaces = stabilize machine-readable event/output contract, embeddable host runtime, richer non-TUI surface, and extension-defined UI primitives. Phase 5 ecosystem hardening = docs/examples/package publishing/import/migration from pi, plus policy/capability boundaries for extensions. Guiding rule: keep imp’s strong native tools, modes, mana worker contract, and /ask-/plan-/work-/improve workflow as the product center while borrowing pi’s hostability and TS customization strengths.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:105:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:113:verify: cd /Users/asher/imp && mana show 264 >/dev/null
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:124:- When evaluating imp vNext, treat imp as a reusable runtime substrate that could power Tower, wizard, familiar, and the early /agents product via clearer task, memory, capability, shell, and approval abstractions rather than only as Tower's mana-attached worker.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:125:- 'Future-facing planning should target option B: imp exposes generalized runtime concepts such as task, memory, evidence, approval, capability, and run, while mana evolves toward or is replaced by a more general durable state engine lineage behind those concepts rather than remaining the explicit long-term public model.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:126:- 'Use the following naming set for the future-facing platform/agent architecture unless later revised: `mana` = platform, `imp` = agent + default human-facing environment, `runtime` = live execution layer, `graph` = durable layer, `extension` = packaged extensibility, `action` = system-doable behavior / concrete request, and `task` = executable work.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:127:- Future planning should not require imp to justify itself via an exclusive technical moat over other agent products. Mana should be broadly integrable by outside agents and shells, including a possible pi-side mana extension. Imp may still be worth building as an ambitious flagship product if it offers a better integrated UI, stronger built-in tools/defaults, Rust-native runtime qualities, and a more opinionated product experience aligned with Tower and /agents.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:128:- Evaluate imp and mana against external agent products using a first-class flagship-product framing, not a monopoly framing. The key question is not whether pi or similar tools can technically integrate with mana, but what imp should uniquely optimize as a product when mana is treated as an open/API-rich substrate accessible to multiple agents.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:129:- 'Current direction: do not pursue a deep refactor that makes imp stop being a strong standalone product. Keep mana as the owner of the platform/runtime substrate (graph, task/packet APIs, approvals/policy, isolation/worktrees, durable evidence/contracts) while imp remains the flagship Rust-native agent product and agent runtime experience above it. Imp should be documented as an opinionated coding agent with an explicit `/ask /plan /work /improve` workflow, strong default tools, and operator-facing UX rather than as the whole platform.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:133:Current state: Tower already frames imp as the native worker/runtime designed to use mana well, with active rebuild work around clearer worker/runtime seams, contracts, and runner boundaries. The open question is what a more drastic vNext would look like if mana became a first-class native task/memory substrate inside imp, extensibility moved beyond Lua, and the runtime became more portable/hostable.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:137:2. What it means for mana to be a native task+memory tool rather than an adjacent integration.

Remaining gap is likely wiki/index integration and relevance/quality, not basic facts injection.

Created follow-up implementation task 317.7 for an opt-in OpenAI WebSocket/persistent transport prototype behind the capability/recovery gate. Verification via native mana inspection: mana show 317, mana show 317.3, mana tree 317.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.6-design-persistent-transport-safety-gate.md:23:verify: mana show 317 && mana show 317.3 && mana tree 317

.mana/archive/2026/05/317.6-design-persistent-transport-safety-gate.md:43:      "description": "Goal: Make imp faster and more resilient for long-running, tool-heavy agent workflows by measuring loop latency first, then introducing provider transport capabilities and persistent-session support where justified.\n\nContext: User reviewed OpenAI's article on speeding up agentic workflows with Responses API WebSockets and agreed the directions are worth pursuing. The strategic sequence is measurement first, provider-neutral capability modeling second, then optional OpenAI-specific persistent transport behind existing provider boundaries.\n\nIn scope:\n- Agent-loop latency tracing across model requests, streaming, context assembly, tool execution, retries, mana persistence, and UI/runtime overhead.\n- Provider capability metadata for stateless HTTP, streaming, continuation IDs, persistent sessions/WebSockets, cancellation, and resumability.\n- Long-session recovery semantics: connection drops, completed tool calls, replay avoidance, durable checkpoints.\n- Optional OpenAI-specific WebSocket transport after interfaces and measurement justify it.\n- Mana/run-state integration as durable execution ledger for long-running workflows.\n\nOut of scope:\n- Rewriting imp around OpenAI-specific API semantics.\n- Making WebSockets the default provider abstraction.\n- Broad provider rewrites before measurement exists.\n- Claiming equivalent support across Anthropic/OpenRouter/local providers without inspection.\n\nPreferred sequence:\n1. Instrument current agent loop and establish latency evidence.\n2. Add provider transport capability modeling with current providers mapped conservatively.\n3. Design recovery/checkpoint semantics for long sessions.\n4. Prototype OpenAI WebSocket mode behind provider boundary and feature/capability checks.\n5. Compare latency before/after on representative tool-heavy workflows.",
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.6-design-persistent-transport-safety-gate.md:45:      "notes": "---\n2026-05-01T18:40:25.077567+00:00\nDurable sequencing confirmed by user and externalized before further work:\n1. Measure current agent-loop latency first to establish baseline evidence.\n2. Model provider transport capabilities conservatively before changing behavior.\n3. Define long-session recovery/replay semantics before persistent transports, especially for side-effecting tools.\n4. Prototype OpenAI WebSocket/persistent-session support only behind provider capability gates and compare against baseline.\n\nScope decision: keep this in imp project mana for now because the concrete work is in imp's agent runtime/provider/tool loop. Escalate to root mana only if the design changes mana/imp platform boundaries, shared provider contracts, or cross-app runtime APIs.\n\n---\n2026-05-01T18:42:22.495182+00:00\nExecution plan for closing this epic:\n- Work 317.1 first in-place, preserving current TimingEvent/AgentEvent::Timing path already present in crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs. Extend it rather than adding a tracing dependency.\n- Current inspection found existing timing stages only cover LLM request start, first stream event, first text delta, first tool call, and message end. Missing baseline coverage: context assembly/masking duration, retry/startup failures, tool execution duration/result status, post-turn assessment, and mana/runtime persistence visibility where reachable.\n- Keep all instrumentation metadata-only/redacted: stage names, turn index, elapsed milliseconds, tool name/status only; no prompts, args, outputs, model text, or secrets.\n- Avoid unrelated dirty worktree changes currently present (.DS_Store, .gitignore, CHANGELOG.md, crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs, etc.) unless the specific task requires them.\n- After 317.1 passes cargo check, move to 317.2 provider capability modeling, then 317.3 recovery semantics. Only create/prototype OpenAI WebSocket task after those pass and evidence justifies it.",
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.6-design-persistent-transport-safety-gate.md:56:      "description": "Goal: Define long-session recovery semantics before adding persistent transport support, so imp can safely recover from dropped connections and avoid duplicate tool side effects.\n\nCurrent state: Persistent WebSocket/session transports can improve latency, but they increase the importance of clear recovery behavior when the connection drops mid-turn or provider state diverges from local tool state.\n\nSteps:\n1. Inspect current agent loop, tool execution, and mana run-state persistence paths.\n2. Identify where imp currently records:\n   - model requests/responses or equivalent turn state\n   - tool call start/completion/failure\n   - mana unit/run progress\n   - retry attempts and errors\n3. Draft recovery semantics covering:\n   - provider connection drop before any tool call\n   - drop after model emitted a tool call but before local execution\n   - drop during local tool execution\n   - drop after local tool execution but before provider receives output\n   - duplicate/replayed tool call detection and user confirmation for unsafe actions\n4. Record durable architecture decisions in mana for replay policy and checkpoint boundaries.\n5. If obvious gaps exist, create follow-up implementation tasks with precise verify gates.\n\nFiles likely relevant:\n- crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs (inspect — turn loop, retries, tool execution)\n- crates/imp-core/src/tools/ (inspect — tool call semantics and side effects)\n- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs (inspect — mana run-state persistence)\n- crates/imp-cli/src/lib.rs and crates/imp-tui/src/app.rs (inspect only for session interruption/cancellation behavior)\n\nIn scope:\n- Design and decision capture.\n- Follow-up task creation for implementation gaps.\n\nOut of scope:\n- Implementing WebSocket support.\n- Broad changes to tool safety policy before the design is approved.\n\nDo not:\n- Assume all tools are idempotent.\n- Replay shell/edit/git/mana writes silently after ambiguous failure.\n- Store raw secrets or prompts in durable logs.",
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.6-design-persistent-transport-safety-gate.md:57:      "acceptance": "A design note or mana decisions describe how imp handles connection drops and replay hazards in long-running sessions, including what state is durable, what can be retried, and what must not be replayed without confirmation.",
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.6-design-persistent-transport-safety-gate.md:58:      "notes": "---\n2026-05-01T18:55:14.977612+00:00\nLoaded mana skill and proceeding per executable-spec guidance.\n\nCurrent execution/decomposition plan for this design unit:\n1. Inspect current recovery-relevant paths before making decisions:\n   - crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs turn loop/provider stream/tool execution/cancellation.\n   - crates/imp-core/src/tools/ for side-effecting vs read-only tool semantics and cancellation/idempotency hints.\n   - crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs for mana run/unit persistence and run-state behavior.\n   - crates/imp-core/src/retry.rs for provider startup retry boundaries.\n   - crates/imp-core/src/imp_session.rs and crates/imp-core/src/session.rs for persisted conversation/session boundaries.\n2. Record evidence in notes: what state is durable today vs only in-memory/event stream.\n3. Add durable decisions for replay policy and checkpoint boundaries:\n   - provider drop before complete assistant message\n   - emitted tool call before local execution\n   - drop/cancel during local tool execution\n   - local tool completed but provider did not receive result\n   - duplicate/replayed tool call policy for safe vs unsafe tools\n4. Create follow-up implementation tasks only for concrete gaps discovered during inspection, each with worker-ready context and verify gates.\n5. Close 317.3 when mana decisions/notes describe recovery semantics and verify gate passes.\n\nInitial inspection evidence from crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs:\n- Agent keeps turn state in-memory during run: self.messages, ordered_content, tool_calls, assistant_msg, queued_follow_ups.\n- Provider stream errors before terminal MessageEnd currently emit Error/AgentEnd and return without pushing partial assistant content except StreamEvent::Error, which pushes a synthetic assistant error message.\n- Unexpected stream end without MessageEnd returns an LLM stream error and does not persist partial ordered_content into self.messages.\n- Tool calls are executed only after a completed assistant message is pushed to self.messages.\n- execute_tools runs read-only tools concurrently and mutable tools sequentially.\n- execute_one_tool emits ToolExecutionStart/End events and returns ToolResultMessage, but most early guard/block returns currently do not emit the new timing end event added in 317.1.\n- Repeated identical tool-call detection exists only in-memory within a run and warns at 3, blocks at 4; it is not durable across resume/restart.\n- Tool execution receives ToolContext with cancellation token, checkpoint_state, file_tracker, turn_mana_review, and config.\n\n---\n2026-05-01T18:56:33.791068+00:00\nAdditional inspection evidence:\n\nRetry boundary (crates/imp-core/src/retry.rs):\n- stream_with_retry retries only before the first meaningful stream event. It buffers leading MessageStart events; once any non-MessageStart event is emitted, later errors surface directly.\n- Retryable: rate limit, reqwest connect/timeout/request errors, Stream errors, some 5xx provider messages. Auth, serialization, IO, context-too-long are not retried.\n- This matches a safe replay boundary: provider startup retries are transparent only before any assistant content/tool call could have been observed by the runtime.\n\nTool semantics (crates/imp-core/src/tools/mod.rs and scanned tools):\n- Tool trait exposes is_readonly() but no idempotency/replay-safety metadata beyond read-only classification.\n- execute_tools uses is_readonly() to run read-only tools concurrently; mutable tools run sequentially.\n- ToolContext includes cancellation token, update channel, command_tx, UI, file_cache, checkpoint_state, file_tracker, anchor_store, Lua loader, mode, turn_mana_review, and config.\n- File checkpointing exists through CheckpointState, but it snapshots file contents in memory and checkpoint records are later persisted to session by ImpSession.\n- Many tools are side-effecting despite not all side effects being file edits: bash, git mutating actions, mana updates/runs, write/edit/multi_edit, worktree mutating actions, imp spawn, memory, extend.\n\nSession persistence (crates/imp-core/src/imp_session.rs and session.rs):\n- ImpSession::prompt persists the user message before starting Agent::run, then loads active session history into agent.messages excluding the just-appended prompt to avoid duplicate context.\n- SessionManager persists AgentEvent::ToolExecutionEnd as a tool result and AgentEvent::TurnEnd as an assistant message plus canonical usage when present.\n- recv_event persists event entries as events are consumed. wait() drains any pending events after the agent task completes. If callers bypass recv_event/event draining, persistence relies on wait()'s final drain.\n- Agent::run itself only mutates in-memory self.messages; durable session persistence happens outside the agent via event consumption/drain.\n- Force abort (ImpSession::abort) aborts the task and sets Cancelled; it does not produce a final AgentEnd/TurnEnd and can leave in-flight tool effects ambiguous.\n\nMana run-state persistence (crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs):\n- NativeRunState records run_id/scope/status/error/summary/units/log_lines/event_count and is persisted to run_state.json.\n- ManaRunStore persists only on finish/fail; update_with_event mutates in-memory store during a run but does not persist every event.\n- Background native mana runs run in a spawned task and update store via event sink; finish/fail persist. A process crash mid-run can lose recent in-memory event updates and leave started/running state stale depending on prior persistence.\n\nImplication for recovery semantics:\n- Current safe replay boundary is before any meaningful provider stream event.\n- After assistant content/tool-call emission, provider retry/replay is unsafe without durable turn/tool-call ledger.\n- After local tool start, replay is unsafe unless tool is read-only or has explicit idempotency metadata and durable completion evidence.\n- Session durable state is event-driven, not currently a synchronous transaction around tool start/end/provider output handoff.",
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.6-design-persistent-transport-safety-gate.md:60:      "close_reason": "Completed design/decision unit. Inspected agent turn loop, tool execution, retry boundaries, tool semantics, session persistence, checkpoint state, ImpSession cancellation/abort, and native mana run-state persistence. Recorded durable decisions for provider retry boundary, tool replay policy, checkpoint boundaries, and dropped-connection cases. Created follow-up implementation/design tasks for concrete gaps: 317.4 redacted turn/tool recovery ledger, 317.5 interrupted mana run-state persistence/classification, 317.6 persistent transport safety gate. Verify passed conceptually with native mana inspection: mana show 317.3 and mana tree 317 now expose decisions and child tasks.",
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.6-design-persistent-transport-safety-gate.md:65:      "verify": "mana show 317 && mana tree 317",
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.6-design-persistent-transport-safety-gate.md:75:          "output_snippet": "verify passed: mana show 317 && mana tree 317"
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.6-design-persistent-transport-safety-gate.md:79:        "text": "{\n  \"id\": \"317\",\n  \"title\": \"Improve imp agent-loop latency and persistent transport readiness\",\n  \"slug\": \"improve-imp-agent-loop-latency-and-persistent-tran\",\n  \"status\": \"open\",\n  \"priority\": 2,\n  \"created_at\": \"2026-05-01T18:39:48.456700Z\",\n  \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-01T18:42:22.495190Z\",\n  \"description\": \"Goal: Make imp faster and more resilient for long-running, tool-heavy agent workflows by measuring loop latency first, then introducing provider transport capabilities and persistent-session support where justified.\\n\\nContext: User reviewed OpenAI's article on speeding up agentic workflows with Responses API WebSockets and agreed the directions are worth pursuing. The strategic sequence is measurement first, provider-neutral capability modeling second, then optional OpenAI-specific persistent transport behind existing provider boundaries.\\n\\nIn scope:\\n- Agent-loop latency tracing across model requests, streaming, context assembly, tool execution, retries, mana persistence, and UI/runtime overhead.\\n- Provider capability metadata for stateless HTTP, streaming, continuation IDs, persistent sessions/WebSockets, cancellation, and resumability.\\n- Long-session recovery semantics: connection drops, completed tool calls, replay avoidance, durable checkpoints.\\n- Optional OpenAI-specific WebSocket transport after interfaces and measurement justify it.\\n- Mana/run-state integration as durable execution ledger for long-running workflows.\\n\\nOut of scope:\\n- Rewriting imp around OpenAI-specific API semantics.\\n- Making WebSockets the default provider abstraction.\\n- Broad provider rewrites before measurement exists.\\n- Claiming equivalent support across Anthropic/OpenRouter/local providers without inspection.\\n\\nPreferred sequence:\\n1. Instrument current agent loop and establish latency evidence.\\n2. Add provider transport capability modeling with current providers mapped conservatively.\\n3. Design recovery/checkpoint semantics for long sessions.\\n4. Prototype OpenAI WebSocket mode behind provider boundary and feature/capability checks.\\n5. Compare latency before/after on representative tool-heavy workflows.\",\n  \"acceptance\": \"imp has measured agent-loop latency, provider transport capabilities, documented recovery semantics, and a gated persistent-session path if warranted by evidence.\",\n  \"notes\": \"---\\n2026-05-01T18:40:25.077567+00:00\\nDurable sequencing confirmed by user and externalized before further work:\\n1. Measure current agent-loop latency first to establish baseline evidence.\\n2. Model provider transport capabilities conservatively before changing behavior.\\n3. Define long-session recovery/replay semantics before persistent transports, especially for side-effecting tools.\\n4. Prototype OpenAI WebSocket/persistent-session support only behind provider capability gates and compare against baseline.\\n\\nScope decision: keep this in imp project mana for now because the concrete work is in imp's agent runtime/provider/tool loop. Escalate to root mana only if the design changes mana/imp platform boundaries, shared provider contracts, or cross-app runtime APIs.\\n\\n---\\n2026-05-01T18:42:22.495182+00:00\\nExecution plan for closing this epic:\\n- Work 317.1 first in-place, preserving current TimingEvent/AgentEvent::Timing path already present in crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs. Extend it rather than adding a tracing dependency.\\n- Current inspection found existing timing stages only cover LLM request start, first stream event, first text delta, first tool call, and message end. Missing baseline coverage: context assembly/masking duration, retry/startup failures, tool execution duration/result status, post-turn assessment, and mana/runtime persistence visibility where reachable.\\n- Keep all instrumentation metadata-only/redacted: stage names, turn index, elapsed milliseconds, tool name/status only; no prompts, args, outputs, model text, or secrets.\\n- Avoid unrelated dirty worktree changes currently present (.DS_Store, .gitignore, CHANGELOG.md, crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs, etc.) unless the specific task requires them.\\n- After 317.1 passes cargo check, move to 317.2 provider capability modeling, then 317.3 recovery semantics. Only create/prototype OpenAI WebSocket task after those pass and evidence justifies it.\",\n  \"kind\": \"epic\"\n}\n[ ] 317 Improve imp agent-loop latency and persistent transport readiness\n├── │   [ ] 317.3 Define long-session recovery and replay semantics\n├── │   [ ] 317.4 Add redacted turn/tool recovery ledger\n├── │   [ ] 317.5 Persist and classify interrupted mana run state\n└──     [ ] 317.6 Design persistent transport safety gate"
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.6-design-persistent-transport-safety-gate.md:143:- Design and mana decisions.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:3:title: Diagnose mana run queued-worker dispatch failure
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:4:slug: diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:13:  Started diagnosis. Verify command baseline passed: `rg "run_with_stream_capture_and_sink|UnitStart|UnitDone|agents" crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs /Users/asher/mana/crates && cargo test -p imp-core background_run --no-default-features` passed 4 background_run tests. This only proves existing tests pass; they do not reproduce queued worker never starting.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:18:  - imp native `mana(action=run)` calls mana-cli library `run_with_stream_capture_and_sink` in `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs`.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:19:  - mana-cli direct mode is used when `.mana/config.yaml` has no `run` template; it requires `imp --version` on PATH, then spawns `imp [--model <model>] run <unit-id> --mana-dir <mana_dir> --defer-verify` in `/Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/ready_queue.rs`.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:21:  - Template mode differs: if config.run exists, `mana run` uses shell template mode. Under json_stream, child stdout/stderr are set to null in `/Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/wave.rs`, so template workers cannot stream details to imp; only wave result is visible.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:24:  - Template-mode disposable smoke with `run: python3 ... close unit` successfully emitted run_start/round_start/unit_done/round_end/run_end and closed the unit. This proves mana CLI scheduling can execute template commands in isolation.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:25:  - Direct-mode disposable smoke with fake `imp` on PATH successfully invoked fake imp as `--model fake-model run 1` (no `--mana-dir` logged because fake parser stopped early) and emitted unit_done/run_end after fake worker closed unit. This proves direct scheduling can spawn an imp-like binary in isolation.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:28:  - The installed real `imp` supports `imp run <id> --mana-dir <path> --defer-verify` per clap tests and help, but if an older or different `imp` is first on PATH inside the TUI/background process, mana direct mode may detect/spawn that binary. Current shell shows `/Users/asher/bin/imp` version 0.1.3. Need compare against current source binary and verify PATH seen by TUI/background.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:29:  - `imp --mode json run 0 --mana-dir /tmp/nope --defer-verify` prints errors to stderr and no JSON stdout on load failure. mana ready_queue only monitors stdout events and waits for process exit; it should still produce UnitDone failure if the process exits, not queued forever.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:33:  - Inspect why direct smoke fake imp log omitted `--mana-dir`; fake parser bug stopped at first `run`, not mana issue.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:38:  - `imp` JSON mode prints `agent_start`, `tool_execution_start/end`, `turn_end`, `agent_end` (`crates/imp-cli/src/lib.rs:2199+`). mana's `pi_output::parse_agent_event` recognizes these (`/Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-cli/src/pi_output.rs:207+`, `276+`). So protocol mismatch is unlikely for real worker progress.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:39:  - `tool_execution_end` emitted by imp does not include `tool`; mana parser falls back to empty name, so tool end file-path details may be weak, but this does not block dispatch/start.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:41:  - `confirm_dispatch_with_decisions` auto-continues in json_stream/non-tty, so unresolved decision warnings should not block native imp mana tool dispatch.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:49:  Need next: inspect `Config::default`/actual project `.mana/config.yaml` for `max_concurrent`, and add/execute a smoke where max_concurrent=0 to see if it matches queued-without-UnitStart.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:53:  Reproduced a deterministic queued-never-started condition: if `.mana/config.yaml` sets `max_concurrent: 0`, `mana run <id> --json-stream -j 1` emits RunStart with the unit listed as queued, emits RoundStart/RoundEnd/RunEnd, but never emits UnitStart or UnitDone. Output:
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:61:  - `/Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/mod.rs` builds `RunConfig { max_jobs: params.jobs.min(config.max_concurrent) as usize, ... }`.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:62:  - `/Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/ready_queue.rs` starts with `running_count=0`, `newly_started=0`; for ready units, the loop immediately `break`s when `running_count >= max_jobs`. With max_jobs=0, no unit is removed/started. Then the `if running_count == 0 && newly_started == 0` branch breaks without error if `remaining` is empty? In the observed run, because ready_units existed but none were started due to capacity, it exits silently and RunEnd reports zero outcomes. No UnitStart/UnitDone is emitted.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:65:  This is an implementation-level root cause with reproducible evidence. Fix should be in mana-cli and imp:
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:66:  1. In mana-cli run config construction and/or validation, reject/elevate effective max_jobs==0 with a clear Error event / anyhow error before RunStart, or clamp to at least 1 when units are ready. Better: validate `params.jobs > 0` and `config.max_concurrent > 0`, error if not.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:68:  3. In imp native mana tool validation/schema, require jobs >= 1 and avoid passing 0.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:69:  4. Add regression test in mana-cli reproducing `max_concurrent: 0` or jobs=0 and asserting error/no queued silent success. Add imp-core validation test for jobs=0 once changed.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:82:  3. Run may have used template mode because `.mana/config.yaml` has `run` set; in json_stream template child stdout/stderr are nulled, so worker detail is hidden. Confirm runtime mode from `RunStart.runtime` and config.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:83:  4. `UnitStart` may have occurred but imp run_store/UI did not receive or persist it. Compare raw stream events from mana-cli with native imp `run_state` events for same disposable run.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:87:  - From the failed real run if available: `mana run_state <run_id>`, `mana logs <run_id>`, `mana agents`, `.mana/config.yaml` run/max_concurrent/run_model values, and whether events include `UnitStart`.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:94:  Confirmed zero-effective-concurrency can still happen with config max_concurrent=4 if caller passes `jobs=0`: disposable project with `max_concurrent: 4` and CLI `mana run <id> -j 0 --json-stream` produced the same queued/no-start/no-done sequence. This remains a plausible primary bug if the model/native tool emitted `jobs:0` or an unset/invalid jobs value was coerced to zero. Need inspect actual failed tool call args/details or TUI transcript for jobs value. Native imp schema currently advertises `jobs` as integer with no minimum; execute parses `params["jobs"].as_u64().unwrap_or(4) as u32`, so explicit `jobs:0` is accepted.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:100:  Confirmed another possible primary cause even with max_concurrent=4: caller-supplied `jobs:0`. CLI reproduction with `max_concurrent: 4` and `-j 0` emits RunStart queued, RoundStart/RoundEnd/RunEnd with zero outcomes and no UnitStart/UnitDone. imp native schema accepts explicit `jobs:0` (`as_u64().unwrap_or(4) as u32`). If the model/tool call used `jobs:0`, that explains the observed bug despite max_concurrent=4. Need actual failed tool call details or transcript to confirm. Regardless, imp should reject jobs<1 and mana should reject max_jobs=0.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:106:  Observed with fake `imp` shim on PATH and both installed/source mana:
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:107:  - mana direct mode invokes fake imp with args only: `--model fake-model run 1`
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:108:  - It does NOT pass `--mode json`, `--mana-dir`, or `--defer-verify` at runtime in this local checkout, despite `/Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/ready_queue.rs` source showing `build_imp_command` should add `--mode json run <id> --mana-dir <path> --defer-verify`.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:109:  - The fake shim exited 7 when `--mana-dir` was absent, and mana emitted UnitDone failure. A real imp invoked as `imp --model gpt-5.4 run <id>` likely enters non-json/default TUI-ish/CLI path or errors/hangs differently. This could explain a queued/running worker that never provides usable JSON progress, plus terminal slowdown.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:112:  - Possibility: built/used mana binary or workspace dependency is stale despite source inspection. But `cargo run --manifest-path /Users/asher/mana/Cargo.toml -p mana-cli` showed same args, so likely there is another build path/function or feature cfg.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:113:  - Need instrument/inspect `build_direct_command`/tests in mana source. There are existing tests asserting expected args around lines 924/952, so if tests pass but runtime differs, the call path may not use `build_imp_command` expected source or fake log missed later args. Fake log uses `printf 'ARG[%s]\n' "$@"`, which should log all args; it logged only four.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:116:  1. Run the mana ready_queue command-arg unit tests to confirm expected args.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:118:  3. If confirmed, fix mana direct spawn to always pass `--mode json`, `--mana-dir`, and `--defer-verify` when invoking imp. Add a runtime/integration test with fake imp asserting full args, not just unit test on helper.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:121:- mana-run
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:124:verify: rg "run_with_stream_capture_and_sink|UnitStart|UnitDone|agents" crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs /Users/asher/mana/crates && cargo test -p imp-core background_run --no-default-features
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:137:Goal: diagnose why native `mana run` can queue an agent that never starts.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:141:- Prior fix only improved UI/observability in `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs`.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:145:1. Inspect `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` run action, especially `spawn_background_run`, foreground `run_with_stream_capture_and_sink`, `NativeRunParams`, run_store updates, and background defaults.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:146:2. Inspect mana run implementation in the local mana workspace (`/Users/asher/mana`, likely `crates/mana-cli` and/or `crates/mana-core`) for how queued units become worker processes/agents. Focus on queue/spawn APIs, runtime/direct_agent config, logs, and error handling.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:147:3. Run a controlled dry/smoke reproduction that cannot damage real work: create/use a disposable mana unit with a trivial verify/acceptance under a temp project or clearly isolated scope; run native/CLI mana orchestration with low jobs (1) and inspect run_state/agents/logs. Prefer commands that avoid using real LLM API if possible; if not possible, stop and report exact missing safe harness.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:148:4. Determine whether failure is in imp integration parameters/context, mana runtime dispatch, background task lifetime/cancellation, missing worker binary/env, or hidden worker error.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:149:5. Record findings in mana notes on this task and hand off a concrete implementation plan.
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:152:- `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` (imp native mana tool)
.mana/331.1-diagnose-mana-run-queued-worker-dispatch-failure.md:153:- `/Users/asher/mana/crates/**/run*.rs` and worker/agent orchestration files found by structural/search inspection
.mana/50.11-implement-first-shell-to-view-handoff-for-sessions.md:30:    - uses `SessionManager::list()`
.mana/50.11-implement-first-shell-to-view-handoff-for-sessions.md:33:    - uses `SessionManager::continue_recent()` for the current cwd
.mana/50.11-implement-first-shell-to-view-handoff-for-sessions.md:34:    - prints a simple textual tree from `SessionManager::get_tree()`
.mana/50.11-implement-first-shell-to-view-handoff-for-sessions.md:36:    - uses `SessionManager::continue_recent()` for the current cwd
.mana/50.11-implement-first-shell-to-view-handoff-for-sessions.md:62:  - `imp view sessions` lists saved sessions via `SessionManager::list()`
.mana/50.11-implement-first-shell-to-view-handoff-for-sessions.md:63:  - `imp view tree` renders a textual tree for the most recent cwd session via `SessionManager::continue_recent()` + `get_tree()`
.mana/50.11-implement-first-shell-to-view-handoff-for-sessions.md:87:  Externalized durable implementation delta from docs/rebuild/imp-view-shell-handoff-patterns.md into mana. The spec now fixes the preferred shell->viewer contract as an explicit `imp view ...` launch with visible boundary cues, separate-process bias, and explicit return lines. Because the current proving-slice implementation in `imp/crates/imp-cli/src/main.rs` still prints `opening viewer: {area}` and directly calls `run_view_mode()` inline, created follow-up unit 50.11.2 to align implementation messaging and return cues with the documented contract without broadening scope into a full viewer redesign.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.11-gate-tui-mana-dependency-for-standalone-imp-instal.md:3:title: Gate TUI mana dependency for standalone imp install
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.11-gate-tui-mana-dependency-for-standalone-imp-instal.md:4:slug: gate-tui-mana-dependency-for-standalone-imp-instal
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.11-gate-tui-mana-dependency-for-standalone-imp-instal.md:11:  - imp-tui does not enable imp-core/mana-integration by default.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.11-gate-tui-mana-dependency-for-standalone-imp-instal.md:12:  - imp-cli default dependency tree has no imp-work and ideally no mana-core/mana-cli.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.11-gate-tui-mana-dependency-for-standalone-imp-instal.md:14:  - Mana-specific TUI/API surfaces are gated or a precise blocker is recorded.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.11-gate-tui-mana-dependency-for-standalone-imp-instal.md:18:  Attempted to make imp-tui standalone by default. Found blocker: TUI app has broad mana-specific UI/runtime coupling (`mana_run_state`, `mana_core::api`, mana navigator, active mana scope/run UI) across `app.rs` and `views/mana_navigator.rs`. Simply disabling TUI default mana integration makes `cargo check -p imp-cli` fail. Reverted workspace/default dependency behavior and kept imp-cli enabling `imp-tui/mana-integration` explicitly for now. Current verified state: imp-cli builds and remains free of imp-work, but still pulls mana through TUI. Next useful action is a larger TUI decomposition: isolate mana navigator/run-state/scope UI behind cfg(feature = "mana-integration") or split it into an optional module before default installed imp can be mana-free.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.11-gate-tui-mana-dependency-for-standalone-imp-instal.md:27:  - Concrete blocker found: imp-tui currently cannot be made mana-free by Cargo feature toggles alone. `cargo check -p imp-tui` without its mana-integration default fails because `app.rs` and `views/mana_navigator.rs` directly import/use `imp_core::{mana_run_summary, stop_mana_run, ManaRunSummary}` and `mana_core::api`, plus broad active mana scope/run and navigator code. Reverted imp-tui default to `default = ["mana-integration"]` so current CLI/TUI builds remain green. imp-cli remains free of imp-work but still pulls mana through TUI until a larger TUI decomposition gates all mana UI/runtime surfaces.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.11-gate-tui-mana-dependency-for-standalone-imp-instal.md:31:  `cd /Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary && cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-cli -p imp-tui && cargo check -p imp-cli && cargo check -p imp-tui && (cargo tree -p imp-cli | rg 'imp-work|mana-core|mana-cli' && exit 1 || true)`
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.11-gate-tui-mana-dependency-for-standalone-imp-instal.md:35:verify: cd /Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary && cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-cli -p imp-tui && cargo check -p imp-cli && cargo check -p imp-tui && (cargo tree -p imp-cli | rg 'imp-work|mana-core|mana-cli' && exit 1 || true)
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.11-gate-tui-mana-dependency-for-standalone-imp-instal.md:45:Make default imp-cli/imp-tui installed path standalone by removing default mana integration from imp-tui where feasible. Mana UI surfaces should be hidden/gated behind a feature so imp-cli default dependency tree does not pull mana crates.

.mana/365.7-define-mana-dataapi-surface-needed-by-the-gui-harn.md:3:title: Define mana data/API surface needed by the GUI harness
.mana/365.7-define-mana-dataapi-surface-needed-by-the-gui-harn.md:4:slug: define-mana-dataapi-surface-needed-by-the-gui-harn
.mana/365.7-define-mana-dataapi-surface-needed-by-the-gui-harn.md:13:  - Task board/chat/run/evidence flows all have required mana state identified.
.mana/365.7-define-mana-dataapi-surface-needed-by-the-gui-harn.md:18:  External framing source from user: GUI API surface should expose durable orchestration state, not agent topology internals. Required read models should include durable step/run status, persistent state/artifacts, parallel work/sub-run structure, event-driven waits/HITL/cancel states, and structured execution traces. This reinforces board + chat + run monitor + trace/evidence panels as mana-owned state consumed by GUI.
.mana/365.7-define-mana-dataapi-surface-needed-by-the-gui-harn.md:22:  Verification checkpoint after adding Inngest framing: `mana verify 365.7` fails because required deliverable `docs/architecture/mana-gui-api-surface.md` has not been authored yet. The task remains open. Next action: define GUI read/write/subscription API around durable runs, steps, events, artifacts, HITL/cancel states, board/work graph, and traces.
.mana/365.7-define-mana-dataapi-surface-needed-by-the-gui-harn.md:26:  Repo Prompt video direction: GUI API surface should expose active run/worker read models and subscriptions: worker list, status, dense activity summaries, recent events, blocker/question states, touched files, artifacts/evidence, verification status, and control mutations such as cancel/interrupt/steer when supported by the adapter. Keep reads/writes/subscriptions against mana-owned run/event state; do not couple GUI to imp internals.
.mana/365.7-define-mana-dataapi-surface-needed-by-the-gui-harn.md:30:  Correction: do not cite or depend on the Repo Prompt video in the GUI API surface deliverable. Keep the generalized requirement: mana should expose active run/worker read models and subscriptions for worker list, status, activity summaries, recent events, blockers/questions, touched files, artifacts/evidence, verification status, and supported control mutations such as cancel/interrupt/steer.

.mana/300.5-verify-imp-crates-package-cleanly-before-publish-a.md:19:Run final imp crates.io package verification after manifest, license, mana dependency, and worktree cleanup. Package order: imp-llm, imp-core, imp-lua, imp-tui, imp-cli. Then run release checks: `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` and `cargo test --workspace` unless the user accepts a narrower check. Do not run `cargo publish` without explicit approval.
.mana/archive/2026/05/364-make-imp-work-a-global-project-scoped-work-store.md:15:  Decision: imp-work should default to a single user-global store (`~/.imp/work`) with `project_root` recorded as first-class metadata, rather than independent cwd/project-local stores. This fixes mana's cwd-scoped `.mana` fragmentation.
.mana/archive/2026/05/364-make-imp-work-a-global-project-scoped-work-store.md:28:  2026-05-21 design insight from user: imp-work must preserve project work history/context across closed epics and related follow-up tasks. Mana's epic/task model loses continuity when agents create an epic for a task, close it, then later create a new epic for related work. A global project-scoped store should not just store isolated tasks; it needs a project-level continuity layer.

.mana/archive/2026/05/362.8-prepare-mana-removal-ledger-and-final-imp-work-cut.md:13:  2026-05-21 ledger prepared in `docs/design/imp-work-mana-removal-ledger.md`. All 362 parity children are closed/verified. Final relevant verification passed: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo test -p imp-work` (74 tests), and `cargo test -p imp-core work_tool -- --nocapture` (29 tests). Removal remains intentionally blocked pending explicit approval and a real-project `.mana` migration/spot-check. Destructive removal of `mana` tool, `mana_worker`, mana-core dependency, or mana-specific agent-loop behavior was not performed.
.mana/archive/2026/05/362.8-prepare-mana-removal-ledger-and-final-imp-work-cut.md:17:  2026-05-21 update: real-project parity gate 362.9 is now complete and closed. Evidence: `mana_migration_real_project_dry_run_and_scratch_write_are_non_destructive` runs against this repo's real `.mana`, performs dry-run import, writes to temp/scratch imp-work store, verifies imported/archive counts match, verifies scratch store contains tasks, and asserts `.mana` top-level md file count unchanged. Verification passed with `cargo test -p imp-work mana_migration -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p imp-work`, `cargo test -p imp-core work_tool -- --nocapture`, and `mana verify 362.9`. Remaining acceptance item for actual destructive cutover is explicit user approval before removing/quarantining mana runtime paths.

.mana/73-code-intelligence-outputs-are-transient-by-default.md:3:title: Code-intelligence outputs are transient by default and should be promoted into mana only as notes, verify evidence, facts, or durable decisions
.mana/73-code-intelligence-outputs-are-transient-by-default.md:11:verify: test -f .mana/73-code-intelligence-outputs-are-transient-by-default.md && rg -q '^id:' .mana/73-code-intelligence-outputs-are-transient-by-default.md
.mana/73-code-intelligence-outputs-are-transient-by-default.md:21:Captured by `docs/design/code-intelligence-evidence-promotion.md`. The design note defines the durable intake boundary: `imp` owns live code-intelligence runtime behavior; `mana` stores only promoted consequences that another worker should inherit cold. It distinguishes retry-relevant notes, closure/review evidence, stable facts, and durable decisions, and recommends normalized summaries plus artifact references over raw diagnostic or protocol dumps.
.mana/344-add-mana-tui-navigator-for-durable-work-graph-brow.md:3:title: Add /mana TUI navigator for durable work graph browsing

Verified targeted gate `cargo test -p imp-tui mana` passes (8 tests). Continuing with polish/edge cases before close.
.mana/344-add-mana-tui-navigator-for-durable-work-graph-brow.md:27:  Created targeted commit 8df7257 (`Add mana navigator TUI overlay`) containing only the `/mana` navigator slice. The main worktree remains dirty with pre-existing/unrelated changes; post-commit diff still shows additional app.rs/command_palette.rs modifications beyond this commit.

.mana/344-add-mana-tui-navigator-for-durable-work-graph-brow.md:49:Design and implement a `/mana` slash command in imp that opens a dedicated mana navigator widget instead of rendering mana command output only in chat. The navigator should make mana visible as a durable project/work graph cockpit inside the TUI.
.mana/344-add-mana-tui-navigator-for-durable-work-graph-brow.md:52:1. Slash command dispatch: `/mana` opens navigator, `/mana <id>` opens a specific unit, and later `/mana next` can jump to suggested next work.
.mana/344-add-mana-tui-navigator-for-durable-work-graph-brow.md:53:2. Read-only tree widget first: render the mana tree/list/show data with keyboard navigation, collapse/expand state, selection, scroll handling, and search/filter. No mutation in the first slice.
.mana/344-add-mana-tui-navigator-for-durable-work-graph-brow.md:56:5. Chat bridge: selected unit can become the current execution contract for an agent turn, e.g. “work on this”, preserving mana as the durable source of truth and chat as discussion/execution.
.mana/344-add-mana-tui-navigator-for-durable-work-graph-brow.md:58:Implementation should preserve existing mana CLI/tool output paths while adding a mode-based TUI surface. Prefer small slices, read-only first, and targeted tests around slash command routing, navigator state transitions, tree selection/collapse bounds, and detail rendering.
.mana/273.5.12-define-sprint-1-typescriptpi-extension-support-bou.md:16:  - README now documents the sprint-1 TypeScript extension boundary: supported registerTool/TypeBox/text/session_start, stubbed registerCommand/ui notify/status/sessionManager, unsupported ctx.ui.custom/viewers/tool overrides/broad native access/dependency management.
.mana/273.5.12-define-sprint-1-typescriptpi-extension-support-bou.md:92:   - Extension loads and works, but touched APIs currently stubbed/recorded by imp compatibility layer, e.g. `registerCommand`, `ui.notify`, `ui.setStatus`, `sessionManager.getBranch/getEntries`.
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:3:title: Introduce pre-execution ManaLoopPolicy in agent tool execution
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:4:slug: introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:9:acceptance: Agent loop can evaluate mana calls before execution and tests demonstrate that read actions pass, mode-denied actions still fail, and high-risk action classes can produce policy decisions without relying on result text parsing.
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:13:  Starting implementation of remaining mana-loop tasks per user request. Mana skill loaded. Scope includes 330.3, 330.4, 330.5, plus system prompt update to reduce reliance on the mana skill once runtime/tool support is stronger.
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:17:  Resuming after user said continue. Current git HEAD no longer contains the earlier 330.1/330.2 module split/classification changes (`crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs` is monolithic again and no `agent/` directory exists). Proceeding against current clean HEAD: implement mana loop policy/classification in the current file layout, then update follow-up units/status accordingly.
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:21:  Implemented initial pre-execution ManaLoopPolicy in `agent/mana_loop.rs` and `agent/tool_execution.rs`. The loop now classifies `mana.action`, blocks mode-denied mana calls before tool execution with structured `mana_loop_policy` details, and enriches successful mana tool results with `mana_action_class`/policy evidence. Verification passed: `cargo check -p imp-core`, `cargo test -p imp-core mana_action_classification`, and `cargo test -p imp-core agent:: --no-run`.
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:26:  error: unexpected argument 'agent_blocks_bash_mana_when_native_action_exists' found
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:34:- mana
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:39:verify: cargo test -p imp-core mana_loop_policy agent_blocks_bash_mana_when_native_action_exists
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:53:    error: unexpected argument 'agent_blocks_bash_mana_when_native_action_exists' found
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:62:- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:83:Goal: introduce a pre-execution `ManaLoopPolicy` hook in the agent tool execution path, initially conservative and mostly observational, so mana becomes a loop-native action rather than an opaque tool result.
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:85:Current state: `execute_one_tool` only applies generic tool mode checks, hooks, repeated-call guard, bash mana-equivalent blocking, schema validation, then executes the tool. `ManaTool` later enforces mode action allowlists internally. Post-turn assessment infers meaning from `ToolResult.details.action`.
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:90:1. Add `ManaLoopPolicy` / `ManaPolicyDecision` types near the mana action classification module.
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:91:2. In `execute_one_tool`, before generic schema validation/execution, if `tool_name == "mana"`, parse `args["action"]` and classify it.
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:100:- Pre-execution mana classification/policy hook.
.mana/330.3-introduce-pre-execution-manalooppolicy-in-agent-to.md:107:- Changing `ManaTool` semantics.
.mana/350.4-redesign-tui-event-loop-around-wakeable-inputrunti.md:25:  - Expensive filesystem/git/session/mana loads run in background tasks and post results back.
.mana/350.4-redesign-tui-event-loop-around-wakeable-inputrunti.md:42:  - 292edd7 Wake TUI from background runtime events: runtime_signal channel, direct agent event forwarding, /resume and /mana background loading/opening with loading UI. Verified targeted tests before commit.
.mana/350.4-redesign-tui-event-loop-around-wakeable-inputrunti.md:56:  User reported remaining delays after sending a message. Implemented and committed 81fe3b6 Persist sent TUI messages off input path. enqueue_visible_agent_turn now echoes user/assistant placeholder and snapshots session with the pending user message for the agent immediately, while actual SessionManager::append persistence runs in spawn_blocking and reports RuntimeSignal::UserMessagePersisted/Failed. Added SessionManager::snapshot_with_pending_user_message and set_leaf_id_for_in_memory; SessionManager derives Clone. This removes synchronous JSONL append/fsync-ish work from the send path while preserving agent context. Verified send_message_defers_agent_start_until_after_echo_redraw, user_message_persist_signal_updates_in_memory_leaf, imp-core session tests, runtime_signal_batch_drains_bursty_agent_events_before_render, event_source::tests, chat_and_sidebar_stream_cache_ignore_animation_tick.

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-cut-remaining-default-mana-dependencies-from-imp-c.md:3:title: Cut remaining default mana dependencies from imp-core
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-cut-remaining-default-mana-dependencies-from-imp-c.md:4:slug: cut-remaining-default-mana-dependencies-from-imp-c
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-cut-remaining-default-mana-dependencies-from-imp-c.md:11:  - `mana-cli` remains optional and only enabled through `mana-integration`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-cut-remaining-default-mana-dependencies-from-imp-c.md:12:  - Audit remaining default `mana-core` dependency in `imp-core` and identify/gate blockers to making it optional.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-cut-remaining-default-mana-dependencies-from-imp-c.md:13:  - If feasible in a focused slice, make `mana-core` optional for default `imp-core`; otherwise record exact module blockers.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-cut-remaining-default-mana-dependencies-from-imp-c.md:18:  Implementation progress after commit `50fd6f1`: cut `mana-core` from imp-core standalone/no-default dependency tree. `mana-core` is now optional and included via `mana-integration`; `mana-cli` remains optional via same feature. Gated mana_next, mana_prompt_context, mana_run_state, mana_worker behind `mana-integration`. Replaced default prompt-context use of mana_prompt_context with local `builder::PromptContext`. Removed mana_core dependency from git/worktree detection by adding local porcelain worktree detection. Gated mana bootstrap create functions and mana bootstrap execution behind `mana-integration`. `mana_review` no longer imports mana_core directly. TUI still opts into `imp-core/mana-integration` for existing mana run-state UI. Verified: `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-tui`; `cargo check -p imp-core --no-default-features`; `cargo check -p imp-core --features mana-integration`; `cargo check -p imp-tui`; `cargo clippy -p imp-core --lib --features mana-integration -- -D warnings`; and `cargo tree -p imp-core --no-default-features | rg 'mana'` has no matches.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-cut-remaining-default-mana-dependencies-from-imp-c.md:21:verify: cd /Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary && cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-cli -p imp-tui && cargo check -p imp-core --no-default-features && cargo check -p imp-cli && cargo check -p imp-core --features mana-integration && cargo clippy -p imp-core --lib --features mana-integration -- -D warnings
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-cut-remaining-default-mana-dependencies-from-imp-c.md:30:  output_snippet: 'verify passed: cd /Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary && cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-cli -p imp-tui && cargo check -p imp-core --no-default-features && cargo check -p imp-cli && cargo check -p imp-core --features mana-integration && cargo clippy -p imp-core --lib --features mana-integration -- -D warnings'
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-cut-remaining-default-mana-dependencies-from-imp-c.md:49:Continue runtime-boundary slimming after archiving imp-work: reduce default mana dependencies from imp-core, starting with auditing and gating mana-core usages that are not required for standalone agent execution.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-hide-mana-tool-unless-mana-is-installed.md:3:title: Hide mana tool unless mana is installed
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-hide-mana-tool-unless-mana-is-installed.md:4:slug: hide-mana-tool-unless-mana-is-installed
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-hide-mana-tool-unless-mana-is-installed.md:11:  - When mana integration is compiled, native tool registration only exposes the `mana` tool if a mana executable is available on PATH or configured detection succeeds.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-hide-mana-tool-unless-mana-is-installed.md:12:  - Standalone imp without mana installed does not show/register the mana tool by default.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-hide-mana-tool-unless-mana-is-installed.md:14:  - Document the behavior in mana notes or code comments where useful.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-hide-mana-tool-unless-mana-is-installed.md:18:  Implemented runtime hiding for the native mana tool: with `mana-integration` compiled, `register_native_tools` now registers `ManaTool` only when `tools::mana::mana_executable_available()` sees a `mana` executable on PATH. This preserves standalone imp behavior on machines without mana while still allowing the integration when installed. Narrow verification passed: `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core && cargo check -p imp-core --features mana-integration,work-integration && cargo clippy -p imp-core --lib --features mana-integration,work-integration -- -D warnings`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-hide-mana-tool-unless-mana-is-installed.md:21:verify: cd /Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary && cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work && cargo check -p imp-core --no-default-features && cargo check -p imp-core --features mana-integration,work-integration && cargo clippy -p imp-core --lib --features mana-integration,work-integration -- -D warnings
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-hide-mana-tool-unless-mana-is-installed.md:30:  output_snippet: 'verify passed: cd /Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary && cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work && cargo check -p imp-core --no-default-features && cargo check -p imp-core --features mana-integration,work-integration && cargo clippy -p imp-core --lib --features mana-integration,work-integration -- -D warnings'
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-hide-mana-tool-unless-mana-is-installed.md:34:- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-hide-mana-tool-unless-mana-is-installed.md:48:Refine standalone imp behavior so optional mana integration is discoverable only when usable. The mana tool should not appear in normal imp tool lists on machines without mana installed, even when the integration code is compiled. Prefer a small runtime detection seam near native tool registration.

.mana/archive/2026/05/352-add-mouse-wheel-and-click-support-to-imp-tui-mana.md:3:title: Add mouse wheel and click support to imp-tui /mana navigator
