.mana/349.1-extract-composable-loop-policy-stack-from-post-tur.md:17:  Implemented composable policy rule stack: `LoopPolicyRule` plus ordered rules for repeated action, runtime stop, work completed, mana stop, text fallback stop, continue recommendation, planning-only no-progress, and default no-follow-up. Added focused unit tests protecting ordering: repeated action precedence, runtime blocker over work completed, mana stop over fallback/continue, continue only when stop reasons absent, planning-only no-progress, and default no automatic follow-up. Cleaned stale max_turns test fixture references discovered during test compile. Verified with `cargo test -p imp-core agent::loop_policy` and `cargo check -p imp-core -p imp-cli -p imp-tui`.
.mana/349.1-extract-composable-loop-policy-stack-from-post-tur.md:31:Refactor imp-core so the agent loop no longer directly owns post-turn continuation/finish policy. Current state: `crates/imp-core/src/agent/loop_policy.rs` has `DefaultLoopPolicy` translating `NextAction` into `LoopDecision`; `run_loop.rs` calls `self.loop_decision_after_turn(...)`. Next steps: define a small policy trait or rule stack around `RunState`/`TurnState` plus `PostTurnAssessment`; move existing repeated-action, execution debt, mana stop, and text fallback decisions behind the policy seam; keep public behavior unchanged; preserve `TurnAssessment` debug events. Avoid reintroducing max-turn policy.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:3:title: Audit and plan standalone imp runtime independence from mana
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:11:  - Audit `imp-core`, `imp-cli`, and `imp-tui` for required runtime dependencies on mana, mana graph state, and `imp-work`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:13:  - Identify the smallest implementation slice that lets imp CLI/TUI/runtime execute normal non-mana agent runs without a mana daemon, mana graph, or imp-work store.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:21:  - `crates/imp-core/Cargo.toml` currently depends on `imp-work`, `mana-core`, and `mana-cli` (`mana` package). This means core library builds are coupled to mana/imp-work crates even if normal runs do not use them.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:22:  - `crates/imp-cli/Cargo.toml` depends on `imp-work` and `mana-core`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:23:  - `crates/imp-tui/Cargo.toml` depends on `mana-core`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:27:  - `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` is the largest mana/work dispatch surface and should become an optional integration/tool rather than part of standalone runtime assumptions.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:29:  - `crates/imp-core/src/agent/workflow_integration/mana_compat.rs`, `mana_review.rs`, `mana_next`, `mana_prompt_context.rs`, `mana_worker.rs`, `mana_run_state.rs`, and `agent/mana_loop.rs` are mana compatibility/runtime adapter surfaces, not normal standalone agent loop requirements.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:31:  - TUI has mana navigation/settings/sidebar surfaces; these should degrade/hide when mana is unavailable rather than blocking normal TUI chat/run.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:34:  - Optional adapters: mana tool, mana review/worker/run-state, mana navigator UI, mana compatibility workflow integration.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:35:  - Likely standalone blockers: direct Cargo dependencies from `imp-core` to `mana-cli`, `mana-core`, and `imp-work`; always-registered mana/work tools if they force linking/config/state; TUI assumptions that mana state exists.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:39:  1. Introduce feature gates or adapter boundaries so `imp-core` can build/run in a default standalone mode without `mana-cli` and ideally without `imp-work`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:40:  2. Move/register `mana` and `work` tools only when integration features are enabled or when host configuration explicitly enables them.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:41:  3. Ensure CLI/TUI normal chat/run paths construct an Agent without opening mana graph/imp-work store.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:44:  Do not delete imp-work mechanically in the release branch; first cut the required dependency from imp-core default runtime, then remove/archive imp-work in a follow-up once mana owns replacement semantics.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-and-plan-standalone-imp-runtime-independence.md:47:verify: cd /Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary && rg -n "imp_work|imp-work|mana|Mana|WorkStore|work tool|workflow" crates/imp-core crates/imp-cli crates/imp-tui Cargo.toml && cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work && cargo check -p imp-core && cargo clippy -p imp-core --lib -- -D warnings

.mana/285-verify-installed-imp-binary-includes-latest-secret.md:17:  User clarified Google remains broken after multiple remove/readd cycles, so this is likely OS keychain persistence/readback rather than user not re-saving. Need diagnose macOS keychain entries for service/account metadata only: expected current service `imp`, account `google:api_key`; legacy services include `imp-cli`, `impeccable`, `mana`. Check whether entries exist, whether CLI write reports success but immediate read via doctor fails, and whether duplicate/stale keychain entries or keychain access control are involved. Avoid printing secret values; use metadata-only keychain commands or `-g` only if output is redacted/not shown.

.mana/archive/2026/05/336-audit-native-mana-run-dispatchrunstate-queue-stall.md:3:title: Audit native mana run dispatch/run_state queue stall and TUI latency regression
.mana/archive/2026/05/336-audit-native-mana-run-dispatchrunstate-queue-stall.md:4:slug: audit-native-mana-run-dispatchrunstate-queue-stall
.mana/archive/2026/05/336-audit-native-mana-run-dispatchrunstate-queue-stall.md:9:acceptance: Findings identify why agents can remain queued/not dispatched via native mana run dispatch/run_state; fixes are implemented if low-risk and within scope, otherwise blockers/next steps are documented; TUI latency relationship is explained; targeted verify commands pass or exact blockers are recorded.
.mana/archive/2026/05/336-audit-native-mana-run-dispatchrunstate-queue-stall.md:17:  Audit findings: native imp tool wraps mana-cli run_with_stream_capture_and_sink. In current ../mana source, direct ready-queue emits RunStart then UnitStart/UnitDone. Two plausible issues found in imp wrapper: (1) jobs=0 from tool input was passed through to mana, producing run_cfg.max_jobs=0; ready_queue then never dispatches, leaving RunStart units queued until the runner reports unresolvable/stuck. Fixed by clamping requested jobs to at least 1. (2) run_state initialized RunStart units with agent/model None, so queued rows did not show which agent/model they were intended for; fixed by copying normalized runtime agent/model into RunStart units and backfilling on UnitStart. TUI latency relationship: prior unit 334 bounded TUI signal draining; this audit found no new TUI code change needed. Remaining upstream risk: mana ready_queue itself still accepts max_jobs=0 if invoked directly; imp wrapper now prevents it only for native tool calls.
.mana/archive/2026/05/336-audit-native-mana-run-dispatchrunstate-queue-stall.md:19:close_reason: 'Implemented and verified. Changes: clamp native mana run jobs to at least 1 to prevent jobs=0 from leaving queued units undispatched; populate run_state unit agent/model from normalized runtime metadata so queued/running rows show intended imp worker context. Verification passed: cargo test -p imp-core tools::mana::tests:: --quiet; cargo check -p imp-core -p imp-tui.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/336-audit-native-mana-run-dispatchrunstate-queue-stall.md:26:- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs
.mana/archive/2026/05/336-audit-native-mana-run-dispatchrunstate-queue-stall.md:32:  notes: 'Implemented and verified. Changes: clamp native mana run jobs to at least 1 to prevent jobs=0 from leaving queued units undispatched; populate run_state unit agent/model from normalized runtime metadata so queued/running rows show intended imp worker context. Verification passed: cargo test -p imp-core tools::mana::tests:: --quiet; cargo check -p imp-core -p imp-tui.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/336-audit-native-mana-run-dispatchrunstate-queue-stall.md:50:Audit imp's native mana run tool for a reported issue where dispatch/run_state leaves agents queued and not dispatched, with a related TUI keyboard latency bug. Scope: inspect crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs run/run_state/background dispatch code, mana orchestration usage, and crates/imp-tui event draining/render update paths. Identify root cause(s), propose or implement minimal fixes if clear, and verify with targeted tests/checks. Build on closed units 333 and 334 but do not assume their fixes solved this report.
.mana/332.1-design-imp-rss-registry-and-trusted-source-researc.md:26:- Migrated from root mana task 330.1 because this work belongs in `/Users/asher/imp` project mana.

.mana/archive/2026/05/44.1.6-sequence-phase-1-read-oriented-imp-code-intelligen.md:23:Externalize the implementation sequence from `docs/design/imp-read-oriented-code-intelligence-plan.md` into root mana so future work does not depend on reconstructing the phase order from the document alone.

.mana/archive/2026/05/44.1.6-sequence-phase-1-read-oriented-imp-code-intelligen.md:39:- The decomposition stays consistent with the plan doc's constraints: read-only phase, no write-oriented semantic actions required, no `mana` runtime orchestration ownership, no requirement to lock a single protocol now.

.mana/83-harden-imp-tui-text-box-cursor-and-bounds-handling.md:12:  Implemented a focused imp-tui text-box hardening pass. In `imp/crates/imp-tui/src/views/editor.rs`, added `clamp_cursor_to_boundary(...)`, normalized editor cursors before any insert/delete/slice/render math, and made `cursor_screen_position(...)` return safely for zero-sized/tiny rects using saturating bounds. In `imp/crates/imp-tui/src/views/ask_bar.rs`, normalized stale option cursors and UTF-8 input cursors, removed direct option indexing in tab-to-edit/confirm paths, kept `editor_cursor` synchronized after typed/backspace edits, and hardened cursor position math for tiny rects. In `imp/crates/imp-tui/src/app.rs`, guarded ask-tab selection with `.get(...)` instead of direct indexing. Also fixed unrelated compile drift so narrow verification could run: `imp/crates/imp-lua/src/sandbox.rs` and `imp/crates/imp-lua/src/lib.rs` now initialize the newer `ToolContext.turn_mana_review` field, and `imp/crates/imp-tui/src/app.rs` now matches/constructs `AgentEvent::TurnEnd { mana_review, .. }` correctly. Added focused regression tests for stale/out-of-range cursors, invalid UTF-8 boundaries, and tiny render areas in both editor and ask-bar modules.

.mana/33-chat-view-replace-duplicated-animation-logic-with.md:20:  2026-05-19: User pointed out same TTFT issue existed before; reviewed mana/commit history deeper. Found archived unit 41.7 “sort tool schemas for prompt cache stability” and commits around prompt caching. Anthropic build_tool_defs sorts tool definitions, but OpenAI Responses build_tool_defs did not, so request tool schema order could vary and bust provider prompt/cache reuse, matching repeated slow first-token for tiny prompts even after TUI input-path fixes. Added OpenAI tool definition sorting plus regression test openai_tool_defs_are_sorted_for_prompt_cache_stability. Kept OAuth fresh-token fast path and TUI startup fixes. Verified focused imp-llm/imp-tui tests and cargo check, then installed.

.mana/archive/2026/03/38-define.md:3:title: Define delegated child-job contract for imp-authored mana work
.mana/archive/2026/03/38-define.md:11:  The contract explains how imp should author child job descriptions for mana.
.mana/archive/2026/03/38-define.md:12:  It explicitly treats mana jobs as the delegated-worker substrate, not a second planning system.
.mana/archive/2026/03/38-define.md:20:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/03/38-define.md:30:We want imp to use mana jobs as its delegated-worker substrate instead of inventing a second planning system. Right now that intention exists conceptually, but we do not yet have a clear, reusable contract for what a good imp-authored child job description looks like.
.mana/archive/2026/03/38-define.md:33:Write the delegated child-job contract that imp should follow when creating mana child jobs.
.mana/archive/2026/03/38-define.md:53:- consistent with mana best practices for descriptions and verification
.mana/archive/2026/03/38-define.md:56:- Do **not** invent a new todo/planning model outside mana

.mana/archive/2026/04/50.27-define-tmux-as-an-optional-host-side-session-surfa.md:9:acceptance: 'Mana contains a durable architecture unit that makes the tmux conclusion inheritable cold: tmux is useful as an optional host-side execution/session surface for persistent observable local work, but the durable source of truth for task state, verification, and handoff remains in imp/mana. The unit explicitly records allowed uses, non-goals, ownership boundaries, and integration affordances.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.27-define-tmux-as-an-optional-host-side-session-surfa.md:15:  Also preserve the planning conclusion that tmux is most defensible as an outer operator/supervision primitive: use normal subprocess/tool execution for one-shot commands, mana for durable work state, and tmux only for interactive persistent terminal hosting where attachability and human observability matter. If future design work sketches integration, candidate affordances are session host lifecycle, attach/takeover, and capture of pane output into inspectable evidence rather than leaving the real state inside tmux.
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.27-define-tmux-as-an-optional-host-side-session-surfa.md:42:verify: test -f .mana/50.27-define-tmux-as-an-optional-host-side-session-surfa.md && rg -q '^id:' .mana/50.27-define-tmux-as-an-optional-host-side-session-surfa.md
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.27-define-tmux-as-an-optional-host-side-session-surfa.md:59:- The key architecture question is the boundary: what tmux may host versus what must remain owned by imp and mana.
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.27-define-tmux-as-an-optional-host-side-session-surfa.md:74:   - mana owns durable task state, evidence, verification outcomes, and resumable handoff
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.27-define-tmux-as-an-optional-host-side-session-surfa.md:83:- root mana units `50.25`, `50.26`, `248.17`
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.27-define-tmux-as-an-optional-host-side-session-surfa.md:91:- relationship to detached/background local execution and durable mana state

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-remove-imp-work-dependency-from-imp-core.md:18:  Implementation progress: removed `imp-work` as an imp-core dependency entirely. `work-integration` feature is gone; `tools::work` is no longer compiled/registered by imp-core. Prototype tool now always uses local DTOs plus no-op recording, so prototype experiments remain standalone. `crates/imp-core/src/tools/work.rs` remains as dead source in tree but is no longer module-reachable from imp-core; deleting/moving that large file is a follow-up. CLI still depends on imp-work and remains a later slice. Verified: `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core`; `cargo check -p imp-core --no-default-features`; `! cargo tree -p imp-core --no-default-features | rg 'imp-work'`; `cargo check -p imp-core --features mana-integration`; `cargo clippy -p imp-core --lib --features mana-integration -- -D warnings`.

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-remove-imp-work-dependency-from-imp-core.md:51:Move beyond optional `imp-work` in imp-core: remove the dependency from imp-core entirely where feasible, keeping prototype experimentation standalone and leaving durable work recording to mana/adapter layers. Do not delete the imp-work crate or broad CLI surfaces in this slice unless proven safe.
.mana/266.4-imp-vnext-ranked-roadmap-and-phased-execution-plan.md:9:acceptance: '`imp/vNext.md` exists, the ranked roadmap is visible in mana and in-repo docs, and follow-on phase epics/jobs are specific enough for cold execution.'
.mana/266.4-imp-vnext-ranked-roadmap-and-phased-execution-plan.md:22:  Concrete proposal: imp vNext remains the flagship Rust-native agent product on mana. Do not copy Hermes product breadth wholesale. Instead, harden the core loop first, then improve continuity and evals, then widen extension/host seams, and only then consider broader surface-area expansion.
.mana/266.4-imp-vnext-ranked-roadmap-and-phased-execution-plan.md:43:  - Completed child jobs archived immediately after close, so `mana show 266.4.1` / `266.4.2` no longer resolve directly even though the archive files exist under `.mana/archive/2026/04/`.
.mana/266.4-imp-vnext-ranked-roadmap-and-phased-execution-plan.md:48:  Conversation synthesis (2026-04-22): likely useful framing for imp improvement discussion. Emerging structure: (1) near-term hardening lane focused on provider/auth resilience, error handling, verification/evals, and storage/session reliability; (2) product/runtime lane focused on imp as the flagship human-facing agent environment on mana with explicit /ask -> /plan -> /work -> /improve workflow and stronger trust surfaces; (3) platform/boundary lane focused on clarifying mana as substrate/platform and imp as agent/product/runtime experience above it without hollowing imp into a thin client. Open question to resolve with user: is the next priority primarily making imp much more reliable at coding work right now, or shaping the longer-term product/platform thesis first and then sequencing implementation around that?
.mana/266.4-imp-vnext-ranked-roadmap-and-phased-execution-plan.md:52:  Priority clarification from user (2026-04-22): top interest is better autonomous work tools, especially confidence in runtime execution paths. User specifically called out uncertainty that `mana run` and `imp run` work well today. Implication: prioritize the worker/orchestration reliability lane ahead of broader product-surface exploration. Likely focus areas: canonical `imp run <id>` single-unit worker path, legacy `mana run` compatibility/orchestration path, run-state/log visibility, archive-aware reporting, and concrete proving/eval coverage for autonomous execution flows.
.mana/266.4-imp-vnext-ranked-roadmap-and-phased-execution-plan.md:56:  Near-term prioritization updated from conversation: before broader autonomous-tool expansion, add a Phase 0 lane focused on runtime trust for bounded autonomous work. Execution order should be: audit canonical `imp run <id>` worker path, audit `mana run` orchestration/compatibility path, define a compact proving matrix from observed behavior, then sequence the first small hardening fixes from evidence. Motivation: user specifically does not yet trust that `mana run` and `imp run` work well today.
.mana/266.4-imp-vnext-ranked-roadmap-and-phased-execution-plan.md:60:  Retry after fixing native archived-show divergence (2026-04-22): current shallow/open phase-0 work under 266.4 is now clearly represented by 266.4.10 (`mana run` orchestration audit) and 266.4.11 (autonomous runtime proving matrix). Historical unit 266.4.9 is no longer directly present in the active tree, so additional flattening should prefer keeping new/open work at this shallow sibling level rather than restoring deeper structure. The archived-show fix in 268.2 removed one source of confusion during cleanup; remaining active work can proceed without recreating a deeper phase-0 subtree.
.mana/266.4-imp-vnext-ranked-roadmap-and-phased-execution-plan.md:72:Create the concrete vNext planning artifacts and phased execution graph for imp based on the comparative review against hermes-agent and existing imp/mana architecture docs. This epic owns the public roadmap artifact (`imp/vNext.md`), the sequencing rationale, and the first pass of implementation-ready phase epics/jobs covering provider resilience, context continuity, extension/runtime seams, hostability, and evaluation/observability.
.mana/266.4-imp-vnext-ranked-roadmap-and-phased-execution-plan.md:82:- the mana graph contains execution-ready phase epics/jobs that another worker can pick up cold
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.1.8.1-define-imp-local-read-oriented-code-intelligence-c.md:57:- No LSP process management, no semantic backend implementation, no write-oriented actions.

Preserve the settled policy that raw payloads, live runtime state, per-turn diagnostics, and LSP orchestration remain out of scope for `mana`.

Compare imp current session/session_index/memory/compaction path against Hermes SessionDB+FTS5, ContextCompressor, MemoryManager/provider model, and skill injection patterns. Preserve imp's mana-first architecture; explicitly separate ideas that should stay in session memory/search from durable mana state.

.mana/archive/2026/04/304.8.2.3-implement-edit-schema-and-metadata-cleanup.md:17:  Native mana verify reports exit 1 for verify commands that pass via shell (`cargo test -p imp-core edit --no-run`, and combined edit/multi_edit no-run commands). Likely mana verify execution context/path issue or command parsing issue. Treat shell command evidence as current verification evidence; do not block indefinitely on mana verify anomaly.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-workflow-continue-recommendations-behind-work.md:20:  Implementation complete. Workflow-specific continue recommendation construction moved behind workflow integration: `agent/mod.rs::assess_post_turn` now calls `self.workflow_continue_recommendation(&workflow_signals)` for orchestration/durable workflow progress. `orchestration_follow_up_text` and mana workflow progress prompt construction live in `agent/workflow_integration.rs`. Failure recovery: first move broke `loop_policy.rs`/`turn_assessment.rs` references to `super::orchestration_follow_up_text`; fixed by re-exporting the function from the agent module with crate visibility. Verification passed: `mana verify 365.9`, `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work`, `cargo check -p imp-core`, direct acceptance checks confirming no `orchestration_follow_up_text(` or `workflow_progress_follow_up_text(` calls remain in `agent/mod.rs`, and no `mana_skill_follow_up_hint` refs remain in `run_loop.rs`. Active native work scope inspected: `work next` reports 0 ready task(s).
.mana/339-replace-imp-native-mana-run-cli-embedding-with-cor.md:3:title: Replace imp native mana run CLI embedding with core planning and direct subagent runtime
.mana/339-replace-imp-native-mana-run-cli-embedding-with-cor.md:4:slug: replace-imp-native-mana-run-cli-embedding-with-cor
.mana/339-replace-imp-native-mana-run-cli-embedding-with-cor.md:9:acceptance: Native imp mana orchestration no longer depends on mana-cli command modules for prompt assembly, dispatch, signal handling, or stream capture; mana-core owns graph/config/planning/context primitives; imp-core owns subagent runtime launch/cancellation/events; CLI compatibility remains available only as external CLI behavior; targeted mana-core, imp-core, and TUI checks pass.
.mana/339-replace-imp-native-mana-run-cli-embedding-with-cor.md:12:- mana
.mana/339-replace-imp-native-mana-run-cli-embedding-with-cor.md:14:verify: 'cd /Users/asher/mana && cargo test -p mana-core && cd /Users/asher/imp && cargo test -p imp-core tools::mana::tests:: --quiet && cargo check -p imp-core -p imp-tui'
.mana/339-replace-imp-native-mana-run-cli-embedding-with-cor.md:17:- /Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-core
.mana/339-replace-imp-native-mana-run-cli-embedding-with-cor.md:18:- /Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run
.mana/339-replace-imp-native-mana-run-cli-embedding-with-cor.md:19:- /Users/asher/imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs
.mana/339-replace-imp-native-mana-run-cli-embedding-with-cor.md:20:- /Users/asher/imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs
.mana/339-replace-imp-native-mana-run-cli-embedding-with-cor.md:26:Goal: replace imp's native mana run implementation with a core/runtime boundary instead of embedding mana-cli. Current state: crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs calls mana::commands::run::run_with_stream_capture_and_sink from the mana-cli crate. That imports CLI concerns into a long-lived imp/TUI host, including process-global signal handlers and shutdown state; this caused queued jobs=4 runs where planning/run_state could show queued units but dispatch refused to spawn workers after stale shutdown state. Architecture direction: mana-core should provide target resolution, index/config loading, dependency/ready planning, retry/guardrail decisions, and worker context/prompt assembly APIs. imp-core should launch subagents directly with explicit runtime config (model/thinking inheritance, cwd/mana_dir, tool policy, cancellation), emit structured run events, and update TUI/widgets without CLI stdout/stderr or signal side effects. Non-goals: do not remove mana CLI compatibility; do not rewrite existing unit graph storage; do not mechanically rename legacy docs/code outside touched paths.
.mana/archive/2026/05/360-migrate-imp-workflows-from-mana-compatibility-to-i.md:3:title: Migrate imp workflows from mana compatibility to imp-work

.mana/349-rebuild-imp-agent-loop-around-explicit-turn-state.md:17:  Second implementation slice: fully removed `max_turns` from the live agent loop and primary agent/session/builder/CLI/TUI wiring. The loop no longer has a turn-count stop guard; long-running work is bounded by explicit decisions, cancellation, runtime failures, timeouts where callers impose them, and semantic final status. Added initial `loop_policy.rs` seam with `DefaultLoopPolicy` translating post-turn `NextAction` into `LoopDecision`; run_loop now consumes `LoopDecision` rather than directly interpreting `NextAction`. Removed max-turn tests/CLI arg/TUI override/settings field and ask_agent profile turn caps. Remaining `max_turns` references are config compatibility / unrelated mana run option names / system prompt fixtures, not Agent::run enforcement. Verified with `cargo fmt -p imp-core -p imp-cli -p imp-tui`, `cargo check -p imp-core`, and `cargo check -p imp-cli -p imp-tui`.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:3:title: Define mana↔imp contract implications of file-native memory architecture
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:4:slug: define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:9:acceptance: 'A root contract/decomposition unit defines how the memory architecture affects mana↔imp contracts: typed durable object families, required scope/provenance metadata, derived-vs-canonical boundaries, and retrieval/compaction responsibilities across the boundary.'
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:17:  Current mana↔imp boundary delta to preserve: current retrieval and handoff are largely unit/fact-oriented (`memory_context` assembles warnings, working_on, relevant_facts, recent_work directly from unit records and fact units). The proposed boundary instead assembles compact runtime context from derived bootstrap/current-state views backed by canonical durable objects, especially checkpoints, claims, lessons, runbooks, and evidence refs. This is a shift from unit-as-container to object-graph-as-truth with materialized current state.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:21:  Subagent/runtime implication from current handoff inspection: today `imp` already uses mana as the canonical worker-assignment substrate via `imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs` (`load_assignment*`, `build_task_context`), while `mana/crates/mana-core/src/prompt.rs` still provides a legacy prompt-shaped compatibility bundle. `imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs` remains the real final prompt assembler. The object-native memory migration should strengthen this split rather than blur it: mana should hand subagents structured task packets plus object-backed inherited context (checkpoints, evidence refs, claims/lessons, receipts, derived bootstrap/current-state views), while imp continues to own final prompt wording, token budgeting, and live runtime behavior.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:25:  Concrete decomposition from the current handoff/runtime discussion: treat the existing `WorkerAssignment -> TaskContext -> imp system prompt` seam as the migration anchor rather than inventing a second prompt center. Current seam today is unit-centric: `imp_core::mana_worker::load_assignment*()` loads unit fields and attempt summaries from mana, `build_task_context()` normalizes them, and `imp` assembles the final prompt in `system_prompt.rs`. Target seam should be object-backed but presentation-neutral: mana hands imp a specialized task packet composed of assignment identity plus inherited durable context such as latest semantic checkpoint, compact bootstrap/current-state view, relevant evidence refs, accepted/current claim summaries, promoted lessons/runbooks, receipt summaries, dependency/open-decision context, and fallback unit-native fields where object-native memory is absent. Final prompt wording, token budgeting, and selective reveal stay in imp.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:29:  Current blocking-model implication from repo inspection: mana today already has derived blocking for dependencies/requires (`mana-core/src/blocking.rs`) and durable unresolved-decision blocking via `Unit.decisions`, but it lacks a general first-class durable field for non-derivable impediments another worker should inherit cold. A future `blockers` section/field would make sense if it is additive and structured: keep computed blockers derived, keep `decisions` semantically distinct, and use a blocker field only for explicit non-derivable impediments such as external access, environment breakage, policy waits, or human responses.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:33:  Cross-project contract implication externalized from blocker discussion: an explicit blocker field should be treated as durable coordination/handoff state, not as a replacement for computed scheduler blocking. If introduced, it belongs on the mana unit contract for non-derivable impediments another worker should inherit cold, and imp/subagent handoff can later surface it as inherited context without requiring scheduler behavior to change immediately.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:37:  Cross-project handoff implication from the blocker planning that should survive this session: explicit blockers are now modeled primarily as pre-decomposition human-in-the-loop inheritance, not just scheduler blockedness. Once the blocker minimal slice lands, mana→imp task packets and future subagent handoff work should expect durable blocker entries as part of inherited planning context, especially on parent/spec units that should remain undecomposed until important questions are answered. This should be treated as context/handoff visibility first; scheduler integration remains intentionally postponed in the first blocker rollout.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:45:  Cross-project durable plan from the current discussion: blocker rollout now has a two-step contract shape. Step 1 adds blocker entries as inherited planning context for mana→imp handoff and operator visibility without changing scheduler behavior. Step 2 later migrates the current unresolved-decision dispatch gate to blocker-based gating. Keep the separate feature human-close path distinct as an end-of-lifecycle human-signoff policy unless intentionally redesigned.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:57:  Root contract update from the current discussion: blocker rollout is now understood to redistribute manual responsibility across the lifecycle. Early blocker visibility and later blocker-based gating take over much of the human-in-the-loop burden that the current `feature: true` close gate has been carrying too coarsely. As a result, the mana↔imp contract should expect future human-close policy to be driven by richer review/heuristic inputs rather than `feature` alone, while remaining distinct from blockers as an end-of-lifecycle signoff layer.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:61:  Cross-project policy clarification from the current discussion: although the human-close redesign may consider richer signals than `feature`, the strongest driver should be explicit review rather than automated heuristic classification. This matters for the mana↔imp contract because any future close-time review requirement should likely be represented as explicit durable policy/review state in mana, not inferred implicitly by imp or by a purely automatic heuristic.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:65:  Cross-project durable plan update: the mana↔imp contract should expect future close-time human review requirements to be represented as explicit durable review/policy state in mana, not inferred mainly from `feature` or from automated heuristics. Blockers own earlier clarification/gating responsibilities; explicit review remains the strongest driver of human-only close as an end-of-lifecycle policy surface.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:69:  Contract clarification from the current discussion: explicit review state should drive the explicit human-close path, not ordinary closure. Mana↔imp contract work should preserve the distinction between (a) normal verify-close, which remains acceptable and expected, and (b) explicit human-close, which should be requested/required by explicit durable review policy rather than inferred from the old `feature` boolean.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:73:  Cross-project contract clarification now fixed in the durable plan: mana should distinguish between ordinary verify-close and explicit human-close. Explicit review state should drive only the explicit human-close path, not normal closure. This keeps the mana↔imp contract honest: blockers and review policy can shape explicit human-close or earlier gating, but verify-close remains the expected default when no explicit human-only close requirement exists.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:77:  Cross-project execution-order update from the current discussion. Before resuming broad mana↔imp object-native memory contract work, finish the blocker and explicit human-close design docs that are already semantically settled: `247.3.7`, `245.1.2.1`, `245.1.2.4`, `245.1.2.5`. This will stabilize what inherited blocker context means and how explicit human-close differs from ordinary verify-close before the vNext handoff-packet and broader memory-contract docs are written.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:81:  Durable execution checkpoint before continuing: the blocker/human-close clarification sequence has advanced to the explicit-review-state step. Completed so far in this cluster: blocker minimal slice (`docs/architecture/mana-blockers-minimal-slice.md`) and blockers vs explicit human-close responsibility split (`docs/architecture/human-close-responsibility-split.md`). Immediate next execution target is `245.1.2.4`, which should define explicit review as first-class durable policy/state in mana for the explicit human-close path only, while preserving verify-close as the normal path and blockers as distinct earlier-lifecycle state.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:88:verify: test -f .mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md && rg -q '^id:' .mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:95:- Use the existing `WorkerAssignment -> TaskContext -> imp system prompt` seam as the anchor for object-native memory migration. Evolve the assignment packet to carry richer presentation-neutral inherited context from mana, but keep final prompt wording and token budgeting in imp.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:96:- When blocker support becomes part of the mana contract, its first cross-project value is inherited planning context for imp and subagents, especially around pre-decomposition human-in-the-loop questions. Do not tie that value to scheduler integration in the first rollout; visibility and inheritance come first.
.mana/245.1-define-manaimp-contract-implications-of-file-nativ.md:101:Write a root architecture/contract note that translates the file-native memory model into mana↔imp contract implications. The goal is not full implementation but a precise decomposition another worker can execute cold.

.mana/archive/2026/05/313.1-improve-mana-unit-parent-placement-hygiene.md:3:title: Improve mana unit parent placement hygiene
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.1-improve-mana-unit-parent-placement-hygiene.md:4:slug: improve-mana-unit-parent-placement-hygiene
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.1-improve-mana-unit-parent-placement-hygiene.md:9:acceptance: Agents create new mana units under the correct epic/scope instead of the currently active-but-unrelated epic. When a new issue emerges during a task, the agent either attaches it to an existing matching epic, creates a properly scoped epic, or records it as a note if it is not actionable yet. Tests or documented workflow checks demonstrate the placement guidance catches mis-scoped units like closure reliability being placed under native tool schema audit.
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.1-improve-mana-unit-parent-placement-hygiene.md:13:  Implemented parent placement hygiene first fix in `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs`: `mana create` schema now exposes optional `parent_reason`; create output details include `parent_reason` and `placement` object. If parent is set without parent_reason, placement includes warning `parent_reason_missing` and hint to confirm the work belongs under that parent rather than the current active epic. If parent_reason is supplied, placement records it and warning is null. Mana delta widget now includes parent reason or `parent_reason missing`. Added tests for placement warning/explicit reason and schema. Verify passed: `cargo test -p imp-core parent_placement -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana_schema -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run`.

The stored verify previously failed due to unrelated broad mana test failures (`mana_prompt_context::maps_memory_context_to_bounded_prompt_facts` and `update_supports_acceptance_labels_and_decisions`), but equivalent focused verification now passes: `cargo test -p imp-core parent_placement -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana_schema -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run`. This is using the new closure reliability path: force close includes equivalent verify evidence and reason.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.1-improve-mana-unit-parent-placement-hygiene.md:210:Bug/design gap discovered in conversation: agents are too quick to attach newly discovered work to the current active epic, even when the issue belongs to a different product/workflow area. Example: `304.14 Fix mana unit closure reliability after implementation` was initially created under `304 Audit and harden imp tool schemas`, but it actually belongs under a broader imp/mana workflow reliability epic. Need improve imp/mana guidance and/or tool affordances so agents choose parents deliberately.
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.1-improve-mana-unit-parent-placement-hygiene.md:212:Potential directions: mana create validation/hints when parent labels/title mismatch child title/labels; an explicit `scope` or `parent_reason` field; agent completion/creation checklist requiring `why this parent?`; native `mana guide topic=task` additions; or tree/status surfacing suspicious parent-child mismatches. Keep solution lightweight and not annoying.
.mana/archive/2026/03/229.14-imp-core-system-prompt-assembly.md:71:**Layer 4: Mana facts** (if .mana/ exists)
.mana/archive/2026/03/229.14-imp-core-system-prompt-assembly.md:77:All project facts from mana-core, with verification timestamps.
.mana/archive/2026/03/229.14-imp-core-system-prompt-assembly.md:100:    facts: &[Fact],        // from mana-core, empty if no .mana/
.mana/archive/2026/03/229.14-imp-core-system-prompt-assembly.md:112:- Missing layers handled gracefully (no .mana/ → skip Layer 4, no task → skip Layer 5)
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.2-add-compact-native-mana-guidetemplate-actions.md:3:title: Add compact native mana guide/template actions
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.2-add-compact-native-mana-guidetemplate-actions.md:4:slug: add-compact-native-mana-guidetemplate-actions
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.2-add-compact-native-mana-guidetemplate-actions.md:9:acceptance: Native mana provides compact on-demand guide/template actions sufficient for routine agent use. Topics include overview, task, epic, decision, notes, verify/red-green, orchestrate/waves, and worker_context. Output is concise and structured. Default prompt guidance can point agents to these actions.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.2-add-compact-native-mana-guidetemplate-actions.md:11:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.2-add-compact-native-mana-guidetemplate-actions.md:16:close_reason: 'Completed in commit cb929cb `Add mana guide and template actions`. Evidence: native mana has guide/template actions, topics overview/task/epic/decision/notes/verify/orchestrate/worker_context, template kinds epic/task/fact, compact structured outputs, and tests. Verification passed: `cargo test -p imp-core mana_guide -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana_template -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana_schema -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run`.'

.mana/archive/2026/05/44-define-memory-and-code-intelligence-architecture-f.md:12:  2026-05-20 direction shift: user wants to reimagine mana from the ground up inside imp, likely as an imp-work crate, removing mana as an imp dependency while carrying forward the best ideas. Key golden-path requirement: every task should own an associated prepared context window/packet so subagents can start instantly with task-specific context, save exploration tokens/time, and maximize prompt-cache hits. imp-work should facilitate orchestration of hundreds of low-memory subagents.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44-define-memory-and-code-intelligence-architecture-f.md:20:  2026-05-20 imp-work continuation update: resolved the remaining agent test blocker by aligning `emits_turn_assessment_event_for_execution_blocker` with intended behavior: failed bash/check commands now produce an execution-debt recovery continuation instead of immediate `execution_blocked`. Also kept mana close/verify out of generic work-completed detection and routed it through explicit mana workflow progress follow-up. Verification now passed: `cargo test -p imp-core agent:: -- --nocapture` (108 passed), `cargo test -p imp-work` (57 passed), `cargo test -p imp-core work_tool -- --nocapture` (27 passed), `cargo test -p imp-core prototype -- --nocapture` (9 passed), `cargo fmt --check` passed. Existing unrelated warnings remain in `crates/imp-core/src/import.rs` for duplicate test attributes/dead helper.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44-define-memory-and-code-intelligence-architecture-f.md:33:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/05/44-define-memory-and-code-intelligence-architecture-f.md:56:Goal: turn two major open architecture questions into concrete, research-backed design work for Tower: (1) what agentic memory should mean in mana, and (2) how native code intelligence tools should fit into the mana ↔ imp contract.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44-define-memory-and-code-intelligence-architecture-f.md:60:- Root unit `28.3` already tracks memory/retrieval consolidation across mana and imp, but it does not yet explicitly include comparative research against existing agent memory systems.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44-define-memory-and-code-intelligence-architecture-f.md:62:- The product direction suggests hybrid systems: agents should use real deterministic code tools where they help, while mana persists only the durable parts that should survive a run.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44-define-memory-and-code-intelligence-architecture-f.md:66:- Tower has a clear position on LSP/diagnostics/linters/AST/code-intelligence tools and their boundary with mana.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44-define-memory-and-code-intelligence-architecture-f.md:71:- cross-project mana ↔ imp boundary implications
.mana/28.5.7.1-add-shared-imp-core-turnmanadelta-renderer-and-man.md:3:title: Add shared imp-core TurnManaDelta renderer and mana review mode config
.mana/28.5.7.1-add-shared-imp-core-turnmanadelta-renderer-and-man.md:4:slug: add-shared-imp-core-turnmanadelta-renderer-and-man

.mana/28.5.7.1-add-shared-imp-core-turnmanadelta-renderer-and-man.md:24:Add the shared imp-core formatting/config seam for rendering canonical between-turn mana review packets before wiring any frontend-specific surface.
.mana/28.5.7.1-add-shared-imp-core-turnmanadelta-renderer-and-man.md:27:Keep TUI and CLI/RPC as adapters over one presentation-neutral TurnManaDelta rendering policy and one user control setting.

.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.3-define-the-first-implementation-slice-after-the-wo.md:12:  Repo-grounded contract recommendation after inspecting `mana-core/src/ops/close.rs`, `mana-review/src/types.rs`, `tower-contracts/src/lib.rs`, `imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs`, and worker-status consumers:

.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.3-define-the-first-implementation-slice-after-the-wo.md:61:  - `tower-contracts` owns the cold-reference vocabulary so imp/mana/runner/reviewer can all point at the same lineage without sharing storage internals.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.3-define-the-first-implementation-slice-after-the-wo.md:64:  - `mana_worker::run_verify_command()` and `map_verify_failed_close_outcome()` show the current raw materials for `VerifierResult`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.3-define-the-first-implementation-slice-after-the-wo.md:65:  - `mana-core::VerifyFailureResult` maps naturally to a failed `VerifierResult` plus attached verify-output artifact ref.

.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.3-define-the-first-implementation-slice-after-the-wo.md:73:  - Convert mixed consumers gradually: code that only needs worker/evidence contracts should import from `tower_contracts` directly; execution helpers remain in `mana_worker`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.3-define-the-first-implementation-slice-after-the-wo.md:77:  - targeted tests around `mana_worker` close/verify mapping and any new `tower-contracts` serialization/shape tests
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.3-define-the-first-implementation-slice-after-the-wo.md:84:  2026-04-23 resumed and reverified. This unit is analysis/planning-only and its acceptance is already satisfied by the repo-grounded contract recommendation recorded in the unit notes. The verify gate still passes: `cd /Users/asher/tower && test -f tower-contracts/src/lib.rs && test -f imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs`. The unit now has a concrete first-slice recommendation: keep the existing worker quartet, add narrow reference-first verification/evidence vocabulary (`VerifierStatus`, `ArtifactKind`, `ArtifactRef`, `VerifierResult`, `EvidenceBundleRef`), keep `CloseEvidence` / `VerifyFailureResult` / review records mana-local for now, and use `imp_core::mana_worker` as the first compatibility adapter seam. No new code change was required on resume.

.mana/331.4-fix-native-mana-run-zero-worker-dispatch-regressio.md:3:title: Fix native mana run zero-worker dispatch regression
.mana/331.4-fix-native-mana-run-zero-worker-dispatch-regressio.md:4:slug: fix-native-mana-run-zero-worker-dispatch-regressio
.mana/331.4-fix-native-mana-run-zero-worker-dispatch-regressio.md:9:acceptance: Root cause is identified and fixed so native mana(action="run", targets=[...]) dispatches a leaf task worker instead of finishing with 0 total. A regression test or focused verification covers explicit target dispatch.
.mana/331.4-fix-native-mana-run-zero-worker-dispatch-regressio.md:13:  Reproduced zero-worker dispatch: `mana(action="run", targets=["374"], jobs=1)` created run-4 but run_state finished with 0 total; then dry-run against claimed `331.4` also finished with 0 total. Inspection showed the active native tool behavior was handing explicit targets to the scheduler in a way that produced an empty plan. Current source already contains the intended fix (`core_target_from_native` -> `mana_core::api::RunTarget`, `compute_run_plan(&core_target, ...)`) and depends on local mana-core/mana-cli path crates where that target-aware API exists. Added regression coverage `run_explicit_target_plans_leaf_task`, which dry-runs explicit target ["1"] and asserts run_state summary total_units == 1. Also updated an IndexEntry test fixture for the local mana-core handle field. Verified focused tests: `cargo test -p imp-core run_supports_explicit_targets -- --nocapture` and `cargo test -p imp-core run_explicit_target_plans_leaf_task -- --nocapture` pass. Note: the currently running imp session still exhibits old behavior until rebuilt/restarted with this source.
.mana/331.4-fix-native-mana-run-zero-worker-dispatch-regressio.md:16:- mana
.mana/331.4-fix-native-mana-run-zero-worker-dispatch-regressio.md:20:verify: cargo test -p imp-core mana_run
.mana/331.4-fix-native-mana-run-zero-worker-dispatch-regressio.md:27:- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs
.mana/331.4-fix-native-mana-run-zero-worker-dispatch-regressio.md:28:- /Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-core/src/ops/run.rs
.mana/331.4-fix-native-mana-run-zero-worker-dispatch-regressio.md:29:- /Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/mod.rs
.mana/331.4-fix-native-mana-run-zero-worker-dispatch-regressio.md:37:User reported mana run is not dispatching workers. Reproduced with disposable unit creation: native mana create returned id 374, but `mana(action="run", targets=["374"], jobs=1)` returned run-4 and finished with `0 total · 0 done · 0 failed`. `mana show 374` then failed in the current imp project scope, and rg found no 374 file under /Users/asher/imp/.mana, suggesting scope/root mismatch may also exist. Code inspection found the native imp mana tool builds a `RunTarget::Explicit` from targets, but `run_native_imp_orchestration()` collapses it through `core_filter_from_native()` into `Option<&str>` and calls `mana_run_core::compute_run_plan(&mana_dir, core_filter, dry_run)`. The currently published mana-core 0.3.1 API treats a filter id as parent-if-any-child-exists, otherwise exact id; a created disposable top-level id should match, so the 0-unit result likely comes from either wrong mana_dir resolution for create vs run/show or stale index/target filtering. Investigate both, fix the native tool/runtime contract, and verify with a disposable unit.
.mana/50.25-specify-detachedbackground-local-execution-contrac.md:20:Write a rebuild-spec doc for detached/background local execution in CLI-first imp, explicitly sequenced after stable output modes, browse/search surfaces, and approval policy. Ground it in the current runner/runtime proposals and the CLI-affordance sequence note. The doc should define the minimum contract for `imp run --detach` or equivalent: run identity, list/status/reattach/log inspection, structured intermediate/final states, approval-required / awaiting-input / waiting states, and how detached local work relates to mana-backed unit execution versus plain session-backed work. Explain why this should not be implemented as shelling-out convenience ahead of the shared runtime/output/policy seams. Keep scope architectural/spec-level, not implementation.
.mana/50.25-specify-detachedbackground-local-execution-contrac.md:36:- imp/docs/proposals/imp-run-worker-contract-and-mana-run-handoff.md
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:3:title: Audit and design first-class mana integration in the imp agent loop
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:4:slug: audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:9:acceptance: Produce an evidence-backed audit of the current imp agent loop and a concrete integration design for treating mana as a first-class loop substrate, including action-class policy, current hook points, risks, and an implementation decomposition with verify gates. No implementation changes are required in this unit unless explicitly approved later.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:18:  - agent.rs is 3342 lines of implementation plus tests; public agent API, event types, run loop, tool execution, post-turn assessment, mana follow-up heuristics, bash→mana guard, and tests all live together.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:19:  - Agent::run builds context/options, streams provider events into assistant content/tool_calls, executes tools, appends ToolResult messages, finalizes TurnManaReview, assesses post-turn state, and queues follow-up user messages.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:20:  - Tool execution has generic mode guard, hook guard, repeated-call guard, schema validation, bash mana-equivalent guard, and ToolContext injection. Mana is not currently pre-classified before execution beyond generic tool name/args handling.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:21:  - ManaTool exposes action in params/details and enforces AgentMode::allows_mana_action internally after allowing guide/template. It returns ToolOutput details with action for mutations and some read actions; post-turn heuristics inspect result.details.action.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:22:  - TurnManaReviewAccumulator currently only begins/finalizes in agent.rs; mutation push sites were not found in tools/mana.rs via rg, so review state may depend on missing/incomplete instrumentation or be effectively inert outside tests.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:23:  - Existing mana-specific loop behavior is mostly heuristic: mana_skill_follow_up_hint pre-turn nudge, externalization follow-up if assistant text looks like planning but did not call mana, execution debt after mana create/update/notes/decision/dep/claim without execution evidence, confidence continue after mana call plus positive text, planner stop on changed mana review, and bash mana CLI blocking.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:25:  - User wants design to include splitting agent.rs into multiple files while exploring deeper mana integration.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:31:  - 330.2 mana action classification vocabulary.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:32:  - 330.3 pre-execution ManaLoopPolicy hook.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:33:  - 330.4 TurnManaReview mutation instrumentation audit/fix.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:34:  - 330.5 post-turn assessment rewrite using structured mana evidence.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:39:  Reviewed prior mana tool work under archived epic 304. Relevant thread: 304.6 Review native mana tool schema and 304.6.4 Rework native mana tool into self-explaining executable spec interface. Key inherited decisions for 330: keep a single native mana tool; make it self-explaining through compact canonical schema, action-specific validation/errors, guide/template actions, worker context assembly, and orchestration recovery UX; do not dump long doctrine into JSON schema; reduce routine reliance on the mana skill without abruptly deleting it; preserve mana as a spec-driven work graph with acceptance/verify/fail_first and wave-based subagent orchestration. Implication for 330: this unit should operate at the runtime/agent-loop layer, not reopen the already-closed schema/tool-interface work. The 330 mana action classification should use the 304.6 action groups while keeping one tool name and classifying by `mana.action`.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:43:  Mana skill loaded via read on explicit user instruction before execution. Current intent: review/update child units if needed, then start unblocked mana tasks. Integration design clarified: preserve a single native mana tool, add loop-level classification and policy around `mana.action`, use structured mana evidence in post-turn assessment, and make runtime/tool support strong enough that routine operation no longer needs the heavy mana skill. Dependency plan to encode: 330.1 should remain first for behavior-preserving split; 330.2 can follow and is prerequisite for 330.3/330.5; 330.4 can proceed independently or after split; 330.5 should depend on 330.2 and 330.4.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:47:  Mana skill loaded via read at user instruction before continuing. User asks for a concise thesis for mana and to finish the 330 epic by ensuring the design/decomposition is complete. Follow mana guidance: durable thesis/plan belongs in this unit; do not create new implementation work unless the design reveals a missing worker-ready task.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:51:  Final thesis for mana/imp loop design:
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:52:  - Mana is imp's durable execution substrate, not a chat memory or a spec document. It stores work state that should outlive the turn: goals, units, dependencies, decisions, notes, facts, verify gates, attempts, runs, and recovery evidence.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:53:  - The native `mana` tool remains one tool, but the agent loop must understand `mana.action` semantically. The loop should classify actions as read/help, inspect, checkpoint, graph mutation, decision/fact, lifecycle/verify/close, orchestration/run, or destructive.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:55:  - The loop should not reflexively create/update mana for every plan-like sentence. It should write mana only when durable project/work state changed, when handoff/recovery/parallelism matters, or when orchestration needs explicit units.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:56:  - Mana writes are usually checkpoints, not completion. Progress checkpoints and graph mutations should often make the loop continue; verify/close/orchestration success are stronger evidence for stopping.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:63:  Final thesis for mana/imp loop design:
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:64:  - Mana is imp's durable execution substrate, not chat memory or a monolithic spec document. It stores work state that should outlive the turn: goals, units, dependencies, decisions, notes, facts, verify gates, attempts, runs, and recovery evidence.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:65:  - The native `mana` tool remains one tool, but the agent loop must understand `mana.action` semantically. The loop should classify actions as read/help, inspect, checkpoint, graph mutation, decision/fact, lifecycle/verify/close, orchestration/run, or destructive.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:67:  - The loop should not reflexively create/update mana for every plan-like sentence. It should write mana only when durable project/work state changed, when handoff/recovery/parallelism matters, or when orchestration needs explicit units.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:68:  - Mana writes are usually checkpoints, not completion. Progress checkpoints and graph mutations should often make the loop continue; verify/close/orchestration success are stronger evidence for stopping.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:78:  crates/imp-core/src/config.rs:    pub fn allowed_mana_actions(&self) -> &'static [&'static str] {
