.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:88:  - 330.3 closed: pre-execution ManaLoopPolicy/classification hook, structured policy details on mana tool results.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:89:  - 330.4 closed: agent run loop records successful mana mutation results into TurnManaReview before TurnEnd.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:90:  - 330.5 closed: post-turn assessment uses ManaActionClass for mana execution debt/evidence and externalization nudge now requires durable-state/explicit-mana signals rather than generic planning wording. System prompt updated with anti-overuse and checkpoint-vs-completion mana guidance.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:91:  Verification passed: `cargo check -p imp-core`, `cargo test -p imp-core mana_action_classification`, `cargo test -p imp-core mana_review`, `cargo test -p imp-core mana_externalization`, `cargo test -p imp-core execution_debt`, `cargo test -p imp-core agent:: --no-run`. Note: repo has unrelated pre-existing dirty files outside this work, and previous agent split files are staged/dirty in the workspace.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:94:- mana
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:97:verify: rg "struct PostTurnAssessment|pub async fn run|impl Tool for ManaTool|allowed_mana_actions|register_native_tools" crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs crates/imp-core/src/config.rs crates/imp-core/src/builder.rs
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:109:    crates/imp-core/src/config.rs:    pub fn allowed_mana_actions(&self) -> &'static [&'static str] {
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:110:    crates/imp-core/src/config.rs:            _ => self.allowed_mana_actions().contains(&action),
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:111:    crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs:impl Tool for ManaTool {
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:117:- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:123:- 'Runtime direction: stronger mana runtime/tool integration should make the heavy mana skill unnecessary for routine operation. The goal is not merely schema polish; mana should become strong enough as native runtime substrate for imp to perform and orchestrate long-running tasks autonomously. Preserve a single native mana tool, but move routine doctrine into runtime policy, structured tool affordances, guide/template actions, worker context assembly, run recovery, and loop-level mana action semantics. Any remaining mana skill should be optional deep reference, not required default context.'
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:124:- 'Clarification for mana loop plan: the problem is not meta-discussion specifically. Meta-discussion was only an example of over-eager mana creation. Runtime policy should address general overuse: imp should create/update mana when durable project/work state has actually changed or when mana-backed orchestration is needed, not as reflexive bookkeeping for every plan-like sentence, small one-pass task, or conversational aside. The mana loop should distinguish observation, checkpoint, graph mutation, lifecycle/verification, and orchestration actions, then use those classes to decide whether to continue, stop, ask, or suppress unnecessary externalization pressure.'
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:140:Goal: audit how the imp agent loop currently treats mana and design a stronger first-class integration model that considers the full mana tool surface, not just generic tool usage.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:143:- A minimal prompt tweak was added in `crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs`: `Treat mana updates as checkpoints when useful, not necessarily completion.` The user then identified a live failure: the assistant created an unnecessary mana task while discussing avoiding unnecessary mana writes.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:144:- The user’s hypothesis: this is not mainly a context/prompt problem; it is an agent loop problem. Mana should probably be core to the agent loop because mana is intended as first-class in imp.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:146:- The mana skill was loaded with `read`; follow it for design/decomposition/handoff.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:149:- `crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs` contains `Agent::run`, `PostTurnAssessment`, `NextAction`, continue/debt heuristics, mana-specific text/tool-result helpers, and follow-up logic.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:150:- `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` contains the native mana tool implementation and many actions including read-only, graph mutation, fact, run/orchestration, close/delete/verify actions.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:151:- `crates/imp-core/src/config.rs` contains `AgentMode`, mode-specific allowed mana actions, and `ContinuePolicy`.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:156:2. How are mana calls currently represented in tool results/details? Is the mana action available to the loop for policy decisions, or only as opaque tool args/details?
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:157:3. What mana-specific logic already exists (`assistant_message_contains_mana_tool_call`, `mana_review_stop_reason`, execution debt, externalization follow-up, mode allowed actions)?
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:158:4. Where should a first-class mana loop layer live: before tool execution, after tool results, during context assembly, post-turn assessment, or all of these?
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:159:5. How should mana action classes be defined and enforced?
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:163:- Classify mana actions by side-effect and purpose, e.g. read/help, inspection, progress notes, graph mutation, decisions/facts, lifecycle/claim/close/verify, orchestration/run, destructive/delete.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:164:- Add a loop-level `ManaActionPolicy` or `ManaLoopPolicy` that evaluates action, mode, active task context, user intent/current turn kind, and whether the conversation is meta-discussing mana behavior.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:166:- Avoid creating mana units merely as completion bookkeeping for small local one-pass changes.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:167:- Let the mana tool schema/guide carry detailed usage guidance; keep system prompt minimal.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:177:- Removing the mana skill from prompt assembly unless identified as a later task.
.mana/330-audit-and-design-first-class-mana-integration-in-t.md:178:- Changing mana CLI/core semantics outside imp unless clearly necessary.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:20:  Open naming question from user: `delegate` is likely not the right verb for the imp tool. Candidate direction: preferred CLI surface `imp work <id>` for headless mana-unit execution, with the native imp tool reworded around running/spawning another imp worker rather than delegating. Treat as an unresolved vocabulary/UX decision to settle before the surface refactor lands.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:56:- 'New naming candidate from user: the headless unit-worker CLI surface may be better expressed as `imp mana <id>` because the runtime is imp acting on mana-shaped work. Do not proceed into the shared runtime-connection refactor (`28.1.5.3.2`) until the CLI naming/vocabulary decision is settled.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:57:- 'Naming evaluation after reviewing current repo state: `imp mana <id>` is semantically attractive because it frames imp as the runtime operating on mana-shaped work, and the repo already treats the native mana surface as first-class. However, a bare `imp mana <id>` form may paint us into a corner if we later want a real `imp mana ...` namespace for multiple mana-aware CLI operations (`status`, `show`, `logs`, etc.). Provisional recommendation: prefer a namespaced form such as `imp mana work <id>` or `imp mana run <id>` for extensibility, with room for a sugar alias later if desired. Do not proceed with 28.1.5.3.2 until the top-level CLI naming is settled.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:58:- 'CLI naming direction is leaning toward `imp mana <id>` as the canonical single-unit worker entrypoint. Reasoning: it expresses imp as the runtime operating on mana-shaped work while remaining extensible if the `imp mana` parser treats reserved verbs (`status`, `show`, `logs`, etc.) as subcommands and otherwise interprets the first positional token as a unit id. Caveat: this stays clean as long as unit IDs do not collide with reserved verb names; current observed unit IDs are numeric/dotted, so that is acceptable for now.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:59:- 'Refined naming direction after user clarification: `imp mana <id>` appears extensible enough if the CLI parser reserves a small set of verbs (`status`, `show`, `logs`, etc.) under the `imp mana` namespace and otherwise interprets the first positional token as a unit id. This would let `imp mana 28.1.5.3.2` coexist cleanly with `imp mana status`. Keep this as the leading candidate while we compare ergonomics against an explicit namespaced form like `imp mana work <id>`.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:60:- 'CLI naming decision direction is now concrete enough for implementation planning: prefer `imp mana <unit-id>` as the canonical single-unit worker entrypoint, keep `imp run <unit-id>` as the compatibility alias during migration, and stop growing the temporary `imp work` spelling. Treat `imp mana` as a namespace with reserved operator verbs such as `status`, `show`, and `logs`, and otherwise interpret the first positional token as a mana unit id.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:61:- 'Architecture clarification from repo inspection: in the active Tower codebase, durable scheduling/orchestration belongs to mana itself, not to the old Pi extension. Evidence: `imp/docs/proposals/imp-run-worker-contract-and-mana-run-handoff.md` explicitly assigns ready-target selection, dependency ordering, parallel scheduling, verify batching, and durable run state to `mana run`; `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` already wraps native mana orchestration; and `mana/crates/mana-pool/src/dispatch.rs` / `mana-core/src/ops/run.rs` implement the ready-queue/dispatch engine. The Pi extension at `~/.pi/agent/extensions/mana/` is useful reference/prototype material, but it is not the active canonical scheduler for Tower.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:62:- 'Local implementation direction from the latest discussion: when refactoring direct worker/runtime selection around `imp mana ...`, optimize for native tool/runtime use rather than bashing the CLI from inside the agent. The eventual common path should be native mana-backed execution surfaces that feel automatic to the agent; CLI spelling changes are secondary operator UX and should not become the primary internal execution mechanism.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:82:- A native `imp` tool delegation smoke test against imp-local mana unit `46.1` reached the worker runtime.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:101:- `imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs`
.mana/archive/2026/05/334-fix-tui-keyboard-latency-while-native-mana-runs-ex.md:3:title: Fix TUI keyboard latency while native mana runs execute
.mana/archive/2026/05/334-fix-tui-keyboard-latency-while-native-mana-runs-ex.md:4:slug: fix-tui-keyboard-latency-while-native-mana-runs-ex
.mana/archive/2026/05/334-fix-tui-keyboard-latency-while-native-mana-runs-ex.md:9:acceptance: Keyboard input should not be delayed by native mana run live output/background execution; live inspector output remains available; runtime naming remains imp; targeted tests/checks pass.
.mana/archive/2026/05/334-fix-tui-keyboard-latency-while-native-mana-runs-ex.md:13:  Starting from clarification: slowdown manifests as delayed keyboard input, so previous background default is insufficient. Will inspect TUI event loop and tool update handling for excessive UI updates/render invalidation during mana run.
.mana/archive/2026/05/334-fix-tui-keyboard-latency-while-native-mana-runs-ex.md:15:close_reason: 'Implemented clarified keyboard-latency fix by bounding TUI runtime-signal draining per tick: agent events are capped at 64 per loop and UI requests at 16 within that budget, preventing mana run live-output/UI request bursts from monopolizing the input loop. Prior fixes for background default, imp runtime naming, and live inspector output remain in place. Verified with cargo check -p imp-core && cargo check -p imp-tui; attempted cargo test -p imp-tui app::tests:: --quiet but no tests matched.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/334-fix-tui-keyboard-latency-while-native-mana-runs-ex.md:20:- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs
.mana/archive/2026/05/334-fix-tui-keyboard-latency-while-native-mana-runs-ex.md:37:User clarified the slowdown is specifically delayed keyboard input in the active imp TUI while native mana run is executing. Investigate runtime/UI event loop interactions after prior background default fix. Suspects: background run still runs CPU/blocking work in same process/threadpool; frequent UI widget/status updates or tool output deltas invalidate render cache too aggressively; global mana stream sink/signal handlers or locks affecting event loop. Scope: measure/inspect relevant paths in crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs and crates/imp-tui/src/app.rs; reduce main TUI event-loop pressure without losing live output; prefer throttling/coalescing output or isolating background work. Verify with targeted tests/checks and, if practical, a manual reasoning note about event-loop impact.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.12-rename-mana-features-and-remove-mana-cli-dependenc.md:3:title: Rename mana features and remove mana-cli dependency where feasible
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.12-rename-mana-features-and-remove-mana-cli-dependenc.md:4:slug: rename-mana-features-and-remove-mana-cli-dependenc
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.12-rename-mana-features-and-remove-mana-cli-dependenc.md:11:  - Rename feature flags from broad `mana-integration` to clearer names: `imp-core/mana-api`, `imp-tui/mana-ui`, and `imp-cli/mana-ui`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.12-rename-mana-features-and-remove-mana-cli-dependenc.md:12:  - Remove the `mana-cli` crate dependency from imp-core if feasible in this slice, or document exact remaining imports blocking removal.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.12-rename-mana-features-and-remove-mana-cli-dependenc.md:13:  - Preserve current green checks for standalone and mana-enabled builds.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.12-rename-mana-features-and-remove-mana-cli-dependenc.md:14:  - Do not attempt the large TUI mana-ui decomposition in this slice unless trivial.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.12-rename-mana-features-and-remove-mana-cli-dependenc.md:18:  Implemented feature rename and mana-cli removal slice. Renamed broad feature flags to `imp-core/mana-api`, `imp-tui/mana-ui`, and `imp-cli/mana-ui`. Removed imp-core's dependency on `mana-cli` by dropping the native `tools::mana` module from imp-core registration/exports for now; mana UI/API surfaces now use `mana-core` only. Updated mana run-state file path helper to use local path construction instead of `mana::commands::agents::agents_file_path`. TUI still defaults to `mana-ui` pending larger 365.11.1 decomposition; this slice intentionally did not attempt that larger change. Verified checks passed: fmt for core/cli/tui, core no-default check, core --features mana-api check, cli default check, cli --features mana-ui check, and imp-core clippy with mana-api. Also confirmed `cargo tree -p imp-core --features mana-api | rg mana-cli` has no matches; `mana-core` remains present as intended for mana-api.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.12-rename-mana-features-and-remove-mana-cli-dependenc.md:21:verify: cd /Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary && cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-cli -p imp-tui && cargo check -p imp-core --no-default-features && cargo check -p imp-core --features mana-api && cargo check -p imp-cli && cargo check -p imp-cli --features mana-ui && cargo clippy -p imp-core --lib --features mana-api -- -D warnings
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.12-rename-mana-features-and-remove-mana-cli-dependenc.md:30:  output_snippet: 'verify passed: cd /Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary && cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-cli -p imp-tui && cargo check -p imp-core --no-default-features && cargo check -p imp-core --features mana-api && cargo check -p imp-cli && cargo check -p imp-cli --features mana-ui && cargo clippy -p imp-core --lib --features mana-api -- -D warnings'
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.12-rename-mana-features-and-remove-mana-cli-dependenc.md:49:Clarify optional mana feature boundaries. User prefers `mana-ui`, and imp should not depend on `mana-cli` when calling mana APIs. Rename features to reflect UI/API surfaces and start removing mana-cli linkage from imp-core, with blockers recorded if tools/mana still depends on CLI internals.
.mana/archive/2026/04/27.3-imp-agent-loop-non-blocking-mana-follow-ups-and-me.md:3:title: 'imp agent loop: non-blocking mana follow-ups and message delivery'
.mana/archive/2026/04/27.3-imp-agent-loop-non-blocking-mana-follow-ups-and-me.md:4:slug: imp-agent-loop-non-blocking-mana-follow-ups-and-me
.mana/archive/2026/04/27.3-imp-agent-loop-non-blocking-mana-follow-ups-and-me.md:9:acceptance: Background mana work returns promptly instead of monopolizing the turn; user messages and steering still reach the active agent while mana work exists in the background; completion arrives through a real follow-up or notification path; tests cover the non-blocking behavior.
.mana/archive/2026/04/27.3-imp-agent-loop-non-blocking-mana-follow-ups-and-me.md:13:  Settled UX default from root planning: longer-running mana work should complete through both surfaces — (1) a status/notification signal and (2) an inline assistant follow-up / delivery path — while still returning promptly and allowing fresh user messages to reach the active agent.
.mana/archive/2026/04/27.3-imp-agent-loop-non-blocking-mana-follow-ups-and-me.md:17:  Architecture clarification from root planning: background mana follow-ups/message delivery belong to the native Rust worker/runtime path for imp execution. This should not depend on Lua extension routing.
.mana/archive/2026/04/27.3-imp-agent-loop-non-blocking-mana-follow-ups-and-me.md:33:  - `tools/mana.rs` background native mana orchestration already does both parts of the settled UX: it emits `ui.notify(...)` / status/widget updates, and when there is no UI it enqueues an inline `AgentCommand::FollowUp(...)` summary on completion or failure.
.mana/archive/2026/04/27.3-imp-agent-loop-non-blocking-mana-follow-ups-and-me.md:39:  2026-04-23 resumed and reverified against current workspace state. The unit verify gate still passes unchanged: `cargo test -p imp-core follow_up ...`, `cargo check -p imp-cli`, and `cargo check -p imp-tui`. Evidence remains consistent with the prior inspection: background mana work already returns promptly, `AgentCommand::FollowUp` is queued/delivered between turns, non-UI background completion enqueues inline follow-up delivery, UI-backed runs surface status/notification without monopolizing the active turn, and user messages/steering remain covered by the existing follow-up tests. No new code change was needed; acceptance is satisfied in the current state.
.mana/archive/2026/04/27.3-imp-agent-loop-non-blocking-mana-follow-ups-and-me.md:41:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/27.3-imp-agent-loop-non-blocking-mana-follow-ups-and-me.md:66:Make native mana runs non-blocking in the same way Pi's mana tool is: the tool should be able to kick off longer-running mana work, return promptly, and later surface completion without preventing fresh user messages from reaching the agent. Audit current handling of queued follow-ups and AgentCommand::FollowUp in imp-core, imp-cli, and imp-tui, replace the current dead-end behavior with a real completion-notification path, ensure background mana activity does not monopolize the active turn, and add regression tests.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:9:acceptance: A root rebuild feature exists with child phase epics in the planned order, plus initial worker-safe jobs for the earliest phases. The graph should be detailed enough that future workers can continue decomposition and execution from mana state rather than from transient chat context.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:33:  New user framing from 2026-04-09: think of agents as effectively string-in/string-out systems. Mana is the substrate of the work and knowledge an agent consumes and produces. The original mana idea was engineering on top of a Ralph-style loop: instead of prompting in the same general direction repeatedly, give each agent a specialized prompt around its task so planning, prototyping, final-plan creation, and implementation can happen against a comprehensive task contract/spec. Imp is the native agent/interface that can fully utilize mana as both tool and substrate.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:37:  Decision from user on 2026-04-09: treat `mana run` as the wrong boundary language and move the architecture toward `imp run {mana_id}` as the primary execution model. Update docs and planning to reflect that imp is the orchestrator/runtime reading mana state and performing tasks, while `mana run` should be treated as legacy/compatibility behavior rather than the intended long-term boundary.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:41:  User clarified on 2026-04-09 that prior specs should be revised now to match the new imp-centered boundary. Do not leave `mana run`-centered rebuild specs as merely historical artifacts; update them so the root documentation and planning graph consistently reflect imp as orchestrator/runtime and `imp run {mana_id}` as the preferred execution framing.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:45:  Superseding decision: the architecture should move away from `mana run`-centered framing and toward `imp run {mana_id}` as the preferred execution model. Legacy `mana run` protocol shapes may still need compatibility mappings during migration, but earlier specs that treated `mana run` as the intended center are now superseded.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:51:  1. Mana is the cross-agent durable substrate/work medium. It owns the work and knowledge agents consume and produce durably.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:52:  2. Imp is the native worker/runtime and orchestrator. It reads mana state, performs the task, and writes back structured durable outcomes.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:53:  3. The happy path is mana → imp → mana.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:54:  4. Mana should hand imp a specialized task packet, not just a raw unit file. That packet should include the task contract, acceptance/verify target, relevant facts, dependency context, prior failures/attempts, path hints, and important constraints/decisions.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:56:  6. Durable rule: if another worker should inherit it cold, it belongs in mana. Ephemeral rule: local reasoning flow, transient tool chatter, UI/session state, and temporary caches can stay in imp.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:58:  8. Repo-grounded ownership reading: `imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs`, `imp/crates/imp-cli/src/main.rs`, and `imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs` already support imp-centered execution. `mana/crates/mana-cli/src/spawner.rs` and `mana/crates/mana-core/src/prompt.rs` represent overlapping/legacy behavior that should be treated as transitional rather than the intended long-term center.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:59:  9. Command boundary answer: move toward `imp run {mana_id}` as the primary execution model. Treat `mana run` as legacy/compatibility behavior on the path to disappearing.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:72:  2026-04-09T07:27Z Phase-1 contracts checkpoint: the first shared-contract ownership move is now landed. `tower-contracts::worker` owns the canonical worker handoff/result vocabulary (`WorkerAssignment`, `WorkerAttempt`, `WorkerResult`, `WorkerStatus`). `imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs` no longer defines those canonical types; it now consumes and re-exports them to preserve current imp-core call sites during migration. Keep the decomposition explicit from here: worker assignment/outcome vocabulary is migrated first; run/view/event protocol work remains on the runner/execution path units, durable result/evidence lineage remains on the evidence units, and migration-shim cleanup remains on the renameability/shim unit. Do not treat 45.2.3 as a signal to broaden into run-stream/view/event moves in the same slice.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:76:  2026-04-09 durable implementation delta: unit 45.8 landed the first mana-owned ApprovalRecord / PromotionRecord schema in mana-core. Implementation constraint discovered and now externalized as fact 70: mana-review currently depends on mana-core, so mana-core cannot directly embed mana-review crate types without a dependency cycle. As a result, the first durable approval schema stores review/risk lineage through refs plus snapshots (review_refs, review_decision_refs, risk_level snapshot, RiskFlagRecord) rather than direct mana-review types. Follow-up unit created to decide long-term ownership for shared review/risk vocabulary.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:80:  2026-04-13 architecture clarification from user: preserve the rebuild around `mana` as a headless durable control plane and `imp` as the live execution shell. Migration consequence: `mana run` should converge into thin compatibility/delegation behavior over the same attach path used by `imp run {mana_id}`, not remain a separate orchestration center. Staged cutover to preserve in downstream phase work: (1) define canonical mana run/node/lease/event/artifact/checkpoint semantics first, (2) dual-write current execution through mana-owned semantics, (3) shadow-schedule in mana until outputs match, (4) cut imp over to lease-based execution where imp attaches, receives leases, heartbeats, checkpoints, and resolves via mana, (5) move transcript ownership fully into imp while mana remains the canonical recovery substrate through structured events/checkpoints/artifacts, (6) publish the embeddable/library-first mana surface and deprecate the old `mana run` path.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:84:  2026-04-13 rebuild framing revalidated against current root docs (`README.md`, `VISION.md`). Feature 45 should continue to serve the active two-part foundation rather than broader ecosystem scope: `mana` as durable substrate/control plane and `imp` as the live execution shell/runtime, with the canonical loop `mana -> imp -> mana`. Migration and phase work under this feature should explicitly strengthen that contract — durable memory/evidence/coordination on the mana side, safe focused execution on the imp side — and should keep `wizard/` / `familiar/` deferred unless a unit explicitly targets later-phase reference material.
.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:93:- mana

.mana/45-tower-rebuild-around-explicit-contracts-durable-le.md:109:Goal: execute the Tower rebuild described in `rebuild_plan.md`, `mana/mana_rebuild_plan.md`, and `imp/imp_rebuild_plan.md` as an explicit mana graph. Current inspected state: the root plans now freeze a top-level order of operations; the root workspace currently has only `mana/*` and `imp/*` crates in `Cargo.toml`; there is not yet a Tower-level shared contracts crate; `mana/crates/mana-cli/src/spawner.rs` is still shell-template driven; `imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs` still defines canonical worker assignment/outcome types inside `imp`; `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` currently consumes `mana-cli` run types directly. Existing units `28`, `29`, and `44` remain useful architectural context but are not the canonical execution graph for this rebuild. This feature should create a clean phase-ordered graph that another worker can execute cold without reconstructing the architecture from chat history.

Existing unrelated warnings in imp-core import.rs remain. Note: mana verify returned exit 1 for some units despite direct shell commands passing; likely due stale root/project verify context or hidden verify command mismatch, so shell verification evidence is recorded in child notes.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4.9-build-imp-work-first-coherent-foundation.md:46:Execute the first coherent imp-work foundation rather than a minimal v0. Build the native replacement for mana around durable memory, tasks/epics/subtasks, first-class prototypes, task-owned context packs, structured outcomes, and scheduler foundations. Scope discipline comes from prototypes, acceptance gates, and sequencing, not shrinking the concept. Defer full TUI replacement, embeddings/semantic search, massive live concurrency, containerized sandboxing, and committed mana importer unless needed to prove the foundation.

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-remaining-imp-workflow-coupling-and-reframe.md:11:  - Remaining workflow/mana/imp-work coupling in `imp-core` is inventoried.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-remaining-imp-workflow-coupling-and-reframe.md:12:  - Each coupling point is classified as recipe-like runtime capability, optional host integration, mana/harness responsibility, compatibility shim, or remove.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-remaining-imp-workflow-coupling-and-reframe.md:22:  Target model: imp workflows should become Goose-recipe-like optional runtime recipes. A recipe can preconfigure prompt/context/tools/policy/verification/evidence or add reusable agent behavior, but it must not be the core runtime's durable work graph owner. Durable task graph/orchestration/claims/board lifecycle belongs to mana/harness.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-remaining-imp-workflow-coupling-and-reframe.md:25:  1. `crates/imp-core/src/agent/workflow_integration.rs`: currently contains a mix of recipe-like runtime support and durable work-graph coupling. Recipe-like/keep: workflow contract as runtime recipe metadata, verification/evidence artifact writing, worktree/autonomy metadata, optional pre/post-turn hints. Optional host integration: turn review accumulator, workflow controller snapshot/persistence, closeout/evidence events. Mana/harness responsibility: mana bootstrap/root creation, child unit management, mana run supervision, graph closeout policy, imp-work run-state nudges. Compatibility shim/remove later: mana skill/externalization nudges and native imp-work follow-up text.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-remaining-imp-workflow-coupling-and-reframe.md:26:  2. `crates/imp-core/src/agent/mod.rs`: after extraction, mostly core runtime plus assessment composition. Remaining direct workflow coupling is through `WorkflowRuntimeLayer`, `WorkflowContract`, `TurnManaReview`, `ManaEvidence`, and tests. Classification: keep assessment composition short term; later make workflow layer capability-gated/no-op by default and rename `WorkflowContract` toward recipe/run contract.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-remaining-imp-workflow-coupling-and-reframe.md:28:  4. `crates/imp-core/src/agent/tool_execution.rs`: only coupling is passing turn mana review accumulator into `ToolContext`. Classification: compatibility shim for mana/work tools; later move to host integration event sink rather than core tool context.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-remaining-imp-workflow-coupling-and-reframe.md:29:  5. `crates/imp-core/src/workflow/*`: split required. `contract.rs`, `verification.rs`, `verification_runner.rs`, and parts of `worktree_run.rs` are useful recipe/runtime capabilities. `bootstrap.rs`, `controller.rs`, and child mana unit orchestration are durable work-graph/harness responsibilities or temporary compatibility. `child_workflow.rs` has some recipe-like role/contract inheritance but its child workflow lifecycle should not be core runtime.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-remaining-imp-workflow-coupling-and-reframe.md:30:  6. `crates/imp-core/src/tools/work.rs` and `crates/imp-core/Cargo.toml`: direct `imp-work` dependency remains active through the `work` tool and prototype/work helpers. Classification: compatibility shim; long-term move tool/work graph to mana/harness or archive. Near-term do not delete while current tools/tests depend on it.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-remaining-imp-workflow-coupling-and-reframe.md:35:  B. Split `workflow_integration.rs` internally into recipe/runtime support vs mana-work-graph compatibility modules, without behavior change.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-remaining-imp-workflow-coupling-and-reframe.md:36:  C. Make `WorkflowRuntimeLayer` capability-gated/no-op by default for standalone runs, with mana/work compatibility explicitly enabled by builder/host config.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-remaining-imp-workflow-coupling-and-reframe.md:77:User decision: workflows in imp should behave similarly to Goose recipes. They are something imp can do — reusable runtime recipes/extensions/patterns — but they are not central to the runtime. Durable work graph/orchestration belongs to mana/harness.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-audit-remaining-imp-workflow-coupling-and-reframe.md:90:Deliverable can be mana notes plus, if useful, a short doc section. Do not do broad deletion in this task; classify first and identify the smallest next implementation slices.

.mana/archive/2026/05/335-make-native-mana-run-inherit-current-imp-model-and.md:3:title: Make native mana run inherit current imp model and thinking defaults
.mana/archive/2026/05/335-make-native-mana-run-inherit-current-imp-model-and.md:4:slug: make-native-mana-run-inherit-current-imp-model-and
.mana/archive/2026/05/335-make-native-mana-run-inherit-current-imp-model-and.md:9:acceptance: mana(action=run) without explicit model/thinking uses current/default imp model and thinking level for workers; explicit model/thinking choices still override defaults; targeted checks pass.
.mana/archive/2026/05/335-make-native-mana-run-inherit-current-imp-model-and.md:15:close_reason: 'Implemented model/thinking inheritance for native mana run across imp and local mana path dependency. imp mana tool now fills NativeRunParams.run_model/run_thinking from explicit mana params model/thinking when present, otherwise from current imp config model/thinking. mana-cli NativeRunParams and direct RunConfig now carry run_model/run_thinking; direct imp worker command adds --model and --thinking. Explicit/unit model precedence preserved by existing unit.model over config_run_model in run_single_direct. Verified cargo check -p mana-cli, cargo check -p imp-core && cargo check -p imp-tui, cargo test -p mana-cli direct_imp_command --quiet, cargo test -p imp-core tools::mana::tests:: --quiet.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/335-make-native-mana-run-inherit-current-imp-model-and.md:20:- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs
.mana/archive/2026/05/335-make-native-mana-run-inherit-current-imp-model-and.md:38:User reported native mana run should use the same model and thinking level as the default/current imp session unless the model/tool caller explicitly chooses otherwise. Investigate mana run param/config plumbing. Ensure default native mana run worker runtime receives current imp model and thinking level from ToolContext/config when params omit them, while preserving explicit model/thinking params. Add/update targeted tests if practical and verify with cargo check.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.1-instrument-imp-agent-loop-latency-baseline.md:9:acceptance: Running a representative imp agent workflow produces structured timing spans for model calls, streaming, context assembly, tool execution, retry delays, mana writes, and UI/runtime handoff. Output is usable to compare baseline vs future persistent transports.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.1-instrument-imp-agent-loop-latency-baseline.md:75:    crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs:    s.push_str("- If work spans more than one project or clearly belongs at the ecosystem layer, target root mana explicitly rather than relying on the nearest project scope.\n");
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.1-instrument-imp-agent-loop-latency-baseline.md:91:Current state: The OpenAI WebSocket article suggests large gains may come from reducing repeated model/tool round-trip overhead, but imp does not yet have a durable baseline in mana for where long-running tool-heavy sessions spend time.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.1-instrument-imp-agent-loop-latency-baseline.md:102:   - mana persistence/write calls involved in runs or unit updates
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.1-instrument-imp-agent-loop-latency-baseline.md:111:- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs (inspect only if mana write timing is not visible from agent loop)
.mana/archive/2026/03/40-teach-imp-to-synthesize-completed-child-job-outcom.md:13:  The work remains imp-local and does not replace mana as the durable work graph.
.mana/archive/2026/03/40-teach-imp-to-synthesize-completed-child-job-outcom.md:17:  Implemented as part of MANA_DELEGATION_GUIDANCE const. The 'After child jobs complete' section tells the model to: check completed children with mana show, synthesize what changed/touched/unresolved, summarize concisely for next step, and diagnose failures before retrying. This is prompt-only v1 — no runtime machinery. Regression tests cover the guidance appearing in Full mode and being skipped for Worker/no-mana.

Treat 47 itself as an orchestration/spec umbrella only.

.mana/archive/2026/05/47-rebuild-imp-around-explicit-runtime-boundaries-pro.md:28:This is the umbrella rebuild epic for imp. Goal: keep imp as a pure-Rust runtime and product, but refactor it so the implementation more literally matches Tower's intended architecture: mana owns durable truth, imp owns live execution, and workflow trust comes from explicit contracts, execution boundaries, verification artifacts, and review gates rather than from large implicit runtime objects.

This avoids feeding giant raw JSON into the clean UI while preserving useful context. Targeted inspector tests pass.

Also had to restore/add `crates/imp-work/src/run_engine.rs` exports in this clean worktree because `cargo check -p imp-core` failed at baseline with unresolved `imp_work::WorkRunEngine` and `WorkRunPolicyInput`; this was confirmed by temporarily restoring those changes and reproducing the same baseline failure. After restoring the missing run_engine module and fixing string status compatibility with current model, `cargo check -p imp-core` passes. `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work` passes. Follow-up seams: move `workflow_controller` field behind a host integration trait or optional runtime layer; isolate `mana_loop` events/actions from core runtime events; remove durable lifecycle decisions from runtime loop once mana daemon/harness owns them; decide whether imp-work run_engine restoration should be a separate prerequisite commit/task because it fixes a baseline compile gap unrelated to the workflow extraction.

.mana/archive/2026/04/304.7.1-implement-askuser-tool-redesign.md:17:  Continued ask_user implementation with real TUI multi-select support. Added `UiRequest::MultiSelect`, `AskReply::MultiSelect`, and `TuiInterface::multi_select_with_context` so ask_user multi_select no longer falls back to single select in TUI. App now begins ask overlay in multi mode and returns all selected indices; cancel sends None. Ask bar quick number selection now toggles in multi-select without immediately confirming, while preserving single-select quick confirm. Added ask_bar test for multi-select quick toggle. Verification passed: `cargo test -p imp-tui ask_bar -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p imp-core ask -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p imp-core ask --no-run`, `cargo test -p imp-core builder_registers_canonical_tools_and_compat_aliases -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p imp-tui --no-run`. Note: `cargo fmt --check` initially reported unrelated already-dirty formatting in committed files; ran `cargo fmt`, then restored unrelated formatted-only files (mana_worker.rs, edit.rs, read.rs, views/chat.rs) to keep diff scoped.

.mana/archive/2026/04/50.2-define-canonical-imp-command-grammar-and-default-e.md:33:  Loaded mana skill per user instruction, claimed unit 50.2, and inspected current baseline sources. Initial evidence: imp/README.md still presents plain `imp` as the default interactive entrypoint, `imp -p` as one-shot print mode, `imp run <unit-id>` as the headless mana-worker path, and login/secrets flows as separate top-level commands. Also confirmed the expected target framing from unit notes: evaluate `imp`/`imp chat` as the CLI-first shell, `imp run` as the machine/worker runtime, `imp view` as the browsing/inspection companion surface, and `imp tui` as an explicit fullscreen entrypoint rather than an assumed default. Next step is to inspect current CLI help/usage strings in imp-cli and then write docs/rebuild/imp-command-grammar.md with the default-entrypoint migration decision centered rather than deferred.
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.2-define-canonical-imp-command-grammar-and-default-e.md:37:  Loaded mana skill and root docs. Inspecting current imp README/main.rs plus adjacent feature-50 rebuild specs to ground a command-grammar recommendation with the default-entrypoint decision as the center.
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.2-define-canonical-imp-command-grammar-and-default-e.md:45:  Wrote docs/rebuild/imp-command-grammar.md and verified the unit gate. The doc is grounded in current repo reality from imp/crates/imp-cli/src/main.rs, imp/README.md, and docs/rebuild/imp-run-primary-execution-path.md. It makes the default-entrypoint decision explicit rather than deferring it: recommended target is plain `imp` == `imp chat`, with `imp tui` becoming the explicit fullscreen entrypoint, `imp prompt` becoming the canonical name for current `imp -p`, `imp run <unit-id>` staying the canonical machine/mana-worker path, and `imp view` grouping browsing/inspection flows. It also specifies transition phases, compatibility aliases (`-p`, slash commands, `web-login`), and exact stdin/tty default-behavior rules so the migration is not hand-wavy.

.mana/archive/2026/04/50.2-define-canonical-imp-command-grammar-and-default-e.md:116:  New command-grammar direction from the current discussion: split single-unit mana-worker execution from multi-unit orchestration explicitly under an `imp mana` namespace. Candidate shape:
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.2-define-canonical-imp-command-grammar-and-default-e.md:117:  - `imp mana <id>` = run one imp worker against one mana unit
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.2-define-canonical-imp-command-grammar-and-default-e.md:118:  - `imp mana run <target>` (or equivalent reserved orchestration verb) = invoke multi-unit mana scheduling/orchestration over ready jobs / epic scope

.mana/archive/2026/04/50.2-define-canonical-imp-command-grammar-and-default-e.md:120:  This keeps single-unit worker entry distinct from orchestration while preserving mana as the scheduling owner.
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.2-define-canonical-imp-command-grammar-and-default-e.md:122:  Command-grammar decision update from the latest discussion: the `imp mana` namespace should cover both the direct single-unit worker path and mana-backed orchestration, but with an explicit split of responsibility. Current preferred shape:
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.2-define-canonical-imp-command-grammar-and-default-e.md:123:  - `imp mana <id>` = imp runtime executes one worker on one mana unit
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.2-define-canonical-imp-command-grammar-and-default-e.md:124:  - `imp mana <orchestration-verb> ...` = orchestration/inspection entrypoints such as `run`, `status`, `show`, `logs`
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.2-define-canonical-imp-command-grammar-and-default-e.md:126:- 'CLI/runtime boundary clarification from the latest discussion: the `imp` command grammar is primarily an operator-facing shell/UX surface, not the mechanism agents should use for day-to-day execution. For 99% of agent work, native mana/imp tools should expose the needed single-unit and orchestration capabilities directly so agents do not need to invoke `imp ...` through bash except for compatibility or debugging cases.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.2-define-canonical-imp-command-grammar-and-default-e.md:127:- 'Agent-facing execution principle from the latest discussion: ordinary agent work should not shell out to `imp` via bash. The common path should be native tool/runtime surfaces (`mana` for durable substrate/orchestration, `imp` for child-worker invocation) so mana-backed behavior feels built-in rather than CLI-mediated. CLI grammar under `imp mana ...` is primarily an operator-facing shell surface and compatibility layer, not the primary internal mechanism agents use for 99% of tasks.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/50.2-define-canonical-imp-command-grammar-and-default-e.md:130:Define the canonical command grammar for a CLI-first imp. Write `docs/rebuild/imp-command-grammar.md`. Use current commands and docs as the baseline (`imp`, `imp -p`, `imp run`, login/secrets/config/usage) and propose the target structure: e.g. whether to keep `-p` or add `imp prompt`, whether to add `imp chat`, whether `imp tui` should become explicit, and how human-facing interactive, machine-facing, and mana-worker flows should differ. Include migration recommendations for compatibility, aliases, deprecation timing, and what plain `imp` should do during transition. Do not hand-wave around the default behavior question; make it the center of the doc.

.mana/archive/2026/04/29.6.3-implement-append-style-mana-actions-for-conversati.md:25:Goal: keep the implemented append-style mana actions for conversational planning updates working and verified. Current state: notes_append, decision_add, and decision_resolve are implemented in imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs, with relevant mode allow-lists in imp/crates/imp-core/src/config.rs. Steps: 1. preserve append-style action handlers, 2. preserve mode access for planner/orchestrator/worker where intended, 3. keep the focused decision add/resolve test passing. Files: imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs and imp/crates/imp-core/src/config.rs (modify only if regressions appear). In scope: append-style actions, mode allow-lists, focused tests. Out of scope: automatic higher-level externalization heuristics.

.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-make-imps-native-mana-tool-the-clear-first-class-o.md:71:Goal: align imp's native mana tool UX with the strengthened imp worker contract so the intended first-class user path is clean, visible, and explicitly layered.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-make-imps-native-mana-tool-the-clear-first-class-o.md:74:- The native mana tool in `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` is already the intended first-class orchestration UX inside imp sessions: it supports `run`, `run_state`, `evaluate`, `logs`, background runs, summaries, and widget/status updates.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-make-imps-native-mana-tool-the-clear-first-class-o.md:75:- However, the current 28.1 decomposition over-emphasized direct human-facing `imp run <id>` polish. The clarified product intent is that imp's native mana tool is the primary first-class UX, with `mana run` as orchestrator and `imp run` as worker runtime.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-make-imps-native-mana-tool-the-clear-first-class-o.md:76:- Once 28.1.1–28.1.3 land, the native mana tool should clearly surface that stack rather than looking like a thin pass-through.

Untracked group C: codeintel module plus C/C++/C#/Java scan files. Untracked group D: imp-work mana_shadow.rs. Untracked group E: design/proposal/rebuild docs. My TUI inspector fix is already committed as 5354c77 and excluded from remaining status except unrelated app.rs changes still present.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:13:  - `crates/imp-cli/src/lib.rs` currently defines only `Commands::Run` for the headless unit-worker CLI surface (around the `Commands` enum block where the `Run` variant is declared with `unit_id`, `mana_dir`, and `defer_verify`).
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:14:  - The main command dispatch also branches only on `Commands::Run` and forwards directly to `run_headless_mode(&cli, unit_id, mana_dir.as_deref(), *defer_verify)`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:33:  - Updated README quick-start and mana integration examples to prefer `imp work`, with explicit note that `imp run` remains a compatibility alias during migration.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:51:  Naming is reopened after the initial `imp work` pass. User raised a stronger candidate: `imp mana <id>` as the canonical single-unit worker CLI because it more clearly names imp as the runtime using mana. Before proceeding further, we should compare `imp work <id>` vs `imp mana <id>` vs possibly a namespaced form like `imp mana work <id>` for extensibility, then either keep, revise, or revert the current `imp work` implementation accordingly.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:55:  Loaded the `mana` skill per user instruction before continuing planning. Next planning step is to evaluate the reopened CLI naming question with mana-native artifact updates before making further surface changes or proceeding to 28.1.5.3.2.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:59:  Naming evaluation update: user argues `imp mana <id>` is still extensible because `imp mana status` simply uses a reserved verb in the first positional slot instead of a unit id. Agreed, provided the parser reserves a small set of words (`status`, `show`, `logs`, etc.) and treats any other first positional token as a unit id. This likely makes `imp mana <id>` the strongest current candidate for the canonical single-unit worker surface, with prior `imp work` work now looking transitional/revisable.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:63:  Proposed parser/migration shape if `imp mana <id>` becomes canonical:
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:64:  - Add a top-level `mana` subcommand under `imp-cli`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:66:  - If the first positional token after `imp mana` is not a reserved verb, interpret it as a mana unit id and route to the existing single-unit worker path.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:68:  - This yields a clean user story: `imp mana <id>` = imp works a mana unit, while future `imp mana status` / `imp mana show 12` can coexist in the same namespace.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:69:  Open question still worth confirming before implementation: whether `imp mana show <id>` should eventually be a true CLI wrapper around native mana inspection or whether `imp mana` should remain narrowly focused on worker-oriented paths plus a few operator conveniences.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:74:  1. Remove the temporary preference for `imp work` and pivot the CLI surface to `imp mana <id>` as canonical.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:76:  3. Treat `imp mana` as a namespace: reserve explicit verbs like `status`, `show`, and `logs`; otherwise parse the first positional token as a mana unit id and route to the existing headless worker path.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:77:  4. Update help text, README examples, and focused parsing/help tests to reflect `imp mana <id>` as the preferred spelling.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:85:  - The operator-facing CLI should likely converge on an `imp mana ...` namespace, with `imp mana <id>` as the canonical single-unit worker entrypoint and reserved verbs like `run/status/show/logs` for broader mana-aware operations.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:88:  - Internal execution should instead lean on the existing native `mana` tool and the native `imp` tool/runtime seams tracked under root 81.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:89:  - Practical implication: once the CLI naming is settled, revise the temporary `imp work` implementation toward `imp mana ...`, but keep the runtime/auth refactor and tool-boundary work model-first/native-first rather than shell-command-first.

.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.1-rename-headless-unit-worker-cli-surface-to-imp-wor.md:157:  2026-04-23 resumed and reverified against current workspace state. The implementation is already present and the original unit verify failed only because the cargo invocation was malformed (`cargo test ... run_headless resolve_model` passes two names instead of one filter). Current evidence: README and CLI help text prefer `imp mana <unit-id>` while `imp run <unit-id>` remains the compatibility alias; there are no remaining `imp work` examples. Focused parser/behavior tests all pass: `cli_parses_mana_namespace_unit_target_for_headless_worker`, `cli_parses_run_as_compatibility_alias_for_headless_mana_worker`, `cli_parses_reserved_mana_namespace_verb_with_passthrough_args`, and `reserved_mana_namespace_commands_error_clearly`. Acceptance for this unit is satisfied; only the stale verify command needed correction.

.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3-audit-mana-and-spawn-orchestration-tool-schemas.md:3:title: Audit mana and spawn orchestration tool schemas
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3-audit-mana-and-spawn-orchestration-tool-schemas.md:4:slug: audit-mana-and-spawn-orchestration-tool-schemas
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3-audit-mana-and-spawn-orchestration-tool-schemas.md:9:acceptance: 'For mana and spawn: capture current schema shape, agent UX issues, orchestration hardening gaps, validation/default issues, output contract issues, utility additions, backward-compat risk, and concrete recommended changes. Include special attention to scope selection, unit lifecycle safety, delegation defaults, run-state observability, and idempotency.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3-audit-mana-and-spawn-orchestration-tool-schemas.md:13:  User explicitly reminded to load and follow mana skill before continuing spawn review. Mana skill loaded via read. For spawn review: continue as auditor/planner; inspect enough code to ground findings; capture durable audit findings as notes; create child implementation/design units only after approach is clear and worker-ready; do not implement during this review unless user explicitly asks.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3-audit-mana-and-spawn-orchestration-tool-schemas.md:23:verify: cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3-audit-mana-and-spawn-orchestration-tool-schemas.md:31:- mana: action enum, scope/path/mana_dir aliases, unit lifecycle fields, dependency updates, run/run_state/logs/agents, verification fields, labels/notes/decisions, output consistency.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3-audit-mana-and-spawn-orchestration-tool-schemas.md:32:- spawn: unit vs ad_hoc modes, model/provider/thinking/max_turns/max_tokens defaults, timeout behavior, no_tools/defer_verify semantics, idempotency_key, mana_dir targeting, output/error contract.

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-define-imp-bounded-subagent-orchestration-and-work.md:59:User direction: imp should be able to autonomously orchestrate its own work, including knowing when to spawn subagents to do work for it. This is distinct from mana/harness durable orchestration.

.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:107:    .mana/45.11.1-resolve-consequential-defaults-for-near-term-imp-i.md:15:  - Credential precedence for direct/headless `imp run`: prefer saved OAuth/auth first, then saved API key, then environment variable.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:108:    .mana/45.11.1-resolve-consequential-defaults-for-near-term-imp-i.md:55:- 'Direct/headless `imp run` should resolve credentials in this order: saved OAuth/auth, then saved API key, then environment variable. If the resulting API/auth path fails, imp should fail immediately and show an explanatory error rather than silently degrading. Long-running mana work should surface completion both as a notification/status event and as an inline follow-up path.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:115:    .mana/263-audit-and-isolate-library-level-stderr-writes-that.md:15:  Scoped the TUI-protection pass to library/runtime stderr writes reachable while ratatui owns the terminal. Initial classification from code inspection: high-risk immediate fixes are imp-core/src/agent.rs: OAuth refresh failure currently eprintlns during live agent execution, and imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs: run_verify_command currently eprintlns failed verify stdout/stderr and is reachable via the imp tool's unit-spawn path. Lower-priority/deferred candidates include imp-core session malformed-line warnings, imp-core shell tool definition load warnings, imp-llm anthropic retry/SSE parse warnings, imp-lua runtime/extension/event-handler warnings, and mana-core ctx_assembler/context/hook post-create/post-update warnings. No eprintln occurrences were found in imp-tui itself.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:179:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.3-refactor-headless-worker-auth-to-normalize-empty-o.md:20:    3. `resolve_api_key()` / CLI auth helper still special-cased `openai` to `resolve_api_key_only(...)`, bypassing saved OpenAI/ChatGPT OAuth even though the chosen route is now already resolved upstream.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:180:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.3-refactor-headless-worker-auth-to-normalize-empty-o.md:32:     - `resolve("openai")` preferring saved OAuth while `resolve_api_key_only("openai")` still ignores OAuth
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:181:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.3-refactor-headless-worker-auth-to-normalize-empty-o.md:51:    - aligned `resolve_provider_api_key()` with the same route-aware behavior: `openai-codex` -> ChatGPT OAuth, `anthropic` -> refresh-aware, all other routes -> `resolve(...)`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:182:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.3-refactor-headless-worker-auth-to-normalize-empty-o.md:200:- The worker/session path special-cases OpenAI auth in ways that bypass saved OAuth.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:183:    .mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:97:  Compared freshly cloned ~/pi-mono against current imp repo for a broad product/runtime gap analysis. High-confidence gap buckets now visible: (1) extensibility/runtime embedding — pi ships mature TypeScript extension API, SDK embedding surface, RPC/extension UI, package distribution; imp currently ships Lua-first extensions plus CLI/TUI/RPC surfaces but lacks comparable TS extension/SDK breadth. (2) provider/auth surface — pi-ai exposes a much broader provider matrix and OAuth set (Copilot, Gemini CLI, Antigravity, Vercel AI Gateway, broader OpenAI-compatible customization) than imp’s current built-ins. (3) customization/community packaging — pi has first-class themes, prompt templates, skills, TS extensions, pi packages, /reload; imp has skills/prompts/Lua tooling/import-from-pi but weaker packaged customization story. (4) UI/product philosophy — imp is stronger on opinionated workflow, native mana integration, modes, worker execution contract, guardrails; pi is stronger on hostability/adaptability and extension-defined UI. (5) architecture/runtime seams — pi is split into ai/agent/coding-agent/tui/web-ui/pods/mom packages; imp remains more concentrated in imp-core despite active rebuild plans to isolate worker/runtime/context/tools/policy seams. Likely follow-on work areas: TS extension seam for imp, stronger SDK/embedding contract, broader provider/OAuth coverage, packaged customization/distribution model, and clearer shared runtime/UI seam for non-TUI hosts.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:184:    .mana/archive/2026/04/45.10-evaluate-a-drastic-imp-vnext-centered-on-native-ma.md:101:  Proposed imp catch-up roadmap versus pi, preserving imp’s mana-native workflow thesis rather than copying pi wholesale. Recommended phases: Phase 1 foundations = define shared runtime/UI seam across CLI/TUI/view/embed, plus public embedding/SDK contract and post-Lua TypeScript extension contract. Phase 2 developer platform = ship TS extension runtime with tools/commands/events/UI hooks, reload loop, packaged extension distribution, and prompt/theme/package loading model. Phase 3 provider parity = broaden provider/auth/custom-provider support (Azure, Bedrock, wider OpenAI-compatible surface, strategic OAuth additions) behind a cleaner provider abstraction. Phase 4 host surfaces = stabilize machine-readable event/output contract, embeddable host runtime, richer non-TUI surface, and extension-defined UI primitives. Phase 5 ecosystem hardening = docs/examples/package publishing/import/migration from pi, plus policy/capability boundaries for extensions. Guiding rule: keep imp’s strong native tools, modes, mana worker contract, and /ask-/plan-/work-/improve workflow as the product center while borrowing pi’s hostability and TS customization strengths.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:185:    .mana/archive/2026/04/266.4.3.4-fix-stale-secret-metadata-and-missing-keychain-dia.md:144:- OAuth provider work.

.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:273:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.2-extract-shared-model-first-runtime-connection-reso.md:33:  3. Move the current OpenAI route-selection logic into that shared resolver, including the current ChatGPT/Codex fallback rule when the selected OpenAI model supports it and only OAuth is available.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:274:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.2-extract-shared-model-first-runtime-connection-reso.md:61:    - saved OpenAI/OpenAI-Codex OAuth exists
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:275:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:3:title: Fix native imp delegate worker defaults for OpenAI OAuth/Codex and empty-string overrides
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:276:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:12:  Smoke-tested native `imp run` on 2026-04-15 with temporary unit 49 using `../target/debug/imp ... run 49 --defer-verify`. Result: CLI path reaches startup + agent build (`agent_built`, `prompt_ready`, `run_loop_started`) but provider resolution is wrong for direct headless runs. `--provider openai -m gpt-5.4-nano` produced HTTP 401 with `Incorrect API key provided: ''`, even though `imp secrets list` shows saved OpenAI/ChatGPT OAuth entries and no `OPENAI_API_KEY` env var is set. `--provider anthropic` produced HTTP 401 `x-api-key header is required` because no API key env var was present. `--provider openai-codex -m gpt-5.3-codex` reached provider call using saved OAuth but failed with HTTP 400 `Instructions are required`. This confirms the native `imp run` path is wired and starts correctly, but direct worker auth/instruction defaults are still broken for OpenAI OAuth / Codex and empty-string API key handling.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:277:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:48:- 'User preference/decision: for direct headless `imp run`, auth resolution should check for saved auth first (especially active ChatGPT/OpenAI OAuth) and only then fall back to API-key sources. Empty-string API key values must not override saved auth.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:278:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:49:- 'Clean auth design direction for direct `imp run`: separate (a) provider-surface selection from (b) credential resolution. Normalize empty-string overrides to `None` at the CLI/session boundary. Then resolve credentials through one shared function with precedence `explicit non-empty override -> saved auth -> stored api key -> env`, returning a typed result or a clear error rather than `""`. Do not special-case `openai` to bypass saved OAuth in worker/session code; compatibility decisions about `openai` API vs ChatGPT/Codex subscription belong in provider-surface selection, not in ad hoc auth branches.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:279:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:79:Make native `imp` worker delegation behave correctly when the operator or caller relies on configured defaults or OAuth-backed OpenAI auth.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:280:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:83:- Forcing `provider="openai"` with OAuth-only auth fails because the provider auto-switch to `openai-codex` only happens when provider selection is left to normal resolution.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:281:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:87:- With OpenAI OAuth / `openai-codex`, that empty override produces an empty instructions field and the provider call fails with `HTTP 400 Bad Request: {"detail":"Instructions are required"}`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:282:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:92:  - preserve auto provider resolution for OAuth-backed OpenAI/Codex flows
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:283:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:109:3. Add regression coverage showing an OAuth-only OpenAI setup can still reach the ChatGPT/Codex path when provider is not forced.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:284:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5-fix-native-imp-delegate-worker-defaults-for-openai.md:114:Done when native `imp` delegation no longer breaks OAuth-backed OpenAI runs via accidental empty-string overrides, and the behavior is covered by tests.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:285:    .mana/archive/2026/03/41.8-add-oauth-beta-header-and-token-refresh-on-401.md:3:title: Add OAuth beta header and token refresh on 401

.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:318:    .mana/archive/2026/04/269-add-chatgpt-55-gpt-55-model-support.md:13:  Implemented first-class OAuth-path model support for GPT-5.5 in crates/imp-llm/src/model.rs without changing plain OpenAI API builtins. Added a ChatGPT/Codex-only built-in model entry in builtin_openai_codex_models(), plus aliases gpt5.5 / gpt-5.5 / chatgpt5.5 / chatgpt-5.5 -> gpt-5.5. This preserves the existing behavior where provider-less resolution still treats gpt-5.5 as an OpenAI-style custom model, but the ChatGPT/Codex route now explicitly recognizes it for OAuth fallback and picker/runtime metadata. Verified with: cargo test -p imp-llm model::tests ; cargo test -p imp-core imp_session::tests::resolve_runtime_connection_prefers_openai_chatgpt_route_when_oauth_exists ; cargo test -p imp-cli resolve_model_prefers_chatgpt_provider_when_only_oauth_is_available
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:319:    .mana/archive/2026/04/269-add-chatgpt-55-gpt-55-model-support.md:17:  Operator rollout / usage note: to start using GPT-5.5 on the ChatGPT/Codex OAuth path after this change, rebuild the installed imp CLI from source (`cargo install --path crates/imp-cli --force` from imp/), then verify with `imp --version`, refresh OAuth if needed via `imp login openai`, and run `imp --model gpt-5.5`. A full local-state wipe is not required for model support; only recommend backing up/removing `~/.config/imp/` when the user explicitly wants a clean reinstall of auth/config/session state.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:320:    .mana/archive/2026/04/269-add-chatgpt-55-gpt-55-model-support.md:21:  New issue found after install verification: GPT-5.5 does not appear in the TUI model picker even though OAuth-path runtime support works. Root cause from inspected code: the picker uses App::filtered_models(), which draws from model_registry.list(); the registry intentionally does not include gpt-5.5 as a built-in model to avoid advertising unsupported plain-API details, so the picker never sees the ChatGPT/Codex-only model. Plan: patch TUI picker filtering to append ChatGPT/Codex-only built-in models that are not present in the normal registry when ChatGPT/OpenAI OAuth credentials exist, and add a focused test.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:321:    .mana/archive/2026/04/269-add-chatgpt-55-gpt-55-model-support.md:25:  Fixed the TUI picker gap in crates/imp-tui/src/app.rs. Added shared filtered_model_options() logic plus a helper that appends ChatGPT/Codex-only built-in models (currently including gpt-5.5) when ChatGPT/OpenAI OAuth exists and no plain OpenAI API key is configured. This keeps the picker/settings aligned with the same OAuth-fallback intent used by runtime selection, while avoiding advertising the OAuth-only model when the normal OpenAI API path is active. Added focused tests covering both inclusion and hiding behavior, plus kept the existing cycle-model integration check passing. Verified with: cargo test -p imp-tui filtered_model_options_includes_chatgpt_oauth_only_models -- --nocapture ; cargo test -p imp-tui filtered_model_options_hides_chatgpt_oauth_only_models_when_openai_api_key_exists -- --nocapture ; cargo test -p imp-tui tui_integration_model_switch_via_cycle -- --nocapture ; cargo test -p imp-cli resolve_model_prefers_chatgpt_provider_when_only_oauth_is_available -- --nocapture

.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:340:    .mana/45.11-capture-near-term-imp-execution-lanes-under-the-im.md:20:  - Whether saved OAuth/saved auth should beat saved API key and env-derived credentials.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:341:    .mana/archive/2026/04/263.1-classify-imp-libraryruntime-stderr-writes-for-embe.md:15:  Imp runtime classification underway. Confirmed high-risk sites reachable from embedded interactive flow: (1) imp-core agent OAuth refresh pre-flight emits eprintln on refresh failure inside the live turn loop; should route through structured agent/UI error handling or stay silent and let the provider auth failure surface normally. (2) imp-core mana_worker verify runner emits raw stdout/stderr on verify failure; this path is used by the imp tool's unit-backed worker execution and can break the TUI. Candidate next changes: remove direct stderr printing from both sites, preserve diagnostics in returned structured results or notifications, then add focused regression coverage where feasible.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:342:    .mana/archive/2026/04/263.1-classify-imp-libraryruntime-stderr-writes-for-embe.md:19:  Implemented first high-risk fixes. (1) imp-core agent OAuth refresh failure no longer writes directly to stderr during live execution; it now routes through ui.notify(..., Warning), which is TUI-safe and a no-op in null/headless contexts. (2) imp-core mana_worker verify runner no longer eprintlns failed verify stdout/stderr; it captures the output into WorkerRunOutcome.verify_output and imp tool details now include verify_output for structured downstream display. Added focused tests for stderr/stdout capture fallback in mana_worker and re-ran imp-core checks. Remaining imp-core eprintln sites after this pass: session.rs malformed-line warning and tools/shell.rs invalid shell-tool-definition warning, both lower risk but still library-level; imp-llm anthropic retry/SSE warnings and imp-lua runtime/extension warnings remain to classify/convert.

This unit should be executed after 275.10/275.11 and preferably after simpler Google/Z.AI work unless sequence says otherwise.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:356:    .mana/archive/2026/04/275.8-port-pi-github-copilot-oauth-provider-into-imp.md:64:4. Implement OAuth device flow:
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:357:    .mana/archive/2026/04/275.8-port-pi-github-copilot-oauth-provider-into-imp.md:81:- Cursor support.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:358:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.1-fix-direct-imp-run-provider-auth-resolution-and-ig.md:14:  1. `crates/imp-core/src/imp_session.rs` special-cases `openai` in `resolve_api_key()` to `auth_store.resolve_api_key_only("openai")`, which bypasses saved OpenAI/ChatGPT OAuth even though `AuthStore::resolve("openai")` would prefer stored OAuth before api-key/env sources.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:359:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.1-fix-direct-imp-run-provider-auth-resolution-and-ig.md:17:  4. `AuthStore::resolve()` already implements the user's preferred precedence (`runtime override -> stored OAuth -> stored/api-key field -> env`). The bug is that `imp_session` / direct worker code bypasses that path for `openai`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:360:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.1-fix-direct-imp-run-provider-auth-resolution-and-ig.md:47:  Settled product default from root planning: direct/headless `imp run` should resolve credentials in this order: saved OAuth/auth -> saved API key -> environment variable. Do not keep any empty-string or silent auth-failure fallback in the headless/no-tools path. If the selected API/auth path fails, fail immediately and surface an explanatory error instead of degrading silently.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:361:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.1-fix-direct-imp-run-provider-auth-resolution-and-ig.md:55:  Starting execution from the agreed near-term implementation order. Will run the unit verify gate first, then inspect the headless/session auth resolution path and apply the settled precedence: saved OAuth/auth -> saved API key -> env, with fail-fast errors and no empty-string fallback.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:362:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.1-fix-direct-imp-run-provider-auth-resolution-and-ig.md:66:  - shared `resolve_runtime_connection()` now selects the OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex route model-first when OAuth exists
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:363:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.1-fix-direct-imp-run-provider-auth-resolution-and-ig.md:80:  1. `cargo run -p imp-cli -- --provider openai -m gpt-5.4-nano --no-tools --max-turns 1 --mode json mana 268 --defer-verify` no longer failed with `Incorrect API key provided: ''`; instead it reached the provider and returned HTTP 400 `The 'gpt-5.4-nano' model is not supported when using Codex with a ChatGPT account.` This is positive auth-routing evidence: the runtime resolved saved ChatGPT/Codex OAuth instead of sending an empty API key.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:364:    .mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.1-fix-direct-imp-run-provider-auth-resolution-and-ig.md:109:- `imp secrets list` shows saved OpenAI/ChatGPT OAuth entries, and there is no `OPENAI_API_KEY` env var in the shell.

.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:367:    .mana/archive/2026/04/275.7-add-zai-api-key-provider-support-to-imp.md:49:Current state: Known evidence from official docs says Z.AI uses API key or JWT-token auth, not OAuth. Base endpoint: `https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4`; coding endpoint: `https://api.z.ai/api/coding/paas/v4`; auth header: `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`; env var in Pi: `ZAI_API_KEY`. imp currently does not have first-class Z.AI provider entries. This unit should implement API-key support only unless the plan doc contains new official OAuth evidence.
