.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:381:    .mana/275-assess-and-sequence-next-llm-oauth-provider-integr.md:3:title: Port Pi provider and OAuth compatibility into imp
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:382:    .mana/275-assess-and-sequence-next-llm-oauth-provider-integr.md:9:acceptance: 'imp reaches rough parity with Pi''s practical provider/OAuth compatibility for high-value local-agent providers: Google Gemini CLI OAuth / Cloud Code Assist, Google Antigravity if accepted after review, Z.AI API-key/Coding Plan support, GitHub Copilot OAuth, and exploratory unofficial Cursor support if a stable user-consented credential route is found. Each shipped provider has setup/model-picker visibility, auth refresh or secret handling, targeted tests, docs/help updates, and a clear risk note for unofficial integrations.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:383:    .mana/275-assess-and-sequence-next-llm-oauth-provider-integr.md:13:  Initial decomposition from discussion: rank likely candidates as Google Gemini first (most plausible official OAuth path, but verify Google app/scopes and whether OAuth unlocks subscription-backed Gemini access), Zai second (investigate whether OAuth exists or API-key/OpenAI-compatible route is the right path), Cursor third/highest risk (only consider if official or stable user-consented credential import exists; avoid brittle/private session scraping).
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:384:    .mana/275-assess-and-sequence-next-llm-oauth-provider-integr.md:17:  Research direction updated from user: goal should be matching Pi's provider and OAuth compatibility rather than evaluating only isolated provider requests. Source repo discovered at /Users/asher/pi-mono (not ~/pi-core). Pi's AI package includes OAuth providers for Anthropic, GitHub Copilot, Google Gemini CLI / Cloud Code Assist, Google Antigravity, and OpenAI Codex; Z.AI appears API-key/JWT based in docs, not OAuth. Cursor has official CLI browser login/API key docs but no inspected provider implementation in Pi yet.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:385:    .mana/275-assess-and-sequence-next-llm-oauth-provider-integr.md:25:- 'Scope target: imp should aim for Pi provider/OAuth compatibility where safe, using /Users/asher/pi-mono/packages/ai as the reference implementation rather than inventing provider behavior from scratch.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:386:    .mana/275-assess-and-sequence-next-llm-oauth-provider-integr.md:26:- 'Cursor policy: unofficial Cursor support is allowed to be researched and potentially implemented, but only with user-consented credentials from an official CLI/API-key/browser-login flow or a stable local credential file. Do not implement silent browser cookie scraping or brittle hidden-token extraction.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:387:    .mana/275-assess-and-sequence-next-llm-oauth-provider-integr.md:29:Port Pi's practical provider and OAuth compatibility into imp while preserving imp's runtime/auth boundaries.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:388:    .mana/275-assess-and-sequence-next-llm-oauth-provider-integr.md:36:- Google Gemini CLI / Cloud Code Assist OAuth (`google-gemini-cli`) from Pi.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:389:    .mana/275-assess-and-sequence-next-llm-oauth-provider-integr.md:39:- GitHub Copilot OAuth/device flow (`github-copilot`) from Pi.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:390:    .mana/275-assess-and-sequence-next-llm-oauth-provider-integr.md:40:- Unofficial Cursor support if possible, but only through user-consented official CLI/API-key/stable local credential paths.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:391:    .mana/275-assess-and-sequence-next-llm-oauth-provider-integr.md:44:2. Cursor unofficial support is allowed to be researched and potentially implemented, but no silent browser-cookie scraping, hidden token extraction, token printing, or credential exfiltration.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:392:    .mana/273-diagnose-and-harden-kimi-code-oauth-model-routing.md:3:title: Diagnose and harden Kimi Code OAuth model routing
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:393:    .mana/273-diagnose-and-harden-kimi-code-oauth-model-routing.md:9:acceptance: A user who completes `imp login kimi` can select and use an OAuth-backed Kimi Code model without accidentally routing to Moonshot API-key auth. If `kimi` remains Moonshot-only, the UI/CLI must make that distinction actionable. Verification should include targeted auth/model routing tests and `cargo check -p imp-cli -p imp-llm -p imp-tui`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:394:    .mana/273-diagnose-and-harden-kimi-code-oauth-model-routing.md:13:  Implemented first pass of option 1 auth-aware routing. In `crates/imp-core/src/imp_session.rs`, runtime model resolution now uses an `auth_preferred_oauth_route` helper: existing OpenAI→openai-codex fallback remains, and Kimi/Moonshot aliases now route to `kimi-code`/`kimi-for-coding` when Kimi Code OAuth exists and no Moonshot API key/API-key override is present. If Moonshot API key exists, `kimi` remains Moonshot (`kimi-k2.6`). In `crates/imp-tui/src/app.rs`, model picker now includes `kimi-for-coding` when Kimi Code OAuth exists and no Moonshot API key exists. Added targeted tests for both routing and picker cases. Verification passed: `cargo fmt --package imp-core --package imp-tui`; `cargo test -p imp-core resolve_runtime_connection -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-tui filtered_model_options -- --nocapture`; `cargo check -p imp-cli -p imp-llm -p imp-tui`. Still not proven: live Kimi Code HTTP endpoint compatibility (`https://api.kimi.com/coding/v1/chat/completions`) against real OAuth token.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:395:    .mana/273-diagnose-and-harden-kimi-code-oauth-model-routing.md:17:  User asked to live-test whether Kimi OAuth works. Next step: inspect available stored auth without exposing token values, then run a minimal Kimi Code/OAuth request through imp if credentials exist. If no usable Kimi OAuth credential exists locally, report exact command/user action needed to complete login.
.mana/archive/2026/04/279-evaluate-next-llm-oauth-provider-integrations-for.md:396:    .mana/273-diagnose-and-harden-kimi-code-oauth-model-routing.md:21:  Live Kimi OAuth test results: local `~/.imp/auth.json` contains `kimi-code` OAuth credentials and `~/.kimi/credentials/kimi-code.json` exists. Direct HTTP POST to `https://api.kimi.com/coding/v1/chat/completions` with Kimi Code bearer token + Kimi CLI headers returned HTTP 200 for both non-streaming and streaming requests using model `kimi-for-coding`. Non-streaming body for prompt `What is 2+2? Answer with the number only.` returned assistant `content:"4"` plus `reasoning_content`, usage 20/33/53. Streaming returned SSE chunks with `reasoning_content` first and HTTP 200. However, running through current `imp-cli` print mode with `--provider kimi-code --model kimi-for-coding` completes with zero token usage and no visible text; this likely means imp's streaming parser/agent path is not surfacing final non-empty content for Kimi Code, or max-token/forced reasoning behavior causes reasoning-only stream with no visible text in the print-mode output. Next implementation should add a Kimi Code provider/streaming regression using mocked SSE where reasoning-only chunks are followed by content, and inspect whether the live stream eventually emits content with sufficient max tokens and whether `MessageEnd` content is dropped from print mode when no TextDelta is emitted.
.mana/archive/2026/04/81.4-define-policy-isolation-and-migration-constraints.md:12:  Parent design note now includes the v1 policy/isolation/migration section. It keeps nested authority conservative, requires mutating child work to externalize into mana, keeps `mana run` compatibility secondary, and leaves the runtime seam runner-ready rather than baking shell subprocess assumptions into the long-term design.
.mana/archive/2026/04/81.4-define-policy-isolation-and-migration-constraints.md:16:- mana

.mana/archive/2026/05/350.4.5-run-tui-responsiveness-smoke-suite-and-reconcile-p.md:17:  Ran focused TUI responsiveness smoke suite: all targeted tests passed. Covered event_source, keybindings, terminal restore, @ plain text input, runtime signal batching, stream render cache ignoring animation tick, TUI tracing, /resume background listing/opening, session picker, /mana background loading, mana navigator, /status background task, /improve merge background task, /clean background task, send-message redraw deferral, agent task replacement/cancel, startup surface metadata cache, startup skill detail cache, OAuth/model metadata caches.
.mana/archive/2026/05/350.4.5-run-tui-responsiveness-smoke-suite-and-reconcile-p.md:56:Run a focused post-refactor verification pass for the TUI responsiveness architecture work. Include targeted cargo tests for event source, runtime signal batching, background /resume, /mana, /status, /improve merge, /clean, render cache tests, startup metadata caches, and known input regressions. Then update/close 350.4.1-350.4.4 as appropriate, explicitly noting the known unrelated full-suite editor-label failure if still present.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.4-design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns.md:3:title: Design UI/runtime affordances for reviewing mana between turns
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.4-design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns.md:4:slug: design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.4-design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns.md:12:  Input from 28.1: the review affordance must support a compact between-turn mana delta whenever durable planning state changes. Minimum reviewable payload should include updated unit ids, newly proposed child units, unresolved decision entries, and an explicit no-change case. The loop should feel continuous but not spammy: update mana quietly, show the delta, ask only for consequential unresolved choices.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.4-design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns.md:16:  Input from 28.1 planning policy: the between-turn review surface should assume imp updates mana before speaking when durable planning state changed, then shows a compact delta rather than raw status noise. Review affordances should expose: reused/created epic, new child jobs, appended notes, recorded decisions, and remaining consequential unresolved choices. The UX target is continuous reviewability of mana mutations without requiring a separate manual status check and without flooding the transcript.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.4-design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns.md:20:  Inspected current imp TUI and wizard surfaces before writing the spec. Findings: imp already has a generic SetWidget/SetStatus path and a 5-line widget tray in imp-tui/src/app.rs, plus WidgetContent::Component in imp-core/src/ui.rs, so a compact between-turn mana delta can ship without a new UI stack. wizard already has card/canvas/runtime scaffolding (wizard/app/desktop/src/cards.ts, canvas.ts, runtime.ts; wizard/crates/wizard-orch/src/lib.rs) suitable for a later richer graph/canvas inspector, but it is clearly deferred/reference scope rather than a required first implementation surface.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.4-design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns.md:24:  Drafted `docs/rebuild/mana-between-turn-review-affordance.md` grounded in current repo reality. Key design decision: compact between-turn mana review must ship in imp first, with Wizard as later richer graph/canvas inspector. Spec defines a canonical turn-batched mana delta (including explicit no_change payload), minimum visible fields (anchor/touched ids/new child jobs/material notes/decision changes/unresolved consequential choices), material-change trigger rules, and the active-conversation loop: mutate mana first, show compact delta, ask only for consequential unresolved choices, then allow inspect/edit/approve/prune. Grounded in inspected imp-core mana delta widget plumbing (`imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs`), existing imp-tui widget tray/sidebar surfaces, and Wizard typed-card/focus-room/review scaffolding.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.4-design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns.md:28:  Drafted `docs/rebuild/mana-between-turn-review-affordance.md`. Core design decisions: (1) imp owns the between-turn mana review loop and turn-level delta aggregation because it performs the mutations during conversation; (2) Wizard is the later rich graph/canvas inspector, not a first-version dependency; (3) minimum payload is explicit no_change/changed/needs_decision state plus touched unit ids/titles, reused/created epic, new child jobs, mutation kinds, unresolved decisions, and one next consequential choice; (4) first ship should replace per-call last-write-wins mana widget updates with a turn-scoped aggregated review packet rendered through existing set_widget/set_status surfaces in imp.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.4-design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns.md:33:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.4-design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns.md:37:verify: test -n "mana-review-affordance-spec"
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.4-design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns.md:57:Goal: design how imp and/or Wizard should expose mana state inline so the user can review the persistent work/idea graph between turns.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.4-design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns.md:59:Current state: even when mana is used, the user does not automatically get good visual review of the evolving unit graph during ordinary conversation turns.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.4-design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns.md:62:1. Define the minimum mana information that should be visible between turns.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.4-design-ui-runtime-affordances-for-reviewing-mana-between-turns.md:64:3. Define the interaction loop for reviewing, editing, approving, or pruning mana structure during active conversations.

.mana/archive/2026/04/44.1.4-plan-imp-local-subsystem-boundary-for-code-intelli.md:12:  Ground this child in the completed strategy note: recommend an imp-local code-intelligence subsystem seam first (module or later crate) for backend discovery, normalization, policy hooks, freshness semantics, and evidence extraction. Do not recommend a shared live code-intelligence runtime crate across `mana` and `imp` yet; only narrow durable typed contracts may later justify sharing.
.mana/archive/2026/04/44.1.4-plan-imp-local-subsystem-boundary-for-code-intelli.md:16:  Grounded design in existing docs/design/code-intelligence-strategy.md, docs/design/imp-read-oriented-code-intelligence-plan.md, docs/design/imp-write-oriented-code-intelligence-policy.md, and current imp-core layout. Current repo reality: imp-core code intelligence is still tool-local today, primarily scan under imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/scan plus generic tool infrastructure in tools/mod.rs. Planned note will recommend an imp-local subsystem seam first for backend discovery, normalization, policy hooks, freshness semantics, and evidence extraction; it will explicitly reject a shared live mana/imp runtime crate for now and keep durable contracts narrow and separate from live orchestration.
.mana/archive/2026/04/44.1.4-plan-imp-local-subsystem-boundary-for-code-intelli.md:20:  Wrote docs/design/imp-code-intelligence-subsystem.md. Grounded the note in current repo reality (imp-core tools/scan and tools/mod.rs) plus existing strategy docs. Final recommendation: introduce an imp-local code-intelligence subsystem seam first, likely imp-core/src/codeintel/, owning backend discovery, normalization, routing, freshness semantics, policy hooks, summarization, and evidence extraction. Keep tool schemas/registration/thin wrappers in tools/ for now. Explicitly states that a shared live mana/imp runtime crate is not recommended; only narrow durable typed evidence/result contracts may later justify sharing. Verify gate passed.

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-skillnudge-state-into-workflow-runtime-i.md:3:title: Move mana skill/nudge state into workflow runtime integration layer
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-skillnudge-state-into-workflow-runtime-i.md:4:slug: move-mana-skillnudge-state-into-workflow-runtime-i
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-skillnudge-state-into-workflow-runtime-i.md:11:  - `Agent` no longer exposes `has_mana_skill`, `has_mana_basics_skill`, `has_mana_delegation_skill`, or `queued_mana_externalization_nudge` as core fields.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-skillnudge-state-into-workflow-runtime-i.md:12:  - Mana skill availability and externalization nudge state live inside `WorkflowRuntimeLayer`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-skillnudge-state-into-workflow-runtime-i.md:13:  - Builder and tests configure mana skill availability through named agent/workflow integration methods.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-skillnudge-state-into-workflow-runtime-i.md:20:  Implementation complete. Mana skill availability and externalization nudge state moved out of core `Agent` fields and into `WorkflowRuntimeLayer`. Builder now configures availability through `set_workflow_mana_skill_available`, `set_workflow_mana_basics_skill_available`, and `set_workflow_mana_delegation_skill_available`; runtime marks repeated externalization nudges through `mark_workflow_externalization_nudge_queued`. Tests were updated to use the named workflow integration methods. Verification passed: `mana verify 365.9`, `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work`, `cargo check -p imp-core`, active native scope `work next` returned 0 ready task(s), and `work validate` returned 0 errors/0 warnings. Direct rg acceptance check confirms only `workflow_integration.rs` owns `queued_mana_externalization_nudge` and mana skill flags; no `agent.has_mana*`, `self.has_mana*`, or core public mana skill fields remain.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-skillnudge-state-into-workflow-runtime-i.md:55:Continue slimming imp runtime by moving mana skill availability flags and queued externalization nudge state out of the core `Agent` struct and into `WorkflowRuntimeLayer`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-skillnudge-state-into-workflow-runtime-i.md:58:- `Agent` still directly stores `has_mana_skill`, `has_mana_basics_skill`, `has_mana_delegation_skill`, and `queued_mana_externalization_nudge`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.1.5-plan-guarded-write-oriented-semantic-actions-and-p.md:20:  Child `44.1.5.1` is now the concrete planning target for the typed semantic-write policy model rather than a high-level placeholder. Its durable contract now specifies: (1) an `imp`-local `SemanticWritePolicyModel` root container; (2) a closed action taxonomy for `RenameSymbol`, `OrganizeImports`, and typed `CodeAction`; (3) a backend/language capability matrix with explicit trust and risk signals; (4) separate approval and verify posture fields; (5) a typed risk-input record covering file-count thresholds, workspace scope, generated/vendored/policy-sensitive file detection, stale state, and opaque-command detection; and (6) minimum preview/result metadata required for policy evaluation. This preserves the translation from prose policy note to implementable runtime model in mana rather than leaving it implicit in docs/chat.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.1.5-plan-guarded-write-oriented-semantic-actions-and-p.md:28:  Additional durable externalization after closing `44.1.5.1`: the semantic-write branch now has a concrete typed-policy contract in mana, not just a prose note. Keep the visible decomposition under this parent as: `44.1.5.1` typed policy/config model (completed), `44.1.5.2` execution contract for preview/checkpoint/approval/verify (open), and `44.1.5.3` backend/language risk tiers plus initial allowlist strategy (open). The closed policy-model slice established the exact implementation-facing categories another worker should inherit: root policy container, closed action taxonomy, capability tiers, trust/risk signals, separate approval vs verify posture fields, typed escalation inputs, and mandatory preview/result metadata envelopes.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.1.5-plan-guarded-write-oriented-semantic-actions-and-p.md:32:  Typed semantic-write policy model is now externalized in `docs/design/imp-semantic-write-policy-model.md` as the implementation-facing planning artifact for this slice. The artifact defines: (1) normalized typed action/request vocabulary for `rename`, `organize_imports`, and allowlisted `code_action` kinds; (2) backend tiers versus language risk tiers plus explicit capability-matrix row shape; (3) imp-local approval posture and verify posture types distinct from generic text-edit and mana closure state; (4) typed escalation inputs covering file-count/blast-radius, workspace scope, generated/vendored/policy-sensitive files, opaque-command detection, stale backend/index state, degraded diagnostics, and preview truncation; and (5) minimum preview/result envelopes required for policy evaluation. Follow-on planning should treat this doc as the canonical typed model consumed by the execution-contract and backend-risk slices rather than re-deriving fields from prose.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.1.5-plan-guarded-write-oriented-semantic-actions-and-p.md:36:  2026-04-09 follow-on artifact for typed policy model completed at `docs/design/imp-semantic-write-policy-model.md`. This note turns the approved semantic-write architecture into an implementation-facing contract: typed action classes (`Rename`, `OrganizeImports`, normalized allowlisted `CodeAction` kinds), backend/language capability tiers (`SemanticBackendTier`, `LanguageRiskTier`, `SemanticWriteSupportTier`), distinct `ApprovalPosture` and runtime `VerifyPosture` fields, typed escalation inputs/signals, and minimum preview/result envelopes required before semantic-write implementation. It remains scoped to write-oriented semantic actions only and explicitly preserves the distinction from plain edits and the general script sandbox. Also observed a root-mana resolution mismatch for child `44.1.5.1`: it appears in list/tree output but native `show`/`notes_append` cannot resolve it and no backing `.mana/44.1.5.1-*.md` file exists. Parent note updated so the planning outcome is still durable.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.1.5-plan-guarded-write-oriented-semantic-actions-and-p.md:60:  Path reconciliation for archived child `44.1.5.3`: renamed the authored artifact to `docs/design/imp-semantic-write-backend-risk-matrix.md` so the repo now matches the archived mana record and close-reason text for that unit. Treat this as the canonical rollout-facing risk/allowlist companion to `docs/design/imp-semantic-write-policy-model.md`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.1.5-plan-guarded-write-oriented-semantic-actions-and-p.md:87:- Initial semantic-write rollout must stay backend/language/action-row specific rather than globally enabled. For the current Tower repo, the repo-local allowlist should start Rust-first because the active workspace is Rust crates under `mana/`, `imp/`, and `tower-contracts`, while non-Rust manifests currently appear only under deferred `wizard/`. Within that Rust-first rollout, `OrganizeImports` is the strongest first guarded candidate; `Rename` comes later with `AlwaysAsk` plus package-scoped deterministic verify; allowlisted `CodeAction` kinds remain narrow, edit-only, and mostly experimental; command-backed, fix-all, refactor, and unknown actions remain blocked in the initial phase.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.1.5-plan-guarded-write-oriented-semantic-actions-and-p.md:88:- Semantic-write rollout must stay backend/language/action-row specific rather than globally enabled. For the current Tower repo, the repo-local allowlist should start Rust-first because the active workspace is Rust crates under `mana/`, `imp/`, and `tower-contracts`, while non-Rust manifests currently appear only under deferred `wizard/`. Within that Rust-first rollout, `OrganizeImports` is the strongest first guarded candidate; `Rename` comes later with `AlwaysAsk` plus package-scoped deterministic verify; allowlisted `CodeAction` kinds remain narrow, edit-only, and mostly experimental; command-backed, fix-all, refactor, and unknown actions remain blocked in the initial phase.

.mana/archive/2026/05/330.4-wire-native-mana-mutations-into-turnmanareview-rel.md:3:title: Wire native mana mutations into TurnManaReview reliably
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.4-wire-native-mana-mutations-into-turnmanareview-rel.md:4:slug: wire-native-mana-mutations-into-turnmanareview-rel
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.4-wire-native-mana-mutations-into-turnmanareview-rel.md:9:acceptance: TurnManaReview is populated by successful mana mutations, and a focused test proves create/update/notes/decision actions appear in the turn review emitted by AgentEvent::TurnEnd.
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.4-wire-native-mana-mutations-into-turnmanareview-rel.md:13:  Implemented runtime-side TurnManaReview population from successful mana tool results. `run_loop` records mutation records after tool execution and before `finish_turn_mana_review`; read-only actions and failed results are ignored. Current implementation records actions with unit snapshots when result details include a `unit`, delete via deleted unit ref, and dep add/remove as mutation records without snapshots. This complements, rather than changes, `tools/mana.rs` behavior.

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-workflow-contract-into-workflow-runtime-integ.md:20:  Implementation complete after failed-command recovery. Root cause of failed check: a mechanical replacement left direct field syntax (`self.workflow_contract.id`) after `workflow_contract` became a method backed by `WorkflowRuntimeLayer`. Fixed by replacing remaining field reads in `agent/mod.rs`, `agent/tool_execution.rs`, `agent/workflow_integration.rs`, and builder tests with `workflow_contract()`/`workflow_contract_mut()` accessors. `Agent` no longer exposes `workflow_contract` as a core public field; `WorkflowRuntimeLayer` owns `WorkflowContract`; builder applies contracts via `set_workflow_contract`; tool execution/evidence/tests read through named methods. Verification passed: `mana verify 365.9`, `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work`, `cargo check -p imp-core`; active native scope inspected with `work next` (0 ready task(s)) and `work validate` (0 errors/0 warnings).
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.8.2.1-make-anchored-edits-first-class.md:17:  Native mana verify still reports exit 1 for anchored verify command after updating verify string, while equivalent shell commands passed earlier (`cargo test -p imp-core anchored -- --nocapture` and `cargo test -p imp-core edit --no-run`). Treat as known mana verify anomaly for this thread; use shell verify evidence and proceed with targeted commit if user approves.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.8.2.1-make-anchored-edits-first-class.md:21:  Next execution plan before continuing: finish the dirty anchored-edit follow-up by closing 304.8.2.1 with shell verification evidence because native mana verify is anomalous; ensure the commit is targeted to `crates/imp-core/src/tools/edit.rs` only; inspect HEAD/status after close/commit. After anchored edit is clean, recommended next implementation unit is 304.7.1 `Implement ask_user tool redesign` because it is contained and user-facing. Alternative next units are 304.9.1 scan cleanup or bash rename/secrets, but those are larger/more consequential.

If confirmed, suppress the proactive mana-skill follow-up hint for headless/no-tools worker runs (or more narrowly for worker-mode single-unit headless execution) so a successful first turn can stop cleanly.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.4-fix-headless-worker-loop-so-successful-one-turn-ru.md:68:  2026-04-22: resuming this unit next because the fresh proving harness confirmed the one-turn headless/runtime bug is still relevant. Plan is to test the queued follow-up hypothesis in `agent.rs` before broadening scope: inspect `mana_skill_follow_up_hint(...)`, `queued_follow_ups`, and worker/headless no-tools session startup, then add the narrowest regression around the actual failing path.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.4-fix-headless-worker-loop-so-successful-one-turn-ru.md:72:  2026-04-22 progress: confirmed the queued follow-up hypothesis and implemented the narrow guard in `crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs`. `mana_skill_follow_up_hint(...)` now receives `tools_available` and returns `None` when the agent has no tools, which prevents worker/headless no-tools runs from auto-queueing a second turn. Added focused regressions: `agent_does_not_queue_mana_basics_hint_when_no_tools_available` and `single_text_turn_with_max_turns_one_and_no_tools_exits_cleanly`. Verification: both new tests pass, along with existing `single_text_turn_with_max_turns_one_exits_cleanly`. `cargo test -p imp-cli run_headless -- --nocapture` currently reports 0 matching tests, so the unit verify command does not yet exercise a headless regression directly.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.4-fix-headless-worker-loop-so-successful-one-turn-ru.md:80:  2026-04-23 completed. Final fix consisted of two narrow changes proven together: (1) suppress proactive mana skill follow-up hints when no tools are available so one-turn worker/no-tools runs do not enqueue a spurious second turn; (2) make `ImpSession::recv_event()` return `None` once the run has already completed (`agent_task.is_none()` and `completed_run_result.is_some()`), instead of waiting forever for the event channel to close while the restored agent still owns an event sender. Added coverage in both layers: `imp-core` tests for no-tools worker follow-up suppression and post-AgentEnd `recv_event()` completion semantics, plus `imp-cli` integration-style test `run_headless_no_tools_worker_single_turn_exits_cleanly` proving the headless worker path emits `SMOKE_OK` and no `Max turns exceeded` error with `max_turns = 1`. Verify used: cargo test -p imp-core single_text_turn_with_max_turns_one_and_no_tools_exits_cleanly -- --nocapture && cargo test -p imp-core agent_does_not_queue_mana_basics_hint_when_no_tools_available -- --nocapture && cargo test -p imp-core recv_event_returns_none_after_agent_end_even_if_sender_is_still_owned -- --nocapture && cargo test -p imp-cli run_headless_no_tools_worker_single_turn_exits_cleanly -- --nocapture
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.4-add-redacted-turntool-recovery-ledger.md:13:  Starting implementation. Scope from unit: add redacted durable recovery ledger only; no WebSocket/resume behavior. Must avoid raw prompts, raw tool args, raw tool output, secrets, or model text. Plan: inspect current SessionEntry serialization and tests, add RecoveryCheckpoint data + SessionEntry variant, emit AgentEvent::RecoveryCheckpoint at provider/tool/context boundaries, persist through SessionManager::persist_agent_event_entries, and add filtered tests containing 'recovery'.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.4-add-redacted-turntool-recovery-ledger.md:15:close_reason: 'Implemented redacted turn/tool recovery ledger. Added RecoveryCheckpointKind/RecoveryCheckpoint and AgentEvent::RecoveryCheckpoint; emitted metadata-only checkpoints for provider_request_start, assistant_tool_call_observed, provider_request_completed, tool_execution_start, tool_execution_end, and tool_result_added_to_context. Persisted checkpoints as session custom entries with custom_type recovery-checkpoint via SessionManager::append_recovery_checkpoint / persist_agent_event_entries. CLI JSON/RPC surfaces recovery_checkpoint events; TUI ignores them to avoid user noise. Checkpoints include turn, kind, optional tool_call_id/tool_name, args_hash, success/error_class, timestamp; they do not include raw prompts, model text, raw args, tool output, or secrets. Added recovery ledger tests for redaction and persistence. Verification passed: cargo test -p imp-core recovery --lib; cargo check -p imp-cli; cargo check -p imp-tui; rg "Recovery|checkpoint|tool_execution_start" crates/imp-core/src/session.rs crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs crates/imp-core/src/imp_session.rs.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.4-add-redacted-turntool-recovery-ledger.md:143:    test tools::mana::tests::close_verify_errors_include_recovery_hint ... ok

.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.2-implement-reusable-imp-side-mana-unit-worker-runti.md:13:  - `WorkerAssignment` struct — canonical execution bundle loaded via mana-core APIs
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.2-implement-reusable-imp-side-mana-unit-worker-runti.md:15:  - `WorkerAttempt` — attempt history from mana unit records

.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.2-implement-reusable-imp-side-mana-unit-worker-runti.md:27:  - Old CLI-local types (`ManaUnit`, `UnitFrontmatter`, `UnitAttempt`, `load_mana_unit`, `find_mana_dir`, `parse_mana_unit`, `split_frontmatter`, `parse_file_spec_str`, `format_attempt`) are now dead code — left in place for now to avoid breaking their tests; a follow-up cleanup can remove them.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.2-implement-reusable-imp-side-mana-unit-worker-runti.md:29:  Verified: `cargo check -p imp-core` ✅, `cargo check -p imp-cli` ✅ (warnings only for now-dead old code), `cargo test -p imp-core mana_worker` ✅ (9/9 pass).
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.2-implement-reusable-imp-side-mana-unit-worker-runti.md:31:  Note: the old CLI-local types and their tests still exist but are unused. A small cleanup pass can remove them once we're confident the new path is stable. The verify/close shell-out to `mana close` also still exists in the human convenience path — that will be addressed in 28.1.3 when mana run integration lands.

.mana/archive/2026/04/29-make-imps-existing-capabilities-discoverable-in-th.md:54:Desired outcome: a user opening imp’s TUI should be able to discover that imp remembers useful context across sessions, can help recall past work, can resume/fork sessions, and uses mana as the canonical planning/dependency system. The user should be able to learn this from the welcome/setup flow, the command palette, and `/help` without reading the README.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:9:acceptance: A design note or mana decisions describe how imp handles connection drops and replay hazards in long-running sessions, including what state is durable, what can be retried, and what must not be replayed without confirmation.

.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:30:  5. Close 317.3 when mana decisions/notes describe recovery semantics and verify gate passes.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:40:  - Tool execution receives ToolContext with cancellation token, checkpoint_state, file_tracker, turn_mana_review, and config.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:54:  - ToolContext includes cancellation token, update channel, command_tx, UI, file_cache, checkpoint_state, file_tracker, anchor_store, Lua loader, mode, turn_mana_review, and config.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:56:  - Many tools are side-effecting despite not all side effects being file edits: bash, git mutating actions, mana updates/runs, write/edit/multi_edit, worktree mutating actions, imp spawn, memory, extend.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:60:  - SessionManager persists AgentEvent::ToolExecutionEnd as a tool result and AgentEvent::TurnEnd as an assistant message plus canonical usage when present.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:65:  Mana run-state persistence (crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs):
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:67:  - ManaRunStore persists only on finish/fail; update_with_event mutates in-memory store during a run but does not persist every event.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:68:  - Background native mana runs run in a spawned task and update store via event sink; finish/fail persist. A process crash mid-run can lose recent in-memory event updates and leave started/running state stale depending on prior persistence.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:76:close_reason: 'Completed design/decision unit. Inspected agent turn loop, tool execution, retry boundaries, tool semantics, session persistence, checkpoint state, ImpSession cancellation/abort, and native mana run-state persistence. Recorded durable decisions for provider retry boundary, tool replay policy, checkpoint boundaries, and dropped-connection cases. Created follow-up implementation/design tasks for concrete gaps: 317.4 redacted turn/tool recovery ledger, 317.5 interrupted mana run-state persistence/classification, 317.6 persistent transport safety gate. Verify passed conceptually with native mana inspection: mana show 317.3 and mana tree 317 now expose decisions and child tasks.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:80:verify: mana show 317 && mana tree 317
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:89:  output_snippet: 'verify passed: mana show 317 && mana tree 317'
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:100:      "description": "Goal: Make imp faster and more resilient for long-running, tool-heavy agent workflows by measuring loop latency first, then introducing provider transport capabilities and persistent-session support where justified.\n\nContext: User reviewed OpenAI's article on speeding up agentic workflows with Responses API WebSockets and agreed the directions are worth pursuing. The strategic sequence is measurement first, provider-neutral capability modeling second, then optional OpenAI-specific persistent transport behind existing provider boundaries.\n\nIn scope:\n- Agent-loop latency tracing across model requests, streaming, context assembly, tool execution, retries, mana persistence, and UI/runtime overhead.\n- Provider capability metadata for stateless HTTP, streaming, continuation IDs, persistent sessions/WebSockets, cancellation, and resumability.\n- Long-session recovery semantics: connection drops, completed tool calls, replay avoidance, durable checkpoints.\n- Optional OpenAI-specific WebSocket transport after interfaces and measurement justify it.\n- Mana/run-state integration as durable execution ledger for long-running workflows.\n\nOut of scope:\n- Rewriting imp around OpenAI-specific API semantics.\n- Making WebSockets the default provider abstraction.\n- Broad provider rewrites before measurement exists.\n- Claiming equivalent support across Anthropic/OpenRouter/local providers without inspection.\n\nPreferred sequence:\n1. Instrument current agent loop and establish latency evidence.\n2. Add provider transport capability modeling with current providers mapped conservatively.\n3. Design recovery/checkpoint semantics for long sessions.\n4. Prototype OpenAI WebSocket mode behind provider boundary and feature/capability checks.\n5. Compare latency before/after on representative tool-heavy workflows.",
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:102:      "notes": "---\n2026-05-01T18:40:25.077567+00:00\nDurable sequencing confirmed by user and externalized before further work:\n1. Measure current agent-loop latency first to establish baseline evidence.\n2. Model provider transport capabilities conservatively before changing behavior.\n3. Define long-session recovery/replay semantics before persistent transports, especially for side-effecting tools.\n4. Prototype OpenAI WebSocket/persistent-session support only behind provider capability gates and compare against baseline.\n\nScope decision: keep this in imp project mana for now because the concrete work is in imp's agent runtime/provider/tool loop. Escalate to root mana only if the design changes mana/imp platform boundaries, shared provider contracts, or cross-app runtime APIs.\n\n---\n2026-05-01T18:42:22.495182+00:00\nExecution plan for closing this epic:\n- Work 317.1 first in-place, preserving current TimingEvent/AgentEvent::Timing path already present in crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs. Extend it rather than adding a tracing dependency.\n- Current inspection found existing timing stages only cover LLM request start, first stream event, first text delta, first tool call, and message end. Missing baseline coverage: context assembly/masking duration, retry/startup failures, tool execution duration/result status, post-turn assessment, and mana/runtime persistence visibility where reachable.\n- Keep all instrumentation metadata-only/redacted: stage names, turn index, elapsed milliseconds, tool name/status only; no prompts, args, outputs, model text, or secrets.\n- Avoid unrelated dirty worktree changes currently present (.DS_Store, .gitignore, CHANGELOG.md, crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs, etc.) unless the specific task requires them.\n- After 317.1 passes cargo check, move to 317.2 provider capability modeling, then 317.3 recovery semantics. Only create/prototype OpenAI WebSocket task after those pass and evidence justifies it.",
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:108:    ├── │   [ ] 317.5 Persist and classify interrupted mana run state
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.3-define-long-session-recovery-and-replay-semantics.md:133:1. Inspect current agent loop, tool execution, and mana run-state persistence paths.

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-externalization-nudges-behind-workflow-r.md:20:  Implementation complete. Fixed failed check root cause: after moving externalization helper functions into `workflow_integration.rs`, the module needed access to `assistant_message_contains_mana_tool_call` and `assistant_message_text`; added those imports from `super` and reran checks. Acceptance verified: `agent/run_loop.rs` no longer imports/calls `mana_skill_follow_up_hint`; `agent/mod.rs::assess_post_turn` no longer calls `should_queue_mana_externalization_follow_up` or `mana_externalization_follow_up_text`; heuristics live in `agent/workflow_integration.rs` behind `workflow_pre_turn_follow_up_hint` and `workflow_externalization_follow_up`. Checks passed: `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work`, `cargo check -p imp-core`, `mana verify 365.9`. Active native work scope inspected per instruction: `work next` reports 0 ready task(s), `work validate` reports 0 errors/0 warnings.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-externalization-nudges-behind-workflow-r.md:55:Continue slimming imp runtime by moving mana/work externalization nudges out of the core run loop/post-turn assessment and into the workflow runtime integration layer.

.mana/archive/2026/05/268.1-diagnose-native-imp-mana-tool-divergence-from-cli.md:3:title: Diagnose native imp mana-tool divergence from CLI archived lookup behavior
.mana/archive/2026/05/268.1-diagnose-native-imp-mana-tool-divergence-from-cli.md:4:slug: diagnose-native-imp-mana-tool-divergence-from-cli
.mana/archive/2026/05/268.1-diagnose-native-imp-mana-tool-divergence-from-cli.md:12:  Concrete divergence found (2026-04-22): imp's native `mana` tool is not equivalent to CLI `mana show` for archived units. In `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs`, action=`show` calls `mana_core::api::get_unit(&mana_dir, id)`, which only resolves active units. By contrast the CLI `mana show` path goes through `mana_core::ops::show::get(...)`, which falls back from `find_unit_file` to `find_archived_unit`, so archived IDs like `266.4.9` resolve correctly there. Likewise native action=`tree` uses `mana_core::api::get_tree(...)`, which explicitly excludes archived units, so archived subtrees disappear even though CLI `show` can still inspect archived items. This explains the earlier cleanup confusion: the in-session native mana tool was stricter than the CLI, not because archive lookup was broken globally but because the native tool used the narrower API path.
.mana/archive/2026/05/268.1-diagnose-native-imp-mana-tool-divergence-from-cli.md:18:  sh: target/debug/mana: No such file or directory
.mana/archive/2026/05/268.1-diagnose-native-imp-mana-tool-divergence-from-cli.md:24:  2026-05-21 resolution update: diagnosis was already complete. Native imp mana tool divergence came from using narrower `mana_core::api::get_unit/get_tree` paths that exclude archived units, while CLI `mana show` uses archive-aware ops lookup. However, current product direction has superseded a native mana-tool fix: imp-work parity/migration work now aims to defunct mana inside imp and make `.mana` import-only/legacy. Therefore the smallest reliable resolution is not to invest in archived lookup parity for the native mana tool unless a legacy/import blocker appears. Track remaining work under imp-work migration/removal tasks, not this mana-tool integration bug.
.mana/archive/2026/05/268.1-diagnose-native-imp-mana-tool-divergence-from-cli.md:26:- mana

Native mana archived lookup parity is no longer the desired outcome unless needed for import-only legacy mode; investing in this mana-tool bug would move against the current removal plan.

.mana/archive/2026/04/304.2.1.2-design-parser-backed-bash-command-policy.md:54:  Security posture / bypass risks: aliases/functions can hide real executables; `sh -c`/`bash -c`, `eval`, `source`, command substitution, subshells, here-docs, redirects to sensitive paths, encoded commands (`base64 | sh`), curl-to-shell, package-manager lifecycle scripts, PATH hijacking, env assignments, shell functions, and scripts invoked by name all limit confidence. Conservative default: deny or ask for eval/source/shell-c, command substitution, here-docs, unknown parser nodes, and pipelines involving network/download tools piped to interpreters. Parser policy is guardrail, not sandbox.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.2.1.2-design-parser-backed-bash-command-policy.md:65:close_reason: 'Completed design-only parser-backed bash command policy note in mana. Evidence inspected: `crates/imp-core/src/ui.rs` UserInterface confirm API, `crates/imp-core/src/config.rs` ShellConfig, and `crates/imp-core/src/tools/bash.rs` run_command integration point. Design recommends parser-backed pre-spawn allow/ask/deny classifier, config under ShellConfig policy, interactive confirm semantics with no-UI deny default, structured policy details, security bypass posture, rollout plan, and dependency candidates without adding dependencies or implementing policy. Verify passed: `rg "pub trait UserInterface|pub struct ShellConfig|async fn run_command" crates/imp-core/src -n`. Forced close because this was design-only mana note work and native close requires non-.mana file changes.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.2.1.2-design-parser-backed-bash-command-policy.md:77:  notes: 'Completed design-only parser-backed bash command policy note in mana. .mana/archive/2026/05/339.2-implement-imp-core-direct-mana-subagent-orchestrat.md:3:title: Implement imp-core direct mana subagent orchestration runtime
.mana/archive/2026/05/339.2-implement-imp-core-direct-mana-subagent-orchestrat.md:4:slug: implement-imp-core-direct-mana-subagent-orchestrat
.mana/archive/2026/05/339.2-implement-imp-core-direct-mana-subagent-orchestrat.md:9:acceptance: imp native mana(action=run) no longer links to mana-cli run command modules for dispatch; run_state/logs receive live structured events; jobs=4 dispatches workers directly; stale CLI signal state cannot affect native runs; targeted imp-core tests and cargo check pass.
.mana/archive/2026/05/339.2-implement-imp-core-direct-mana-subagent-orchestrat.md:13:  Started 339.2 after closing 339.1. Inspected imp single-unit worker runtime. Existing imp_core::mana_worker already provides direct in-process ImpSession worker execution (prepare_worker_run/run_worker_assignment/finalize_worker_run) and should be reused as the subagent substrate. Next implementation step is to replace tools/mana.rs run_with_stream_capture_and_sink calls with an async orchestration loop over mana_core::ops::run::compute_run_plan + mana_worker::run_worker_assignment, emitting compatible run state events. Need care: current tools/mana.rs imports RunView/RunSummary/RunUnitStatus/StreamEvent/NativeRunParams from mana-cli; replacing this cleanly likely requires local imp-owned run event/state structs or a compatibility adapter.
.mana/archive/2026/05/339.2-implement-imp-core-direct-mana-subagent-orchestrat.md:17:  Implemented first direct imp-core native mana orchestration path. tools/mana.rs now owns local run event/view structs, uses mana_core::ops::run::compute_run_plan for planning, and executes units through mana_worker::run_worker_assignment in-process instead of calling mana-cli run_with_stream_capture_and_sink. Background and foreground native run paths now use run_native_imp_orchestration. This removes the CLI signal/global shutdown-state dependency from imp native dispatch. Current limitation: execution is wave-ordered but serial within each wave; jobs concurrency is not yet honored in the direct runtime loop, so acceptance is only partially met for parallel jobs=4 behavior. Verification passed for imp-core mana tests and imp-core/imp-tui check. mana-cli still emits an unrelated warning about unused ready_queue::all_deps_closed after 339.1 refactor.
.mana/archive/2026/05/339.2-implement-imp-core-direct-mana-subagent-orchestrat.md:21:  Status caveat: direct native run no longer embeds mana-cli, but jobs concurrency remains incomplete. Current loop runs each wave serially. Need a follow-up concurrency implementation using bounded async tasks while preserving event updates, per-unit models, cancellation, keep_going semantics, and avoiding ToolContext/ImpSession sharing hazards.
.mana/archive/2026/05/339.2-implement-imp-core-direct-mana-subagent-orchestrat.md:29:  Blocker discovered before continuing: current /Users/asher/imp working tree does not contain the direct native mana run changes described in prior notes; git status only shows unrelated scan tool edits, and tools/mana.rs still calls mana::commands::run::run_with_stream_capture_and_sink at both background and foreground paths. Need to reconstruct or reapply 339.2 direct-run changes before implementing jobs concurrency.
.mana/archive/2026/05/339.2-implement-imp-core-direct-mana-subagent-orchestrat.md:33:  Reconstructed direct imp-core native mana run path in current worktree and implemented bounded per-wave concurrency. tools/mana.rs no longer calls mana-cli run_with_stream_capture_and_sink. Direct runner uses mana_core::ops::run::compute_run_plan, emits compatible StreamEvent state updates, launches units via mana_worker::run_worker_assignment, and batches each wave up to jobs.max(1) concurrent tokio tasks. It inherits ctx config model/thinking into NativeRunParams/run workers. Verified: cargo test -p imp-core tools::mana::tests:: --quiet && cargo check -p imp-core -p imp-tui. Remaining caveat for 339.2: direct worker event stream currently emits UnitStart/UnitDone/Round/Run events but not live UnitTool/UnitThinking token events from inside each subagent; run_state/logs still receive structured lifecycle events. Existing unrelated dirty scan tool changes remain in worktree.
.mana/archive/2026/05/339.2-implement-imp-core-direct-mana-subagent-orchestrat.md:38:close_reason: 'Implemented and verified direct imp-core native mana orchestration. crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs no longer calls mana-cli run_with_stream_capture_and_sink; native run uses mana_core::ops::run::compute_run_plan and launches direct imp workers through mana_worker::run_worker_assignment. jobs is clamped to at least 1 and honored as bounded per-wave concurrency via tokio tasks. Runtime model/thinking are inherited from ToolContext config. run_state/logs receive compatible RunStart/RoundStart/UnitStart/UnitDone/RoundEnd/RunEnd events. Verification passed: cd /Users/asher/imp && cargo test -p imp-core tools::mana::tests:: --quiet && cargo check -p imp-core -p imp-tui. Caveat: direct path does not yet forward live inner subagent UnitTool/UnitThinking/token events, but structured lifecycle events are present and no CLI signal/global shutdown state can affect dispatch.'

.mana/archive/2026/05/339.2-implement-imp-core-direct-mana-subagent-orchestrat.md:75:Replace crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs native run path that calls mana::commands::run::run_with_stream_capture_and_sink with direct imp runtime orchestration. Use mana-core planning/context APIs to select ready units and assemble worker prompts. Launch subagents through imp-core runtime/session APIs rather than command templates or mana-cli. Requirements: inherit current imp model and thinking defaults unless unit/config overrides; preserve jobs concurrency, dependencies, file/path conflict policy if still applicable, keep_going/loop/review semantics where supported; provide structured RunStart/UnitStart/UnitTool/UnitDone/RunEnd-style events for run_state/logs/TUI; support cancellation without process-global signal handlers; preserve retry guardrails. Avoid CLI stdout/stderr assumptions and shelling out to imp for native path.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:3:title: Define clean mana vs imp boundary and mana-first planning workflow
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:4:slug: define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:12:  Decomposition update from 28.1: the mana-first planning workflow is now specified as continuous conversation-time externalization rather than end-of-task logging. The durable split under this parent is now clearer: 28.1 owns the policy for automatic externalization triggers, artifact mapping (epic/job/note/decision), confirmation thresholds, and between-turn mana delta summaries; 28.4 owns the user-facing review affordances that expose those deltas and graph state between turns; 28.5 should later convert the agreed policy outputs from 28.1–28.4 into an implementation sequence across imp runtime/prompt behavior, mana-native update/create flows, and review surfaces.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:16:  28.1 durable policy output, surfaced onto the live parent graph for visibility: imp should treat planning/design conversations as conversation-time mana-maintenance work, not end-of-task logging. Automatic externalization triggers are: plan/design/architecture/decomposition requests; multi-outcome or multi-turn structure; discovered blockers/dependencies/handoffs; consequential forks worth preserving; user-supplied constraints or acceptance changes; and any case where a future worker would otherwise need transcript reconstruction. Artifact mapping: epic for stable multi-outcome goals, child job for concrete executable/reviewable sub-outcomes, notes for evolving rationale/constraints/risks/sequencing, and decisions for consequential forks or commitments. Confirmation threshold: imp should auto-capture low-risk structure but still ask before consequential scope/ownership/architecture commitments or before launching execution when the user asked only for planning. Between substantive planning turns, imp should update mana first when durable state changed and then summarize the mana delta in the reply so the graph remains continuously reviewable.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:20:  Boundary spec landed in `docs/rebuild/mana-imp-ownership-boundary.md`. Practical decomposition captured from the spec: (1) mana side should converge from `PromptResult { system_prompt, user_message, file_ref }` toward a structured execution bundle carrying durable task contract + inherited context + provenance/trust metadata; (2) imp side remains the model-facing assembly layer that combines that bundle with identity, tools, AGENTS.md, session/personal memory, environment, and live repo reads; (3) retrieval split is mana = durable shared project retrieval semantics, imp = live retrieval application and token-budgeted reveal.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:24:  Memory/retrieval taxonomy spec landed in `docs/rebuild/memory-taxonomy-and-retrieval-boundary.md` after inspecting `imp/crates/imp-core/src/memory.rs`, `imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/session_search.rs`, `mana/crates/mana-core/src/ops/fact.rs`, and `mana/crates/mana-core/src/ops/recall.rs`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:29:  - `mana` owns all shared project memory: project work/attempt/decision memory, verified facts, and future synthesized project knowledge.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:30:  - Canonical project retrieval belongs to `mana` (`mana recall`, fact retrieval, `mana context`/unit briefings); `imp` should consume that retrieval rather than own a second durable shared project-memory system.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:31:  - Project memory should be maintained as workflow-shaped mana artifacts (units, attempts, decisions, facts, later wiki pages), not as a dedicated project session/chat log.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:32:  - Explicit answer to the open question: dedicated mana workflow yes, dedicated mana session no.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:35:  1. verified mana facts
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:36:  2. mana unit state / decisions / attempt history
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:37:  3. synthesized mana knowledge pages
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:43:  - make imp consume mana project memory more directly in interactive and headless flows
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:44:  - move any remaining project-memory pressure out of imp and into mana rather than adding a third imp memory store
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:48:  Memory/retrieval consolidation output from archived child 28.3 has now been materialized as `docs/design/memory-taxonomy-and-retrieval.md`. Key decisions to preserve on the live parent graph: (1) four memory classes — session memory and personal/private memory stay in imp; project work memory and project knowledge memory belong in mana; (2) retrieval contract — `imp session_search` remains transcript recall only, `imp` personal memory remains private preference/env memory, `mana fact` is the verified project-knowledge workflow, and `mana recall` should become the canonical query surface for shared project memory rather than remaining unit-history-only; (3) migration direction — do not add an imp-local project memory store, and treat transcript discoveries as candidates to externalize into mana during conversation; (4) project memory structure — workflow yes, session no: maintain shared memory through explicit mana workflows/records, not a pseudo-session transcript.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:56:  2026-04-09 — externalized from the 28.2 boundary-spec execution session. The landed spec is `docs/rebuild/mana-imp-ownership-boundary.md`. Durable outputs to preserve on the live graph: `mana` owns durable/shared/coordinated/verified work state and inherited project memory; `imp` owns live/local/model-facing/user-personal runtime behavior; canonical handoff is `mana -> ExecutionBundle -> imp -> WorkerResult -> mana`; final prompt assembly stays in `imp`; `mana/crates/mana-core/src/prompt.rs` is the current wrong-owner overlap because it renders final prompt text rather than only structured execution context. Also observed a root-mana traceability inconsistency during closeout: active graph resolution no longer exposed child units 28.1/28.2/28.3 even though parent 28 and 28.5 still carry their outputs/dependencies. Treat the landed docs plus parent notes as current truth until graph traceability is repaired.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:60:  2026-04-09 — externalized the 28.4-derived review/decomposition into live root mana. Added child jobs under 28.5 to preserve the agreed order: `28.5.3` defines the canonical turn-batched mana delta contract with explicit `no_change`; `28.5.4` defines the imp-first compact between-turn review block and expanded textual inspector that asks only on consequential unresolved choices; `28.5.5` defines the later Wizard review-queue/focus-room layer as an optional richer consumer of the same delta semantics. This preserves the core decision that imp ships the mandatory review affordance first and Wizard remains a later, non-required visual layer.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:64:  2026-04-13 repo-grounded orientation refresh after reading `README.md` and `VISION.md` from the Tower root. Preserve this current top-level decomposition on the live graph: Tower is the active umbrella root for `mana` + `imp` only; `wizard/` and `familiar/` remain deferred reference material and are not active implementation scope unless a task explicitly targets them. `mana` is the durable cross-agent substrate/control plane owning work graph, dependencies, facts, attempts, verification/evidence history, and shared project memory. `imp` is the live worker/runtime shell owning context assembly, tool use, session/runtime behavior, edits/command execution, runtime policy, and structured result reporting back into mana. The intended happy path remains `mana -> imp -> mana`. The project goal stated in `VISION.md` is reliable autonomy for software work through durable memory, verified execution, runtime policy, and coordinated/isolation-aware multi-agent work. Use this as the baseline repo explanation for future boundary/onboarding/planning work.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:68:  Execution checkpoint on 2026-04-14 for the continue-until-blocked loop work. Implemented another `imp/crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs` slice that broadens execution-side evidence grounding for `NextActionStopReason::WorkCompleted`: `tool_results_indicate_work_completed(...)` now treats a turn as completion-evidence when it contains both a successful edit-like tool result (`write`, `edit`, `multi_edit`) and a successful check-like command result with structured `command` + `exit_code == 0` (`check`, `test`, `verify`, `pytest`, `cargo check`, `cargo test`), in addition to the earlier close/verify-backed paths. Focused tests passed: `tool_results_indicate_work_completed_detects_edit_plus_successful_check`, `tool_results_indicate_work_completed_detects_closed_unit_details`, `execution_stops_after_mana_close_tool_result_without_done_text`, `execution_stops_after_work_completed_text`, `agent_multiple_tool_calls`, plus `cargo check -p imp-core`. Repo-state oddity to preserve: direct root lookup for transient follow-on unit id `28.6` failed after prior close/create churn, so this checkpoint is being attached to parent 28 to avoid losing the implementation delta while root mana lookup consistency is unclear.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:72:  SuperProduct alignment assessment (from 2026-04-14 discussion): current Tower maps most strongly below the 4-part product core rather than directly implementing it. mana is closest to a proto-SuperState for software-work autonomy: durable work graph, facts, evidence, verification, dependency state, attempt/failure memory, and coordination. imp is closest to a proto-SuperAction plus proto-SuperShell: live execution/runtime, tool use, policy-gated actions, CLI/TUI shell, and emerging review/approval UX. Current extension seams (Lua tools/skills/config, runtime contracts, planned embedding/runner seams) are only a partial proto-SuperComp, not yet a declarative versioned module system. Major gap versus the 4 core: Tower lacks a general Object/Assertion/Event/Rule substrate and stable product-level action vocabulary; most current concepts are still specialized around coding work and agent orchestration. Conclusion: strong structural alignment with the runtime/substrate stance in PROJECT 1.4, medium alignment with SuperState/SuperAction directionally, weak-to-medium alignment with SuperComp/SuperShell as full business-product layers.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:76:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:94:  1. Semantic ownership split: mana owns durable/shared/coordinated/verified work state and inherited project memory; imp owns live/local/model-facing/user-personal runtime behavior.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:95:  2. Contract split: mana should provide structured execution context as an execution bundle; imp remains the owner of final prompt assembly. Do not move final prompt rendering into mana.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:96:  3. Redundancy direction: remove mana-side final prompt rendering, user-message generation, generic worker coaching prose, and live file-to-prompt embedding from the long-term mana boundary; retain imp-side projection/adaptation types and UX-local state mirrors as acceptable layering when mana remains the durable source of truth.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:97:- 'Memory boundary decision: `imp` keeps session/runtime memory and personal/private persistent memory only; `mana` owns all shared project memory, including work/attempt/decision memory, verified facts, and future synthesized project knowledge.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:98:- 'Retrieval boundary decision: `session_search` is transcript recall only. Canonical project retrieval belongs to `mana` via recall, fact retrieval, and context/unit briefing assembly; `imp` may consume these surfaces but should not own a second durable shared project-memory retrieval system.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:99:- 'Project-memory modeling decision: project memory should be workflow-shaped mana state, not a dedicated project session/chat log. Dedicated workflow yes; dedicated mana session no.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:100:- '2026-04-13 boundary clarification from user: mana is the headless control plane for work; imp is the live execution shell for a user/agent session. Refined ownership rule: mana owns inter-step truth while imp owns intra-step experience. Preferred command boundary is `imp run {mana_id}` meaning attach a live runtime/session to a mana-owned run. Anything that must survive a crash or affect global orchestration must be projected back into mana as a typed, verified state change.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:103:Goal: define the clean ownership boundary between mana and imp, and specify a stronger mana-first workflow where planning/design conversations are proactively externalized into mana as a persistent work and idea graph.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:106:- mana owns coordination/work graph while imp owns runtime, but there is overlap around prompt shaping, retrieval, durable memory, and planning behavior.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:107:- imp currently underuses mana during conversations like this by logging work late instead of continuously externalizing plans and architecture decisions.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:108:- the user wants imp to automatically write implementation plans and idea decomposition into mana during conversations, only asking for decisions it cannot make autonomously.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28-define-clean-mana-vs-imp-boundary-and-mana-first-planning-workflow.md:109:- the user also wants visual space in imp/Wizard to review mana information between turns.

.mana/archive/2026/04/304.14-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:3:title: Fix mana unit closure reliability after implementation
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.14-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:4:slug: fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.14-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:13:  Moved from 304 to 313 after user correctly identified scope hygiene issue: mana unit closure reliability is not part of the imp native tool schema audit; it belongs under broader imp/mana workflow reliability. This move is itself evidence of the organization bug: agents can create plausible tasks under the active epic even when the discovered issue belongs to a different product/workflow reliability thread.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.14-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:17:  Corrected parent to 313 again. Previous update note claimed the move but the parent remained 304, confirming the mana update UI/behavior can make parent changes look successful while tree state remains unchanged or reverted. Keep this as additional evidence for workflow reliability/scope hygiene bugs.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.14-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:21:  Attempted to reparent with mana update parent=313, but show still reports parent=304. This suggests either native mana update does not support parent changes despite accepting the field, or the tool output is misleading. Need use a dedicated reparent action or fix mana update behavior; for now, do not assume parent changes succeeded unless show/tree confirms.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.14-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:23:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.14-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:28:close_reason: Not part of native tool schema audit. Conceptually moved to workflow reliability epic 313; native mana reparent attempts reported success but parent remained 304, which is itself evidence for 313/313.1. Closing here to keep 304 scoped to tool audit work.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.14-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:30:verify: cargo test -p imp-core mana -- --nocapture && cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.14-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:47:Bug/design gap discovered during tool audit: imp eagerly creates mana tasks, completes/commits implementation, but often leaves the mana unit open or in_progress when native close/verify fails due to stale or invalid verify commands. Later agents become over-conservative because they lack immediate session memory and see failed/stale mana status despite commit/test evidence. Need improve the completion loop so implementation evidence, corrected equivalent verifies, and commits can close units reliably or mark them as completed-with-override instead of leaving stale blockers.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.14-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:51:Scope: diagnose native mana close/verify behavior and imp's completion discipline. Add runtime/tool UX changes if appropriate: close should accept explicit equivalent verify evidence with force reason; failed verify should produce actionable corrected-command hints where possible; completion summaries should remind/require closing claimed unit after successful commit; mana tree/status should distinguish stale failed verify from current completion evidence.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.14-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:53:Files likely relevant: crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs, crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs, crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs, mana close/verify implementation in mana crates if outside imp-core.

.mana/archive/2026/04/29.6.2-implement-safe-partial-mana-update-semantics-in-im.md:25:Goal: keep the implemented safe partial-update behavior in imp\'s native mana tool working and verified. Current state: optional-string normalization and append-style update usage now avoid empty-field clobbering during conversational mana updates. Steps: 1. preserve safe optional field handling in imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs, 2. preserve notes-only update safety, 3. keep the focused safe-partial-update test passing. Files: imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs (modify only if regressions appear). In scope: safe partial updates and focused tests. Out of scope: UI review rendering.

.mana/archive/2026/04/280.4-strengthen-agent-loop-completion-discipline-for-im.md:16:close_reason: 'Implemented first loop-native execution-debt stop gate. Agent now tracks planning-only mana progress vs execution evidence, queues a follow-up objective when a non-planner turn records/plans work but attempts to finalize without execution evidence, and uses outcome-oriented continuation wording instead of ''next action''. Also separated pre-turn mana skill nudges from post-stop follow-ups so skill-loading hints do not appear after an otherwise complete one-turn answer. Added targeted tests for execution-debt follow-up, mana planning without execution, and mutating-tool execution evidence. Verification: cargo test -p imp-core agent -- --nocapture passed (99 tests); cargo check -p imp-core passed.'

Removed claim that `imp mana` is preferred. README now presents interactive `imp` + asking the agent to work on a mana unit as the primary workflow, `imp run <unit-id>` as the clearer direct CLI execution path, and `imp mana <unit-id>` only as an explicit supported subcommand. .mana/archive/2026/04/295-update-readme-mana-execution-wording-around-imp-ru.md:53:Revise /Users/asher/imp/README.md to reflect user feedback: `imp mana` is confusing as the preferred command; `imp run <unit-id>` makes more sense for CLI execution, and most users should be able to open imp and ask the agent to work on mana rather than use a CLI subcommand. Remove wording that says `imp mana` is preferred. Present primary user workflow as interactive imp + natural language request to work on mana. Present `imp run <unit-id>` as the direct/headless CLI execution path. Mention `imp mana <unit-id>` only if still supported as an explicit mana subcommand/alias, not preferred.

.mana/archive/2026/05/341.5-test-native-mana-explicit-target-filtering-does-no.md:33:    test tools::mana::tests::core_filter_from_native_keeps_single_unit_scope ... ok
.mana/archive/2026/05/341.5-test-native-mana-explicit-target-filtering-does-no.md:39:- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs
.mana/archive/2026/05/341.5-test-native-mana-explicit-target-filtering-does-no.md:58:Audit finding: `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` now adapts `RunTarget` to `mana_run_core::compute_run_plan` using `core_filter_from_native`, returning `None` for `AllReady` and also for multi-ID `Explicit`. That may be intentional due current mana_run_core API, but it is risky because multi explicit targets could behave like all-ready if not separately filtered. Add focused tests for `core_filter_from_native` and/or native run planning behavior. If multi explicit cannot be represented by mana_run_core yet, document the limitation in test/task notes and add a guardrail test ensuring the tool reports an unsupported/clear behavior rather than silently running broader scope.
