Proceeding with direct implementation under existing unit 330.6. Follow skill guidance: keep scope narrow, update existing unit rather than creating more, verify focused tests.
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:14:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:18:close_reason: 'Implemented: native mana list now returns compact text/details by default, caps at 20 unless count supplied and hard-caps at 50, omits verbose fields like verify/dependencies/requires/produces, and includes explicit guidance to show/update/notes_append existing relevant units before create. Mana overview guide and create validation hint now bias toward inspecting/updating existing units before creating new ones. Agent mana follow-up signal no longer triggers on generic ''unit/units'', reducing unnecessary mana nudges. Verification passed: cargo test -p imp-core mana -- --nocapture (118 passed).'
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:20:verify: cargo test -p imp-core mana -- --nocapture
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:24:- crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:41:Goal: adjust imp's mana runtime/tool behavior so agents are nudged/policied toward updating existing relevant mana units instead of creating new duplicate units, and reduce token-heavy output from the native mana list action.
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:44:- Make the agent more likely to update existing mana instead of creating new mana.
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:45:- The mana list tool consumes a ton of tokens.
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:48:1. Inspect native mana tool list rendering and create/update guidance/policy paths.
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:49:2. Make the smallest behavior change that biases toward inspect/update existing units before create when relevant existing mana likely exists.
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:50:3. Reduce default `mana list` output size/detail while preserving enough information to choose a relevant existing unit. Prefer explicit opt-in for larger output if already supported; otherwise cap/truncate/render compactly.
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:54:- imp runtime/native mana tool only.
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:55:- Do not change mana CLI/core semantics outside imp unless unavoidable.
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:58:- Agents see/runtime receives guidance to list/show/update existing relevant mana before creating a new unit when durable state belongs to existing work.
.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-prefer-updating-existing-mana-and-trim-mana-list-t.md:59:- Native mana list output is compact by default and avoids very large token dumps.

Verify by tests covering task context assembly and prefill behavior.

.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.1-specify-the-first-shared-mana-imp-protocol-types-a.md:54:  2026-04-23 resumed and reverified. Verify gate still passes (`test -n "analysis-only"`). No new code change was required; acceptance is satisfied and the unit can be closed.

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3.1-engineer-the-generalized-imp-message-loop-with-fix.md:16:  - Mana/workflow behavior is explicitly outside imp core and modeled as host/harness orchestration over imp runtime events/outcomes.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3.1-engineer-the-generalized-imp-message-loop-with-fix.md:20:  Current status after creation/inspection: acceptance is not yet satisfied because the runtime boundary doc/linked design doc has not been written. `mana verify 365.3.1` failed as expected: `docs/rebuild/imp-runtime-boundary-after-mana-pivot.md` does not yet contain the required message-loop/fixin design content. This is an open design task, not ready to close. Next useful action is to continue the question-driven design and then write the doc section.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3.1-engineer-the-generalized-imp-message-loop-with-fix.md:24:  Verification after updating terminology still fails because the deliverable doc has not been authored yet. Required check now expects `docs/rebuild/imp-runtime-boundary-after-mana-pivot.md` to contain `message loop`, `runtime layer`, and `workflow behavior is outside imp core`. This is correct for an open planning task; do not close until the doc is written. Current blocker is not technical; it is unresolved design content from the ongoing question sequence.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3.1-engineer-the-generalized-imp-message-loop-with-fix.md:28:  Terminology decision from user: use `runtime layers` for the internal architecture around the core message loop, and `capabilities` for host/protocol negotiation. Avoid `fixins` in final architecture/product docs. Also preserve the earlier boundary: workflow behavior is outside imp core and belongs to mana/harness or another host/orchestrator.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3.1-engineer-the-generalized-imp-message-loop-with-fix.md:36:verify: cd /Users/asher/imp && test -f docs/rebuild/imp-runtime-boundary-after-mana-pivot.md && rg -q "message loop" docs/rebuild/imp-runtime-boundary-after-mana-pivot.md && rg -q "runtime layer" docs/rebuild/imp-runtime-boundary-after-mana-pivot.md && rg -q "workflow behavior is outside imp core" docs/rebuild/imp-runtime-boundary-after-mana-pivot.md
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3.1-engineer-the-generalized-imp-message-loop-with-fix.md:45:  output_snippet: 'verify passed: cd /Users/asher/imp && test -f docs/rebuild/imp-runtime-boundary-after-mana-pivot.md && rg -q "message loop" docs/rebuild/imp-runtime-boundary-after-mana-pivot.md && rg -q "runtime layer" docs/rebuild/imp-runtime-boundary-after-mana-pivot.md && rg -q "workflow behavior is outside imp core" docs/rebuild/imp-runtime-boundary-after-mana-pivot.md'
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3.1-engineer-the-generalized-imp-message-loop-with-fix.md:64:Design the core abstraction for slim imp's generalized agent loop. User direction: imp should remain largely the same and remain a message loop at its core, but the runtime loop should be generalized with explicit runtime layers/capabilities rather than hard-coded task/mana/workflow assumptions.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3.1-engineer-the-generalized-imp-message-loop-with-fix.md:66:Important correction: avoid the term `fixins` in final docs unless used as conversational shorthand. Prefer a clearer term such as runtime layers, capabilities, facets, or services. Also, imp should not own workflow behavior; the larger mana/harness wrapper owns workflow orchestration. imp may expose runtime capabilities such as context, tools, policy checks, verification command execution, artifact/event emission, and structured outcomes, but workflow state/lifecycle/close/fail/dispatch belongs outside imp.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3.1-engineer-the-generalized-imp-message-loop-with-fix.md:78:- `AgentEvent` already exposes a useful event stream but includes workflow/mana-specific events that should become host/harness-level events or optional integration events, not core runtime behavior.
.mana/archive/2026/04/266.4.4-phase-2-epic-context-continuity-session-recall-and.md:26:- imp already has promising primitives: JSONL sessions, branchable session tree, session index, session_search, compaction, and mana-first durable planning boundaries.
.mana/archive/2026/04/266.4.4-phase-2-epic-context-continuity-session-recall-and.md:40:   - smallest implementation slice that improves continuity without collapsing durable mana state into session memory
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-parent-reparentupdate-behavior.md:3:title: Fix mana parent reparent/update behavior
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-parent-reparentupdate-behavior.md:4:slug: fix-mana-parent-reparentupdate-behavior
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-parent-reparentupdate-behavior.md:9:acceptance: A mana unit can be reparented reliably through the native imp mana tool or receives an explicit unsupported error instead of a false success. Regression tests cover child metadata and parent tree/list consistency after reparenting.
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-parent-reparentupdate-behavior.md:11:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-parent-reparentupdate-behavior.md:16:close_reason: 'Implemented native mana reparent fix across mana and imp. Commits: mana b3edb0a `Add mana unit reparent operation`; imp edc6477 `Expose mana reparent action`. Behavior: mana-core has reparent operation that validates parent existence, rejects self/cycles, updates child parent metadata, records reason in notes, rebuilds index, and exposes API; imp mana tool exposes action=reparent with validation and tests. Verification passed: `cargo test --manifest-path ../mana/Cargo.toml -p mana-core reparent -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core reparent -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run`.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-parent-reparentupdate-behavior.md:18:verify: cargo test -p imp-core mana_reparent -- --nocapture && cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-parent-reparentupdate-behavior.md:28:  notes: 'Implemented native mana reparent fix across mana and imp. Commits: mana b3edb0a `Add mana unit reparent operation`; imp edc6477 `Expose mana reparent action`. Behavior: mana-core has reparent operation that validates parent existence, rejects self/cycles, updates child parent metadata, records reason in notes, rebuilds index, and exposes API; imp mana tool exposes action=reparent with validation and tests. Verification passed: `cargo test --manifest-path ../mana/Cargo.toml -p mana-core reparent -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core reparent -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run`.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-parent-reparentupdate-behavior.md:46:Bug discovered while trying to move `304.14` under workflow reliability epic `313`: native `mana update parent=313` appeared to succeed, but subsequent `mana show` still reported parent `304`. This makes graph hygiene unreliable because agents can believe they moved mis-scoped work while the tree remains wrong.
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-parent-reparentupdate-behavior.md:48:Scope: inspect mana update/reparent implementation and either (a) make `update parent=...` actually reparent units, or (b) add a dedicated native `reparent` action and make unsupported parent updates return a clear error. Prefer the smallest compatible fix that allows agents to correct mis-scoped units safely.
.mana/archive/2026/05/47.1-contracts-and-ownership-boundary-for-mana-imp-rebu.md:3:title: Contracts and ownership boundary for mana ↔ imp rebuild
.mana/archive/2026/05/47.1-contracts-and-ownership-boundary-for-mana-imp-rebu.md:4:slug: contracts-and-ownership-boundary-for-mana-imp-rebu
.mana/archive/2026/05/47.1-contracts-and-ownership-boundary-for-mana-imp-rebu.md:22:- 'Preferred placement for the first shared mana↔imp protocol slice is the existing Tower-level `tower-contracts` crate, not a mana-owned or imp-owned crate. Current repo evidence already supports this: `tower-contracts` exists, `imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs` already re-exports shared worker types from it, and multiple rebuild docs treat it as the intended neutral boundary.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/47.1-contracts-and-ownership-boundary-for-mana-imp-rebu.md:25:This epic formalizes the shared boundary between mana and imp as code. Goal: replace the current partly implicit boundary with a canonical protocol layer that both sides can depend on.
.mana/archive/2026/05/47.1-contracts-and-ownership-boundary-for-mana-imp-rebu.md:34:1. should shared contracts live at Tower root, under mana, or under imp?
.mana/archive/2026/05/47.1-contracts-and-ownership-boundary-for-mana-imp-rebu.md:35:2. which current structs in `imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs` and nearby areas should migrate first?
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-add-status-filtering-to-work-tool-list-action.md:56:Improve native work tool list filtering so agents can inspect work by lifecycle/status without mana.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.2-add-self-teaching-mana-action-validation.md:3:title: Add self-teaching mana action validation
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.2-add-self-teaching-mana-action-validation.md:4:slug: add-self-teaching-mana-action-validation
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.2-add-self-teaching-mana-action-validation.md:9:acceptance: Mana tool runtime returns concise, action-specific validation errors with machine-readable missing fields and human-readable recovery hints. Common misuse cases teach canonical field names and worker-ready unit quality expectations through the native tool. Tests cover validation for create, update/notes/decisions, dependencies, run/run_state, and path-vs-paths confusion.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.2-add-self-teaching-mana-action-validation.md:11:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.2-add-self-teaching-mana-action-validation.md:15:close_reason: 'Implemented and committed mana validation/error guidance in d47b592 `Add mana action validation hints`. Added pre-dispatch validation that returns structured errors with action, missing fields, invalid fields, hints, and canonical field suggestions for common mana actions (`show/claim/release/close/reopen/verify/fail/delete`, create/update, notes_append, decisions, deps, fact_create, logs, run_state/evaluate, run target misuse). Added guard against confusing `path` with `paths` for unit file attachments. `decision_add` now accepts canonical `decisions` entries as well as description/notes. Tests cover required-field hints, path-vs-paths guidance, and valid create/decision_add. Verification passed: `cargo test -p imp-core mana_schema -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana_validation -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run`.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.2-add-self-teaching-mana-action-validation.md:19:verify: cargo test -p imp-core mana_validation --no-run || cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.2-add-self-teaching-mana-action-validation.md:32:  output_snippet: 'verify passed: cargo test -p imp-core mana_validation --no-run || cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run; changed 1 file (+244/-2)'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.2-add-self-teaching-mana-action-validation.md:37:  notes: 'Implemented and committed mana validation/error guidance in d47b592 `Add mana action validation hints`. Added pre-dispatch validation that returns structured errors with action, missing fields, invalid fields, hints, and canonical field suggestions for common mana actions (`show/claim/release/close/reopen/verify/fail/delete`, create/update, notes_append, decisions, deps, fact_create, logs, run_state/evaluate, run target misuse). Added guard against confusing `path` with `paths` for unit file attachments. `decision_add` now accepts canonical `decisions` entries as well as description/notes. Tests cover required-field hints, path-vs-paths guidance, and valid create/decision_add. Verification passed: `cargo test -p imp-core mana_schema -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana_validation -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run`.'

.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.1-canonicalize-native-mana-tool-schema.md:3:title: Canonicalize native mana tool schema
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.1-canonicalize-native-mana-tool-schema.md:4:slug: canonicalize-native-mana-tool-schema
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.1-canonicalize-native-mana-tool-schema.md:9:acceptance: The native mana tool schema exposes one canonical spelling per concept and removes model-facing aliases/convenience fields that increase token/reasoning cost without clear value. Runtime still works for intentional compatibility only if explicitly kept outside model-facing schema. Tests cover schema properties and representative calls using canonical fields.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.1-canonicalize-native-mana-tool-schema.md:13:  Started execution. Loaded mana skill per instruction. Verified baseline with `cargo test -p imp-core mana_schema --no-run || cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run` (passes via fallback build). Inspected `ManaTool::parameters()` and confirmed current model-facing aliases/convenience fields still advertised: `mana_scope`, `mana_dir`, `paths_csv`, `fact_title`, `add_label`, `remove_label`, and `kind` enum includes legacy `job`. Runtime already has alias parsing paths for scope/path and `parse_unit_kind` accepts `job`, so schema can be slimmed without removing compatibility parsing.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.1-canonicalize-native-mana-tool-schema.md:17:  Implemented schema slimming in `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs`: removed model-facing `mana_scope`, `mana_dir`, `paths_csv`, `fact_title`, `add_label`, `remove_label`; narrowed `kind` enum to `epic|task|fact`; compacted a few descriptions; kept runtime compatibility parsing for legacy aliases with explicit comments in `resolve_mana_dir`, `parse_unit_kind`, and `fact_create`. Updated tests to assert canonical schema shape and representative canonical calls (`labels` for update, `title` for fact_create). Verify gate is currently blocked by unrelated compile errors already present elsewhere: `TaskContext` and `WorkerAssignment` initializers in `crates/imp-core/src/imp_session.rs`, `crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs`, and `crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs` are missing `design`, `fail_first`, and `verify_timeout_secs`. Narrow sanity check `cargo check -p imp-core --lib` passes after this change.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.1-canonicalize-native-mana-tool-schema.md:19:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.1-canonicalize-native-mana-tool-schema.md:23:close_reason: 'Canonical mana schema cleanup was already present in working tree from prior implementation. Reverified now after dependent compile issues were resolved. Evidence inspected: `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs` shows model-facing schema advertises canonical `scope`, `path`, `paths`, `title`, and `kind` enum `epic|task|fact`, with legacy aliases only in runtime compatibility paths (`mana_scope`, `mana_dir`, `paths_csv`, `fact_title`, `job`). Verification passed: `cargo test -p imp-core mana_schema --no-run || cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run`. No new commit needed; repo was clean.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.1-canonicalize-native-mana-tool-schema.md:25:verify: cargo test -p imp-core mana_schema --no-run || cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run

.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.1-canonicalize-native-mana-tool-schema.md:61:Goal: reduce model read/reason/write token cost while preserving the single `mana` tool mental model.

.mana/archive/2026/04/302-audit-oversized-input-token-usage-for-trivial-imp.md:45:Diagnose why a simple user prompt such as "hi" can produce ~16k input tokens in imp. Inspect context assembly, system/developer prompt construction, project instructions, skills list, mana memory injection, and provider request accounting. Identify concrete contributors and recommend/implement narrow fixes only if clearly safe. Verify with the smallest command or instrumentation available that reports prompt/request size for a trivial prompt.

.mana/archive/2026/04/292-revise-readme-into-technical-mana-forward-structur.md:14:close_reason: Revised README into a more technical, organized, mana-forward structure per user feedback and create-readme skill. .mana/archive/2026/04/292-revise-readme-into-technical-mana-forward-structur.md:54:Revise /Users/asher/imp/README.md after user feedback. Current rewritten README is too prose-heavy and mana appears too late. Goal: make README primarily technical, more organized, concise, and mana-forward. Use create-readme skill constraints: concise GFM, no license/contributing/changelog-style extra sections, professional open-source README. Keep shipped/current-state claims accurate. Put mana near the top as a major component. Reduce opinionated marketing prose and remove/soften debatable claims, especially broad influences/philosophy. Preserve useful technical content: install, quickstart, architecture, mana integration, command modes, tools, sessions/context, providers/secrets/web, extensions, SDK, config, diagnostics. Do not change code.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4.9.3-build-prototype-primitive-and-prototyperun-evidenc.md:30:  Renamed registered tool from prototype_run to prototype to match imp's existing simple tool naming style (bash/read/write/mana). Future create/promote/discard can be represented as an action parameter if needed; current behavior remains run-only. Verified cargo test -p imp-core prototype_run -- --nocapture and cargo test -p imp-work prototype.

.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-add-work-tool-context-pack-creation-action.md:56:Extend the imp-core work tool with a context action backed by imp-work context pack compilation. This lets agents prepare schedulable work from native .imp/work items without mana.
.mana/archive/2026/04/259.1-replace-obvious-production-unwrapunreachable-sites.md:12:  Implemented first cleanup pass. Replaced interactive continue-session fallback unwrap in `imp-cli/src/lib.rs` with proper `Result` propagation when creating a new session if no recent session exists. Replaced `child.stdout.take().unwrap()` and `child.stderr.take().unwrap()` in `imp-core/src/tools/bash.rs` with recoverable `Error::Tool(...)` branches. Replaced `_ => unreachable!()` in `imp-core/src/tools/extend.rs` with an explicit user-facing error branch. Verification: `cargo test -p imp-cli` passed; focused `cargo test -p imp-core tools::bash::tests` and `cargo test -p imp-core tools::extend::tests` passed. Full `cargo test -p imp-core` still has 4 pre-existing unrelated failures in builder/config/mana_prompt_context/tools::imp tests.
.mana/archive/2026/04/259.1-replace-obvious-production-unwrapunreachable-sites.md:37:   - In interactive session setup, remove `unwrap_or_else(|| SessionManager::new(...).unwrap())` and propagate a normal error instead.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-write-imp-work-implementation-plan-with-task-conte.md:16:  Golden path: imp-work replaces mana inside imp as a native work/memory crate. Its center is prepared work: every schedulable task owns a versioned Task Context Pack that lets an isolated subagent start cold with goal, constraints, relevant memory, source refs, checks, prior attempts, tool/model profile, and expected structured outcome. Context packs must be deterministic/cache-stable, immutable by version, source-referenced, and stale-detectable.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-write-imp-work-implementation-plan-with-task-conte.md:18:  Conversational memory requirement: imp-work must preserve mana's useful behavior where durable ideas from chat can be stored during conversation, but must fix retrieval so users do not need to remember where the note went. Memories should be classified as fact/preference/decision/follow-up/note, linked to topics/tasks/paths/sources/time, and retrievable by text search, recent conversation memory, task context assembly, topic, path, and later semantic/entity search.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-write-imp-work-implementation-plan-with-task-conte.md:20:  V0 posture: do not build a throwaway mini-mana clone. Build the right foundational abstractions first (memory, task, epic/subtask, decision, check, run/attempt, lease, context pack, source ref). Keep v0 bounded by proving the core loop before full orchestration. Use disposable prototypes for risky seams, then implement proven designs properly in production modules.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-write-imp-work-implementation-plan-with-task-conte.md:24:  Migration policy: mana becomes a reference and migration source, not a runtime dependency. Existing mana data migration should be handled by local/off-repo scripts that emit imp-work files/index seeds. Do not commit import/mana.rs or a mana import module into crates/imp-work.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-write-imp-work-implementation-plan-with-task-conte.md:33:  7. Document off-repo mana migration script policy.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-write-imp-work-implementation-plan-with-task-conte.md:47:  Build these as the real primitives now; defer broad surfaces (full TUI replacement, embeddings/semantic search, massive live concurrency, containerized sandboxing, committed mana importer) unless needed to prove the foundation.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-write-imp-work-implementation-plan-with-task-conte.md:73:- imp-work v0 should be implemented as a proper foundation rather than a throwaway minimal clone of mana, but risky/uncertain mechanisms should be proven through small disposable prototypes before being committed to the production crate.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-write-imp-work-implementation-plan-with-task-conte.md:76:- 'User preference: do not frame imp-work as a minimal v0 if that risks undershooting the vision. The main conversation is about imp-work as the native replacement for mana in imp, and a focused sprint may build the envisioned foundation directly rather than artificially constraining to a tiny slice. Keep scope disciplined through prototypes, acceptance gates, and sequencing, not through shrinking the concept.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-write-imp-work-implementation-plan-with-task-conte.md:79:Write the implementation plan for imp-work as the native replacement for mana inside imp. Keep v0 ambitious but not over-complex: build the right core abstractions first, allow prototypes as disposable proving slices, and avoid committing mana import code into the repo. The plan should preserve mana's best conversational-memory behavior while making retrieval automatic so users do not need to remember where notes were stored.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.8.3.1-implement-read-line-range-schema-and-safety-cleanu.md:21:  Mana skill loaded on explicit user instruction before close/commit decision. Follow it for unit completion: record evidence, verify before closing, review git diff before commit, and do not include unrelated dirty files. Current repo dirty includes unrelated files (`Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.workspace.toml`, `LICENSE`, `README.md`) plus intended `crates/imp-core/src/tools/read.rs`; only read.rs should be staged/committed for this unit unless user says otherwise.
.mana/archive/2026/04/280.2.5-build-repeatable-dirac-derived-eval-harness-for-im.md:50:- imp has usage reporting work and mana verify gates, but not a Dirac-comparison-specific harness artifact yet.
.mana/archive/2026/04/280.2.5-build-repeatable-dirac-derived-eval-harness-for-im.md:69:6. Record results back into mana 280.1.
.mana/archive/2026/04/263.1-classify-imp-libraryruntime-stderr-writes-for-embe.md:11:  Known locations from initial scan: imp-core/src/agent.rs:599, session.rs:222, mana_worker.rs:489,491, tools/shell.rs:367; imp-llm/src/providers/anthropic.rs:688,974,986,1007; imp-lua/src/lib.rs:34,39,46 and bridge.rs:322.
.mana/archive/2026/04/263.1-classify-imp-libraryruntime-stderr-writes-for-embe.md:15:  Imp runtime classification underway. Confirmed high-risk sites reachable from embedded interactive flow: (1) imp-core agent OAuth refresh pre-flight emits eprintln on refresh failure inside the live turn loop; should route through structured agent/UI error handling or stay silent and let the provider auth failure surface normally. (2) imp-core mana_worker verify runner emits raw stdout/stderr on verify failure; this path is used by the imp tool's unit-backed worker execution and can break the TUI. Candidate next changes: remove direct stderr printing from both sites, preserve diagnostics in returned structured results or notifications, then add focused regression coverage where feasible.
.mana/archive/2026/04/263.1-classify-imp-libraryruntime-stderr-writes-for-embe.md:19:  Implemented first high-risk fixes. (1) imp-core agent OAuth refresh failure no longer writes directly to stderr during live execution; it now routes through ui.notify(..., Warning), which is TUI-safe and a no-op in null/headless contexts. (2) imp-core mana_worker verify runner no longer eprintlns failed verify stdout/stderr; it captures the output into WorkerRunOutcome.verify_output and imp tool details now include verify_output for structured downstream display. Added focused tests for stderr/stdout capture fallback in mana_worker and re-ran imp-core checks. Remaining imp-core eprintln sites after this pass: session.rs malformed-line warning and tools/shell.rs invalid shell-tool-definition warning, both lower risk but still library-level; imp-llm anthropic retry/SSE warnings and imp-lua runtime/extension warnings remain to classify/convert.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.4-strengthen-mana-worker-context-assembly.md:3:title: Strengthen mana worker context assembly
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.4-strengthen-mana-worker-context-assembly.md:4:slug: strengthen-mana-worker-context-assembly
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.4-strengthen-mana-worker-context-assembly.md:9:acceptance: 'Mana worker prompts/prefill include enough compact context for a subagent to execute cold: unit title, description/design, acceptance, verify/fail_first, paths/file snippets, dependencies/blockers, decisions, notes/attempt history, relevant facts where available, and explicit scope boundaries. Tests or fixtures prove representative worker assignments include expected context without relying on the mana skill.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.4-strengthen-mana-worker-context-assembly.md:13:  Loaded mana skill and unit 304.6.4.4. Audited current imp-core worker context assembly against mana-core unit fields and run planning context. Found likely missing handoff context: unit design field, fail_first, effective verify_timeout, and stronger dependency/attempt/fact rendering coverage in worker prompt/task context tests.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.4-strengthen-mana-worker-context-assembly.md:17:  Ran the task verify gate. Primary command form is partially wrong for cargo test: `cargo test -p imp-core mana_worker context_prefill --no-run` errors with `unexpected argument 'context_prefill'`. Fallback `cargo test -p imp-core mana_worker --no-run` succeeds, so implementation should preserve compatibility with the fallback and note the verify-gate mismatch if unchanged.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.4-strengthen-mana-worker-context-assembly.md:21:  Implementation decomposition for this unit is now explicit: (1) extend imp-owned WorkerAssignment with low-churn mana-native handoff fields already present in mana-core (design, fail_first, effective verify_timeout), (2) thread those fields into TaskContext and concise worker prompt/task rendering, (3) keep dependency/decision/attempt/fact context compact rather than expanding doctrine, (4) update representative mana_worker + system_prompt tests to assert cold-start handoff coverage, and (5) finish by rerunning the unit verify gate, noting that the first cargo test form in the unit verify string is malformed and the fallback form is the effective gate today.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.4-strengthen-mana-worker-context-assembly.md:29:  Patched the remaining TaskContext initializers in imp_session/system_prompt tests. Fallback verify gate `cargo test -p imp-core mana_worker --no-run` now passes. Running focused mana_worker tests next for stronger evidence beyond compile-only coverage.

.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1.1-simplify-spawn-to-adhoc-helper-agents-only.md:9:acceptance: The ad_hoc helper-agent tool is exposed canonically as `ask_agent` with `prompt` as the only required field. The old durable unit mode is removed from this tool and callers are directed to native `mana action=run` for unit work. Schema supports optional `worker_type` enum (`reviewer`, `worker`, `no_tools`) defaulting to `reviewer`, optional bounded `timeout_secs` with defaults from config profiles/fallback 300, and only explicitly retained model knobs. Config supports per-worker-type defaults for reasoning/model/turns/tokens/timeout/tool mode where practical. Runtime maps worker_type to the appropriate AgentMode/tool availability, returns structured timeout/error details, and tests cover the new schema/name/defaults/config profiles and old unit/spawn payload guidance. Verify command passes.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1.1-simplify-spawn-to-adhoc-helper-agents-only.md:16:close_reason: 'Implemented and committed spawn-to-ask_agent redesign in 4fbd0fe `Redesign spawn as ask_agent`. The tool is now canonical `ask_agent` with lean schema (`prompt`, `worker_type`, `timeout_secs`); `prompt` is required; durable mana-unit execution is rejected with guidance to use native mana `action=run`; worker_type enum supports reviewer/worker/no_tools with configurable default profiles in Config; no_tools uses no-tool reviewer-mode helper; removed old model-facing spawn/delegate/action/mode/unit fields and unit-worker implementation from this tool; kept `imp` alias pointing to ask_agent; updated allowlists, tests, TUI summaries/output handling, and blocker signal references. Verification passed: `cargo test -p imp-core imp -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core builder_registers_canonical_tools_and_compat_aliases -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core agent_mode_orchestrator_allows_read -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core non_orchestrator_modes_block_ask_agent -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core imp --no-run`; `cargo test -p imp-tui sidebar -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-tui tool_output -- --nocapture`.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.3.1.1-simplify-spawn-to-adhoc-helper-agents-only.md:46:  notes: 'Implemented and committed spawn-to-ask_agent redesign in 4fbd0fe `Redesign spawn as ask_agent`. The tool is now canonical `ask_agent` with lean schema (`prompt`, `worker_type`, `timeout_secs`); `prompt` is required; durable mana-unit execution is rejected with guidance to use native mana `action=run`; worker_type enum supports reviewer/worker/no_tools with configurable default profiles in Config; no_tools uses no-tool reviewer-mode helper; removed old model-facing spawn/delegate/action/mode/unit fields and unit-worker implementation from this tool; kept `imp` alias pointing to ask_agent; updated allowlists, tests, TUI summaries/output handling, and blocker signal references. Verification passed: `cargo test -p imp-core imp -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core builder_registers_canonical_tools_and_compat_aliases -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core agent_mode_orchestrator_allows_read -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core non_orchestrator_modes_block_ask_agent -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core imp --no-run`; `cargo test -p imp-tui sidebar -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-tui tool_output -- --nocapture`.'

.mana/archive/2026/04/29.6.1-implement-native-mana-scope-targeting-in-imp-tool.md:20:verify: cd /Users/asher/tower/imp && cargo test -p imp-core tools::mana::tests::root_scope_targets_tower_root_mana -- --nocapture
.mana/archive/2026/04/29.6.1-implement-native-mana-scope-targeting-in-imp-tool.md:25:Goal: keep the implemented root/project/auto scope support in imp\'s native mana tool working and verified. Current state: scope and mana_scope support plus root resolution logic are implemented in imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs. Steps: 1. preserve the scope fields in the mana schema, 2. preserve resolve_mana_dir logic for auto/project/root, 3. keep the focused root-scope test passing. Files: imp/crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs (modify only if regressions appear). In scope: scope schema, resolver logic, focused tests. Out of scope: higher-level planning behavior.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.6.2-decide-whether-imp-sdk-v1-needs-an-impruntime-wrap.md:18:verify: test -f .mana/45.10.6.2-decide-whether-imp-sdk-v1-needs-an-impruntime-wrap.md && rg -q '^id:' .mana/45.10.6.2-decide-whether-imp-sdk-v1-needs-an-impruntime-wrap.md
.mana/archive/2026/05/340.2-write-concrete-imp-gui-imp-tui-parity-contract.md:9:acceptance: Epic 340 has a concrete, testable imp-gui/imp-tui parity spec in mana notes covering transcript ownership, prompt behavior, runtime/event behavior, UI requests, commands/overlays, and visual/layout rules. The spec identifies current gaps as follow-up tasks rather than vague concerns.
.mana/archive/2026/05/340.2-write-concrete-imp-gui-imp-tui-parity-contract.md:53:Deepen the 1:1 imp-gui/imp-tui spec based on repeated user feedback. The current notes are too broad and allowed superficially usable work. Write a concrete parity contract in mana for epic 340: core chat invariants (user messages appear immediately and persist, assistant streams at bottom, transcript bottom-aligns), runtime ownership rules, prompt/editor semantics, UI request/approval behavior, command palette/overlay requirements, session startup/history behavior, visual layout constraints, and manual smoke scenarios. Create follow-up child tasks for any known gaps that remain after the sent-message fix.
.mana/archive/2026/04/266.4.6.3-prioritize-the-next-hostability-and-sdk-moves-for.md:17:verify: cd /Users/asher/imp && mana show 266.4.6.3 >/dev/null
.mana/archive/2026/04/266.4.6.3-prioritize-the-next-hostability-and-sdk-moves-for.md:40:   - root mana units around SDK/UI seam work (e.g. 45.10.6, 50.14)
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:12:  Tool-vocabulary planning constraint from the CLI naming decision: because the canonical CLI surface is now leaning toward `imp mana <id>`, do not reuse `run` casually for the imp-to-imp tool. This child should choose tool wording that stays distinct from the CLI mana-worker path even after `imp mana` lands.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:16:  Tool-vocabulary constraint strengthened: this child should optimize for a native agent-facing tool contract that 'just works' with mana-backed execution, not for a design where the agent shells out to the CLI. The tool vocabulary should make clear that agents use native surfaces directly while `imp mana ...` remains the operator-facing command grammar.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:20:  Starting implementation. First pass is inspection of the native `imp` tool contract and user-facing docs to find current `delegate` wording, action names, and how tightly the tool schema is coupled to existing wording. Goal is to choose cleaner tool vocabulary that stays distinct from the new operator-facing `imp mana ...` CLI grammar and remains native-tool-first for agent use.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:26:  - README/operator docs also still use `delegate` / `delegating` language in several places, especially around orchestrator mode and mana child jobs.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:27:  - The current root/proposal direction says the CLI namespace should likely converge on `imp mana ...` for operator-facing execution, so the native imp tool should avoid `run` to prevent another collision.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:28:  - Strong candidate verb after inspection: `spawn` (clear child-worker meaning, distinct from CLI `imp mana <id>`, works for both `unit` and `ad_hoc` modes). Likely contract shape: preferred `action='spawn'` with `spawn_mode` or `mode`; keep `action='delegate'` as backward-compatible alias during migration.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:29:  - Alternative candidates still possible but weaker: `handoff` (less literal for transient helper execution), `worker` (noun-like and awkward as an action), `assist` (too broad), `dispatch` (too orchestration-heavy and close to mana scheduling ownership).
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:40:  6. Avoid `run` as the tool action to keep it distinct from the operator-facing `imp mana ...` CLI namespace.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:51:  Current blocker: focused verification via `cargo test -p imp-core tools::imp -- --nocapture` failed before running tests because the local build cache hit `No space left on device (os error 28)` while writing incremental data for `mana-cli`. Need a small safe disk-space cleanup (likely target/incremental artifacts) before rerunning targeted tests.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:60:  - README wording now says orchestrator mode is for `Plan and spawn workers`, and the mana child-job section is reframed around spawning workers.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:61:  - `crates/imp-core/src/config.rs` orchestrator instruction now says `spawn worker agents via mana for all file work`.

.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:424:    docs/proposals/mana-aware-runtime-context-read-path.md:244:   relevant wiki pages in `imp run` context alongside unit files.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:437:- 'Tool-verb alternatives under active evaluation after inspection: `spawn` (strong candidate: clear child-worker semantics, distinct from CLI `imp mana`), `handoff` (emphasizes transfer of responsibility, weaker for transient helpers), `assign` (works well for unit-backed work, weaker for ad hoc helper sessions), and `launch` (clear execution start, but less explicitly worker-oriented). Avoid `run` because it collides with operator CLI/runtime language, and avoid keeping `delegate` as the preferred term because the user finds it conceptually off.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.3.4-clarify-imp-to-imp-tool-vocabulary-and-align-docs.md:439:- 'Naming decision direction after evaluating alternatives: treat `worker` as the noun/concept, not the tool action verb. Current leading native imp tool contract: preferred `action=''spawn''` with `mode=''unit'' | ''ad_hoc''`, where the tool is described as spawning another imp worker (either on a mana unit or as a bounded transient helper). Keep `action=''delegate''` as a compatibility alias during migration rather than the preferred wording.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/253-map-current-tower-impmana-foundation-against-the-4.md:3:title: Map current Tower imp+mana foundation against the 4-part SuperProduct core and sequence bridging work
.mana/archive/2026/04/253-map-current-tower-impmana-foundation-against-the-4.md:4:slug: map-current-tower-impmana-foundation-against-the-4
.mana/archive/2026/04/253-map-current-tower-impmana-foundation-against-the-4.md:13:  Concrete bridge sequence from the 2026-04-14 follow-up question should be preserved for future execution: moving Tower from proto-runtime to direct 4-core alignment likely requires (1) an explicit scope decision that Tower itself should become the generalized product substrate rather than only the software-work runtime beneath it; then four bridge tracks — SuperState: evolve mana from unit/fact-centric durable work records into a typed Object/Assertion/Event/Rule object graph with universal scope/provenance/time/version dimensions while preserving file-native/workflow strengths; SuperAction: introduce stable inspectable action contracts over that shared state, with imp tools/runtime verbs becoming adapters/executors beneath product actions rather than the actions themselves; SuperComp: define a declarative versioned module/package contract that can add object vocab, workflows, projections/views, action affordances, permissions presets, and integration bindings without free-form product forks; SuperShell: treat imp CLI/TUI/viewer surfaces as an operator-shell proving ground but add a presentation-neutral shared-state surface model so shells render objects/queues/workflows/approvals/modules coherently rather than only agent runtime state. Cross-cutting bridge rules: keep mana as canonical durable truth, keep imp as live execution/runtime, keep final prompt/session behavior in imp, and migrate by adding canonical object/action/module/surface contracts first, then projecting current coding-agent-specific structures onto them rather than replacing everything at once.
.mana/archive/2026/04/253-map-current-tower-impmana-foundation-against-the-4.md:18:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/253-map-current-tower-impmana-foundation-against-the-4.md:23:verify: test -f .mana/253-map-current-tower-impmana-foundation-against-the-4.md && rg -q '^id:' .mana/253-map-current-tower-impmana-foundation-against-the-4.md
.mana/archive/2026/04/253-map-current-tower-impmana-foundation-against-the-4.md:30:Goal: externalize a repo-grounded alignment and gap map between the current Tower stack (`mana` + `imp`) and the 4-part SuperProduct core defined in `/Users/asher/agents/PROJECT.md` and `/Users/asher/agents/spec.md`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/253-map-current-tower-impmana-foundation-against-the-4.md:33:- current Tower docs (`/Users/asher/tower/VISION.md`, `mana/README.md`, `mana/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `imp/README.md`) position `mana` as durable substrate/control plane and `imp` as the live runtime/shell.
.mana/archive/2026/04/253-map-current-tower-impmana-foundation-against-the-4.md:35:- repo-grounded assessment from the 2026-04-14 discussion: `mana` is closest to a proto-SuperState for software-work autonomy; `imp` is closest to proto-SuperAction plus proto-SuperShell; current extension seams are only a partial proto-SuperComp.
.mana/archive/2026/04/253-map-current-tower-impmana-foundation-against-the-4.md:42:4. Sequence the resulting bridge work so future product/runtime planning can reference mana instead of re-deriving the gap from chat.

Add durable coverage around OpenAI request/tool serialization stability so future provider refactors cannot reintroduce unstable tool ordering.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.1-fix-direct-imp-run-provider-auth-resolution-and-ig.md:80:  1. `cargo run -p imp-cli -- --provider openai -m gpt-5.4-nano --no-tools --max-turns 1 --mode json mana 268 --defer-verify` no longer failed with `Incorrect API key provided: ''`; instead it reached the provider and returned HTTP 400 `The 'gpt-5.4-nano' model is not supported when using Codex with a ChatGPT account.` This is positive auth-routing evidence: the runtime resolved saved ChatGPT/Codex OAuth instead of sending an empty API key.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.1.5.1-fix-direct-imp-run-provider-auth-resolution-and-ig.md:81:  2. Follow-up smoke with a supported route shape `cargo run -p imp-cli -- --provider openai -m gpt-5.4 --no-tools --max-turns 1 --mode json mana 268 --defer-verify` reached the model successfully and completed a full streamed response without any empty-string auth error.

.mana/archive/2026/04/266.4.8-review-recent-imp-documentation-and-revise-impvnex.md:12:  Reviewed recent imp docs and updated `imp/vNext.md` accordingly. Main deltas: elevated shared runtime/bootstrap consolidation into phase 0; made CLI-first/default product shape an explicit cross-cutting constraint; strengthened the canonical `imp run` + native `mana` orchestration split; reframed phase 4 around guest-runtime substrate + host-authoritative policy + script-tool separation; clarified mana/imp ownership that `mana` owns structured execution bundles while `imp` owns final prompt assembly and live execution. The Hermes comparison remains useful, but vNext is now more tightly aligned with current imp docs than before.
.mana/archive/2026/04/266.4.8-review-recent-imp-documentation-and-revise-impvnex.md:21:  3. Should canonical worker-runtime clarity (`imp run` + native `mana` orchestration split) be pulled even earlier and more explicitly into phase 0/1?
.mana/archive/2026/04/266.4.8-review-recent-imp-documentation-and-revise-impvnex.md:55:Review the recent imp architecture/product/runtime documentation and update `/Users/asher/imp/vNext.md` so the roadmap reflects the current documented direction rather than only the Hermes comparison. Focus on recently authored or recently active imp docs and proposals, especially those that affect sequencing, hostability, extension direction, CLI/TUI product direction, worker/runtime seams, and mana/imp ownership. Ground findings in inspected docs, discuss the implications, then patch `vNext.md` to align with the current documentation set while preserving the ranked roadmap. Keep edits focused to `vNext.md` unless a tiny discoverability tweak is clearly warranted.
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:3:title: Add a separated interactive prompt layer for mana project memory status context
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:4:slug: add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:9:acceptance: There is an explicit imp-owned prompt seam for compact mana project-memory status at interactive session start, wired through builder/runtime assembly, bounded in size, tested, and resilient when no `.mana/` exists.
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:13:  Decomposition carried forward from 51.1: keep Layer 4 facts as the verified-fact seam and add a separate optional prompt seam for dynamic project-memory status only. Planned shape: `mana_prompt_context` should continue owning runtime retrieval/formatting, but extend `SessionPromptContext` with a bounded optional text block for warnings / working-on / recent-work. `system_prompt::AssembleParams` should gain a distinct optional field rather than overloading `facts`. Formatting constraints: compact preview-oriented lines only, no raw unit bodies, no large notes dumps, no duplication of already-injected facts, and degrade to no-op when `.mana/` is missing or memory-context loading fails. Verify focus should stay narrow: `cargo test -p imp-core mana_prompt_context && cargo test -p imp-core system_prompt`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:19:  - Keep `mana_prompt_context` as the imp-owned runtime retrieval/formatting seam.
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:23:  - Preserve the same resilience contract as `51.1`: no `.mana/` or load failure must degrade to no-op context.
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:27:  Inspected imp-core system_prompt, builder, and mana_prompt_context plus mana-core memory_context. Planned implementation: add a distinct optional `project_memory_status` field/layer in system_prompt assembly, extend SessionPromptContext with a bounded optional status block derived from warnings/working_on/recent_work, keep load failures as no-op, then add focused plumbing/formatting tests and run the unit verify gate.
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:35:  Loaded mana skill per user instruction before continuing. Execution plan: extend `SessionPromptContext` with a bounded optional project-memory status block, add a distinct `project_memory_status` seam in `system_prompt::AssembleParams`, thread it through `builder.rs`, then verify with the task's focused imp-core tests.
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:39:  Repo state differs from the original task context: `system_prompt.rs` and `builder.rs` already contain a distinct `project_memory_status` seam, and `mana_prompt_context.rs` already has initial status-formatting helpers. Adapting plan to finish/verify the slice by fixing `mana_prompt_context` to compile against the new borrowed `MemoryContext` helpers, adding focused formatting tests for the status block, and adding/confirming prompt-layer tests for the new optional seam before running the narrow verify gate.
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:43:  Ran the unit verify gate. Found one issue from this change set (duplicate `system_prompt_empty_project_memory_status_skipped` test name in system_prompt.rs) plus a broader existing imp-core compile blocker unrelated to this unit: missing `finish_turn_mana_review`, missing `mana_review` / `turn_mana_review` fields in several initializers/patterns, and missing `Ord` derives in mana_review enums. Fixing the duplicate test first, then re-running to confirm the remaining failures are external blockers.
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:47:  Implemented/confirmed the imp-side prompt seam in the working tree: `system_prompt::AssembleParams` has distinct optional `project_memory_status`; `builder.rs` threads it alongside facts and keeps task fact overrides status-free; `mana_prompt_context.rs` loads via `mana_core::api::{find_mana_dir,memory_context}`, formats a bounded preview block for warnings / working_on / recent_work, and keeps `.mana` absence/load failure as no-op. Added/confirmed focused tests for status formatting and plumbing. Verify gate remains blocked by unrelated branch-wide `imp-core` compile errors in mana-review/tool-context event plumbing (`finish_turn_mana_review`, missing `mana_review`/`turn_mana_review` fields, missing `Ord` derives), plus the local borrowed-memory test refactor was corrected.
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:51:  Implemented the intended prompt seam in the touched imp-core files: `system_prompt::AssembleParams` now carries separate optional `project_memory_status`, builder passes it independently from facts, and `mana_prompt_context` formats bounded warnings/working-on/recent-work without duplicating facts. Verify is currently blocked by unrelated existing `imp-core` compile drift outside this task’s files: missing `finish_turn_mana_review`, `mana_review`/`turn_mana_review` field propagation gaps, and missing `Ord` derives in `mana_review.rs`, plus many stale `ToolContext` initializers. The verify command fails before reaching this task’s targeted tests.
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:57:  - `imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_prompt_context.rs`: extended `SessionPromptContext` with `project_memory_status: Option<String>`, switched retrieval to `mana_core::api::{find_mana_dir,memory_context}`, and added bounded preview formatting for warnings / working_on / recent_work with focused resilience + no-duplication tests.
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:60:  - `cargo test -p imp-core mana_prompt_context` and `cargo test -p imp-core system_prompt` are both currently blocked by broader pre-existing imp-core compile failures unrelated to this unit (`finish_turn_mana_review` missing, `mana_review` / `turn_mana_review` field mismatches, missing `Ord` derives in `mana_review.rs`).
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:66:  1. Keep the prompt-layer implementation scoped to `imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs`, `imp/crates/imp-core/src/mana_prompt_context.rs`, and `imp/crates/imp-core/src/builder.rs`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/51.5-add-a-separated-interactive-prompt-layer-for-mana.md:69:  4. After the unblocker closes, rerun this unit's verify gate unchanged: `cargo test -p imp-core mana_prompt_context && cargo test -p imp-core system_prompt`.

.mana/archive/2026/04/42-per-agent-cached-context-assembly-for-mana-dispatc.md:3:title: Per-agent cached context assembly for mana dispatch
.mana/archive/2026/04/42-per-agent-cached-context-assembly-for-mana-dispatc.md:4:slug: per-agent-cached-context-assembly-for-mana-dispatc
.mana/archive/2026/04/42-per-agent-cached-context-assembly-for-mana-dispatc.md:13:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/42-per-agent-cached-context-assembly-for-mana-dispatc.md:15:close_reason: 'Already complete in repo: context_prefill module exists, SessionOptions/ImpSession inject prefill, headless mana dispatch parses files and assembles context, and the unit verify tests pass. Closing stale-open unit after confirmation.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/42-per-agent-cached-context-assembly-for-mana-dispatc.md:30:When `mana run` dispatches agents, each agent starts cold — it has to spend 3-5 turns calling `read` on files that the orchestrator already identified in the unit description. Those read calls cost output tokens and add latency, and the file contents aren't in the cached prefix so they're re-sent on every subsequent turn.
.mana/archive/2026/04/42-per-agent-cached-context-assembly-for-mana-dispatc.md:61:3. **Budget management**:
.mana/archive/2026/04/42-per-agent-cached-context-assembly-for-mana-dispatc.md:69:5. **Cache strategy**: The prefill messages use the cache options from the agent config. For mana agents specifically, use `extended_ttl: true` so the cache survives across turns (agent sessions are short — 5-30 minutes — well within the 1-hour TTL).
.mana/archive/2026/04/42-per-agent-cached-context-assembly-for-mana-dispatc.md:73:### 1. Unit file declaration (mana-core)
.mana/archive/2026/04/42-per-agent-cached-context-assembly-for-mana-dispatc.md:100:    /// Whether to use extended TTL on cache. Default: true for mana.
.mana/archive/2026/04/42-per-agent-cached-context-assembly-for-mana-dispatc.md:193:let unit = load_mana_unit(&cwd, unit_id)?;
.mana/archive/2026/04/42-per-agent-cached-context-assembly-for-mana-dispatc.md:218:For mana agents, also set `extended_ttl: true` so the cache persists across the full agent session.
.mana/archive/2026/04/42-per-agent-cached-context-assembly-for-mana-dispatc.md:240:- `crates/imp-core/src/context_prefill.rs` — assembler, file specs, budget management

.mana/archive/2026/05/340.1-add-internal-imp-gui-runtime-shell.md:47:Introduce an internal GUI runtime boundary for imp-gui before wiring real agent/mana execution. The runtime should own app-facing state transitions and communicate with the egui app through explicit commands/events so the GUI remains decoupled from imp-core internals. Initial implementation should stay local to crates/imp-gui, use std channels or simple in-process queues, and preserve the current mock workbench behavior while making future live mana/agent integration straightforward.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-review-accumulation-behind-workflow-runt.md:3:title: Move mana review accumulation behind workflow runtime integration layer
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-review-accumulation-behind-workflow-runt.md:4:slug: move-mana-review-accumulation-behind-workflow-runt
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-review-accumulation-behind-workflow-runt.md:11:  - Mana review finalization and mana mutation accumulation are no longer implemented in `agent/mod.rs`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-review-accumulation-behind-workflow-runt.md:12:  - `agent/run_loop.rs` calls named integration methods for mana review reset/record/finalize.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-review-accumulation-behind-workflow-runt.md:13:  - Existing mana review stop-reason and post-turn assessment behavior is preserved.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-review-accumulation-behind-workflow-runt.md:19:  Implementation complete. Mana review reset/finalize/mutation accumulation moved behind workflow runtime integration methods. `agent/run_loop.rs` now calls `begin_turn_mana_review`, `record_turn_mana_mutations`, and `finish_turn_mana_review`; direct lock/reset/finalize and mutation recording mechanics live in `agent/workflow_integration.rs`. Mana mutation parsing helpers were moved out of `agent/mod.rs` into the integration module. Existing post-turn behavior is preserved: `assess_post_turn` still consumes the finalized `TurnManaReview`, and `mana_review_stop_reason` remains in mod.rs for now because it is part of post-turn assessment behavior. Verification passed with `mana verify 365.9`, `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work`, and `cargo check -p imp-core`. Native `work next` in `/Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary` reports 0 ready task(s).
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-review-accumulation-behind-workflow-runt.md:54:Continue slimming imp runtime by moving mana review/mutation accumulation out of the core Agent module and behind workflow integration methods.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-review-accumulation-behind-workflow-runt.md:57:- `agent/mod.rs` still owns `finish_turn_mana_review`, `record_mana_mutation_results`, mana mutation parsing helpers, and mana review stop-reason helper.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-review-accumulation-behind-workflow-runt.md:58:- `agent/run_loop.rs` directly locks/resets `turn_mana_review`, records mana mutations, and finalizes turn mana review.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-review-accumulation-behind-workflow-runt.md:60:Make a behavior-preserving extraction to `agent/workflow_integration.rs`. Keep the runtime event shape unchanged for now. The goal is not to remove mana review yet; it is to stop the core runtime module and run loop from owning mana-specific mutation/review mechanics directly.
.mana/archive/2026/04/297-prepare-imp-and-mana-crates-for-cratesio-publishin.md:4:slug: prepare-imp-and-mana-crates-for-cratesio-publishin

Need to design this as a multi-phase release-prep epic spanning /Users/asher/imp and /Users/asher/mana. Constraint: do not run cargo publish without explicit user approval. Likely phases: 1) audit publish readiness/dependency graph for both repos; 2) prepare mana crates first because imp depends on mana-core and mana-cli; 3) replace imp local mana path deps with versioned deps or workspace-compatible publish metadata; 4) add/align package metadata and license fields; 5) cargo package --dry-run in dependency order; 6) final publish checklist/tag/version plan.
.mana/archive/2026/04/297-prepare-imp-and-mana-crates-for-cratesio-publishin.md:23:  - imp-core also depends on local mana path crates: mana-core via workspace path ../mana/crates/mana-core and mana-cli path ../../../mana/crates/mana-cli package=mana-cli. These must be published/versioned or the dependency removed/reworked.
.mana/archive/2026/04/297-prepare-imp-and-mana-crates-for-cratesio-publishin.md:24:  - imp worktree is dirty and ahead 7; includes README/mana notes and unrelated code changes in config/settings.

.mana/archive/2026/04/297-prepare-imp-and-mana-crates-for-cratesio-publishin.md:36:  User clarified: mana publishing is handled in another chat. Narrow this epic to imp-only publishing readiness. No cargo publish without explicit approval.
.mana/archive/2026/04/297-prepare-imp-and-mana-crates-for-cratesio-publishin.md:40:  User clarified README positioning before publish: imp should be presented first as a coding agent with many useful features and active development. Mana ships with/alongside imp but readers will not know what mana is, so the README should introduce mana plainly as an included durable task/work graph rather than assuming familiarity or over-centering it. Need another README pass before publish readiness. Also answer publish readiness as not yet: imp package metadata/dependency/version readiness still needs work after README positioning.
.mana/archive/2026/04/297-prepare-imp-and-mana-crates-for-cratesio-publishin.md:51:Prepare both /Users/asher/imp and /Users/asher/mana for crates.io publishing. Do not run `cargo publish` without explicit user approval. Prefer small reversible manifest/docs changes and verify with cargo package/dry-run.
.mana/archive/2026/04/268-smoke-test-direct-imp-run-openai-auth-resolution.md:28:Temporary verification unit for 28.1.5.1. Run headless with `imp run` / `imp mana <unit-id>` using `--provider openai -m gpt-5.4-nano --no-tools --max-turns 1 --mode json --defer-verify`. The worker should emit a single short response containing `SMOKE_OK` and must not fail with `Incorrect API key provided: ''` or any empty-string auth path. Prompt intent: reply with exactly `SMOKE_OK` and stop.

.mana/archive/2026/05/359.1-implement-real-mana-to-imp-work-shadow-import-pari.md:13:  2026-05-21 planning/graph checkpoint: inspected active parent 360 and child 359.1 after reparenting from archived 359. Tree is now `360 -> 359.1`; no blockers are recorded. Verify currently passes (`cargo test -p imp-work && cargo test -p imp-core work_tool -- --nocapture`) against existing foundation, but acceptance is not complete because real mana unit/file/API shadow import into an imp-work store plus parity reporting is not yet implemented. Keep 359.1 open.
.mana/archive/2026/05/359.1-implement-real-mana-to-imp-work-shadow-import-pari.md:17:  2026-05-21 direction correction: this unit should be treated as a one-way migration-script slice, not a long-term compatibility/shadow-sync slice. Acceptance now means dry-run `.mana` -> `.imp/work` importer with parity/loss report and no `.mana` mutation. Current implementation does not satisfy this yet; keep open. Required next checks once implemented: focused imp-work migration tests, full `cargo test -p imp-work`, and `cargo test -p imp-core work_tool -- --nocapture`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/359.1-implement-real-mana-to-imp-work-shadow-import-pari.md:21:  2026-05-21 state sync after direction update: inspected 360 and 359.1 plus tree/next. 359.1 is open under 360 with no blockers. Updated the unit verify metadata/file to the migration-script-focused gate (`cargo test -p imp-work mana_migration -- --nocapture && cargo test -p imp-work && cargo test -p imp-core work_tool -- --nocapture`). `mana verify 359.1` currently passes, but the unit remains open because acceptance requires implementing the real dry-run `.mana` -> `.imp/work` importer and parity/loss report. Current git worktree is dirty from unrelated scan files (`scan/mod.rs`, new odin/perl/shell/zig scan adapters); do not mix those with the mana-migration importer slice.
.mana/archive/2026/05/359.1-implement-real-mana-to-imp-work-shadow-import-pari.md:25:  2026-05-21 implementation update: dry-run `.mana` -> imp-work migration is now implemented in `crates/imp-work/src/mana_shadow.rs`. Added `ManaMigrationReport`, `dry_run_mana_migration`, `migrate_mana_to_store`, mana frontmatter/body parsing, field parity tracking, lossy/missing/warning reporting, dry-run non-mutation tests, write-mode store persistence test, and skipped/missing-fields test. Verification passed: `cargo test -p imp-work mana_migration -- --nocapture` (3 focused tests), `cargo test -p imp-work` (62 tests), `cargo test -p imp-core work_tool -- --nocapture` (27 tests), and `mana verify 359.1`. Remaining concern before close/commit: worktree also contains unrelated scan adapter changes (`crates/imp-core/src/tools/scan/mod.rs`, new odin/perl/shell/zig adapters); keep importer commit scoped to `crates/imp-work/src/mana_shadow.rs` and mana metadata.

.mana/archive/2026/05/320-audit-imp-release-branch-for-personal-development.md:17:  Completed read-only audit pass on /tmp/imp-release-audit release branch (HEAD 290a362). Evidence gathered: git status clean before scans; 529 tracked files; gitleaks reports 3 generic-api-key findings in git history for crates/imp-llm/src/providers/anthropic.rs, all matching Anthropic beta header string `oauth-2025-04-20` rather than an actual secret; current file around lines 660-705 contains no secret. Pattern audit found tracked local/generated artifacts: .tmp/imp-run-trial/one-shot-print.txt, .vibecheck SQLite db/shm/wal, root file `=`, duplicate `AGENTS copy.md`, art/draft HTML/MD files, tmp-find-django.sh, eval task JSONs hardcoding /Users/asher/imp/target/debug/imp, tools/imp-fix-signature.sh defaulting to /Users/asher/.local/imp-current/bin/imp, many .mana files with /Users/asher and root Tower paths. Also found tracked crates/imp-cli/auth.json with dummy `sk-test`. OSV dependency scan reports 10 affected Rust packages / 17 advisories, including high openssl 0.10.76 and libyml/serde_yml advisories. Semgrep unavailable in audit wrapper despite tool listing. Accidentally opening .vibecheck db with sqlite mutated WAL files in audit worktree; restored them with git restore and status returned clean.

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-durable-workflow-post-turn-classification-beh.md:20:  Implementation complete after recovering from failed refactor command. Root cause of failure: a broad textual replacement changed struct field syntax in `RuntimeEvidence` to `workflow_signals.execution_evidence,` and introduced `workflow_signals.workflow_signals.execution_evidence`. Fixed by explicitly naming fields and using `workflow_signals.execution_*` values. Durable workflow post-turn classification helpers are now in `agent/workflow_integration.rs`; `agent/mod.rs::assess_post_turn` obtains a `WorkflowPostTurnSignals` value through `self.workflow_post_turn_signals(...)` and no longer implements the mana/work durable classification helpers. Existing tests were updated to call workflow-layer test accessors. Verification passed: `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work`, `cargo check -p imp-core`, and `mana verify 365.9`. Native `work next` in `/Users/asher/imp-runtime-boundary` reports 0 ready task(s).
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-durable-workflow-post-turn-classification-beh.md:54:Continue slimming imp runtime by moving mana/work durable workflow classification out of the core post-turn assessment and into `agent/workflow_integration.rs`.

.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.4-define-imp-product-thesis-assuming-mana-is-open-an.md:3:title: Define imp product thesis assuming mana is open and externally integrable
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.4-define-imp-product-thesis-assuming-mana-is-open-an.md:4:slug: define-imp-product-thesis-assuming-mana-is-open-an
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.4-define-imp-product-thesis-assuming-mana-is-open-an.md:17:  These signals suggest imp should be treated as an ambitious product with specific UX/runtime tastes, not merely a mana client or generic shell clone.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.4-define-imp-product-thesis-assuming-mana-is-open-an.md:22:  - Imp should be treated as a product-first effort, not justified only by exclusive mana capability.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.4-define-imp-product-thesis-assuming-mana-is-open-an.md:26:  - Areas to keep strong but secondary to core product quality: mana integration, subagent/delegation story, review/verify loop, autonomous execution path.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.4-define-imp-product-thesis-assuming-mana-is-open-an.md:28:  - Open product questions to resolve next: what `more opinionated than pi` should concretely decide for the user; what `more streamlined than hermes` specifically means; and whether the next architecture focus should shift from broad mana-platform refactor talk to the imp product loop itself.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.4-define-imp-product-thesis-assuming-mana-is-open-an.md:36:  - Important follow-up architecture/product question: what should imp's own canonical loop be, given the user's attraction to OMX's clarify/plan/execute/team structure and prior interest in mana task packets, verify, and operator experience?
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.4-define-imp-product-thesis-assuming-mana-is-open-an.md:46:  This suggests the next product-definition step should be to define the semantics, transitions, and auto-escalation rules for this explicit 4-part imp loop, and then map mana integration onto it rather than the other way around.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.4-define-imp-product-thesis-assuming-mana-is-open-an.md:58:  Open architecture question surfaced: should imp remain the runtime, or should future planning keep mana as the owner of the runtime while imp becomes the flagship product/agent on top? This is now a key fork because it affects whether imp is treated primarily as a product/application layer or as the authoritative execution host. Need evaluate against recent product-direction signals: imp as ambitious coding-agent product, possible GUI, native worktrees, explicit `/ask /plan /work /improve` loop, and mana remaining broadly integrable by external agents.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.4-define-imp-product-thesis-assuming-mana-is-open-an.md:62:  Provisional architecture recommendation to pressure-test next: keep the future-facing target architecture where mana owns the platform/runtime substrate, but refine the wording so imp still owns the agent runtime/product loop. Concretely: mana would own the execution substrate, graph, isolation/worktrees, approvals/policy, and task/packet APIs; imp would own the flagship agent product, prompt/context assembly, `/ask /plan /work /improve` workflow, operator UX, and mana-native execution behavior. This is a proposal to discuss, not a settled decision.

.mana/archive/2026/05/366-build-imp-native-programmatic-workflow-engine.md:15:  - Relationship to mana is explicitly decided: whether this supersedes, coexists with, or narrows mana to optional indexing/aggregation.
.mana/archive/2026/05/366-build-imp-native-programmatic-workflow-engine.md:39:New epic for the revised direction: workflows are an imp-native programmatic orchestration layer, not primarily a mana-owned durable ledger and not necessarily portable without imp. The workflow engine should be built into imp as a first-class tool/runtime layer that can plan, enforce, execute, update, verify, review, delegate bounded subagents, assemble context, and block invalid closeout. CLI may exist, but as a minimal control surface over the imp workflow engine, not the product center. This epic should research existing agent workflow systems and define a design that takes the best ideas while focusing on coding-agent quality and systematic mitigation of LLM coding weaknesses.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.3-map-future-subagent-handoff-and-scoped-task-packet.md:3:title: Map future subagent handoff and scoped task packet over the mana work-memory graph
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.3-map-future-subagent-handoff-and-scoped-task-packet.md:19:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.3-map-future-subagent-handoff-and-scoped-task-packet.md:27:verify: test -f .mana/45.10.3-map-future-subagent-handoff-and-scoped-task-packet.md && rg -q '^id:' .mana/45.10.3-map-future-subagent-handoff-and-scoped-task-packet.md
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.3-map-future-subagent-handoff-and-scoped-task-packet.md:38:- Future subagent handoff should not require every worker assignment to originate from an already-existing durable task. Mana should support both graph-backed tasks and parent-created ad hoc delegated tasks by normalizing them into the same scoped task packet schema, assembled on demand and cached/materialized only as an optimization.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.3-map-future-subagent-handoff-and-scoped-task-packet.md:41:Goal: preserve and improve the current high-value mana workflow where imp points a subagent at a bounded job and the worker receives exactly the context it needs without making decisions outside scope.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.3-map-future-subagent-handoff-and-scoped-task-packet.md:42:Current state: the future architecture is shifting toward `mana` as platform, `imp` as agent, and a broader work-memory graph rather than only file-native unit/fact records. The refactor must not lose the strong existing handoff pattern where a subagent executes a bounded mana job with a purpose-built context window and clear scope.
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.3-map-future-subagent-handoff-and-scoped-task-packet.md:49:5. How to preserve compatibility with current mana-job worker flow during migration.

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-add-no-op-bounded-subagent-coordinator-api.md:22:  Implementation complete for no-op bounded subagent coordinator API. Added `SubagentPlan`, spawn/cancel/merge result structs, `SubagentCoordinatorError`, `SubagentCoordinator` trait, and `NoopSubagentCoordinator` in `crates/imp-core/src/agent/subagent.rs`; exported coordinator types from `agent/mod.rs`. The no-op coordinator allows planning but returns explicit `Unsupported` errors for spawn/cancel/merge. Tests now cover planning, explicit unsupported execution operations, terminal status, and runtime-scoped event serialization. Verification passed: `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work`, `cargo check -p imp-core`, `cargo test -p imp-core subagent --lib`, `mana verify 365.9`, native `work validate` 0 errors/0 warnings, native `work next` 0 ready task(s). Worktree remains dirty with the two modified source files and should be committed before continuing.

.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4-rework-native-mana-tool-into-self-explaining-execu.md:3:title: Rework native mana tool into self-explaining executable spec interface
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4-rework-native-mana-tool-into-self-explaining-execu.md:4:slug: rework-native-mana-tool-into-self-explaining-execu
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4-rework-native-mana-tool-into-self-explaining-execu.md:9:acceptance: Native mana can support routine durable planning, task creation, verification, decisions/notes/facts, and subagent orchestration with less reliance on heavy external guidance. Tool schema is slimmer/canonical, errors are self-teaching, guide/template actions exist, worker context assembly is tested, orchestration outputs support recovery, and the mana skill/default guidance is compact and aligned with native affordances.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4-rework-native-mana-tool-into-self-explaining-execu.md:13:  Mana skill loaded on explicit user instruction before decomposing the mana tool rework. Must follow unit design/decomposition/retry/handoff guidance: create concrete executable child tasks with current state, steps, file paths, scope boundaries, and verify gates; use deps for sequence; do not create vague placeholder jobs; keep worker units narrow; record decisions/notes durably before continuing.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4-rework-native-mana-tool-into-self-explaining-execu.md:17:  Loaded current mana skill via read at user request before continuing cleanup. Must follow it for unit design, decomposition, retries, and worker handoff. Current next step: clean wording in mana units before running workers; avoid implementation or orchestration until wording is aligned.

Agents can inspect a run, update failed units with new diagnosis, and rerun without guessing. Tests cover representative output shapes or rendering helpers.

Verified: cd /Users/asher/mana && cargo test -p mana-core --quiet && cargo test -p mana-cli commands::run --quiet.'

Verified: cd /Users/asher/mana && cargo test -p mana-core --quiet && cargo test -p mana-cli commands::run --quiet.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/339.1-extract-mana-run-planning-and-worker-context-apis.md:52:Move or expose the non-CLI parts of mana run needed by native embedders from mana-cli into mana-core. Include target resolution, config/index loading, ready/dependency planning, skipped/blocked reasons, retry/decision guardrails, and worker context/prompt assembly. Preserve CLI behavior by making mana-cli call these core APIs. Avoid CLI stdout/stderr, signal handlers, process-global shutdown flags, and command spawning in mana-core. Current references: /Users/asher/mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/run/{mod.rs,plan.rs,ready_queue.rs}; existing prompt/context assembly lives under mana CLI today and should become core-owned API. Output should be a small, documented core API suitable for imp-core embedding.
