.mana/archive/2026/05/362.9-run-real-project-mana-to-imp-work-migration-parity.md:13:  2026-05-21 real-project parity gate completed non-destructively. Added `mana_migration_real_project_dry_run_and_scratch_write_are_non_destructive` to `crates/imp-work/src/mana_shadow.rs`. The test runs against this repo's real `.mana`, performs dry-run import, writes to a temp/scratch imp-work store, verifies imported counts match dry-run, verifies archive counts match, verifies scratch store contains tasks, and asserts `.mana` top-level md file count is unchanged. Verification passed: `cargo test -p imp-work mana_migration -- --nocapture` (5 tests), `cargo test -p imp-work` (75 tests), `cargo test -p imp-core work_tool -- --nocapture` (29 tests), and `mana verify 362.9`. No `.mana` data was mutated/deleted.

.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:4:slug: draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:12:  Repo-grounded tightening after reading `specialized-task-packet.md`, `integration-point-task-result-execution.md`, `imp-run-primary-execution-path.md`, `rich-durable-result.md`, `mana-core/src/prompt.rs`, and the current `tower-contracts` crate:
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:17:  - `WorkerAssignment` in `imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs` is already the best repo-grounded specialized task packet.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:19:  - `mana-core/src/prompt.rs` should be treated as durable task/input preparation for compatibility flows, not proof that v1 needs a large standalone planning schema in shared contracts.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:23:  - `CloseEvidence`, `CloseResult`, `VerifyFailureResult` in mana-core
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:24:  - `Review`, `RiskLevel`, `RiskFlag` in mana-review
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:42:  - `mana` owns durable unit truth, verify history, close evidence, and review/risk persistence
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:48:  - keep compatibility re-exports in `imp_core::mana_worker` during migration
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:57:  - the next child under 47.1 should not be a broad crate-authoring task; it should define the first execution-ready post-worker-quartet slice: `VerifierResult` + minimal `ArtifactRef` + minimal evidence bundle/reference vocabulary in `tower-contracts`, plus compatibility adapters in `imp_core::mana_worker`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:58:  - current consumer state supports this sequencing: `imp-cli` still imports worker contracts through `imp_core::mana_worker` in tests/helpers, while `imp-core/src/tools/imp.rs` already imports `tower_contracts::worker::WorkerStatus` directly. That mixed state is exactly why the next slice should pair new contract types with adapter-cleanup guidance rather than doing a speculative larger redesign.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:62:  2026-04-23 resumed and reverified. This analysis/spec unit is already materially complete in mana: the narrowed repo-grounded v1 contracts surface, ownership rules, crate-placement recommendation, deferred scope, and concrete follow-on slice are all explicitly captured in the notes and decisions. The verify gate still passes (`test -n "analysis-only"`). Final resolved outcome: `tower-contracts` remains the neutral Tower-level boundary; v1 should stay narrow around the strongest existing seam (`WorkerAssignment`, `WorkerAttempt`, `WorkerResult`/`WorkerStatus`) plus the next verification/evidence lineage types, while broader planning-schema families remain deferred until a second concrete consumer appears. No new code change was required; acceptance is satisfied and the unit can be closed.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:83:- After the worker quartet already present in `tower-contracts`, the next implementation slice should add verification/evidence contracts before any broader task-planning schema. This is the narrowest repo-grounded move because `mana_worker.rs` already runs verify/close flows and the current result lineage needs a shared verifier/evidence vocabulary more urgently than a new planning packet.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:86:Goal: draft the first concrete shared contracts surface between mana and imp directly in mana so implementation can start from a stable typed boundary instead of ad hoc runtime structs and prose-only doctrine.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:90:- `crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs` currently contains important worker-facing types that are cross-boundary in spirit but owned by imp.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:91:- `mana-core` already owns durable unit, verify-history, dependency, and artifact-adjacent state, but the exact shared protocol layer is still implicit.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:108:Recommended default: a Tower-level shared crate, conceptually sibling to mana and imp, even if the first practical implementation lands under `mana/` or the root workspace for convenience.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:116:- place the crate where it is easiest to wire immediately, but document that the API is a Tower boundary and not a mana-internal or imp-internal detail
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:119:1. Tower-root crate now vs temporary mana-owned crate with planned extraction later
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:123:### mana should own durable truth about
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:147:- if another worker, reviewer, scheduler, or substrate process must trust it cold, the boundary type should exist in shared contracts and the durable record should live in mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:191:These define what mana hands to imp.
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:223:- this should be derived initially from the current `imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs` assignment shape, but promoted into the shared boundary crate
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:269:1. Which of these statuses are shared contracts vs mana-internal state machine labels?
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:272:- keep the shared contract slightly richer than the current mana status enum, then let mana map them into durable unit state intentionally
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:342:3. Which artifact references must be durable in mana vs allowed to remain ephemeral implementation detail?
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:440:2. Which fields must be durable in mana immediately?
.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.2-draft-v1-shared-mana-imp-contracts-crate-and-proto.md:477:4. add compatibility adapters from current `imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs`

.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:3:title: Implement SQLite-derived index for mana agent context assembly
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:4:slug: implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:9:acceptance: Implementation preserves canonical Markdown+YAML files, adds SQLite only as a derived/rebuildable index behind stable mana APIs, and follows the embedded canonical implementation plan in this unit notes. The worker should not need to read external docs first; docs/architecture copies are supporting artifacts only.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:16:  1. Add mana-core internal derived-index module, not public raw SQLite access.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:21:  6. Wire `ops/context::assemble_agent_context` and `ops/memory_context::memory_context` to prefer fresh SQLite through stable mana-core query APIs.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:22:  7. Extend existing `mana doctor`/`doctor fix` for parse/schema and SQLite index diagnostics/rebuild.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:27:  Long-run implementation epic should not start until 247.1.2.5 and 247.1.2.6 are complete. Once unblocked, run children in order: 247.1.2.7.1 schema/freshness metadata, 247.1.2.7.2 full rebuild builder, 247.1.2.7.3 write-path updates, 247.1.2.7.4 context assembly read path, 247.1.2.7.5 doctor/index rebuild surfaces. Preserve canonical Markdown+YAML throughout and keep SQLite behind stable mana APIs.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:31:  2026-04-27 canonical design docs were written to `docs/architecture/`, but user clarified the implementation source should be mana itself so the next agent does not need to read docs twice. Embedded canonical implementation plan below; external docs are only supporting human-readable artifacts.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:33:  # Mana SQLite derived index — canonical implementation plan
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:36:  Mana keeps canonical storage as human-editable typed Markdown+YAML files. SQLite is a derived, rebuildable index for fast/richer agent context assembly. Flow: human edit or mana command -> canonical `.mana/*.md` YAML frontmatter + Markdown body -> parse + validate typed fields -> derived SQLite -> stable mana-core APIs -> agent context/model input. SQLite is not source of truth; if missing/stale/corrupt, rebuild from canonical files.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:39:  - Canonical source remains human-readable/editable `.mana/*.md`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:45:  - Mana APIs may read SQLite internally when fresh/valid.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:47:  - Every mana command cheaply checks index health/freshness.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:48:  - Every mana-controlled write validates candidate canonical content before landing, then updates SQLite.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:49:  - Manual edits are detected on next mana command via freshness metadata.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:50:  - Invalid YAML/frontmatter/schema blocks mana-controlled writes and blocks agent/scheduler use of relevant invalid units.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:51:  - Existing `mana doctor` is extended for parse/schema and SQLite diagnostics.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:54:  - Public/core API: `mana/crates/mana-core/src/api/mod.rs` (`create_unit`, `update_unit`, `close_unit`, `get_unit`, `list_units`, `load_index`, `assemble_context`, `get_status`, `get_tree`, dependency/ready-queue helpers).
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:55:  - Context surfaces: `ops/context.rs::assemble_agent_context(mana_dir, id)`, `ops/memory_context.rs::memory_context(mana_dir)`, prompt assembly paths that load units/context.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:57:  - Doctor: `mana-cli/src/commands/doctor.rs`; currently checks stale index, mixed formats, duplicate IDs, orphaned deps, missing parents, archived parent refs, stale/missing index entries, cycles, config findings; helper scans can skip unparsable unit files via `if let Ok(unit) = Unit::from_file`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:58:  - SQLite must be internal to mana-core, replacing/augmenting index/context internals, not becoming public DB API.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:63:  Add internal module like `mana-core/src/derived_index/{mod,schema,rebuild,freshness,query}.rs`. Suggested internal API: `DerivedIndex::open`, `health`, `rebuild`, `update_unit_from_file`, `mark_stale`. Context assembly should call query helpers like `query_agent_context`, `query_memory_context`, `query_relevant_facts`, `query_dependency_providers`, `query_child_summaries`; return Rust structs owned by mana-core, never raw SQL/table access.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:66:  Use derived project-local state, likely `.mana/index.sqlite`. It coexists with existing file index during migration. It can be deleted/rebuilt and should not be required for correctness or committed for correctness.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:69:  Every SQLite read needs freshness check. Metadata: schema version, mana/index build version, mana root, last full rebuild timestamp, last incremental update timestamp, global stale flag/reason, source path/hash/mtime/size, parse/validation status. SQLite read allowed only when schema supported, not stale/corrupt, relevant source files match metadata, and no relevant unit invalid. Otherwise rebuild/update/fallback/block depending on command.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:79:  - `index_meta(key PRIMARY KEY, value)` for `schema_version`, `mana_version`, `mana_root`, `last_full_rebuild_at`, `last_incremental_update_at`, `stale`, `stale_reason`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:95:  `assemble_agent_context(mana_dir, id)`: open derived index -> check health/freshness -> rebuild/update if stale/safe -> load requested unit -> fail if invalid/missing -> load child summaries by parent -> load dependency providers from dependencies/artifacts -> load attempt notes -> load relevant facts from facts/paths/labels/text -> load referenced file contents from filesystem as today -> return existing `AgentContext` or stable equivalent. Preserve external `AgentContext` shape if possible. `memory_context` similarly queries active/claimed/in-progress units, recent updated/closed units, verified/non-stale facts, failed attempts/last failures.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:98:  Extend existing `mana doctor`. New checks: SQLite missing/unsupported/stale/corrupt, source hash/mtime mismatch, parse/schema errors, unit rows missing for valid files, unit rows present for invalid/missing files, stale diagnostics older than current source hash. `doctor fix` may rebuild SQLite, remove stale derived rows, clear obsolete diagnostics. It must not silently rewrite canonical files, change status, delete files, resolve decisions, close units, or alter deps.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:101:  For mana-controlled writes: build candidate, serialize, parse via `Unit::from_string`, validate required invariants, only then write/replace. For manual edits: detect source metadata changes, parse/validate before use, block relevant invalid units, report file/field diagnostics. Classes: parse error, schema error, referential error, policy blocker, stale index warning. Parse/schema block relevant units; referential may block scheduler while still visible in list/doctor.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:119:  # Mana human-edit validation and doctor workflow — canonical implementation plan
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:122:  Mana canonical files are edited by humans and consumed by agents. Human editability is first-class, but invalid typed mana files must block agent/scheduler use until repaired. Existing `mana doctor` is the main diagnostic/repair surface and should be extended for YAML/frontmatter parse errors, schema errors, and SQLite-derived index drift.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:125:  - Preserve direct human editing of `.mana/*.md`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:126:  - Prevent invalid YAML/frontmatter from becoming durable state through mana-controlled writes.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:127:  - Detect manual edit breakage on the next mana command.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:130:  - Extend existing `mana doctor`/`doctor fix`, not a parallel command.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:134:  `mana doctor` lives at `mana/crates/mana-cli/src/commands/doctor.rs`. It checks stale file index, mixed formats, duplicate IDs, orphaned deps, missing parents, archived parent refs, stale/missing index entries, cycles, config findings. Current scans may silently skip unparsable unit files using `if let Ok(unit) = Unit::from_file(&path)`, which is unsafe once agents rely on mana for context/scheduling.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:137:  - Parse errors: invalid YAML frontmatter, missing frontmatter delimiter, malformed envelope, shapes that prevent deserialization. Policy: block mana-controlled write before landing, block relevant agent/scheduler use, record doctor diagnostic, do not skip.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:143:  ## Mana-controlled write contract
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:147:  Humans may edit `.mana/*.md` directly. Next mana command detects changed path/hash/mtime/size. If file parses/validates, refresh derived state. If invalid, record diagnostic, show exact path and field/location when possible, block agent/scheduler use of relevant unit/context, do not auto-rewrite file.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:153:  `mana doctor` should report parse/schema/referential/policy/index/config diagnostics. Diagnostic fields: severity, kind, path, unit id if known, field if known, message, suggested action. `doctor fix` may rebuild SQLite/file index, remove stale derived rows, clear diagnostics that no longer match current hashes, repair purely derived metadata. It must not silently rewrite canonical YAML, change statuses, delete units, resolve decisions, close units, or alter deps. Auto-fixing canonical files would require explicit separate design/flag.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:159:  Inspect/modify: `unit/mod.rs` (`from_file`, `from_string`, `to_file`, validation helpers), `index.rs` (stale/rebuild parse error handling), `api/mod.rs` (write API/context/list/index behavior), `ops/*` write operations, `ops/context.rs`, `ops/memory_context.rs`, `mana-cli/src/commands/doctor.rs`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:162:  Create shared validation layer used by doctor, SQLite rebuild, write paths, and context assembly. Suggested shapes: `ValidationDiagnostic { severity, kind, path, unit_id, field, message, suggested_action }`, `ValidationKind::{Parse, Schema, Referential, Policy, Index}`, `validate_unit_file(path) -> ValidationResult<Unit>`, `validate_unit_candidate(content) -> ValidationResult<Unit>`, `validate_graph(mana_dir) -> Vec<ValidationDiagnostic>`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:166:  2. Protect mana-controlled writes with serialize -> parse -> validate -> atomic write.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:171:  Do not make human editing read-only. Do not silently rewrite canonical files. Do not make SQLite canonical. Do not add a parallel doctor. Do not block all mana usage because one unrelated file is invalid. Do not solve numbering migration here.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:183:- mana

.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:215:3. Update mana write paths to update affected SQLite rows after canonical writes and mark stale on failure.
.mana/archive/2026/04/247.1.2.7-implement-sqlite-derived-index-for-mana-agent-cont.md:217:5. Add `mana doctor` / `mana index rebuild` commands and diagnostics.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.7-prototype-gated-openai-persistent-transport.md:36:  5. Implement WebSocket path inside crates/imp-llm/src/providers/openai.rs only. Keep OpenAI-specific API names such as previous_response_id private to the provider module; do not leak provider session/continuation IDs to imp-core events, session entries, logs, or mana.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.7-prototype-gated-openai-persistent-transport.md:52:  - OpenAI transport_capabilities returns conservative default unless opt-in env is set; when enabled reports WebSocket persistent_session, ProviderManagedId continuation, ResumeProviderState resumability.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.7-prototype-gated-openai-persistent-transport.md:66:close_reason: 'Implemented and verified opt-in OpenAI persistent/WebSocket transport prototype. Added workspace dependencies tokio-tungstenite/tungstenite and imp-llm dependency wiring. Added OpenAI-only env gate IMP_OPENAI_PERSISTENT_TRANSPORT; default remains existing HTTP SSE path and default transport capabilities remain conservative. When opt-in is enabled, OpenAI provider reports WebSocket persistent_session, provider-managed continuation, and ResumeProviderState capabilities and uses stream_response_websocket. The WebSocket path connects to wss://api.openai.com/v1/responses, sends a response.create JSON payload with store=false and stream omitted, parses returned JSON events through the same OpenAI stream event processor as HTTP SSE, handles ping/pong/close, and surfaces setup/stream errors without logging provider session IDs or raw secrets. Added tests for default stateless capabilities, opt-in persistent capabilities, and redacted websocket create payload. Verification passed: cargo check -p imp-llm; cargo test -p imp-llm openai_transport --lib -- --test-threads=1; cargo test -p imp-llm openai_websocket_payload --lib; cargo test -p imp-llm openai --lib -- --test-threads=1; cargo test -p imp-core persistent_transport --lib; cargo check -p imp-core. Concern: no live OpenAI WebSocket integration test was run, so protocol compatibility is compile/unit verified only.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.7-prototype-gated-openai-persistent-transport.md:166:- 317.5 added interrupted/stale mana run-state classification.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.7-prototype-gated-openai-persistent-transport.md:181:8. Compare baseline latency against 317.1 timing output on a representative tool-heavy workflow and record result in mana.
.mana/archive/2026/05/317.7-prototype-gated-openai-persistent-transport.md:197:- Store raw secrets, prompts, tool args, tool output, model text, provider session IDs, or continuation IDs in logs/session/mana.

.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-validate-mana-skill-demotion-after-first-class-loo.md:13:  User approved reducing/removing routine mentions of the mana skill in system prompt and AGENTS.md. .mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-validate-mana-skill-demotion-after-first-class-loo.md:20:close_reason: 'Implemented approved demotion: removed mode-aware system-prompt Trigger lines that instructed agents to load mana/mana-basics skills; updated follow-up hints to point to native mana guide/template actions instead of read-loading skills; externalization follow-up no longer depends on mana skill availability; AGENTS.md inspected and had no mana skill mentions to remove. Verification passed: cargo test -p imp-core mana_skill_trigger -- --nocapture; cargo test -p imp-core system_prompt_includes_conversation_time_mana_planning_doctrine -- --nocapture; cargo test -p imp-core system_prompt_worker_does_not_add_mana_basics_trigger -- --nocapture; cargo test -p imp-core mana_externalization -- --nocapture. Broader cargo test -p imp-core system_prompt_ had one unrelated pre-existing builder project-memory-status assertion failure.'

.mana/archive/2026/05/330.6-validate-mana-skill-demotion-after-first-class-loo.md:51:1. Inspect where/when the `mana` skill is loaded or recommended in imp's prompt/context/skill-selection flow.

.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6.4.3-add-native-mana-guide-and-template-actions.md:16:close_reason: 'Implemented and committed compact self-teaching mana actions in cb929cb `Add mana guide and template actions`. Added `guide` and `template` actions to the mana schema; `topic` supports overview/task/epic/decision/notes/verify/orchestrate/worker_context; templates support kind epic/task/fact with structured JSON examples. Outputs are compact text plus structured details (summary, guidance/template, next_actions). Tests cover guide output, template output, topic/kind validation, and schema. Verification passed: `cargo test -p imp-core mana_guide -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana_template -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana_schema -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run`.'

.mana/archive/2026/04/47.1.4-implement-the-first-shared-verifier-and-evidence-r.md:16:  2026-04-22: implemented the first shared verifier/evidence contract slice. Added serde-backed `VerifierStatus`, `ArtifactKind`, `ArtifactRef`, `VerifierResult`, and `EvidenceBundleRef` to `tower-contracts`; updated `imp_core::mana_worker` to build a `VerifierResult` at the verify-failure seam; surfaced that contract through `WorkerRunOutcome` and the `imp` tool details; added focused contract and adapter tests. Verified with `cargo check -p tower-contracts -p imp-core -p imp-cli`, `cargo test -p tower-contracts -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p imp-core map_close_outcome_verify_failed_is_failed -- --nocapture`, and `cargo test -p imp-core build_verify_failure_verifier_result_omits_artifact_when_output_empty -- --nocapture`.

.mana/archive/2026/05/354.4-replace-slow-session-mana-memory-context-with-fast.md:4:slug: replace-slow-session-mana-memory-context-with-fast
.mana/archive/2026/05/354.4-replace-slow-session-mana-memory-context-with-fast.md:10:acceptance: TUI trace for a basic prompt shows agent_builder_phase phase=mana_prompt_context in low milliseconds rather than ~14s; imp-core mana_prompt_context tests pass; cargo check -p imp-tui -p imp-core passes; installed imp includes the change.
.mana/archive/2026/05/354.4-replace-slow-session-mana-memory-context-with-fast.md:14:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/05/354.4-replace-slow-session-mana-memory-context-with-fast.md:22:- crates/imp-core/src/mana_prompt_context.rs
.mana/archive/2026/05/354.4-replace-slow-session-mana-memory-context-with-fast.md:38:Trace showed interactive imp TUI startup still spent ~14s in agent_builder_phase phase=mana_prompt_context after input-path fixes. Root cause is session prompt context calling mana_core::api::memory_context(), which reparses archived mana units for recent work/fact relevance and is too expensive for every chat start. Use a fast index-backed session context path for normal interactive startup, preserving compact facts/project status while avoiding full archive parse; keep task-specific context behavior separate if it needs full relevance.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:3:title: Plan implementation migration for mana-first imp behavior
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:4:slug: plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:12:  Migration input from 28.1: implementation should make imp aggressive about durable structure capture and conservative about consequential commitment. The roadmap should account for (1) doctrine/prompt updates that instruct conversation-time mana externalization, (2) runtime behavior for automatic unit/note/decision creation and update during planning turns, (3) confirmation thresholds for high-impact scope/architecture/ownership choices, and (4) user-visible between-turn mana delta summaries, likely informed by 28.4.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:22:  - between-turn rule: update mana first when durable planning state changed, then summarize the delta in the reply
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:25:  1. Patch `imp/crates/imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs` so the runtime doctrine explicitly teaches conversation-time mana externalization, artifact mapping, confirmation thresholds, and between-turn delta behavior.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:26:  2. Update `imp/.imp/skills/mana/SKILL.md` and `imp/.imp/skills/mana-delegation/SKILL.md` so the skill guidance matches 28.1 and does not frame mana only as a late orchestration/delegation tool.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:29:  - runtime enforcement or pre-reply hooks for mana sync and delta reporting, because 28.4 is still the spec unit for UI/runtime affordances and this unit should not guess the interaction surface before that closes
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:30:  - any mana model/API changes that depend on 28.2 or 28.3 boundary/memory outcomes
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:34:  - after 28.4 closes, create a separate runtime/UI implementation job for pre-reply mana sync and visible delta reporting
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:35:  - keep this work in root mana because it spans imp prompt doctrine, mana usage guidance, and the cross-project planning workflow contract
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:43:  New required input from 28.2: migration planning should be organized around replacing mana-side prompt rendering with a typed/structured execution bundle seam. Explicit long-term removals from the mana boundary: final `system_prompt` rendering, final `user_message` rendering, generic runtime coaching prose, and live file-to-prompt embedding. Explicit imp-owned assembly inputs to preserve: identity/personality/soul, tool registry/mode guidance, AGENTS.md/project context, session/personal memory, environment, guardrails, and live file/code-intelligence reads. Acceptable layering to retain during migration: imp-local projection types over canonical durable data and UX-local mirrored run state.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:47:  2026-04-09 — decomposition added from the 28.2 boundary-spec session. Immediate durable follow-ons now worth tracking explicitly: (1) a shared-contracts spec that turns the abstract `ExecutionBundle` / `WorkerResult` boundary into a concrete field-level contract and exclusion list, grounded in current `mana-core/src/prompt.rs`, `imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs`, and `imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs`; (2) a migration-sequencing spec for replacing mana-side final prompt rendering with imp-owned final assembly while preserving `mana context` as a human/operator view; and (3) a root-mana traceability repair for the missing 28.1/28.2/28.3 child-unit records so parent/dependency structure is truthful.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:51:  2026-04-09 — decomposition from 28.4 between-turn review spec has now been externalized. Agreed rollout order to preserve in the root graph: (1) imp-first runtime contract for a canonical turn-batched mana delta, replacing per-action review noise with one per-turn review object that includes explicit `no_change`; (2) imp compact between-turn review block plus an expanded textual local inspector for touched units/subtree/decisions/deps, asking only for consequential unresolved choices; (3) Wizard later consumes the same delta semantics as an optional review queue / focus-room entry / graph-highlighting layer, but must not be required for the first usable version. Concrete follow-on child jobs should track these phases separately so imp can ship the mandatory review affordance before any Wizard dependency.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:56:  - 28.5.6 `Implement turn-scoped mana review packet aggregation in imp-core` — runtime-side aggregation unit grounded in `imp-core/src/agent.rs`, `tools/mod.rs`, and `tools/mana.rs`; goal is to introduce an explicit turn-level packet/state (`no_change` / `changed` / `needs_decision`) and stop relying on last-write-wins per-call widget updates as the durable turn story.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:57:  - 28.5.7 `Render between-turn mana review packets across imp TUI and CLI/RPC surfaces` — presentation unit depending on 28.5.6; uses existing `set_widget`/`set_status` surfaces in `imp-tui` and `imp-cli` to show one compact post-turn summary and ask only for consequential unresolved choices.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:58:  - 28.5.8 `Design Wizard as a richer consumer of between-turn mana review packets` — later design/spec unit for canvas/card/graph inspection downstream of the imp-first implementation, explicitly not a v1 dependency.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:68:  - 28.5.3 = canonical turn-batched mana delta contract
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:85:  2026-04-09 decomposition update after 28.5.1 implementation: the mana-first migration now has a clearer staged split. `28.5.1` is the prompt-doctrine layer and is implemented/verified in `imp-core/src/system_prompt.rs`. `28.5.2` remains the skills-layer wording follow-on so skill guidance matches the prompt doctrine. `28.5.3` / `28.5.4` / `28.5.6` / `28.5.7` remain the runtime/UI follow-ons for canonical between-turn mana delta contracts, aggregation, and rendering. This keeps prompt wording, skill wording, and runtime enforcement/reporting as separate durable artifacts rather than collapsing them into one unit.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:90:  - Completed the skill-guidance alignment work for the already-listed child `28.5.2 Update mana skills for conversation-time externalization policy`, but that child currently does not resolve via `mana show` even though it appears in the root index.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:92:    1. Broaden `imp/.imp/skills/mana/SKILL.md` triggers so planning/design conversations that produce durable structure load the mana skill, not only larger execution work.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:95:    4. Add between-turn review-loop guidance: batch related durable changes, update mana before the substantive planning reply when needed, and summarize the mana delta briefly in chat.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:96:    5. Mirror the relevant policy into `imp/.imp/skills/mana-delegation/SKILL.md` without duplicating the full mana skill or weakening its decomposition/worker-handoff focus.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:97:  - Verification used for the doc slice: `grep -q "Do not ask permission merely to capture plan artifacts" imp/.imp/skills/mana/SKILL.md && grep -q "between-turn" imp/.imp/skills/mana/SKILL.md && grep -q "conversation-time" imp/.imp/skills/mana-delegation/SKILL.md`
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:98:  - Result: both skill docs now explicitly describe conversation-time externalization triggers, artifact selection, confirmation thresholds, and between-turn mana-delta review behavior.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:102:  2026-04-09 — 28.5.3 is now complete and defines the canonical turn-batched contract in `docs/rebuild/mana-turn-delta-contract.md`. Preserve this sequencing in follow-on work: (1) 28.5.4 defines imp-side rendering/interaction over the contract, not a new payload; (2) 28.5.5 defines Wizard as a later consumer of the same semantics; (3) 28.5.6 implements turn-scoped assembly in imp-core from mutating mana tool results; (4) 28.5.7 renders the resulting packet across TUI and CLI/RPC surfaces. Canonical field names now fixed by spec: `TurnManaDelta`, `touched_units`, `proposed_children`, `material_field_changes`, `notes_appended`, `decision_events`, `unresolved_consequential_choices`, and `no_change`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:106:  28.5.3 is now closed with docs/rebuild/mana-turn-delta-contract.md as the canonical contract for between-turn review. Durable contract: imp assembles one presentation-neutral TurnManaDelta per assistant turn from successful mutating mana tool calls; state is explicit no_change | changed | needs_decision; canonical fields include anchor_unit, touched_units, proposed_children, material_field_changes, notes_appended, decision_events, and unresolved_consequential_choices. mana remains durable truth; imp owns turn-window assembly and summarization; Wizard is a later consumer of the same semantics, not a separate payload owner.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:110:  Durable decomposition for the between-turn mana review surface is now externalized under 28.5.7. Follow-on units created in root scope: 28.5.7.1 shared imp-core TurnManaDelta renderer plus review-mode config (`off` / `compact_on_change` / `pinned`), 28.5.7.2 imp-tui compact widget-tray block plus textual inspector, and 28.5.7.3 imp-cli/RPC rendering of the same semantics. 28.5.7.2 and 28.5.7.3 both depend on 28.5.7.1, and the whole rendering track continues to depend on 28.5.6 for canonical turn packet assembly. This preserves the plan outside chat and keeps the UI work explicitly line-oriented first, without requiring a graph browser or component widgets.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:114:  2026-04-09 — externalized the closed 28.5.5 Wizard-later review design into root mana because the child is now archived. Durable downstream plan from `docs/rebuild/wizard-mana-review-queue.md`: Wizard remains a later optional consumer of canonical `TurnManaDelta`, not a separate payload owner and not a dependency for the first usable review flow. The later Wizard slice should project canonical turn fields into (1) review queue entries, (2) focus-room entry points, (3) touched-cluster canvas highlights, and (4) unresolved-decision badges, while reading broader branch context directly from mana graph state. Minimum runtime feed remains narrow: one frozen completed-turn delta plus ordering metadata; everything else should come from direct mana reads. Preserve sequencing: imp-first contract/rendering stays mandatory in 28.5.6/28.5.7; any Wizard follow-on must stay additive, must not invent Wizard-only semantics, and must not assume a full live backend.
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:118:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/28.5-plan-implementation-migration-for-mana-first-imp-behavior.md:139:Goal: turn the boundary/planning/memory/UI decisions into an implementation roadmap across mana, imp, and possibly Wizard.

.mana/archive/2026/05/314-draft-imp-013-changelog-from-git-history.md:22:  text: "## [Unreleased]\nUse this section for changes that have landed on `main` after the 0.1.3 draft.\n## [0.1.3] - Unreleased\n[Unreleased]: https://github.com/kfcafe/imp/compare/v0.1.3...HEAD\n[0.1.3]: https://github.com/kfcafe/imp/compare/v0.1.2...HEAD\ndiff --git a/CHANGELOG.md b/CHANGELOG.md\nindex b095c7f..9752c1b 100644\n--- a/CHANGELOG.md\n+++ b/CHANGELOG.md\n@@ -6,15 +6,43 @@ The format is inspired by [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0\n \n ## [Unreleased]\n \n-Use this section for changes that have landed on `main` but are not published yet.\n+Use this section for changes that have landed on `main` after the 0.1.3 draft.\n \n-Suggested categories:\n+## [0.1.3] - Unreleased\n \n-- Added\n-- Changed\n-- Fixed\n-- Documentation\n-- Internal\n+### Added\n+\n+- Added a native `worktree` tool for creating, listing, and removing git worktrees separately from the general `git` tool.\n+- Added `ask_agent` as the focused helper-agent interface, replacing the older spawn-oriented workflow.\n+- Added `ask_user` schema support, including TUI multi-select prompts.\n+- Added mana `guide` and `template` actions so agents can inspect task/epic/decision/verify/orchestration guidance from the native mana tool.\n+- Added validation hints to mana actions to make schema errors and missing arguments easier for agents to recover from.\n+- Added skill-provided slash command discovery and command-palette display for Lua slash commands.\n+\n+### Changed\n+\n+- Renamed the `shell` tool to `bash` and simplified secret injection for shell commands.\n+- Simplified `git`, `scan`, `edit`, and related tool schemas and metadata so agents receive cleaner, more bounded tool interfaces.\n+- Made anchored edits a first-class edit mode and added explicit line-range support to `read`.\n+- Made TUI `/compact` run asynchronously with visible progress instead of blocking the interface.\n+- Strengthened mana worker context assembly and improved mana unit closure reliability after implementation.\n+- Switched project licensing metadata and repository license text to MPL-2.0.\n+\n+### Fixed\n+\n+- Hardened web read/search schema handling, URL read safety, and YouTube metadata/transcript extraction paths.\n+- Added clearer write overwrite behavior without expanding the write schema surface.\n+- Removed stale spawn references after the helper-agent redesign.\n+- Improved prompt wrapping, selected-tool indicators, tool-call inspector styling, and startup version display in the TUI.\n+\n+### Documentation\n+\n+- Documented Lua slash command behavior in the README.\n+- Added skill-writing reference updates.\n+\n+### Internal\n+\n+- Continued schema hardening across core tools, including `read`, `write`, `edit`, `scan`, `git`, `bash`, `web`, `ask_user`, and mana.\n \n ## [0.1.2] - 2026-04-28\n \n@@ -69,7 +97,8 @@ Initial crates.io release of the imp crate family.\n - Standardized published crates on MIT license metadata.\n - Added crates.io metadata and versioned internal dependencies for published crates.\n \n-[Unreleased]: https://github.com/kfcafe/imp/compare/v0.1.2...HEAD\n+[Unreleased]: https://github.com/kfcafe/imp/compare/v0.1.3...HEAD\n+[0.1.3]: https://github.com/kfcafe/imp/compare/v0.1.2...HEAD\n [0.1.2]: https://github.com/kfcafe/imp/compare/v0.1.1...v0.1.2\n [0.1.1]: https://github.com/kfcafe/imp/compare/v0.1.0...v0.1.1\n [0.1.0]: https://github.com/kfcafe/imp/releases/tag/v0.1.0"
.mana/archive/2026/04/45.10.6.3-sequence-the-first-implementation-slice-that-produ.md:26:verify: test -f .mana/45.10.6.3-sequence-the-first-implementation-slice-that-produ.md && rg -q '^id:' .mana/45.10.6.3-sequence-the-first-implementation-slice-that-produ.md
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:3:title: Review native mana tool schema
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:4:slug: review-native-mana-tool-schema
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:9:acceptance: Produce a schema-first review of the native mana tool that starts from the current exposed JSON schema, maps actions to their actually-used parameters, identifies aliases/convenience fields that add token/reasoning cost, and recommends a canonical slimmer schema. Findings must separate low-risk schema cleanup, runtime validation improvements, and larger architecture proposals. No implementation changes in this unit.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:16:  - One `mana` tool with required `action` and a single large flat `properties` map for every action.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:18:  - This preserves a simple tool identity (`mana` = work graph) but forces the model to read/reason about many irrelevant fields for each action.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:21:  1. Keep one `mana` tool for now; do not split into `mana_read`/`mana_write`/`mana_run` unless the audit finds overwhelming pressure.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:23:     - prefer `scope`, not `mana_scope`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:24:     - prefer `path`, not `mana_dir`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:29:     - `path` targets mana/project location.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:40:  New high-level mana schema goal from user: evaluate whether the native `mana` tool schema/description/output contracts can become self-teaching enough to eliminate or drastically shrink the heavy `mana` skill from routine context. User views removing the mana skill as a major token win. This changes the audit frame: not just slim schema, but decide what guidance belongs in tool schema/descriptions/errors vs system prompt vs optional skill. Need assess whether the tool can teach core mana usage (when to create/update/run/notes/decisions, durable planning doctrine, worker-ready unit requirements) without making schema itself too large. Likely answer may be: tool schema can absorb compact action-level guidance and runtime errors, while the full skill becomes optional/deep reference loaded only for complex orchestration; complete removal may require better built-in affordances/templates/validation rather than prose.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:44:  Mana skill loaded on explicit user instruction. Follow it for unit design, decomposition, retries, and worker handoff. For current mana-tool review: continue as planning/audit, externalize durable design decisions, create worker-ready units only after enough inspection and settled approach. User's key question: can improved mana tool schema/descriptions/runtime affordances eliminate or shrink the token-heavy mana skill?
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:48:  Mana skill loaded on explicit instruction before continuing. Apply it for unit design, decomposition, retries, and worker handoff. User's north star to preserve in smarter mana tool: mana is a spec-driven work graph rather than a monolithic spec file; supports red/green-style TDD via fail_first, acceptance, verify gates; orchestrates subagents in waves through mana tasks; needs context assembly for mana workers; goal is to make native mana affordances smart enough to remove/replace heavy mana skill while preserving this workflow.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:52:  User wants to finalize native mana tool details and identify imp gaps to implement the full vision: mana as self-explaining executable spec substrate, replacing heavy skill with compact schema/errors/guide/templates and stronger worker context/orchestration. Need answer should cover concrete mana tool schema direction plus implementation gaps in imp: schema cleanup, action-specific validation, guide/template actions, worker context assembly, run orchestration UX, prompt/skill loading changes, testing/golden prompt checks, and config/policy boundaries.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:56:  Mana skill loaded via read immediately before finalizing mana tool details. Apply unit design/decomposition/retry/handoff guidance. User asks: finalize mana tool details and identify imp gaps to implement full vision. Must answer with concrete schema/tool design plus gap analysis, and capture any resulting decisions/units durably.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:59:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:64:verify: rg "fn parameters|match action|\"mana_scope\"|\"paths_csv\"|\"dep_add\"" crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs && cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:68:- 'Decision: aim to retire the token-heavy mana skill from default/always-loaded context. Do not move the full skill prose into the JSON schema. Instead use a three-layer approach: (1) tiny always-on system instruction for when to use mana, (2) a slimmer/more opinionated native mana tool schema plus action-specific runtime validation/errors, and (3) on-demand guidance/template affordances for complex orchestration/decomposition. Full mana skill becomes optional deep reference only if still needed.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:69:- 'North-star decision for smarter mana tool: mana should remain a spec-driven work graph, not a monolithic spec file. The preferred workflow is: capture durable product/architecture/spec intent as mana epics/tasks/decisions/notes/facts; use red/green-style verify gates (`fail_first`, explicit `verify`, acceptance criteria) to make units executable; then orchestrate subagents in waves through native mana run/run_state/logs/agents until work is verified/closed. Smarter native mana affordances should preserve this workflow while reducing reliance on the token-heavy mana skill.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:70:- 'Updated wording decision: avoid framing the mana skill as defunct/deleted/retired in current implementation units. The desired product direction is positive: native mana should become increasingly self-explaining through compact schema, validation hints, guide/template actions, worker context assembly, and orchestration outputs; the skill should remain concise and aligned with that direction while it exists.'
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:73:Review and refine the native `mana` tool schema and behavior so mana can become a self-explaining executable spec substrate with less reliance on long external guidance.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:76:- Keep a single native `mana` tool instead of splitting into multiple tools.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:80:- Improve worker context assembly so mana units become the subagent handoff contract.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:85:- Remove model-facing aliases: `mana_scope`, `mana_dir`, `paths_csv`, legacy `kind=job`.
.mana/archive/2026/04/304.6-review-native-mana-tool-schema.md:108:3. Audit/improve mana worker context assembly.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4.9.6-build-scheduler-leases-path-locks-runs-and-coordin.md:14:  Implemented scheduler foundation in crates/imp-work/src/scheduler.rs. Added WorkerProfile, LeaseRequest, LeaseRecord, WorkOutcome, CoordinatorSummary, Scheduler, SchedulerError. Scheduler supports adding tasks, ready_queue over TaskStatus::Ready not already leased, lease_ready with optional preferred_work_id, worker profile, worktree, and path_locks; heartbeat updates logical tick; complete releases locks, updates task status from RunOutcome, records Run and structured WorkOutcome; summary reports ready/leased/done/blocked/failed/needs_context/path_conflicts and recent compact outcomes without transcripts. Path locks conflict on same/prefix paths. Added implementer/researcher worker profiles. Verification passed: cargo test -p imp-work scheduler; cargo test -p imp-work lease; cargo test -p imp-work path_lock; cargo test -p imp-work outcome; cargo test -p imp-work. Note: mana verify command form with all filters in one cargo test command is invalid for cargo; tests were run as separate filters.
.mana/archive/2026/05/276-investigate-and-harden-tui-esc-cancellation-for-hu.md:26:  Caveat: repo was already dirty with unrelated Kimi/TypeScript-extension/sidebar work; this task touched only `crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs`, `crates/imp-tui/src/app.rs`, and mana metadata. The original unit verify command remains malformed because Cargo accepts only one test filter; use the verified split commands above.

.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:3:title: Fix mana unit closure reliability after implementation
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:4:slug: fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:13:  Implemented targeted first fix in `crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs`: `mana close force=true` now requires a non-empty reason, and the reason is recorded in structured details as `equivalent_verify_evidence`; close errors that look verify/command related now return structured details with `verify_related=true`, `force_requires_reason=true`, and a recovery hint telling agents to run equivalent focused checks and close with force=true + evidence. Added tests `close_force_requires_reason_with_evidence`, `close_verify_errors_include_recovery_hint`, and `close_non_verify_errors_stay_plain`. Verify passed: `cargo test -p imp-core close_force -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core close_verify_errors -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core close_non_verify_errors -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run`. Repo has unrelated staged CHANGELOG.md; do not include it in this task commit.
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:15:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:20:close_reason: 'Completed closure reliability first fix. Current HEAD f285c7c includes the mana close recovery implementation; repo is clean. Evidence passed: `cargo test -p imp-core close_force -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core close_verify_errors -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core close_non_verify_errors -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run`. This directly addresses stale/invalid verify closure recovery by requiring force-close reasons, recording equivalent verify evidence, and returning structured recovery hints for verify-related close failures.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:22:verify: cargo test -p imp-core mana -- --nocapture && cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:32:  notes: 'Completed closure reliability first fix. Current HEAD f285c7c includes the mana close recovery implementation; repo is clean. Evidence passed: `cargo test -p imp-core close_force -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core close_verify_errors -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core close_non_verify_errors -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p imp-core mana --no-run`. This directly addresses stale/invalid verify closure recovery by requiring force-close reasons, recording equivalent verify evidence, and returning structured recovery hints for verify-related close failures.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:50:Bug/design gap discovered during tool audit: imp eagerly creates mana tasks, completes/commits implementation, but often leaves the mana unit open or in_progress when native close/verify fails due to stale or invalid verify commands. Later agents become over-conservative because they lack immediate session memory and see failed/stale mana status despite commit/test evidence. Need improve the completion loop so implementation evidence, corrected equivalent verifies, and commits can close units reliably or mark them as completed-with-override instead of leaving stale blockers.
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:54:Scope: diagnose native mana close/verify behavior and imp's completion discipline. Add runtime/tool UX changes if appropriate: close should accept explicit equivalent verify evidence with force reason; failed verify should produce actionable corrected-command hints where possible; completion summaries should remind/require closing claimed unit after successful commit; mana tree/status should distinguish stale failed verify from current completion evidence.
.mana/archive/2026/05/313.2-fix-mana-unit-closure-reliability-after-implementa.md:56:Files likely relevant: crates/imp-core/src/tools/mana.rs, crates/imp-core/src/agent.rs, crates/imp-core/src/mana_worker.rs, mana close/verify implementation in mana crates if outside imp-core.
.mana/archive/2026/05/44.4-add-work-tool-next-ready-task-action.md:55:Add a small scheduler-facing action to the imp-core work tool so agents can ask for the next ready native imp-work tasks without using mana.
.mana/archive/2026/05/264.4-audit-and-fix-imp-session-index-lifecycle-wiring-f.md:26:  2026-05-20 lifecycle audit completed in `docs/rebuild/imp-session-index-lifecycle-audit.md`. Production search found `SessionIndex::index_session(...)` only in tests, not in CLI/TUI/session runtime lifecycle. `SessionIndex` implementation is usable and tested, but indexing is unwired in production; raw sessions can exist while `recall` reports no sessions indexed. Recommendation: add an imp-core explicit indexing/rebuild helper (`index_session_file`, `index_session_manager`, `rebuild_global_session_index`), improve missing-index messaging, then add manual reindex command and later cautious lifecycle hooks. Keep storage path policy aligned with canonical `~/.imp/sessions` and `~/.imp/indexes/session_index.db`; legacy stores are migration inputs.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-workflow-evidence-artifact-writing-behind-wor.md:20:  Implementation complete. Workflow contract/evidence artifact writing moved behind workflow runtime integration. `agent/run_loop.rs` no longer imports evidence/eval candidate/trust policy types, no longer directly writes `artifacts.workflow_contract_path()`, and routes evidence/closeout artifact writing through integration methods. Root cause of failed check was that extracted methods were private and lacked `RunFinalStatus` import in `workflow_integration.rs`; fixed by importing `RunFinalStatus` and making the methods `pub(super)`. Verification passed: `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work`, `cargo check -p imp-core`, `mana verify 365.9`, and direct rg inspection showing evidence/eval construction now lives in `workflow_integration.rs` while run_loop keeps only runtime trace/artifact setup and integration calls.

.mana/archive/2026/05/362.6-add-imp-work-archive-and-history-migration-model.md:32:  2026-05-21 implementation complete: `crates/imp-work/src/mana_shadow.rs` now recursively imports `.mana/archive/**/*.md`, records `archived_units`, and preserves history metadata through `ManaHistoryRef { unit_id, source_path, archived, status }`. Archived units retain closed/dropped status mapping and archive/history source preservation is reported as a lossy migration mapping instead of being silently dropped. Added focused archive import test covering active + archived units, closed status mapping, history refs, lossy archive metadata, and non-mutation of source `.mana`. Verification passed: `cargo test -p imp-work mana_migration -- --nocapture` (4 tests) and `cargo test -p imp-work` (74 tests).

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-turn-review-accumulator-into-workflow-ru.md:3:title: Move mana turn review accumulator into workflow runtime integration layer
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-turn-review-accumulator-into-workflow-ru.md:4:slug: move-mana-turn-review-accumulator-into-workflow-ru
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-turn-review-accumulator-into-workflow-ru.md:11:  - `Agent` no longer has a `turn_mana_review` field.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-turn-review-accumulator-into-workflow-ru.md:12:  - `WorkflowRuntimeLayer` owns the `TurnManaReviewAccumulator`.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-turn-review-accumulator-into-workflow-ru.md:14:  - Existing mana review behavior is preserved.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-turn-review-accumulator-into-workflow-ru.md:20:  Implementation complete. `Agent` no longer has a `turn_mana_review` field; `WorkflowRuntimeLayer` owns the `TurnManaReviewAccumulator`. `agent/tool_execution.rs` now gets the accumulator through `self.turn_mana_review_accumulator()`, a named workflow integration accessor. Existing begin/record/finish mana review behavior remains in `workflow_integration.rs`. Verification passed: `mana verify 365.9`, `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work`, `cargo check -p imp-core`, direct field acceptance check, active native scope `work next` returned 0 ready task(s), and `work validate` returned 0 errors/0 warnings.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-turn-review-accumulator-into-workflow-ru.md:55:Continue slimming imp runtime by moving the mana turn review accumulator out of the core `Agent` struct and into `WorkflowRuntimeLayer`.

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-move-mana-turn-review-accumulator-into-workflow-ru.md:59:- `agent/tool_execution.rs` passes that field into `ToolContext` so mana-aware tools can record mutations.
.mana/archive/2026/05/313-improve-impmana-workflow-reliability.md:3:title: Improve imp/mana workflow reliability
.mana/archive/2026/05/313-improve-impmana-workflow-reliability.md:4:slug: improve-impmana-workflow-reliability
.mana/archive/2026/05/313-improve-impmana-workflow-reliability.md:9:acceptance: 'Imp/mana workflows make it easy for agents to keep units correctly scoped and closed: completed units do not remain stale/open due to invalid verify commands, new bug tasks are attached to the right reliability epic rather than unrelated work epics, and tool/runtime guidance nudges agents to keep the graph organized without heavy manual cleanup.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/313-improve-impmana-workflow-reliability.md:11:- mana
.mana/archive/2026/05/313-improve-impmana-workflow-reliability.md:22:Durable product/workflow reliability issues discovered while using mana heavily: agents create and execute mana units but do not reliably keep the graph organized, closed, and scoped. Scope includes unit closure reliability after implementation, stale verify recovery, parent/epic placement hygiene, and agent behaviors that prevent completed work from remaining open or being attached to the wrong epic.

.mana/archive/2026/05/350.4.3-phase-3-move-slow-tui-data-loads-to-background-tas.md:28:  Continued larger plan: moved /resume session listing and selected session open off UI thread. SessionPickerState now has loading state and renders 'Loading sessions…'. /resume immediately opens loading picker and spawns SessionManager::list in spawn_blocking; selecting a session spawns SessionManager::open in spawn_blocking and posts SessionOpened result. Added tests resume_command_opens_loading_session_picker and session_list_load_finishes_into_picker. Verified targeted tests: resume_command_opens_loading_session_picker, session_list_load_finishes_into_picker, session_picker suite, plus event_source/keybindings/terminal/@/git/oauth/send-message tests. Remaining: runtime/agent events still idle-polled; mana navigator/status git still blocking; render cost work not started.
.mana/archive/2026/05/350.4.3-phase-3-move-slow-tui-data-loads-to-background-tas.md:36:  Moved /mana navigator load off UI thread. ManaNavigatorState now supports loading state plus try_load returning Result. open_mana_navigator immediately enters loading overlay, spawns blocking mana tree/detail load in background wrapper task, and posts RuntimeSignal::ManaNavigatorLoaded or ManaNavigatorLoadFailed through runtime_signal_tx. View renders 'Loading mana…' while pending. Added mana_navigator_task tracking and runtime signal handling. Updated slash mana test to tokio runtime. Verified: tui_integration_slash_mana_opens_navigator, mana_navigator tests, event_source tests, session_picker, resume loading test, send-message redraw test.
.mana/archive/2026/05/350.4.3-phase-3-move-slow-tui-data-loads-to-background-tas.md:44:  Committed 5945865 Move TUI improve cleanup work off input path. /improve merge now captures cwd/sandbox and runs changelog checks, git status checks, and optional git merge in spawn_blocking; /clean now runs sandbox status check and git worktree remove in spawn_blocking. Both post RuntimeSignal completions through runtime_signal_tx and show immediate loading messages. Added tests improve_merge_command_opens_background_task, clean_command_opens_background_task, clean_signal_clears_improve_sandbox_when_requested. Verified those plus status background, event_source, send-message deferral, session_picker, and mana_navigator tests.
.mana/archive/2026/05/350.4.3-phase-3-move-slow-tui-data-loads-to-background-tas.md:51:close_reason: 'Implemented and verified for major slow UI data loads: @ file finder deleted; /resume list/open backgrounded; /mana load backgrounded; /status backgrounded; /improve merge and /clean backgrounded with loading/status messages. Targeted tests for each path pass in 350.4.5 smoke suite.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/350.4.3-phase-3-move-slow-tui-data-loads-to-background-tas.md:59:- crates/imp-tui/src/views/mana_navigator.rs
.mana/archive/2026/05/350.4.3-phase-3-move-slow-tui-data-loads-to-background-tas.md:74:Phase 3: move slow UI-triggered data loads to background tasks that post RuntimeSignal/UI state updates: @ file finder project walk, /resume session listing/opening, /mana navigator load, startup skill detail reads, and status/improve git snapshots. Acceptance: each action shows immediate loading/placeholder state and never blocks text input/redraw while data loads.

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3-define-the-slim-imp-runtime-boundary-after-moving.md:11:  - Runtime boundary doc exists and treats imp as an agnostic hostable runtime, not only a mana worker.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3-define-the-slim-imp-runtime-boundary-after-moving.md:12:  - Ownership table lists keep/remove/migrate decisions for imp runtime, imp-work, imp-cli, imp-tui, mana/harness, and external hosts.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3-define-the-slim-imp-runtime-boundary-after-moving.md:15:  - The doc identifies runtime seams that make imp usable outside mana as well as inside mana.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3-define-the-slim-imp-runtime-boundary-after-moving.md:16:  - First implementation slices are listed in safe order and do not depend on completing all mana migration work first.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3-define-the-slim-imp-runtime-boundary-after-moving.md:20:  Scope correction from user: this unit should lead the planning now. It is not mainly a transition/upgrade-back-to-mana task. It is the independent slimming/cutting of imp into a more agnostic, flexible, hostable runtime. The mana daemon/harness is one important host, but the runtime boundary should be designed so imp can also be embedded or driven by other hosts. Prioritize defining imp's stable runtime interfaces, adapters, and internal/external ownership boundaries before migration details.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3-define-the-slim-imp-runtime-boundary-after-moving.md:29:- mana

.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3-define-the-slim-imp-runtime-boundary-after-moving.md:44:Specify what imp should own as a flexible, embeddable, agentic runtime independent of any single work orchestrator. This is not only a migration-back-to-mana task; it is a product/runtime redesign task to cut imp down to its strongest core and make it more agnostic, hostable, and adaptable.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3-define-the-slim-imp-runtime-boundary-after-moving.md:47:- What is imp when it is not a task manager?
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3-define-the-slim-imp-runtime-boundary-after-moving.md:57:- How does imp remain useful outside mana while still fitting cleanly inside a mana daemon/harness?
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.3-define-the-slim-imp-runtime-boundary-after-moving.md:59:Goal: imp becomes a production-grade, hostable agent runtime with a small stable surface. Mana/harness is one host, not the only possible host. imp should be the best runtime for agentic work, not a competing system of record.
.mana/archive/2026/05/340.1.1-inventory-imp-tui-parity-requirements-for-imp-gui.md:21:  - Transcript/session: load prior session messages from SessionManager active messages like App::load_session_messages, render roles and assistant tool blocks like views/chat.rs DisplayMessage/DisplayAssistantBlock.
.mana/archive/2026/05/340.1.1-inventory-imp-tui-parity-requirements-for-imp-gui.md:73:Inventory imp-tui behavior and turn it into the executable implementation checklist for imp-gui parity. Current state: imp-gui is only a mocked plain shell and is not functionally equivalent. Inspect imp-tui app/runtime/editor/chat/sidebar/overlay flows and imp-core ImpSession/AgentEvent APIs. Produce a concise parity map in mana notes covering: session bootstrap/config/auth, transcript loading/rendering, editor/keybindings/history/paste, submit/steer/follow-up/cancel flow, AgentEvent -> UI state mapping, tool calls/sidebar/output/approval UI requests, overlays (model selector, command palette, file finder, settings/personality/secrets/login/session picker), scrolling/focus/selection, slash commands, compaction/context/status indicators, and persistence. Non-goal: implementing code in this inventory task.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-introduce-bounded-subagent-runtime-dtos-and-events.md:12:  - Types are runtime-scoped and contain no mana/work graph concepts such as task IDs, leases, claims, board status, or durable lifecycle state.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-introduce-bounded-subagent-runtime-dtos-and-events.md:20:  Implementation checkpoint: added `crates/imp-core/src/agent/subagent.rs` with bounded subagent runtime DTOs/events/outcomes and exported them from `agent/mod.rs`. Types are runtime-scoped: `ParentRunId`, `SubagentRunId`, roles, input/context/artifact refs/resource limits/merge policy/status/outcome/confidence/events. No mana task IDs, leases, claims, board status, scheduling, or durable lifecycle concepts are present. Added tests for terminal statuses and event serialization avoiding task/lease/board terms. Also restored test-only re-exports for moved workflow helper tests after the earlier workflow split: `workflow_layer_may_override_finish` and `mana_run_status_from_result`; `mana_run_status_from_result` needed crate visibility in `mana_compat.rs` so the test-only facade re-export works. Verification passed: `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core`, `cargo check -p imp-core`, `cargo test -p imp-core subagent --lib`, full task verify command `cargo fmt --check -p imp-core -p imp-work && cargo check -p imp-core`, `mana verify 365.9`, native `work validate` 0 errors/0 warnings, native `work next` 0 ready task(s).
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-introduce-bounded-subagent-runtime-dtos-and-events.md:58:- mana owns durable work orchestration across runs and runbooks as system-of-record artifacts.
.mana/archive/2026/05/365.9-introduce-bounded-subagent-runtime-dtos-and-events.md:62:- No mana task IDs.
.mana/archive/2026/05/341-audit-and-strengthen-imp-test-coverage-by-risk-are.md:13:  User direction: imp-gui is not in active development, so do not prioritize its zero-test gap right now. Continue improving imp's test suite elsewhere, especially core/runtime/tool/mana/provider/TUI areas and first fix the workspace test compilation blocker.
.mana/archive/2026/05/341-audit-and-strengthen-imp-test-coverage-by-risk-are.md:14:design: 'Current priority order: 1) restore workspace test compilation (`cargo test --workspace --all-targets --no-run`) by fixing the imp-core test compile errors; 2) audit/add tests for active core agent/runtime loop behavior, especially long-session safety and retry/assessment boundaries; 3) audit/add tests for tool execution boundaries, policy enforcement, and tool error handling; 4) audit/add tests for mana tool dispatch/orchestration/rendering behavior; 5) audit/add tests for provider/auth/streaming edge cases in imp-llm; 6) audit/add tests for TUI input/cursor/bounds panic boundaries; 7) review imp-lua sandbox/bridge edge cases. Deprioritize imp-gui zero-test gap until imp-gui becomes active again. Prefer small targeted regression tests and crate/filter-specific verify commands before broad workspace checks.'
.mana/archive/2026/05/363-review-and-resolve-remaining-dirty-worktree-slices.md:54:Review and resolve remaining dirty worktree files after imp-work parity commits, without mixing unrelated changes into mana-removal work.
.mana/archive/2026/05/363-review-and-resolve-remaining-dirty-worktree-slices.md:72:4. Do not combine these changes with imp-work mana-removal commits.
